# Probes tried — master log

Every probe shape (regardless of outcome) goes here. Before constructing a new probe, **scan this file** to avoid duplicates.

Each row: probe shape (concise), result (CONFIRMED-Fxx | FOLDED-Fxx | SHAPE-REJECTED | INCONCLUSIVE), file path, date.

## Format

`<one-line shape>` — `<result>` — `<probe file or /tmp/...>` — `<YYYY-MM-DD>`

For each result type:
- **CONFIRMED-Fxx**: new defect class, filed as Fxx
- **FOLDED-Fxx**: same root cause as Fxx, no separate filing
- **SHAPE-REJECTED**: BSC parser/Sema/Prologue prevents the construct from reaching the hypothesized analyzer state
- **INCONCLUSIVE**: probe compiled but didn't surface a defect (analyzer behavior matched expected — invariant holds)

## Probes — by date (newest first)

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — GENERICS × _Borrow LOAN/LIFETIME tracking through MONOMORPHIZATION (BSCBorrowChecker) — SATURATED-SOUND
Focus (per directive): does monomorphization lose borrow-LOAN/LIFETIME tracking inside a
generic body/struct (distinct machinery from ownership/moves: loans+liveness, not setToMoved)?
HYPOTHESIS: the G12 nullability-attribute-sugar strip might have a `_Borrow`-loan analogue.
Distinct = BORROW-CHECKER loan tracking through a monomorphized generic body/struct, NOT
rewriter mangling (G14) or ownership (bi9znvfyo sibling — _Owned-through-monomorph, refuted).
Every probe has a non-generic BASELINE at parity; all on fresh bin 34e6f26e.
- shape 1: `use2<T>(T a,T b){}` + `use2<int*_Borrow>(&_Mut x,&_Mut x)` (two simultaneous mut borrows through generic) → REJECTED "cannot borrow x as mutable more than once" (exit 1), IDENTICAL to non-generic `use2(&_Mut x,&_Mut x)`. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.use_generic.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.use2.cbs
- shape 2: `int*_Borrow fwdg<T>(T b){return b;}` + caller passes `&_Mut local` of a dying local → REJECTED "local does not live long enough / dropped while still borrowed" (exit 1), IDENTICAL to non-generic `fwd`. Returned-borrow dangling detection through generic. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.fwdg_dangling.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.dangling_return3.cbs
- shape 3: `struct Ref<T>{T b;}; struct Ref<int*_Borrow> r={.b=&_Mut x}; int*_Borrow q=&_Mut x;` (field-held loan through generic struct) → REJECTED (exit 1), IDENTICAL to non-generic. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.ref_field_generic.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.ref_field.cbs
- shape 4: call-site conflict — caller holds `outer=&_Mut x`, calls `g<int*_Borrow>(&_Mut x)` → REJECTED "cannot borrow x as mutable more than once" (x already mut-borrowed), parity non-generic. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.body_write_through_b.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.body_write_through_b.cbs
- shape 5: NLL write-while-borrowed INSIDE the generic body (`b=&_Mut local; local=2; (void)*b;`) → REJECTED "cannot assign to local because it is borrowed"; last-use boundary respected. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.nll_inside_body.cbs
- shape 6: generic struct `_Borrow` field passed BY VALUE (`sink(struct Ref<int*_Borrow>)`) then concurrent `&_Mut x` → CLEAN (exit 0), parity non-generic (both clean — correct NLL; not a gap). SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.generic_struct_borrow_copyvalue.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.struct_borrow_copyvalue.cbs
- shape 7: generic returns struct holding `_Borrow` of a dying local → REJECTED "local dropped while still borrowed", parity non-generic. Dangling-borrow-through-generic-return caught. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.generic_return_struct_borrow_dangling.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.return_struct_borrow_dangling.cbs
- shape 8 (F109-fold check): generic struct field reassign `r.b=&_Mut y` then `&_Mut x` → CLEAN (exit 0), parity non-generic (both clean — field-reassign correctly NLL-kills the loan; no F109-style gap through the generic field). SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.f109_field_through_generic.cbs ; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.field_reassign.cbs
NET: no-new-pattern. The GENERICS × _Borrow LOAN/LIFETIME surface is SATURATED-SOUND on 34e6f26e:
every loan/lifetime dimension (creation, freeze/conflict at call site, NLL last-use kill inside
the body, field-held loan through a generic struct, dangling-return detection, by-value-copy loan
propagation) is at PARITY with the non-generic baseline. Hypothesis REFUTED: monomorphization
does NOT lose borrow-loan tracking — `_Borrow` is a real qualifier surviving getCanonicalType
(only nullability AttributedType-SUGAR is stripped, per G12), so DefUse/ActionExtract/LoansInScope/
Liveness see the borrow on the monomorphized body exactly as if hand-written. This is the _Borrow-loan
analogue of the bi9znvfyo sibling's _Owned-through-monomorph refutation (both survive; only G12
nullability sugar is stripped). DISTINCT from G14 (rewriter mangle), G12 (nullability sugar, not
_Borrow qualifier), F109/F39/F111 (non-generic loan/move sites). Recommend next: pivot OFF
generics×borrow substitution (exhausted-sound); residual F109-style loan-kill gaps routed through
generics FOLD into the filed non-generic F109 root (same UsesArraySubscriptNotation collapse /
field-liveness mechanism, no generic-specific divergence).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — STRUCT-FIELD borrow holder: reassign does NOT NLL-kill prior loan (CONFIRMED-new, FP)
- shape: `struct B{int *_Borrow _Nullable f;} s; s.f=&_Mut x; (void)*s.f; s.f=&_Mut y; int *_Borrow q=&_Mut x;`
  → CONFIRMED-new (FP: `cannot borrow x as mutable more than once at a time`; exit 1) — /tmp/explorer_repro.field_holder_loan_not_killed.cbs
- asymmetry baseline (scalar twin): `int *_Borrow p=&_Mut x; (void)*p; p=&_Mut y; int *_Borrow q=&_Mut x;`
  → CLEAN (exit 0; scalar reassign correctly NLL-kills x's loan) — /tmp/explorer_baseline.field_holder_scalar_twin.cbs
- variant A (field reassign to nullptr): `s.f=nullptr; &_Mut x` → CLEAN (null-drop ends loan via different path)
- variant B (whole-struct reassign): `s=(struct B){.f=&_Mut y}; &_Mut x` → CLEAN (whole-struct overwrite ends loan)
- variant C (reassign DIFFERENT field, control): `s.f=&_Mut x; s.g=&_Mut y; &_Mut x` → REJECTED (correct: s.f still borrows x)
  → sanity check: the checker is not blindly accepting; the gap is specifically same-field reassign to a new borrow.
- dump proof: field-case first x-loan region = [BB1/5..BB1/16] (extends to fn-end, NOT killed at the s.f=&_Mut y
  reassign at BB1/10); scalar twin first x-loan region = [BB1/4, BB1/5] (ends at the reassign). Root: loan driven by
  whole-struct `s` liveness (live until StorageDead:s); field reassignment does not model that the field's prior
  referent is dropped. LoansKilledByWriteTo(s.f) correctly does NOT fire (x-loan path `x`, prefixes {x}, no `s.f`).
  DISTINCT from F109 (subscript UsesArraySubscriptNotation collapse, arr[0]=&_Mut y) + F39 (subscript write over-kill FN):
  this is the MemberExpr FIELD holder — different AST path, different root cause (field liveness vs subscript path collapse).
  Scope-clean (struct + _Borrow + _Safe; no OOS keywords). Severity MEDIUM (FP, rejects valid _Safe code, no soundness hole;
  scalar-equivalent runs clean under valgrind ERROR SUMMARY 0).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — GENERICS × _Owned through monomorphization (G12-ownership-analogue hypothesis)
- HYPOTHESIS: the known nullability-sugar strip at template-arg substitution (G12, _probed.md:28/6105,
  filed MEDIUM) may have an OWNERSHIP analogue — instantiating a generic with `T=int*_Owned` could
  strip/mistrack the `_Owned` AttributedType sugar on the monomorphized decl, so the body ownership
  analysis (leak/move/drop) treats the param/field as raw and the move/leak/drop is NOT tracked.
  ALL PROBES on fresh bin 34e6f26e → **SOUND / hypothesis REFUTED**: `_Owned` is NOT stripped
  (it survives in the mangled specialization name `sink<int *_Owned>`), and the body/field/return/
  caller ownership gates all fire correctly. Re-confirms _probed.md:5648-5649 ("getCanonicalType
  keeps real qualifiers `_Owned`/`_Borrow`, strips only attribute-sugar nullability") + the 2026-06-17
  gm1/go1/gorun SOUND results on the current binary. No ownership-analogue of G12 exists here.
- BASELINE: non-generic `sink(int*_Owned x){(void)x;}` → "memory leak of value: x" (rc=1, reference behavior) — /tmp/explorer_baseline.xHLNkT.cbs
- shape: `sink<T>(T x){}` + `sink<int*_Owned>(p)`, body drops x → SOUND: "memory leak of value: x" FIRES (rc=1); note shows specialization `sink<int *_Owned>` (_Owned survives) — /tmp/explorer_probe.7B4NR3.cbs
- shape: `id<T>(T x){return x;}` + `id<int*_Owned>(p)` then `safe_free(p)` → SOUND: move diagnosed "incompatible _Owned types, cannot cast 'int *_Owned' to 'void *_Owned _Nullable'" (return-move tracked) — /tmp/explorer_probe.IXGZDM.cbs
- shape: `struct Box<T>{T v;}` + `Box<int*_Owned>` dropped w/o freeing v → SOUND: "field memory leak of value: b, b.v is leak" FIRES; vg rejects at compile (no silent codegen leak) — /tmp/explorer_probe.BoJWu4.cbs
- shape: `Box<int*_Owned _Nullable>` null-field interaction (cast-of-0 idiom) → SHAPE-REJECTED (cast `(int*_Owned _Nullable)0` + global `_Owned` forbidden identically in generic & non-generic; both reject same way, no asymmetry — test-artifact) — /tmp/explorer_probe.TtCz1k.cbs, /tmp/explorer_probe.6fCKD1.cbs
- shape: `make<T>(void){return ...}` + `make<int*_Owned>()`, caller drops p → SOUND: "memory leak of value: p" FIRES; identical to non-generic baseline (return-side owned tracked through substitution) — /tmp/explorer_probe.PKLWXK.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.fzpETA.cbs
- DISTINCT-FROM: G10 (owned-array bypass via `typedef Arr<T>=T[N]` — declarator-vs-substitution check-skip, NOT sugar-strip; owned-array only), G14 (struct mangle collision — TypePrinter record-name path drops qual from the *printed name*, Sema still tracks the type distinctly; not a tracking FN), G12 (nullability sugar strip — the nullability dimension; ownership dimension is SOUND here per these probes). NOTE: SemaTemplateInstantiateDeclBSC.cpp:79-225 is actually `VisitBSCMethodDecl` (member fns, OUT OF SCOPE); the free-function/generic-struct sugar-strip happens in the CORE clang template-arg-substitution path (SubstFunctionType/SubstType/TreeTransform), shared with non-BSC SemaTemplateInstantiate. Note: code_notes/SemaTemplateInstantiateDeclBSC.md.

### 2026-05-30 — bsc-explorer cycle — Chain P: strict vs non-strict pointer-assignment compat SELECTION (NO-NEW)

Chain P = the TWO pointer-assignment-compat predicates over the shared Impl (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:395).
Exhaustive caller table (tree-wide grep, confirmed):
- non-strict `DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraints` → ONE caller: `CheckCallAssignmentConstraints`
  (:335), the heterogeneous-redecl SELECTION gate for a function CALL (AllowImplicitConversions=true).
- strict `...Strict` → ONE caller: `BorrowParamTypesMatch` in `CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType`
  (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:891), fnptr-assignment borrow-variance (false).
- Impl(false) direct → `DoesFunctionPointerSatisfyConstraints` (:515,:527), fnptr-assignment.

KEY STRUCTURAL FINDING (why the non-strict laxity is NOT reachable as a soundness gap):
the non-strict caller operates ONLY on heterogeneous-redecl candidates, which by C redecl rules
have IDENTICAL underlying C types and differ ONLY by BSC qualifiers (_Owned/_Borrow/_ArrayElem/SZ).
So the non-strict path's C-implicit-conversion laxity (base-type int→char :416, single-level
const-drop on pointee :470) is DEAD for this caller — there is never a base-type or single-level-C
difference between two redecls. The only thing the gate discriminates is BSC qualifiers, handled by
`AreBSCPointerQualifiersCompatible` (:432, SHARED with strict, outer-level). And whatever the lax
gate accepts at the pointee level, the POST-SELECTION BSC arg-conversion (CheckBSCQualTypeAssignment
/ CheckOwnedQualTypeAssignment / CheckBorrowQualTypeAssignment / CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType) is
the real soundness gate and re-checks it. The gate is a selection heuristic, not the soundness check.

- probe1 nested `_Borrow`-of-`_Borrow` hetero param `const int*_Borrow*_Borrow` — SHAPE-REJECTED (`int*_Borrow` cannot be re-_Borrow-qualified; redecl-incompatible) — /tmp/explorer_probe.q86fFP.cbs
- probe2 void* relaxation: `int*_Borrow` arg → `void*_Borrow` _Safe param via selection — INCONCLUSIVE→SOUND (accepted; sound widening, borrow qual preserved) — /tmp/explorer_probe.GdK5YB.cbs
- probe3 nested OWNED mismatch hetero call `int**` arg → `int*_Owned*` _Safe param — SOUND (REJECTED by post-selection CheckOwnedQualTypeAssignment, "incompatible _Owned types") — /tmp/explorer_probe.0JeXVw.cbs
- probe4 int→char narrowing via non-strict base-type laxity (`_Safe cfun(char)`/`_Unsafe cfun(long)`) — SHAPE-REJECTED (hetero redecls must have compatible C types; `char`/`long` is a hard redecl conflict → the int→char laxity at Impl:416 is UNREACHABLE from this caller) — /tmp/explorer_probe.9oJdGD.cbs
- probe5 nested const-DROP on borrow-pointee `const int*_Borrow*` arg → `int*_Borrow*` param — SOUND (REJECTED AT THE GATE itself; non-strict `typesAreCompatible` on pointee only strips ONE level of const, the deeper const survives → "no matching declaration") — /tmp/explorer_probe.ZVHEx8.cbs
- probe6 `_ArrayElem` reborrow asymmetry at the call gate: plain `_Borrow`→`_Borrow _ArrayElem` REJECTED; `_Borrow _ArrayElem`→plain `_Borrow` ACCEPTED — SOUND (AreBSCPointerQualifiersCompatible :380-385 directional branch correct both ways) — /tmp/explorer_probe.gyRs3k.cbs + .VJ4gtY.cbs
- probe7 fnptr-param borrow-qual mismatch via call: raw-param fn → `_Safe` param expecting `void(int*_Borrow)` callback — SOUND (REJECTED by post-selection CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType, "incompatible _Borrow function pointer types") — /tmp/explorer_probe.1fXZuo.cbs
- probe8 F76-shape: borrow buried 2 fnptr levels deep (`void(*)(void(*)(int*_Borrow))`) via call path — ACCEPTED (slips) but **FOLDED-F76** (same DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraintsImpl:482 canonical-pointee compare dropping nested fnptr-param quals; the call path reaches the SAME Impl root via CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType→strict Impl — already noted in Chain L as "3rd caller of Impl:482"; NOT a strict-vs-non-strict selection gap) — /tmp/explorer_probe.SEXeM3.cbs

**Conclusion**: Chain P → SATURATED @ 28656aa9. No strict-vs-non-strict SELECTION gap. The non-strict
gate's laxity is (a) UNREACHABLE for base-type/single-level-C differences (hetero redecls share C
types) and (b) MASKED for BSC-qualifier differences by the post-selection conversion. The one slip
(probe8 deep-fnptr-nesting) FOLDS into F76 (Impl:482 root), not a selection defect. Caller table
written to SemaBSCSafeZone.md. Reopen if a NEW non-strict caller is added (where no post-selection
BSC conversion runs), or if hetero-redecls are ever allowed to differ by C base type.

### 2026-05-30 — bsc-explorer cycle — Chain N branches (2)(3)(4): CheckInit ↔ CheckGlobalInit parity (NO-NEW)

Chain N = CheckInit (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:483, function-CFG/static-local path) vs
CheckGlobalInit (SemaDecl.cpp:14812, global path) near-identical clones. Branch (1) = F78
(`!Init` union-narrowing missing in CheckInit). Probed the 3 remaining structural branches:

- (2) pointer-RHS helper divergence `getExprPathNullability` (local) vs `GetExprNK` (global): designated-IN-order `{.nn=&x}` record, static-local vs file-scope — **INCONCLUSIVE→SOUND** (both clean) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.boQSCT.cbs` + `.tvytxx.cbs`
- (2) FN attempt: out-of-order designator placing _Nullable VAR `np` into _Nonnull field — **SHAPE-REJECTED** (global var not a compile-time constant; both scopes reject before nullability check) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.dfxXLY.cbs` + `.ZbiGK7.cbs`
- (3)/(4) FN: out-of-order designator `{.q=&x, .nn=nullptr}` placing constant `nullptr` into non-first _Nonnull field, static-local vs file-scope — **SOUND** (BOTH correctly diag err_nonnull_assigned_by_nullable; `VD->getInit()` delivers SEMANTIC-form ILE to the local path, so syntactic-vs-semantic divergence does not manifest) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.Ka5PNm.cbs` + `.3KNka3.cbs`
- (4) array-recursion `getAsArrayTypeUnsafe` (local) vs `getAsArrayType` (global, moves quals into elem): `int *_Nonnull const arr[2] = {&x}` trailing-default-init, both scopes — **SOUND** (both diag err_nonnull_init_by_default; element `_Nonnull` read identically) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.YCoBeh.cbs` + `.Yxmyfj.cbs`
- (2) array-transfer builtin `__take_array_from_raw(raw)` into `_Nonnull _Owned _ArrayElem` local — **SOUND/benign** (`handleBSCRawTransferBuiltin` SemaChecking.cpp:266-269 propagates arg nullability ONTO the result type, so the global path's `getDefNullability(CE->getType())` fallback equals the local recursion → the 4-builtin vs 2-builtin set divergence is a no-op; globals can't take the non-constant raw arg anyway) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.CN7EPL.cbs`
- (3) `NumInits==0` empty-ILE union `= {}` (first field int, later _Nonnull), static-local vs file-scope — **SOUND** (both clean; the `NumInits==0` union-narrow fix from 09074459 landed in BOTH peers) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.OZYEH1.cbs` + `.4O74zg.cbs`
- AST-form verification: nested designator `{.in.nn=&x, .b=9}` static-local — confirmed `VD->getInit()` returns the fully-reordered SEMANTIC InitListExpr (no DesignatedInitExpr survives), so CheckInit's syntactic-`getInit(idx)` indexing is always correct → the `getSemanticForm()` call in CheckGlobalInit is defensive/redundant, not a parity gap — `/tmp/ast_nested.cbs`
- struct-recursion reaching `!Init` on a NESTED union: `struct S{int a; union{int first; int*_Nonnull nn;}u;} = {.a=1}` (union member missing → recursed with nullptr init → `!Init` branch), static-local rejects / file-scope accepts — **FOLDED-F78** (same `CheckInit` `!Init` branch :488-493 missing the union-narrowing; the nested-via-struct form is reached by the SAME one-line fix surface; only the entry path differs) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.ueNNW8.cbs` + `.KimhzL.cbs`

**Conclusion**: Chain N branches (2)(3)(4) are SOUND at commit 28656aa9. The only live parity
gap on the CheckInit↔CheckGlobalInit clone-pair is branch (1) = F78 (`!Init` union-narrowing),
of which the nested-via-struct form folds. Recommend Chain N → SATURATED @ 28656aa9 (F78 the lone
filing; reopen if CheckInit/CheckGlobalInit/FindNonnull/GetExprNK/getExprPathNullability change).

### 2026-05-20

- BSCIRBuilder `lowerSwitchStmt` FIXME `dyn_cast<CompoundStmt>(Body)` (line 569) — brace-less switch body should bypass region lowering. Probed:
  - `switch (x) case 1: p = mk();` then use → diags use-of-uninit on x!=1 path (correct)
  - `switch (x) default: p = mk();` then use → CLEAN (correct: all paths init p)
  - `switch (x) default: free(p);` runtime → 1 alloc 1 free (correct)
  - **SHAPE-REJECTED**: empirical behavior matches "body IS lowered" despite the FIXME. The FIXME comment is stale or some other code path (Sema wrapping?) makes Body a CompoundStmt before reach. Probes: `repro/probe_braceless_switch_owned_init.cbs`, `repro/probe_braceless_default_init.cbs`, `repro/probe_braceless_default_owned.cbs`.

## Session 2026-05-20 (continued)

- **ActionExtract::VisitCallExpr / DefUse::VisitCallExpr callee not visited** — **CONFIRMED-F24**.
  - Probe: `repro/F24_callee_position_borrow_unchecked.cbs` — `struct S { fn_t fp; }`, mut borrow `mb = &_Mut s`, then `(s.fp)(20)`. Compiles clean despite the read of `s.fp` overlapping `mb`'s loan. Direct-read control `fn_t c = s.fp;` IS flagged at line 14.
  - Root cause: `ActionExtract::VisitCallExpr` (:469-478) iterates `CE->arguments()` only; `CE->getCallee()` never visited. `DefUse::VisitCallExpr` (:117-122) same. `BorrowCheckerPrologue::TransformCallExpr` (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:786-803) hoists args + the CallExpr itself but NOT the callee subexpression — callees like `s.fp`, `*pf`, or `(get_fn())(arg)` reach the analyzer intact and their internal reads vanish.
  - Defect class: **C3 (Visit/switch coverage gap)** — sibling of F11 (`BO_Comma`), F16 (`_Generic` unselected arm).
  - **Filed**: Gitee IJOJQZ.

- **`int *_Owned q = c ? s->f : s->g;` (s `_Borrow`) ternary-wrapped move-through-borrow** — **CONFIRMED-F25 (IJOJX4) 2026-05-20** HIGH.
  - Probe: `repro/F25_ternary_move_through_borrow.cbs`. Compile-time: clean. Runtime under valgrind: 2 allocs / 3 frees → `Invalid free()`.
  - Root: `Sema::CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:547-559) only handles `dyn_cast<UnaryOperator>` and `dyn_cast<MemberExpr>` after `IgnoreParenCasts`. ConditionalOperator matches neither; no recursion into arms. All four call sites (ParseDecl:2517, ParseExpr:646, SemaStmt:3937, SemaExpr:6728) inherit the bypass.
  - Defect class: C1 (Ignore-asymmetry). Distinct from F21 (paren-deref within MemberExpr base) — F25's surface is the function-entry dispatch failing to recurse into wrappers.
  - **Folded follow-ups (NOT to re-file)**: `BO_Comma` wrapper, `BinaryConditionalOperator` (GNU `?:`), `_Generic`-selected-arm wrapper, `__builtin_choose_expr`-selected-arm wrapper, `StmtExpr` result, and any composition of the above. One fix (wrapper-strip + recurse) closes them all.

- **`(s).f = mk()` and `int *_Owned q = (s).f;` paren-wrapped struct member access** — **CONFIRMED-F30 (IJOLEG) 2026-05-20** HIGH.
  - Probe: `repro/F30_paren_struct_member_skips_tracking.cbs`. Write-side: 2 allocs/1 free (silent leak under valgrind). Read-side: false-positive `field memory leak: s.f is leak` at scope end (`/tmp/probe_paren_move_simple.cbs`).
  - Root: `getMemberFullField` (BSCOwnership.cpp:92-109) strips MemberExpr + ImplicitCastExpr in its loop but NOT ParenExpr. For `(s).f` the MemberExpr's base is `ParenExpr(DRE(s))`, returned unchanged. Callers' `dyn_cast<DeclRefExpr>(base)` returns null, dispatch silently skipped.
  - Defect class: C1 (Ignore-asymmetry, wrapper-list incomplete). Distinct from F21 (UnaryDeref base in CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak — different function, different missing wrapper).
  - **FOLDED variant 2026-05-20**: `(*p).f = mk()` and `int *_Owned q = (*p).f;` for p `struct S *_Owned`. Same function `getMemberFullField`, same C1 class — missing wrapper is UnaryDeref instead of ParenExpr. Probes `/tmp/probe_overwrite_test.cbs` and `/tmp/probe_owned_deref_v3.cbs`. Write-side: silent overwrite leak (compare `p->f = mk();` diag "assign to part of _Owned value" vs `(*p).f = mk();` clean); read-side: false-positive `p.f is _Owned` at safe_free site. Distinct from F21 (which fires in Sema's CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak for `_Borrow` base); this surfaces at the dataflow analyzer with `_Owned`-pointer dispatch. Proper fix: normalize `(*p).f` → `p->f` in `getMemberFullField` (treat UnaryDeref-of-pointer + MemberExpr as arrow access).

- **goto crossing into `_Safe { }` block** — **SHAPE-REJECTED 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_goto_into_safe.cbs` (label inside safe block, jumped to from non-safe code, body contains unsafe call). Compiler correctly emits `_Unsafe function call is forbidden in the safe zone` — IsStmtInSafeZone walks parent chain regardless of how the block was entered. The safe-zone enforcement is SYNTACTIC, not control-flow-dependent.

- **file-scope `static int *_Owned _Nullable g = nullptr;`** — **SHAPE-REJECTED 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_static_owned.cbs`. Compiler emits `type of global variable cannot be qualified by '_Owned'`. Static-lifetime + _Owned is blocked at the decl level.

- **Calling Nullable local fn-pointer DRE: `fp(42)` with `fn_t _Nullable fp`** — **FOLDED-F31 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_f31_local_fnptr.cbs`. With `-nullability-check=all`, callee null-check bypassed for same reason as F31's MemberExpr-callee case: `VisitCallExpr` (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:649) iterates `CE->arguments()` only and never inspects the callee expression. F31's fix (check callee's getExprPathNullability) covers both shapes.

- **`_Generic((char)0, char: mk(), default: nullptr)` for `_Owned _Nullable` init — runtime verify F09** — **FOLDED-F09 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_generic_mk_selected.cbs`. valgrind shows 0 allocs / 0 frees despite the selected arm being `mk()`. Confirms F09's codegen drops the selected-arm side-effect. q ends up garbage/zero; the `if (q != nullptr)` branch happens to take the null path so no crash. Folded into F09 (IJO88R).

- **`int *_Owned q = (struct S){.f = mk(), .g = mk()}.f;` un-consumed CompoundLiteral temp field** — **POSTED-AS-F32 (IJOM06) but FOLDED into F20 on re-audit 2026-05-20** HIGH at runtime.
  - Probe: `repro/F32_compound_literal_temp_unconsumed_field.cbs`. Compile-clean. valgrind: 2 allocs / 1 free + "definitely lost".
  - **Root cause** (corrected): same as F20. `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` IS invoked at `SemaExprMember.cpp:1288` with `BaseExpr=CompoundLiteralExpr` when building the `.f` access; F20's predicate-extension fix (recognize CompoundLiteralExpr) would catch F32 too. Earlier filing incorrectly claimed distinct fix surface.
  - **Lesson**: "different invocation site" ≠ "different fix surface". When the fix is at the called predicate, all call-chain sites are covered by one change.
  - Sibling probes (don't re-file): `(struct Outer){.i = (struct Inner){.ix=mk()}, .ox=mk()}.ox;` (nested compound-lit, `/tmp/probe_nested_clit_field.cbs`). Same fold.

- **`s->f = mk();` overwrite via `_Borrow struct *` silently leaks** — **CONFIRMED-F34 (IJOM8W) 2026-05-20** HIGH.
  - Probe: `repro/F34_overwrite_owned_field_via_borrow.cbs`. Compile-clean. valgrind: 2 allocs / 1 free + "definitely lost". Control case `s: struct S *_Owned` correctly diagnoses overwrite.
  - Root: `IsTrackedType` (BSCOwnership.cpp:58-69) excludes `_Borrow` pointers to owned-bearing structs. Param init at runOwnershipAnalysis:2660 gated on `IsTrackedType`, so `s` enters NO tracking map. `HandleDREAssign` for `s->f` finds VD in no map, falls through silently.
  - Sibling of F21 (READ direction via `(*s).f` in Sema's CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak) and F30 (paren-wrapper at getMemberFullField). F34 is the WRITE direction at the dataflow-analyzer entry-point type-recognition. Each needs its own fix.
  - **FOLDED variant 2026-05-20**: `*pp = mk();` where `pp: int *_Owned *_Borrow`. Same root: IsTrackedType returns false for the borrow-pointer-to-owned, so pp enters no tracking map; HandleDREAssign for `*pp` falls through. valgrind confirms 2 allocs / 1 free + "definitely lost" (`/tmp/probe_borrow_to_owned_overwrite.cbs`). One IsTrackedType fix covers both.

- **Array of owned via typedef: `typedef int *_Owned IntOwned; IntOwned arr[3];`** — **SHAPE-REJECTED 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_typedef_owned_array.cbs`. Compiler emits `type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned', 'IntOwned' (aka 'int *_Owned') contains '_Owned' type`. Sema's array-of-owned check fires through the typedef. ✓

- **fnptr-param-pointee qualifier mismatch: `fn_owned ←/→ fn_borrow` cross-assign** — **INCONCLUSIVE 2026-05-20** (correctly rejected).
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_fnptr_pointee_qualifier_mismatch.cbs`. Both directions (`int *_Owned *` fnptr ↔ `int *_Borrow *` fnptr) are rejected by `CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType` via `DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraintsStrict`. The `removeLocalOwned`/`removeLocalBorrow` strip in `BorrowParamTypesMatch` (SemaBSCOwnership:897-900) is bypassed by the strict-pointer-types check at line 888 firing first.

- **`BuildAction` Assign uses only `Sources[0]` — dropped second-arm borrow in `__builtin_choose_expr`** — **INCONCLUSIVE 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_sources0_dropped_arm.cbs` (`int *_Borrow b = __builtin_choose_expr(0, &_Const x, &_Const y);` with `int *_Borrow my = &_Mut y;` live) — y-mut+y-const conflict IS correctly detected.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_choose_dangling_v2.cbs` (selected arm has shorter lifetime, returned through borrow param) — `y does not live long enough` is correctly emitted.
  - Reason: loans/regions are registered at the point of `&_Mut`/`&_Const` visit (per-arm), not only via `Sources[0]`. `BuildAction`'s `Sources[0]`-only usage at line 238/247 only affects the outer assignment/borrow action's recorded path, not the loan generation. Per-arm region constraints are added at line 247 (RNR) — which gets overwritten by the LAST arm visited, but loans from all arms persist in LoansInScope and are checked by `BorrowCk`.
  - This is a CODE SMELL (`Sources[0]` instead of full vector) but not exploitable from valid BSC.

- **AllMoved|Owned merge state in BSCOwnership** — **SHAPE-REJECTED 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_merge_allmoved_owned.cbs`. Cast `(int*_Owned)void_owned` is forbidden in safe zone (`error: conversion from type 'void *_Owned' to 'int *_Owned' is forbidden`). So the only path to `setToAllMoved` (`IsCastFromVoidPointer` at VisitDeclStmt:2326 / VisitBinaryOperator:2167) requires the user to be in unsafe code. The merged ambiguous state isn't constructible from safe BSC.

- **`(void *_Owned)(c ? p1 : p2)` ConditionalOperator subexpr of cast-to-void*** — **INCONCLUSIVE 2026-05-20**.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_cast_void_ternary.cbs`. Hypothesis: VisitCStyleCastExpr (BSCOwnership.cpp:2236) only handles DRE/ME/UO inner, falls through to generic `Visit(subExpr)` for ConditionalOperator → `checkCastOPS` skipped → no "consume" bookkeeping. Compiler output: BOTH arms correctly diag'd as `use of moved value` AND `memory leak`. Reason: `VisitAbstractConditionalOperator` (:2187) merges per-arm states, and the outer-context op=Move propagates to each arm's DRE via `HandleDREUse`. The cast-specific bookkeeping is bypassed but the move-tracking via DRE-visit is sufficient.

- **`(const int *_Borrow)mut_borrow` explicit cast rejected though implicit accepted** — **CONFIRMED-F27 (IJOKAC) 2026-05-20** MEDIUM.
  - Probe: `repro/F27_explicit_cast_add_const_borrow.cbs`. `const int *_Borrow cb = mb;` accepted; same as cast rejected with `incompatible _Borrow types`.
  - Root: `CheckBorrowQualTypeCStyleCast` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:642-644) hard-rejects any `isConstBorrow()` mismatch. `IsSafePointerConversion` (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:731-740) distinguishes drop-const vs add-const and only rejects the former. Implicit and explicit paths disagree.
  - Defect class: C1 (Ignore-asymmetry, implicit-vs-explicit).
  - Workaround: omit cast, or use `&_Const *mb` reborrow.

- **`if (a && b)` false branch demote-already-NonNull `a`** — **INCONCLUSIVE 2026-05-20** (analyzer correctly preserves).
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_short_circuit_v3.cbs` — narrow `a` to NonNull via `if (a == nullptr) return -1;` then `if (a && b) {} else { *a; }`. Expected wrong-demote → diag in else. Result: clean compile.
  - Root: SetCFGBlocksByExpr (:746) guards `CurrStatusVD[VD] != NullabilityKind::NonNull` — narrowing-override is skipped when the var is already NonNull. So short-circuit && / || in the false branch DON'T clobber prior NonNull narrowings. Defensive code correctly handles this case.

- **ActionExtract::VisitUnaryAddrMutDeref empty-Sources guard missing** — **SHAPE-REJECTED 2026-05-20**.
  - Asymmetry: `VisitUnaryAddrConstDeref` (:747) guards `if (Sources.empty()) { Kind = Action::Init; return; }`; `VisitUnaryAddrMutDeref` (:773) accesses `Sources[0]` unconditionally.
  - Probed shapes: `&_Mut *p`, `&_Mut *&_Mut x`, `&_Mut *(p)`, `&_Mut *(int *_Borrow)(0L)`. All either compile clean or are rejected by Sema before reaching the analyzer (`incompatible _Borrow types, cannot cast 'long' to 'int *_Borrow'`).
  - Reason: Prologue hoists complex subexprs to DREs which DO push Sources; in safe BSC there's no way to make `*expr` parse with empty Sources after analysis. The guard is dead code in either branch, and the missing guard in the mut variant is theoretically a crash vector but unreachable from valid input. Mark as DEFENSIVE-CODE-INCONSISTENCY not exploitable.

## Notes on session-end frontier (2026-05-19)

- All 7 defect classes in `_playbook.md` are CONFIRMED except **C5 (dataflow merge state hole)**.
- C5 thoroughly explored: Null|Owned, Owned|Moved, Owned|AllMoved post-merge states all produce CORRECT analyzer behavior because (a) nullability check catches derefs independently, (b) leak check on multi-bit states is conservative (e.g. Owned|Moved → can't assign → leak diag fires), (c) HandleDREUse's cascade emits diag when `has(Moved)` is true. **No exploit for C5 found** — remains speculative.
- Reading frontier: BSCIRBuilder full file, RegionCheck/BorrowCk, RewriteBSC, BSCIRInitAnalysis full. All heavyweight reads. Reading them without a strong invariant hypothesis is high-cost / unknown-yield.

## Earlier sessions (pre-2026-05-19)

See `bug_log.md` for filed bugs (F09 IJO88R, F10 IJO88S, F11 IJO88T, F12 IJO88U, F14 IJOAO8). See `probes/` for probe files from prior sessions — most are noted in `bug_log.md`'s individual entries.

- non-`_Safe` function: `*p = X` after `p = nullable_fn(...)` — **CLEAN** (nullability check is `safeonly` by default). `/tmp/probe_nullability_no_comma.cbs`, `/tmp/probe_nullability_extern_fn.cbs`
- `_Safe` zone: same shape — **CORRECTLY REJECTED** "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced". `/tmp/probe_nullability_doc_repro.cbs`

- `++p` / `p = nullptr; ++p; *p;` unary inc on `_Nullable _ArrayElem` — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** (pre-inc doesn't promote nullable to nonnull). `/tmp/probe_nullable_unary_inc.cbs`

- BSCIRBuilder `lowerToPlace` (:101) — **READ**, IgnoreParenImpCasts strip, DRE/ME/UO_Deref/ASE explicit handlers, temp-fallback for complex lvalues. CompoundLiteralExpr falls into temp-fallback (potential write-back lossiness, unprobed but unlikely in safe-zone BSC).
- `struct W { int *_Borrow _Nullable f; }; w by-value into reader; read again` — **CLEAN** (borrow field is copyable view; pass-by-value semantics correct). `/tmp/probe_struct_borrow_field_copy_semantics.cbs`

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 4 — F26 confirmed

- Conclusion: C5 confirmed for the first time via DPVD (only un-pre-populated tracking map). mergeVD/mergeFP have the same code defect but are protected by initStatus.

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 12

- Reading: BSCOwnership::HandleDREAssign (:2431) and HandleDREUse (:2504) — 6 situations dispatch on OPSStatus/SStatus/BOPStatus, all with similar dot/star-suffix double-call branches. F19 (filed) covers checkSFieldAssign's S-side gap; F23 covers HandleInitListExpr's star-suffix-prefix family gap. These DRE-side dispatchers use the same star→dot conversion pattern but their checkX{FieldAssign,FieldUse} delegation appears symmetric.
- checkSAssign (:1382) — for whole-struct reassignment, emits "InvalidAssignOfOwned" / "InvalidAssignOfPartiallyMoved", then unconditionally `SOwnedOwnedFields[VD] = SAllOwnedFields[VD]` (line 1414). Doesn't reset SNullOwnedFields. Possible state confusion if a field was previously null-initialized AND then the whole-struct is reassigned — but the prior null-init would have been moved INTO null-field, then whole-struct reassign moves all back to owned via line 1414. Probably correct.
- No NEW defect class found this cycle. Reading frontier in BSCOwnership exhausted of obvious holes — remaining is the deep state-machine of OPS/S/BOP × all the field-suffix family handling, which is the territory F19/F23 already cover.
- `struct S s = {.f = nullptr, .g = mk()}; s = mk_pair();` reassign whole-struct after partial null-init — **CORRECTLY REJECTED** with diag "assign to partially moved value: s, s.f is moved". Diag wording says "moved" though .f was null-initialized (never owned) — LOW wording issue, not soundness. Behavior is conservative (rejects the assign, preventing leak of s.g). `/tmp/probe_null_field_then_struct_reassign.cbs`

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 13 — F31 confirmed

- `s.fp()` where `s.fp` has type `fn_t _Nullable` (calling Nullable fn pointer via implicit callee-deref) with `-nullability-check=all` — **CONFIRMED-F31** (filed IJOLRW; runtime SIGSEGV; `VisitCallExpr` doesn't check callee nullability). `repro/F31_nullable_fnptr_callee_unchecked.cbs`
- `(*s.fp)()` explicit-deref form, same shape — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" via `VisitUnaryOperator` UO_Deref check. Proves the gap is specifically in the implicit-callee-deref path. `/tmp/probe_explicit_deref_fnptr.cbs`
- Two mut borrows on `_ArrayElem` (`p = &_Mut arr[0]; q = p + 1; *p + *q`) — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "cannot use `*p` because it was mutably borrowed". `/tmp/probe_overlap_mut_borrows.cbs`
- Pointer arithmetic on Nullable (`p = nullptr; q = p + 1`) — **CLEAN** (no deref → no diag; technically UB but not detected since no observable null-deref). `/tmp/probe_null_ptr_arith.cbs`
- Reading: BSCBorrowChecker EnsureBorrowSource (:1702), RelateRegions (:1728), PreprocessForParamAndReturn (:1741), BorrowCk entry (:1778), InferenceContext::Solve (:916). No NEW defect class beyond F31.

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 16

- Nested struct: assign `s.i.f = mk()` then no free at scope-end — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "field memory leak of value: s, s.i.f is leak". `/tmp/probe_nested_struct_set_inner_owned.cbs`

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 19 — F35 confirmed

- Forward `goto skip; int *_Owned p = mk(); skip: drop_box(p);` (default `-uninit-check=safeonly`) — **CONFIRMED-F35** (filed IJOMEA; runtime `Invalid free()` on uninit memory; ownership-analyzer merge gives Owned state when one pred has VD tracked and other doesn't). `repro/F35_goto_skip_owned_init.cbs`
- Control: `-uninit-check=all` correctly fires "use of possibly uninitialized value: p". Init-analysis catches it; ownership-analysis alone doesn't.
- Forward goto with braces around the decl (`{ int *_Owned p = mk(); drop_box(p); }`) — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** (scope-end check). `/tmp/probe_switch_owned_fallthrough.cbs` (similar shape).
- C5 family expansion: F35 is the OWNERSHIP-ANALYZER twin of F26 (DPVD merge asymmetry). Both share the asymmetric-meet pattern at the merge step. F26's fix template (symmetric meet with absent-default) applies directly to F35's `OwnershipImpl::merge`.

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 20

- Switch fall-through where `case 1` declares `_Owned p = mk();` and `case 2:` uses `drop_box(p)` — **FOLDED-F35** (same root: switch's case-2 entry skips case-1's declaration; ownership merge gives Owned state at the joined post-case point; runtime invalid-free). `/tmp/probe_switch_cross_case_use.cbs`
- `goto skip; int *_Borrow b = &_Mut x; skip: return *b;` in `_Safe` — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "use of possibly uninitialized value: b" (init analysis runs in _Safe). `/tmp/probe_goto_skip_borrow_init.cbs`

Suspicious: should be `statsB.OPSAllOwnedFields[VD]`. May be a latent bug — AllOwnedFields could be incorrectly narrowed to "currently owned" only after merge. Logged for future investigation; not probed.

## Cycle 2026-05-20 sanity probes (all INCONCLUSIVE/correct, appended late)

- `init_field(&_Mut s)` for uninit s → correct diag (`/tmp/probe_transitive_init.cbs`).
- `__assume_initialized(&s.f)` → raw `&` forbidden in safe, SHAPE-REJECTED (`/tmp/probe_assume_init_field.cbs`).
- `int *_Owned _Nullable arr[3]` → array-of-owned blocked, SHAPE-REJECTED (`/tmp/probe_owned_array_direct.cbs`).
- `int *_Owned _Nullable p = safe_malloc(...); *p;` → assignment-narrowing makes p NonNull, deref allowed (`/tmp/probe_safe_malloc_nullable_deref.cbs`). Correct.
- `int *_Owned _Nullable p = maybe_alloc(); *p;` → Nullable return preserved, correctly diag'd (`/tmp/probe_nullable_owned_returned.cbs`).
- `void take_struct(struct S s) {}` for `S` w/ owned field → correctly diag leak inside (`/tmp/probe_pass_owned_struct_by_value.cbs`).
- Partial-move-then-free: 1 leak / 0 leaks variants both correctly handled (`/tmp/probe_partial_move_both_freed.cbs`, `/tmp/probe_partial_move_one_leak.cbs`).
- `int x = *(s.f); safe_free(s.f);` for owned field → correct (read doesn't move) (`/tmp/probe_read_through_owned.cbs`).

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 21

- `_Generic` selecting between `_Owned`-returning functions with different return types — **SHAPE-REJECTED** (type-incompatible arms). Not a feasible probe shape.

- Suspicious typo in OwnershipImpl::merge (line 232/240): assigns `statsA.OPSAllOwnedFields[VD] = statsB.OPSOwnedOwnedFields[VD]` (mixing AllOwnedFields with currently-OwnedFields). Hard to construct a triggering shape because the "A doesn't track VD" precondition requires VD declared in only one branch — combined with field-level moves in that branch — and the runtime UB from the uninit path on the other branch typically obscures the typo's effect. Logged as DEFENSIVE-CODE-INCONSISTENCY for future investigation.

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 22

- `int *_Borrow get_nonnull(int *_Borrow _Nullable p) { if (p != nullptr) return p; return p; }` — narrowing propagates to return: true-branch return CLEAN, else-branch return CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "cannot return nullable pointer type". `/tmp/probe_narrow_return.cbs`
- OPSAllOwnedFields typo (line 232/240) probed via goto-skip-decl with partial field move — the typo doesn't manifest as a missed diag because s.f is moved (not owned) in the only branch where it's known; the merge-narrowing-to-{g} drops f from AllOwnedFields but f is correctly seen as moved anyway, so no downstream check is wrongly skipped. **Latent defect, not exploitable** via the obvious shape. `/tmp/probe_field_typo_via_goto.cbs`

- **`*b = mk();` where `b: int *_Owned *_Borrow` (mut-borrow of _Owned field)** — **FOLDED-F34 2026-05-20** HIGH at runtime.
  - Probe: `/tmp/probe_mut_borrow_owned_field_write.cbs`. valgrind: 2 allocs / 1 free + "definitely lost". `b = &_Mut s.f` then `*b = mk()` silently overwrites s.f without a leak diag for the previous value. Same F34 root: `int *_Owned *_Borrow` doesn't enter status maps because `IsTrackedType` only accepts owned-qualified pointers. F34's IsTrackedType fix covers this.

- **Adding const to `_Owned` pointer (both implicit and explicit)** — **DESIGN-CHOICE 2026-05-20**.
  - Probes: `/tmp/probe_owned_const.cbs` (explicit cast), `/tmp/probe_owned_const_implicit.cbs` (implicit assign). BOTH rejected with same diag `conversion from type 'int *_Owned' to 'const int *_Owned' is forbidden in the safe zone`. Symmetric (unlike F27 for borrow). Likely intentional language design (owned pointer must remain mutable for destructor semantics).

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 38 — end of obvious probe surface

Frontier exhaustion summary (2026-05-20 session):
- BSCNullabilityCheck: F18, F26, F28, F29, F31, F33 — class-by-class coverage complete on get/Visit dispatch, merge, narrowing, fn-ptr handling, FieldPath collisions.
- BSCOwnership: F17, F19, F20, F23, F35 — class-by-class on init list / field assign / merge.
- SemaBSCOwnership: F14, F21, F22, F25, F27, F30 — class-by-class on temp leak / move-through-borrow / fn-ptr variance / cast-wrap.
- BSCBorrowChecker: F11, F16, F24 — class-by-class on opcode-switch holes.
- BSCIRBuilder: F22 (control-flow positions), F35 (merge).
- BSCIRInitAnalysis: F15 — only low finding.
- Rewriter: F09 (`_Generic`/`__builtin_choose_expr` codegen drop), F32 (compound-literal temp).

Remaining frontier surfaces (low yield):

(`/tmp/probe_f30_borrow_conflict.cbs`)

- F37's root cause class now spans THREE Sema-level check sites: UO_Deref (SemaExpr:17120), comparison-op (SemaExpr:16556), member access (SemaExprMember:1288). All call CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak at AST-build time without isUnevaluatedContext gating.

The ensure_init contract is about init-state (uninit→init), not ownership. The init analyzer marks the arg as init after the call (line 281-339) but doesn't verify the BEFORE state was uninit. The OWNERSHIP analyzer's F34 root causes the leak; ensure_init annotation doesn't add safety beyond what F34 already gates on.

### 2026-05-20 — cycle 43

- F37 family extends to three Sema sites. Each fires `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` on CallExpr args/operands at AST-build time. Per F14's predicate definition (`dyn_cast<CallExpr>(E)`), this only matters when the input expression is a CallExpr returning `_Owned`. With non-CallExpr inputs (DRE, ME, etc.), the predicate returns false at the start and the F37 issue doesn't manifest.
- This is the COMPLETE F37 picture: three Sema call sites + the CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak predicate's CallExpr-gating = false positives only for sizeof(*mk()) / sizeof(mk() == nullptr) / sizeof(mk_call().field) patterns where an `_Owned`-returning CallExpr appears in those specific positions inside an unevaluated context.
- Plain `int v = mk_container().marker;` (not sizeof) — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** (F32-territory; mk_container's owned field leaked when only .marker is taken). `/tmp/probe_sizeof_member_struct_call.cbs`

### F09 scope refinement (2026-05-20)

F09 codegen-drop manifests ONLY for:
- Variable DECL initialization via `_Generic` (`int *_Owned p = _Generic(...)`)
- Bare expression statement (`_Generic(...);`)

F09 does NOT manifest for:
- Variable assignment (`p = _Generic(...);`)
- Struct field initialization via init list
- Function return statement

- `union U { int *_Borrow f; ... }`: borrow union field rejected at decl level (`/tmp/probe_union_borrow_field.cbs`).
- `_Atomic int *_Owned p = safe_malloc<int>(1)`: conversion from int*_Owned to _Atomic(int)*_Owned rejected in safe zone (`/tmp/probe_atomic_owned.cbs`).
- `consume(_Generic((char)0, char: mk(), default: nullptr))` arg position — **CLEAN** (1/1/4). F09 doesn't fire for call-arg position. `/tmp/probe_arg_generic_mk.cbs`
- F09's INIT-drop is really narrow: specifically `int *_Owned p = _Generic(...)` declaration-with-init style.

- `modify(struct S *_Borrow s) { safe_free(s->f); s->f = nullptr; }`: F21 correctly catches `safe_free(s->f)` (move-through-borrow) at the Sema level, blocking the read-side. User can't easily write a "modify-and-reset" via borrow without unsafe constructs.
- `int *_Owned _Nullable p = (int *_Owned _Nullable)_Generic(...);` decl init with explicit cast — **CLEAN** (1/1/4). The cast wrapper makes F09 NOT fire — a workaround. F09's drop is narrowly `decl-init = bare _Generic with mk()`. `/tmp/probe_init_cast_generic.cbs`
- `decl = (_Generic(...));` paren-wrapped init — **CLEAN** (1/1/4). Same workaround as cast: any wrapper avoids F09. `/tmp/probe_init_paren_generic.cbs`
- **F09 narrowest form**: `T *_Owned p = _Generic(..., char: mk(), ...);` — exact shape with NO wrappers between `=` and `_Generic` keyword.

- `ensure_init` pattern with uninit `int *_Owned _Nullable p;`: ownership analyzer diag uninit at safe_free even though init-analyzer respects ensure_init (`/tmp/probe_ensure_init_pattern.cbs`). The two analyzers disagree.
- With `int *_Owned _Nullable p = nullptr;` start: works clean (`/tmp/probe_ensure_init_null_start.cbs`). Documented BSC idiom.
- `int *_Owned p = __builtin_choose_expr(1, mk(), nullptr);` decl-init via __builtin_choose_expr — **FOLDED-F09** (0/0/0; same INIT-position codegen drop). F09 applies to both _Generic AND __builtin_choose_expr at init-position. `/tmp/probe_init_choose_expr.cbs`
- `int v = _Generic((char)0, char: mk()->v, default: 0);` — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** temp leak (mk()'s result accessed via arrow doesn't capture; the bare CallExpr would leak). `/tmp/probe_init_int_via_generic_mk.cbs`

- `int *_Owned q = (side_effect(), s).f;`: **FOLDED-F30** false-positive leak diag. base of MemberExpr is `(side_effect(), s)` (paren+comma); getMemberFullField doesn't walk through ParenExpr or BO_Comma → returns (BO_Comma, "f") → dyn_cast<DRE> null → handler skipped → s.f's move not tracked. Diag at scope-end "s.f is leak" (`/tmp/probe_comma_struct_field.cbs`). Same root as F30/F30-UnaryDeref; missing wrappers in getMemberFullField's loop now include ParenExpr (F30), UnaryDeref (F30-folded), BO_Comma (this case).

Equivalent shape with MemberExpr (`a->x = X` between two struct mut-borrows, `/tmp/probe_member_def_baseline.cbs`) is correctly rejected. Root cause: `DefUse` class in BSCBorrowChecker.cpp has no `VisitArraySubscriptExpr` — falls through to `VisitStmt` which preserves Action=Def for the base, killing its liveness. Peer asymmetry vs `DefUse::VisitMemberExpr` which switches Def→Use under isAssign. Defect class C1/C4. **NEW root cause, not folded into prior F-numbers.**
