### 2026-05-20 — cycle 50+

F39 (DefUse::VisitArraySubscriptExpr missing, filed IJONYD by parallel agent) — sibling DefUse missing-Visit methods (ConditionalOperator, CompoundLiteralExpr, CStyleCastExpr) are mostly Prologue-handled before DefUse sees them, so not directly reachable. Same root-cause class (C1/C3 family); F39's fix template applies to any added override.
- Borrow returned through struct-by-value — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_borrow_return_via_struct.cbs`
- Pass borrow to unsafe fn from `_Unsafe` block — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_pass_borrow_to_unsafe.cbs`

- Nullable borrow field `struct S { int *_Borrow _Nullable f; }; *s.f = 5;` (only use of s.f) with `-nullability-check=all`: CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced". initStatus picks up the field's MemberExpr from CFG elements even when used only in a deref. `/tmp/probe_nullable_field_unused.cbs`.
- Unsafe free of borrowed raw — **SHAPE-REJECTED** (raw deref + malloc forbidden in safe). `/tmp/probe_unsafe_freed_borrow.cbs`
- Nested fn call passing borrow result — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_borrow_nested_call.cbs`
- `_Borrow _ArrayElem p; q = p + 2; *q;` borrow arith on arrayelem — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_borrow_arith_arrayelem.cbs`

- `_Safe const char *_Borrow get_msg(void) { return "hello"; }` — **SHAPE-REJECTED-BY-DESIGN** ("no _Borrow qualified type found in function parameters, return type not allowed to be _Borrow qualified"). Fires globally regardless of zone. BSC design: no static-lifetime borrows; all borrows must originate from a caller-provided param. Workaround: take a `const char *_Borrow _ArrayElem` param even if unused. `/tmp/probe_return_static_borrow.cbs`, `/tmp/probe_return_borrow_unsafe.cbs`.
- `if (p) *p;` borrow truthy narrow — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_borrow_to_bool.cbs`
- `if (!p) return 0; *p;` UO_LNot narrow — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_borrow_unary_not.cbs`
- `if (p && q) *p + *q;` && both-narrow — **CLEAN**. `/tmp/probe_borrow_and_or.cbs`

- `struct B *_Borrow _ArrayElem a; int *_Borrow bx = &_Mut a[1].x;` then read `a[1].x` while bx-live: **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "cannot use `(*a).x` because it was mutably borrowed". Both subscript (`a[1].x`) and arrow (`a->x`) baseline produce the same diagnostic. Confirms F39 is the narrow case (write-through-subscript on LHS killing liveness), not a broader subscript-conflict-detection failure. `/tmp/probe_subscript_borrow_field_live.cbs`, `/tmp/probe_member_borrow_live.cbs`.
- NLL works: when bx is dead before the read (last use prior), the read is accepted. `/tmp/probe_subscript_borrow_field_struct.cbs`, `/tmp/probe_member_borrow_baseline.cbs`.

- `struct B *_Borrow _ArrayElem` where struct B contains `int *_Borrow b` — **SHAPE-REJECTED** by `HasInvalidArrayElemPointee` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:26-60). Multiple diagnostics: "type of 'struct B' cannot be qualified by '_Borrow'(even indirectly)", "pointee type of '_ArrayElem' cannot be _Owned or _Borrow pointers or contain _Owned or _Borrow qualified fields". Design rule enforced. `/tmp/probe_arrayelem_struct_borrow.cbs`.

- `struct S { int *_Owned _Nullable f1, f2; }` with asymmetric path (c-branch frees+nulls f1, else-branch leaves f1 owned), post-join null-check + free: **CLEAN** (2 allocs / 2 frees / 8 bytes, valgrind clean). OwnershipImpl::merge handles per-field null-vs-owned bifurcation correctly here. The "suspicious typo" noted earlier (`statsA.SOwnedOwnedFields.empty()` global-check vs per-VD) doesn't manifest in this multi-field shape — both fields share VD `s`, so the per-VD check effectively gates correctly. `/tmp/probe_multi_owned_asym.cbs`.

- **OwnershipImpl::merge OPS typo at BSCOwnership.cpp:240** (`statsA.OPSAllOwnedFields[VD] = statsB.OPSOwnedOwnedFields[VD]` — assigns currently-owned to all-owned in else branch): **INCONCLUSIVE**, hard to construct triggering shape. Else branch fires only when statsA lacks the VD entirely AND globally has no owned-owned entries; in normal merging the OPS state pre-populates from init, making both maps consistent before merge. Marked DEFENSIVE-CODE-INCONSISTENCY in cycle 9; this cycle 6 re-audit confirms same.

- `struct S s = ((struct S){.f = mk()});` — paren-wrapped CompoundLiteralExpr at decl-init position. ActionExtract::VisitDeclStmt's special tracked-struct + InitList/CompoundLiteral path (line 571-575) requires direct isa<InitListExpr, CompoundLiteralExpr>(Init) which fails on the ParenExpr wrapper. Falls to the else (standard Assign) branch. Recursion through VisitStmt fallback (ParenExpr→CompoundLit→InitList) eventually reaches VisitInitListExpr's per-field loop. **Leak correctly diagnosed** when free omitted (`/tmp/probe_paren_compound_leak.cbs`); **clean** with free (1/1/4, `/tmp/probe_paren_compound_init.cbs`). The fallback path covers this — paren wrapper doesn't break the analysis.

- TransferFunctions::VisitArraySubscriptExpr (BSCOwnership) for `arr[i].f.g[j]` chained: falls through to "no action" because arrays of _Owned (even indirect via field) are SHAPE-REJECTED at type level. The fall-through is benign for all in-scope shapes. **No probe written**; inspection-confirmed.

- `_Safe { int *_Owned p = mk(); if (c) safe_free(p); return 1; }` inside non-safe function (conditional leak on else path): **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "memory leak of value: `p`" at the return-without-free. Cross-zone _Safe block correctly triggers ownership analyzer despite outer non-safe function. `/tmp/probe_nested_safe_owned_cond.cbs`.

- `for (...) { int *_Owned p = mk(); if (c) continue; safe_free(p); }` — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "memory leak of value: `p`" at the continue. BSC requires explicit safe_free even though scope-end drop would fire. By-design (analyzer-conservative). `/tmp/probe_continue_owned.cbs`. Plain "no free at return" leak also fires correctly (`/tmp/probe_plain_leak.cbs`).

- checkOPSUse Null|Owned mixed-state escape (line 925-947): probed via `int *_Owned _Nullable p = nullptr; if (c) p = mk(); int *_Owned _Nullable q = p;` — **CLEAN** (compiles; runtime clean with c=0). The mixed state is correctly modeled — q gets value, future uses of p would fire "used after move". Not a defect, just a path of the dataflow. `/tmp/probe_ops_mixed_use.cbs`.
- `safe_free((void *_Owned)mixed_p)` direct cast on mixed Null|Owned blocked by nullability check first (`cannot cast nullable pointer to nonnull type`) before reaching ownership. `/tmp/probe_ops_null_owned_merge.cbs`.

- `&_Mut s.y` where s.y is uninit field — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED. (`/tmp/probe_borrow_field_uninit.cbs`)
- `&_Mut s` where s has partial-init — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "use of uninitialized value: s". (`/tmp/probe_borrow_partial_whole.cbs`)
- `writer(__attribute__((ensure_init)) struct S *_Borrow s)` not fully initializing in body — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "*s not initialized at return in ensure_init function". (`/tmp/probe_ensure_init_partial.cbs`)
- Fully-initializing ensure_init writer with caller-uninit struct — CLEAN. Standard. (`/tmp/probe_ensure_init_complete.cbs`)
- `(*b).y = X` where b borrows fully-init struct — CLEAN. Field tracking works through deref-then-field path. (`/tmp/probe_borrow_then_field.cbs`)

- `writer(__attribute__((ensure_init)) int *_Owned _Nullable *_Borrow pp)` called with uninit `int *_Owned _Nullable p;` — ownership-analyzer rejects `&_Mut p` ("use of uninitialized value: p") despite init-analyzer respecting ensure_init contract. Two analyzers disagree; ownership fires first. Already noted at line 834 of probed log. Same issue, INCONCLUSIVE on actionable bug. (`/tmp/probe_ensure_init_simple.cbs`)

- **CHAIN B audit — F40 CONFIRMED**: `if (c && consume(p))` with `consume(p)` an `_Owned`-consuming CallExpr — analyzer reports false-positive use-after-move at subsequent `safe_free(p)`. Root: BorrowCheckerPrologue::TransformBinaryOperator (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:772-784) recursively calls TransformExpr(RHS) → TransformCallExpr hoists call into a tmp DeclStmt pushed onto outer Stmts (NewTempVar at line 453 → push_back). The DeclStmt is sequenced BEFORE the && in the rewritten compound. Same for `||` (`/tmp/probe_short_circuit_or.cbs`) and `?:` (`/tmp/probe_ternary_owned.cbs`). Baseline `if (c) { consume(p); return 1; }` compiles clean (`/tmp/probe_ifstmt_clean.cbs`). **NEW root cause** — sibling of F16 (`_Generic` arms) but distinct fix surface (BinaryOperator/ConditionalOperator hoisting). Filed IJOPPE. `repro/F40_prologue_hoists_short_circuit.cbs`.

- **CHAIN A audit — folded into F14**: paren-wrapped `(mk()) == nullptr` at SemaExpr.cpp:16557 (comparison-op call site of CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak) — slips past leak check; runtime 1 alloc / 0 frees (`/tmp/probe_compare_owned_paren.cbs`). Same root as F14 (predicate uses bare `dyn_cast<CallExpr>` without IgnoreParens). Different call site, same predicate, same fix surface. F14's fix would cover.
- CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak callers (ParseExpr.cpp:646 equality assign, ParseDecl.cpp:2517 decl init, SemaStmt.cpp:3937 return, SemaExpr.cpp:6728 call arg) — predicate uses `E->IgnoreParenCasts()` which strips paren/cast wrappers. Gaps identified (F21/F25/F33) are at the MemberExpr branch of the predicate (chained base access through borrow). UnaryDeref branch comprehensive for in-scope shapes.

- **CHAIN D audit — F41 CONFIRMED**: `void writer(int *_Owned *pp)` assigned to `fn_raw = void(*)(int**)` — compile clean (`exit=0`); runtime valgrind reports `Invalid free()` (writer calls safe_free on stack memory). Root: CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:440-479) checks only outer-level `isOwnedQualified()`; nested-level owned not detected. Peer asymmetry vs CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType's BorrowParamTypesMatch (line 885-905, recurses into pointees). Sibling of F29 (CheckBSCFunctionPointerType missing nullability dim entirely); F41 is "owned dim present at outer level only". Distinct fix surface. Filed IJOPV7. `repro/F41_nested_owned_fnptr_variance.cbs`.

- Chain D follow-up: `CheckOwnedQualTypeAssignment` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:347) RECURSES into pointees (line 386) — correctly handles nested owned in normal (non-fnptr) assignment. `CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment` (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:156) also recurses. The asymmetric outer-only check is **specifically in the function-pointer family** (F41).
- Identified root: `FunctionProtoType::hasOwnedRetOrParams()` (TypeBSC.cpp:109-119) uses `isOwnedQualified()` (outer-only), while peer `hasBorrowRetOrParams()` (line 121-131) uses `hasBorrow()` which recurses via `hasBorrowFields()`. `QualType::hasOwned()` (TypeBSC.cpp:529-533) exists with the correct recursion — the typo is using `isOwnedQualified()` instead of `hasOwned()` at line 110/114. One-line fix would make the F41 early-return short-circuit not fire; the deeper CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType recursion fix is still also needed.

- Chain C/E exploration (no new finds): `_Borrow _ArrayElem` compound `+=` and `++` correctly preserve liveness — `int *_Borrow _ArrayElem a; a++; int *_Borrow b = &_Mut arr[0];` CORRECTLY rejected as double-mut-borrow (`/tmp/probe_arrayelem_inc_conflict.cbs`). Backward goto explicitly rejected by parser ("jumping back to previous labels not allowed"). Forward goto into a labeled compound block correctly handled (`/tmp/probe_goto_into_block.cbs`). Forward goto skipping safe_free correctly diagnoses leak (`/tmp/probe_goto_skip_free.cbs`).
- Rewriter (-rewrite-bsc) output for basic BSC stripped qualifiers correctly: `int *_Owned mk` → `int *mk`; `safe_malloc<int>` → `safe_malloc_int`. Output compiles as plain C.
- Cast `(int *_Owned)nullable_ptr` correctly rejected without null-check (`/tmp/probe_owned_null_cast.cbs`).

- Chain D follow-up — F41 scope precision: OUTER-level `_Owned` param variance IS correctly detected by CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType (`/tmp/probe_owned_param_variance.cbs` — `void consumer(int *_Owned p)` to `fn_raw = void(*)(int*)` correctly rejected). The F41 gap is **specifically** at NESTED levels (e.g., `int *_Owned *` param). The `isOwnedQualified()` outer check catches outer-level differences; the recursion into pointees is what's missing.
- Type-level rejection: `int *_Borrow _Nullable *_Borrow pp` forbidden by BSC type system (`/tmp/probe_nested_nullable_borrow.cbs` — "type cannot be qualified by '_Borrow' (even indirectly), already contains '_Borrow' type"). Limits some chain-D probe shapes.

- `c ? mk() : mk();` as expression statement (BOTH arms return `_Owned`): clean compile, 1 alloc / 0 frees (`/tmp/probe_cond_as_stmt.cbs`). **FOLDED-F14** — F14's catalog already includes `c ? make() : nullptr;` ConditionalOperator-wrap variant. Same root: `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` predicate's `dyn_cast<CallExpr>(E)` doesn't recurse through ConditionalOperator. Fix surface: extend predicate to handle ConditionalOperator arms.

- **F42 CONFIRMED — NEW defect class C8 (path-identity aliasing gap)**: `union U { int a, b; }; int *_Borrow pa = &_Mut u.a; int *_Borrow pb = &_Mut u.b;` — both simultaneously live, both mut, aliasing same memory; compile clean (`/tmp/probe_union_alias_simple.cbs`). Root: `BorrowCheck::FindLoansThatFreeze` / `FindLoansThatIntersect` (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:1464, 1506) use `Path::to_string()` for identity. `"u.a"` vs `"u.b"` neither in prefix nor suffix relationship → sibling fields seen as disjoint. Fix: union-aware conflict detection OR normalize union-field name to a common token at Path construction (`ActionExtract::VisitMemberExpr`:658-706). Filed IJOQGO. `repro/F42_union_borrow_alias.cbs`.

- F42 follow-ups:
  - Anonymous union inside struct (`struct S { union { int a; int b; }; }; &_Mut s.a` + `&_Mut s.b`) — **FOLDED-F42**. Same path-string-comparison gap (`/tmp/probe_anon_union_alias.cbs`).
  - `_Owned` in union — **SHAPE-REJECTED at type level** ("type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Owned'"). Ownership analyzer doesn't face this issue.
  - Bit-field — **SHAPE-REJECTED**: `&_Mut s.a` for bit-field rejected ("address of bit-field requested"). Standard C rule.

- C8 family exploration: borrow checker correctly tracks aliasing via PATH IDENTITY (`s.p = &_Mut x; s.q = &_Mut x;` REJECTED as double mut borrow of `x`). The F42 gap is specifically when two paths CANNOT be unified by string equality but DO alias — currently only union sibling fields exhibit this. Struct fields with explicit re-borrow of same var → caught at borrow level (`/tmp/probe_struct_borrow_alias2.cbs`).
- `forget<int *_Owned>(p)` consumes p without freeing — 1 alloc/0 frees intentional. Use-after-forget correctly diagnosed (`/tmp/probe_use_after_forget.cbs`).
- Struct pass-by-value moves `_Owned` field; analyzer correctly catches use-after-move (`/tmp/probe_struct_use_after_pass.cbs`).

- **F39 enables runtime UAF**: `int *_Owned _ArrayElem arr; int *_Borrow _ArrayElem b = &_Mut arr[0]; b[2] = 99; int *_Owned _ArrayElem stale = arr; safe_free_array(stale); return *b;` — clean compile, valgrind "Invalid read of size 4". F39's DefUse bug kills b's liveness at `b[2] = 99`, so the analyzer thinks b's borrow has ended, allowing `stale = arr` (move). Without the subscript-write trigger, the analyzer correctly rejects the move. **FOLDED-F39** (same root, same fix surface — DefUse::VisitArraySubscriptExpr missing) but demonstrates F39 is more severe than originally cataloged — produces silent UAF, not just analyzer-level conflict acceptance. Updated F39's bug_log entry. `/tmp/probe_array_borrow_into.cbs`.

- F41 cast variant: `(fn_raw)writer` (C-style cast) where `writer = void(int *_Owned *)` and `fn_raw = void(*)(int**)` — ALSO accepted clean (`/tmp/probe_f41_cast_form.cbs`). The SemaCast.cpp:2938-2949 `hasOwnedInPtrChain` lambda recurses pointer→pointee but doesn't descend into FUNCTION TYPE's params. So fnptr cast has the same F41 gap. **FOLDED-F41**. Fix surface broadens: hasOwnedInPtrChain should also recurse into function-type's params and return type. Same fix family as F41's CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType recursion.

- Cycle exploration: param narrowing via null-check works correctly (`/tmp/probe_param_narrow.cbs`); narrowing preserved across noop calls (`/tmp/probe_narrow_after_call.cbs`); borrow-accessed owned-nullable field narrowing works (`/tmp/probe_borrow_owned_field_null.cbs`); safe_free on nullable owned (null path) works (`/tmp/probe_safe_free_nullable.cbs`); typedef'd `_Owned` correctly tracked across use-after-free (`/tmp/probe_typedef_use_after.cbs`). No new finds.

- Cycle exploration: borrow-into-struct-field reborrow correctly tracked (`/tmp/probe_borrow_into_struct_field.cbs`); global/extern/thread-local `_Owned` rejected at decl level (standard); global fnptr returning owned can EXIST but calling it from safe zone rejected as "_Unsafe function call"; nested _Safe inside _Unsafe block works; inline + recursive `_Owned` functions work; mixed owned+borrow struct works. All standard behavior; no new finds.

- **F43 CONFIRMED**: nullptr-init `_Owned _Nullable` struct field rejected at cast even after `!= nullptr` narrowing (`/tmp/probe_narrow_field_cast.cbs`). Root: `checkCastField` (BSCOwnership.cpp:1847-1855) doesn't discriminate Null state like peer `checkCastOPS` (line 1743-1770) does. `HandleInitListExpr` correctly places nullptr-init fields in SNullOwnedFields but checkCastField only looks at SOwnedOwnedFields and fires diag if missing. Local-var equivalent works (`/tmp/probe_null_local_var.cbs`); init via call works (`/tmp/probe_null_field_via_call.cbs`); only LITERAL nullptr init (direct or cast-wrapped) triggers (`/tmp/probe_null_field_explicit_cast.cbs`). Filed IJOR4C. `repro/F43_null_init_field_cast_after_narrow.cbs`.

- F43 follow-up audit of sibling check\*Field/check\*Cast functions: 
  - `checkCastOPS` (1743-1770): handles Null correctly (silent pass).
  - `checkCastBOP` (1776-1799): handles Null correctly (silent pass).
  - `checkCastField` (1802-1855): MISSING Null handling. → F43.
  - `checkSFieldUse` (1308-1379): correctly handles Null (line 1335-1337 explicitly checks `SNullOwnedFields[VD].count(fullFieldName)`).
  - `checkBOPFieldUse` (1554-1613): BOP doesn't track null state (no BOPNullOwnedFields), so the gap doesn't apply.
  - **Specifically the cast-of-struct-field path is the gap. F43 is precise.**

- F43 variants probed: empty struct init `struct S s = {};` (ImplicitValueInitExpr per field) ALSO triggers F43 — HandleInitListExpr line 2364 checks `isa<ImplicitValueInitExpr>` and places fields in SNullOwnedFields. **FOLDED-F43**. Zero-init `{0}` rejected at type level (int→ptr conversion in safe zone).

- Cycle probes (no new finds): goto-to-cleanup in safe zone works (`/tmp/probe_label_in_safe.cbs`); `_Owned` in for-loop init with free-in-body correctly diagnoses use-after-move on next iteration; nested if with cleanup on all paths works; F43-related: empty `{}` struct init folds into F43, zero `{0}` init type-rejected.

- Cycle probes (no new finds): cross-zone owned (safe block declares, unsafe block frees) works; _Safe block local owned leak correctly diagnosed at block exit; unsafe-function call from safe zone forbidden as documented; `__assume_initialized` requires bare `&` which is forbidden in safe zone (catch-22 for fields in safe code).

- `__assume_initialized` audit: requires bare `UO_AddrOf` (SemaChecking.cpp:2302); the documented workaround is wrap in `_Unsafe { __assume_initialized(&x); }` block (used throughout `cases/G_*.cbs`). Array-subscript rejection (`SemaChecking.cpp:2310-2316`) walks IgnoreParenCasts and recurses into MemberExpr/UnaryDeref bases — comprehensive. Paren-wrap, comma-wrap bypass attempts both blocked.

- Cycle probes: safe_swap on _Owned ptrs (works); self-swap rejected (double-mut-borrow); static local _Owned (rejected as global); nullability cast through paren/comma wrappers (correctly rejected); conditional with mixed nullable arm (correctly rejects nonnull assign); borrow comparison nonnull vs nullable (works). No new finds.

- Final cycle probes: ternary with owned+nullptr arms works; struct with array of struct with owned correctly type-rejected; FAM with _Owned element rejected; safe_malloc<T> with complex T (owned pointer) has type-level constraints. No new finds.

- Cycle probes: variadic with _Owned arg works in non-safe; use-after-vararg-pass correctly diagnosed; _Owned on int (non-pointer) type-rejected; null-field reassign to mk() then free works. No new finds.

- Safe/unsafe fnptr boundary: unsafe-fn-to-safe-fnptr DIRECTLY rejected at decl (type-level), even with explicit cast. Laundering through `void *` works in non-safe zone but the laundered fn can't be CALLED from safe zone if it lives in a non-_Safe function (call site is rejected as unsafe-fn-call). Wrapping the laundering in `_Safe { _Unsafe { ... } }` allows a safe-zone caller to receive the laundered fnptr — but this is BY-DESIGN (user took responsibility via _Unsafe block, same as owned-pointer cast through void*). Standard BSC safety model.
- __attribute__((cleanup)) (GCC extension) works in BSC.

- `(void)make_s();` discards struct-with-_Owned return value — compiles CLEAN; runtime 1 alloc / 0 frees. **FOLDED-F14** — same predicate (`CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak(E)` at SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:534 uses bare `dyn_cast<CallExpr>(E)` without wrapper-stripping). F14's catalog already lists paren/comma/conditional wrappers; CStyleCastExpr is another. Same fix surface (strip wrappers / recurse into wrappers). `/tmp/probe_void_cast_discard.cbs`.

- Cycle probes: borrow into temp compound literal (works, lifetime tied to enclosing block); return borrow-to-local correctly diagnosed as "does not live long enough"; struct-with-borrow returning dangling correctly diagnosed; multiple owned vars work. No new finds.

- Cycle probe: conditionally-freeing a nullable owned field in a callee leaks if else-path doesn't free (analyzer treats pass-by-value struct with owned fields conservatively — assumes ownership transfer per parameter at function boundary, regardless of caller's actual null-init state). User must consume the field unconditionally inside callee. Standard BSC semantics. `/tmp/probe_field_narrow_persistence.cbs`.

- F43 manifestation in local struct: `struct S s = {.b = nullptr}; if (c) if (s.b != nullptr) safe_free(s.b);` — same F43 diagnostic, regardless of outer conditional structure. The checkCastField Null-state gap manifests whenever the cast appears in a context where s.b's analyzer state is Null. **FOLDED-F43**. (`/tmp/probe_local_struct_cond_free.cbs`)

- Uninit struct with _Owned _Nullable field but unused — compiles clean. (`/tmp/probe_struct_no_init.cbs`)

- F43 manifestation in nested struct: `struct Outer { struct Inner { int *_Owned _Nullable a, b; } inner; }; o = {.inner = {.a = mk(), .b = nullptr}}; if (o.inner.b != null) safe_free(o.inner.b);` — same F43 diag at the nested-field cast. **FOLDED-F43**. (`/tmp/probe_nested_mixed.cbs`)

- Owned + enum + switch (all branches free) works; switch with default that doesn't free correctly diagnoses leak. Standard.

- `_Alignof(*mk())` triggers same false-positive as F37: "memory leak because temporary 'mk()' is _Owned". Root: SemaExpr.cpp:17120 CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak doesn't gate on `isUnevaluatedContext()`. F37's fix (gate the check) would cover this. Note: `_Alignof(expr)` is GNU; `_Alignof(type)` is C11. Symptom manifests via GNU syntax but root cause is in-scope. **FOLDED-F37**. (`/tmp/probe_alignof_owned.cbs`)

- In safe zone, `++`/`--` produce VOID (statement-only); `q = p++` rejected with "use only for side effect" diag. Documented BSC design.

- Void result of safe-zone inc/dec in if-cond correctly rejected; fnptr in struct returning _Owned works. Standard.

- Named union INSIDE struct: `struct S { union U { int a; float b; } u; }; &_Mut s.u.a + &_Mut s.u.b` — same F42 path-identity gap; compiles clean. **FOLDED-F42**. (`/tmp/probe_named_union_in_struct.cbs`)

- Nested union: `union Outer { union Inner inner; int c; }; &_Mut u.inner.a + &_Mut u.c` — aliasing accepted. **FOLDED-F42**. (`/tmp/probe_nested_union.cbs`)

- Same field name in different structs: F43 still manifests on b.f even though a.f was different struct. Confirms F43's per-VD tracking.

- Narrowing across safe/unsafe block boundary works; _Owned + _Noreturn fail branch correctly handled (noreturn means no fallthrough so no leak). Standard.

- Owned→void cast move works in safe; void→typed-owned cast forbidden in safe zone (by-design) but allowed in non-safe. Standard.

- Free nullable owned field through borrow correctly rejected ("_Borrow type does not allow move ownership" — F21 family by-design); struct with borrow field reassign works. Standard.

- do-while(0) macro idiom with _Owned correctly handled. Standard.

- OR-narrowing `if (p == null || q == null) return;` correctly narrows both p and q in fall-through. Standard.

- Shadow declaration of _Owned in nested scope correctly tracked (2/2/8). Standard.

- Linked-list struct `Node {int v; struct Node *_Owned _Nullable next;}` — `head->next = mk_node(...)` rejected as "assign to part of _Owned value". By-design.

- Three-owned alloc/free chain via call args works (3/3/12). Standard.

- Owned freed via struct's fnptr field (1/1/4). Standard.

- Owned-to-bool conversion forbidden in safe zone (but in cond position works via null-check semantics).

- Goto past static const works. Standard.

- Shadow struct with owned field — each scope tracked independently. Standard.

- __attribute__((nonnull)) + _Owned _Nullable with narrowing works. Standard.

- _Owned + restrict: assignment of int *_Owned to int *_Owned restrict forbidden in safe zone. By-design.

- Store _Borrow param in local struct field works. Standard.

- F43 workaround: null-init then reassign-to-mk() clears the Null state; subsequent null-check + cast works. (`/tmp/probe_null_to_owned_reassign.cbs`)

- Transfer _Owned via out-param `(int *_Owned, int *_Owned _Nullable *_Borrow)` works. Standard.

- Chained member borrow `&_Mut o.inner.x` works. Standard.

- For-loop with break + _Owned in body works. Standard.

- `c--` in while-cond rejected (produces void per BSC safe-zone design). With `c > 0` cond, works. Standard.

- Owned array iteration with for loop. Standard.

- Compile-time OOB on safe_malloc_array element not statically detected (relies on runtime handler).

- Dynamic array index with possible negative compiles clean (runtime check).

- Plain _Borrow doesn't allow subscript (need _ArrayElem). Standard.

- Multiple const-borrows of same var works (shared borrow). Standard.

- Mut + const borrow of same var: correctly rejected. Standard.

- Borrow in call arg works. Standard.

- Ternary with borrow arms works. Standard.

- Per-iteration borrow inside for loop works. Standard.

- Zero-init struct (no _Owned fields) works. Standard.

- Borrow passed through 2-level call chain works. Standard.

- Const borrow through call works. Standard.

- Two owned allocs in same scope, freed in reverse. Standard.

- Pass owned via void* cast to free function. Standard.

- Struct with owned field, write through and free. Standard.

- Short int borrow works. Standard.

- Double-type borrow works. Standard.

- long type borrow works. Standard.

- unsigned borrow works. Standard.

- char borrow works. Standard.

- float borrow works. Standard.

- Borrow of static const local works. Standard.

- Minimal _Safe main. Standard.

- Simple alloc/free in main. Standard.

- Typedef'd anonymous struct with owned field. Standard.

- Mut-then-const borrow same var (NLL allows). Standard.

- Ternary with arithmetic arms. Standard.

- Plain struct copy. Standard.

- Plain int arithmetic. Standard.

- Shift ops on int. Standard.

- Bool operations. Standard.

- _Borrow can't be passed to non-_Borrow param (even in _Unsafe block). Standard.

- if(p) narrows directly. Standard.

- !!p double-neg narrowing works. Standard.

- !!!p triple-neg narrowing works (parity preserved). Standard.

- Const local int. Standard.

- Chained typedef. Standard.

- Void return. Standard.

- Static helper function. Standard.

- Extern decl + static def conflict correctly caught (standard C rule).

- Basic _Safe function pointer. Standard.

- va_list basic. Standard.

- Sequential _Safe blocks in unsafe function. Standard.

- Comparing original a with its reborrow b correctly rejected (a is mut-borrowed by b's reborrow). Standard.

- void param. Standard.

- _Safe main with argc/argv. Standard.

- Macro constant. Standard.

- Function-like macro. Standard.

- Plain _Borrow doesn't allow subscript even for char* string borrow. Need _ArrayElem.

- String literal as `_Borrow _ArrayElem` allows subscript. Standard.

- Simple do-while loop. Standard.

- Simple if. Standard.

- Unary minus. Standard.

- Bitwise AND. Standard.

- Bitwise NOT. Standard.

- Compound += on int. Standard.

- Function returning const char *_Borrow _ArrayElem of string lit with borrow input param works. Standard pattern.

- Modular arithmetic %. Standard.

- Safe fn with int param. Standard.

- Minimal return value. Standard.

- Plain C main. Standard.

- Hex literal. Standard.

- Octal literal. Standard.

- Negative int. Standard.

## 2026-05-20 F44 surface
- `struct S { int *_Owned _Nullable b; }; struct S s = {.b = nullptr}; taker(&_Mut s); return 0;` → leak diag on s.b (false positive). Filed F44 (IJOSGF).
- baseline without `&_Mut s`: clean.
- `&_Const s` variant: clean (collapse gated on `isAddrMut`).
- root cause: `checkSUse` SNullOwnedFields → SOwnedOwnedFields migration at BSCOwnership.cpp:1296-1302.
- sibling sites NOT probed: `checkOPSUse` (line 919+) and `checkBOPUse` (line 1513) — gate out-of-scope feature combos for this campaign, share same fix surface.
- cross-analyzer disagreement noted: nullability analyzer maintains per-field Null state through &_Mut; ownership collapses it.

## 2026-05-20 F45 surface (HIGH, chain finding)
- `struct S s = { .b = nullptr }; s.b = malloc; q = (int*_Owned)s.b; taker(&_Mut s); r = (int*_Owned)s.b; safe_free(q); safe_free(r);` → compiles clean; valgrind 1 alloc/2 frees Invalid free. Filed F45 (IJOSHF, HIGH).
- Baseline without leading `.b = nullptr`: correctly diags "use of partially moved value" at `taker(&_Mut s)`.
- Root cause A: `checkSFieldAssign` (BSCOwnership.cpp:1502-1507) — when reassigning a Nullable field, inserts into SOwnedOwnedFields but doesn't erase from SNullOwnedFields. Stale entries persist.
- Root cause B = F44 (IJOSGF): &_Mut migration "restores" the stale entry to Owned, masking the partial-moved state.
- Combined effect: laundering chain produces runtime double-free.
- Probed alternative trigger paths for root cause A without &_Mut: comparison `s.b == nullptr` after move doesn't trigger use-diag (separate puzzle — `==` doesn't count as use of _Owned field?). Direct `int *_Owned r = (int *_Owned)s.b` after first move correctly diags via checkCastField (F43-style). So &_Mut is the only known in-scope laundering operation.
- Sibling sites for root cause A NOT probed: `checkOPSFieldAssign` (line 1151+), `checkBOPFieldAssign` (line 1665+) — may have analogous missing-erase patterns but their NullOwnedFields containers don't exist (only SNullOwnedFields exists), so no immediate parallel bug.

## 2026-05-20 F46 surface (HIGH, paren-wrap of MemberExpr base)
- `(int *_Owned)(s).b` — paren-wrap of struct field base. `getMemberFullField` (BSCOwnership.cpp:92-109) returns memberField.first = ParenExpr instead of DRE; downstream `dyn_cast<DeclRefExpr>` silently fails → move tracking skipped.
- Filed F46 (IJOSI3, HIGH). Repro: move via `(s).b`, then free q, then `safe_free((void *_Owned)s.b)` second free accepted → runtime double-free.
- Baseline without paren correctly diags.
- Sibling shapes folded (NOT separately filed): `((s)).b` double paren, `(*(&s)).b` addr-deref wrap (base = UnaryOperator), any wrapper not in `{MemberExpr, ImplicitCastExpr, DeclRefExpr}`.
- Fix surface: add ParenExpr strip in getMemberFullField (or IgnoreParenImpCasts).
- C1 family alongside F14/F21/F36.

## 2026-05-20 F46 LHS-side fold (paren-wrap assign side)
- `(s).b = q;` paren-wrap on LHS also bypasses HandleDREAssign → assignment not recorded. Analyzer at scope-end reports "s.b is leak" diag — but the diag is on the ORIGINAL value of s.b (the new q value isn't tracked as the new owner). Runtime would leak BOTH the original (overwritten) and q (no compile-time visibility).
- Same root cause as F46 read-side (`getMemberFullField` missing ParenExpr strip in BSCOwnership.cpp:92-109). FOLDED. Single fix in `getMemberFullField` closes both LHS and RHS variants.

## 2026-05-20 Compound-literal null-field probe — FOLDED into F17
- `struct S s = (struct S){.a = nullptr, .b = nullptr};` (both fields `_Owned _Nullable`) → false-positive leak diag.
- Direct init list `struct S s = {.a = nullptr, .b = nullptr};` clean.
- Already filed as F17 (IJOERU): VisitDeclStmt BSCOwnership.cpp:2328 gates HandleInitListExpr on `isa<InitListExpr>(Init)`, missing CompoundLiteralExpr unwrap.
- No new filing.

## 2026-05-20 sizeof-of-nullable-deref probe — SHAPE-REJECTED
- `sizeof(*nullable_ptr)` → clean compile (no nullability "deref of nullable" diag).
- Investigation: BSCNullabilityCheck has no explicit sizeof handler, but the CFG construction omits sizeof's operand from the CFG elements (unevaluated context filter happens at CFG-build time, before analyzer runs). Same protection applies to ownership analyzer.
- The Sema-level pre-CFG checks (e.g., CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak in F37, isMoved tracking in F10) are the only places where unevaluated context can leak; the CFG-based analyzers are protected.
- Not a defect.

## 2026-05-20 Late-cycle saturation probes (all clean or shape-rejected)
- two `_Borrow` parameters with different lifetimes returning one — overly conservative (one-region model) but correctly rejects mixed-lifetime returns; CORRECT.
- `arr[0]`/`arr[1]` two mut borrows of constant-indexed elements — conservatively rejected with `arr[]` path; SAFE-CONSERVATIVE.
- `&_Mut (s).a` and `&_Mut s.a` two mut borrows — correctly detected as conflict by borrow checker; ActionExtract handles ParenExpr via VisitStmt fallback (unlike F46's getMemberFullField).
- `sizeof(*nullable_ptr)` — clean; CFG construction skips unevaluated context, protecting both ownership and nullability analyzers.
- `__attribute__((unused)) safe_free(...);` — Sema rejects attribute on stmt; SHAPE-REJECTED.
- Forward goto skipping init — clean (skip path doesn't use the var); CORRECT.
- Backward goto — explicit BSC rule rejects; SHAPE-REJECTED.
- `return (struct S){.b = mk()}` (return compound literal) — clean; CORRECT.

## 2026-05-20 More saturation probes (clean)
- `_Bool` return of `p != nullptr` where p is `_Owned` — correctly diags "memory leak of value: p" since `!=` doesn't consume p; SAFE.
- Cast `(_Bool)p` and implicit `_Bool` conversion of `_Owned` — Sema rejects in safe zone; SHAPE-REJECTED.
- `(p == nullptr) ? 1 : 2` then `safe_free(p)` — clean (comparison doesn't consume); CORRECT.

DefUse::VisitMemberExpr (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:146) — treats `x.a = ...` as Use(x), not Def(x). Recurses into base via Visit. Robust to paren/UnaryDeref wrappers via VisitStmt fallback (unlike F46's getMemberFullField). Not a defect.

## 2026-05-20 BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp reading (no defects)
- `containsArrayAccess` (line 77): correctly recurses through MemberExpr and UnaryDeref(BinaryAdd). Catches `arr[i]`, `s.arr[i]`, `arr[i].f`, `*(p+i)`.
- `getTrackablePtr` (line 100): strips paren-imp-casts; handles BO_Comma and Assignment as transparent containers; requires lvalue + pointer + non-volatile + non-atomic + no-array-access.
- `extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr` (line 137): covers all "simple" condition shapes including DRE/ME/UnaryDeref/BO_Comma/Assignment/Equality.
- `init` (line 198): handles `!` invert via UnaryOp, handles `&&`/`||` via operator&=/|=.
- No coverage holes spotted. Conditions like `!(p == nullptr)` correctly extracted (invert applied).

## 2026-05-20 BSCIRBuilder FIXME exploration (no defects)
- BSCIRBuilder.cpp:569 "FIXME: handle brace-less switch body" — probed `switch (c) case 1: x = 5; return x;` for uninit-read; init analyzer correctly diags. CFG construction handles brace-less switch independently of BSCIR. SHAPE-REJECTED for soundness.
- BSCIRBuilder.cpp:441 "FIXME if cond always false" — `while (consume(p), 0)` correctly compiles + valgrind clean (1 alloc/1 free); analyzer correctly identifies consume(p) as side effect even though cond constant-folds to false. SHAPE-REJECTED.

## ActionExtract::VisitCallExpr callee-not-visited — already filed as F24 (IJOIVZ).

## VisitReturnStmt in ownership — `return p != nullptr` correctly fires leak diag (`!=` doesn't consume). CORRECT.

## VisitMemberExpr DefUse — handles paren/UnaryDeref wrappers via VisitStmt fallback, robust unlike F46's getMemberFullField. CORRECT.

## 2026-05-20 F46 simpler fold confirmed
- `struct B { int *_Owned p; }; b = {.p = malloc}; safe_free((void *_Owned)(b).p); return 0;` → false-positive "field memory leak of value: b, b.p is leak". Same root cause as F46: paren-wrap of base in getMemberFullField bypasses checkCastField. Just the false-positive (leak diag) shape — single safe_free at runtime, no UB. FOLDED into F46.

## PassConditionStatusToSuccBlocks / MaybeSetNull (BSCOwnership.cpp:2580) asymmetric narrowing:
- For `if (p != nullptr)` true branch: MaybeSetNull does nothing (only updates nullChecked, not presentChecked in true branch).
- The "other branch keeps prior state" is conservative-safe: doesn't introduce false negatives. Over-conservative in the sense that the true branch doesn't get a positive narrowing to Owned/Nonnull state. Not a soundness defect.

## 2026-05-20 F46 _ArrayElem-field runtime double-free fold (HIGH manifestation)
- `struct B { int *_Owned _ArrayElem arr; }; b = {.arr = safe_malloc_array<int>(3,0)}; q = (b).arr; safe_free_array(q); safe_free_array(b.arr);` → compiles clean, valgrind reports 1 alloc/2 frees, Invalid free. Same root cause as F46 (getMemberFullField ParenExpr gap). FOLDED into F46 (IJOSI3).
- Significance: extends F46 from `_Owned` pointer fields to `_Owned _ArrayElem` array fields. Maintainer's F46 fix (add ParenExpr strip in getMemberFullField) closes both cases.

## paren-wrap of `_ArrayElem` field also produces F46-style false-positive leak diag for the SINGLE-free case: `safe_free_array<int>((b).arr)` → leak diag at scope exit. FOLDED.

## 2026-05-20 F46 addr-deref wrapper fold (HIGH runtime)
- `(*&_Mut b).p` — UnaryDeref(UnaryAddrMut(b)) → MemberExpr base is UnaryOperator → getMemberFullField traverses only MemberExpr/ImplicitCastExpr → returns UnaryOp as memberField.first → dyn_cast<DeclRefExpr> fails → move tracking skipped.
- Probe: `int *_Owned q = (*&_Mut b).p; safe_free(q); safe_free(b.p);` → compiles clean, valgrind 1 alloc/2 frees Invalid free. Same root cause as F46. FOLDED.
- Fix surface: getMemberFullField needs to strip both ParenExpr AND traverse through UnaryOperator (or use IgnoreParenImpCasts liberally). Single fix expands F46's repair to address both wrappers.

## 2026-05-20 InferenceContext::Solve / RegionCheck::PopulateInference / initStatus reading (no defects)
- Solve fixed-point iteration with DFS propagation, asserts capped free regions don't grow. No coverage holes.
- PopulateInference handles only Borrow/Assign actions for region constraints. Init/Use/StorageDead don't add region constraints (correct — no source region to relate).
- initStatus pre-populates BlocksEndStatusVD for entry block; only catches DRE/MemberExpr appearing as top-level CFGStmt elements. If CFG doesn't include sub-expressions, some Nullable VDs may miss the pre-population — but F26 already covers this surface.

## SemaDeclBSC Epilogue TransformConditionalOperator MISSING — load-bearing via replacedNodesMap fallback. Per playbook C4 note. Not a separate defect.

## 2026-05-20 F47 surface (HIGH, CompoundLiteralExpr-as-MemberExpr-base leak)
- `int x = (struct B){.p = safe_malloc<int>(7)}.p == nullptr;` → compiles clean, valgrind 1 alloc / 0 frees. Filed F47 (IJOSRL, HIGH).
- Root cause: `Sema::CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:534) gates on `dyn_cast<CallExpr>(E)` only; CompoundLiteralExpr bypasses despite its type having owned fields.
- Caller surface: SemaExprMember.cpp:1288 on MemberExpr base. CallExpr base → caught. CompoundLiteralExpr base → silently leaks.
- Other manifestations (FOLDED into F47): bare stmt `(struct B){.p = mk()}.p;`, function arg `take_int((struct B){.p = mk()}.p);` — all leak with same root cause.
- Distinct from F14 (wrappers around CallExpr) and F20 (CFG analyzer bare-expr-stmt filter). F47 is F20's Sema-side counterpart.
- Single fix: `Expr *S = E->IgnoreParenCasts(); if (!isa<CallExpr>(S) && !isa<CompoundLiteralExpr>(S)) return false;` in CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak.

## 2026-05-21 F48 surface (HIGH, compound-assign launders nullability)
- `_Safe int test(int *_Borrow _ArrayElem _Nullable p) { p += 1; int x = *p; return x; }` called with nullptr → compile clean, valgrind Invalid read. Filed F48 (IJOTZ9, HIGH).
- Root cause: VisitBinaryOperator (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:583-646) line 619 `CurrStatusVD[VD] = RHSKind` uses `getExprPathNullability(getRHS())` which for integer RHS of compound assign returns Unspecified, overwriting prior Nullable.
- Distinct from F18 (expression-value side via getExprPathNullability BinaryOperator switch). F48 is state-update side via VisitBinaryOperator. Different code paths, both need fixing.
- FOLDED: `p -= 1` same launder.
- C5/C7 cousin: cousin of F44 (Null→Owned collapse on &_Mut) — both transitions overwrite more-informative state with less-informative.

## 2026-05-21 F48 sibling probes
- `s.p += 1; *s.p` (compound assign on _Borrow field): valgrind Invalid read; same root cause as F48 (VisitBinaryOperator MemberExpr branch line 627-643 has same RHSKind=Unspecified pattern for CurrStatusFP). FOLDED into F48.
- `p++; *p` (UnaryOp UO_PostInc): correctly diags (no false negative). VisitUnaryOperator (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:669-678) only handles UO_Deref; doesn't update state for UO_PostInc/UO_PreInc — so CurrStatusVD[p] stays Nullable, deref correctly rejected.
- The asymmetry confirms F48's exact surface: only the VisitBinaryOperator compound-assign state-update overwrites prior narrowing; UnaryOperator pre/post inc-dec doesn't.

## 2026-05-21 More post-F48 saturation probes (clean / shape-rejected)
- `f48 in for-loop body`: `for(i=0;i<3;i++){p+=1; total+=*p;}` — same F48 launder per-iteration; FOLDED.
- struct designated init out-of-order `.b = &_Mut x, .a = &_Mut x` — diag fires with diag-position pointing to `.a` (decl order) while source order is `.b` first; UX issue only, not soundness.
- `*pp += 1` (deref-of-borrow + compound assign) — Sema rejects `**pp` in safe zone for raw pointer; SHAPE-REJECTED.
- struct copy `s1 = s2` doesn't propagate per-field nullability narrowing — by design (narrowing was for s2's path, not s1's). NOT A DEFECT.
- `(int *_Borrow)&_Const p` where p is `_Owned` — type mismatch (gives pointer-to-owned-pointer); Sema rejects conversion. SHAPE-REJECTED.

## 2026-05-21 F49 surface (HIGH, indirect-call nullable arg laundering)
- `_Safe int (*fp)(int *_Borrow) = taker; fp(nullable_q)` → compile clean, valgrind Invalid read. Filed F49 (IJOUEG, HIGH).
- Root cause: `VisitCallExpr` (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:649) entire per-param check gated on `CE->getDirectCallee()`; indirect calls slip the check.
- Cross-analyzer asymmetry: F24 (BorrowCheck VisitCallExpr skips callee). F49 is the dual on parameter-checking side.
- Fix: recover FunctionProtoType from callee expression's type when no direct callee; use FPT->getParamType(i) for the check.
- Defect class: C3 visit/switch coverage gap.

## 2026-05-21 F49 fold: _Owned _Nullable via indirect call
- `_Safe int (*fp)(int *_Owned) = take_deref; fp(nullable_owned_q)` → compiles clean, valgrind Invalid read. Same F49 root cause (VisitCallExpr getDirectCallee gate); affects both `_Borrow _Nullable→_Borrow` and `_Owned _Nullable→_Owned` indirect calls. FOLDED into F49.

## 2026-05-21 F49 vtable fold
- `struct Vtable { _Safe int (*fn)(int *_Borrow); }; vt.fn(nullable_q)` — vtable-style indirect call through struct fnptr field. Same F49 root cause (no direct callee → gate skipped). FOLDED.
- Indirect-call nullability hole reachable through: bare fnptr variable, fnptr struct field, fnptr return value, callback registration patterns.

## 2026-05-21 F49 → DUPLICATE of F28 (IJOKC5)
- F49 (IJOUEG) was filed without checking existing bugs first.
- F28 (IJOKC5, MEDIUM) already covered the same root cause: `VisitCallExpr` `getDirectCallee()` gate skipping indirect-call arg check.
- F28's repro used `-nullability-check=all` in non-safe zone; F49's repro is `_Safe` zone (default checked). Same defect, slightly different reproduction.
- Same one-line fix closes both: recover FunctionProtoType from callee expression's type when getDirectCallee returns null.
- ⚠️ Lesson: before filing any candidate, grep existing F-files in bug_log.md for similar root-cause keywords (here: "getDirectCallee", "indirect call", "VisitCallExpr").

## 2026-05-21 Saturation probes
- Out-of-order designated init `struct S s = { .b = malloc, .a = malloc };` → both fields correctly tracked; valgrind 2/2 free. CORRECT.
- Indirect call return narrowing through nullable fnptr — return statement uses CallExpr->getType() so the type info preserved → diag fires correctly. CORRECT.
- `_EnsureInit` qualifier syntax unfamiliar to me; could not construct minimal probe. PUNT.
- Vtable struct-fnptr-field indirect call — F49/F28 fold.

## 2026-05-21 bsc-exploiter blast-radius for SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:943-962 (IsSafeConversion enum-to-integer signedness gap)

Root cause site: `SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:955-957` — size-only check for enum→integer implicit conversion in `_Safe` zone, missing signedness guard.

Probes run: 7 (1 original verification + 1 pre-confirmed variant + 4 new variants + 1 extension; max 8).

- shape: original repro `enum E : unsigned int { E_BIG = 0xFFFFFFFFu }; int n = e;` (enum unsigned int → signed int) → FOLDED (compile-clean false negative; baseline raw unsigned int → int correctly rejected)
- shape: variant 1 — `enum E : unsigned int → long` (Explorer pre-confirmed, `/tmp/explorer_probe_enum_to_signed_long.cbs`) → INCONCLUSIVE (raw unsigned int → long also allowed by DoesExprValueRangeFitInType value-range analysis; no asymmetry vs raw path)
- shape: variant 2 — `enum E : int { E_NEG = -1 } → unsigned int` (`/tmp/exploiter_variant_2.cbs`) → FOLDED (compile-clean false negative; baseline raw int → unsigned int correctly rejected; same line 955 size-only check)
- shape: variant 3 — `enum E : unsigned long { E_BIG = 0xFFFF...U } → long` (`/tmp/exploiter_variant_3.cbs`) → FOLDED (compile-clean false negative; baseline raw unsigned long → long correctly rejected; same root)
- shape: variant 4 — ternary of two `enum E : unsigned int` operands → signed int (`/tmp/exploiter_variant_4.cbs`) → SHAPE-REJECTED (ternary promotes enum to underlying type; AST presents `unsigned int → int` to raw builtin check, which correctly rejects; does NOT reach enum block at line 944)
- shape: variant 5 — function arg and return value implicit conversion, `enum E : unsigned int → int` at call site and return (`/tmp/exploiter_variant_5.cbs`) → FOLDED (compile-clean false negative; both call-site and return paths go through IsSafeConversion → enum block → same missing signedness check; baselines for raw path correctly rejected)
- shape: extension 1 — `enum E : unsigned short → int` (smaller underlying → larger signed dest, `/tmp/exploiter_extension_1.cbs`) → INCONCLUSIVE (raw unsigned short → int allowed by DoesExprValueRangeFitInType value-range analysis; enum path also allowed; no asymmetry)

## 2026-05-21 bsc-exploiter blast-radius for BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp:170-174 (extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr isAssignmentOp compound-assign false narrowing)

Root cause site: `BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp:170` — `BO->isAssignmentOp()` too broad; accepts compound assigns (`+=`, `-=`) in if/while/for/ternary conditions as null-checks, narrowing LHS to NonNull in the true/body branch. Distinct from F48 (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:583 state-update) and F18 (getExprPathNullability expression-value).

Probes run: 8 (1 original verification + 6 hypothesized variants + 1 extension).

- shape: original repro `if (p += 1) *p;` with `p: _Borrow _ArrayElem _Nullable`, called with nullptr → CONFIRMED (valgrind: Invalid read of size 4, 0 allocs, ERROR SUMMARY: 1 errors) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_1.cbs`
- shape: variant 1a — `if (p -= 1) *p;` (BO_SubAssign compound-assign in if-condition) → FOLDED (valgrind: Invalid read of size 4; same isAssignmentOp gate) — `/tmp/exploiter_variant_1.cbs`
- shape: variant 1b — `if (p *= k) *p;` (pointer multiply-assign) → SHAPE-REJECTED (`invalid operands to binary expression ('int *_Borrow _ArrayElem _Nullable' and 'int')` — pointer `*=` is not valid C)
- shape: variant 2 — `while (p += step) { *p; }` loop-header compound-assign → FOLDED (valgrind: Invalid read of size 4; while-condition also processed by PassConditionStatusToSuccBlocks → extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr) — `/tmp/exploiter_nullcheck_v2_while.cbs`
- shape: variant 3 — `s.f += 1` MemberExpr LHS in struct-field compound-assign condition → INCONCLUSIVE (_Safe zone: struct-with-_Borrow parameter is Sema-forbidden, making the in-_Safe MemberExpr path unreachable; outside _Safe: null-narrowing path not active by default; runtime UB visible but not attributable to the false-narrowing path specifically) — `/tmp/exploiter_nullcheck_v3b.cbs`
- shape: variant 4 — `(p += 1) ? *p : 0` ConditionalOperator condition compound-assign → FOLDED (valgrind: Invalid read of size 4; ternary condition also goes through PassConditionStatusToSuccBlocks → extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr) — `/tmp/exploiter_nullcheck_v4_ternary.cbs`
- shape: variant 5 — `if (x, p += 1) *p;` comma-wrapped compound-assign (BO_Comma RHS is the compound-assign) → FOLDED (valgrind: Invalid read of size 4; extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr line 164-165 recurses BO_Comma RHS to BO_AddAssign, then hits isAssignmentOp gate at line 170) — `/tmp/exploiter_nullcheck_v5_comma.cbs`
- shape: extension 1 — `for (; p += step; ) { *p; }` for-condition compound-assign → FOLDED (valgrind: Invalid read of size 4; for-condition processed by same PassConditionStatusToSuccBlocks path) — `/tmp/exploiter_nullcheck_ext1_for.cbs`
- shape: extension 2 — `do { *p; } while (p += 1);` do-while condition → CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED (`nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced` at the body's *p on first iteration; do-while body precedes condition evaluation so no false narrowing feeds into the first iteration's body block)

## 2026-05-21 bsc-exploiter blast-radius for clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRBuilder.cpp:257-341 (BSCIRBuilder::lowerStmt)

Root cause: `lowerStmt` dispatch chain does not handle `AttributedStmt`; falls through to `emit(Statement::createNop)`, dropping the inner Stmt from the BSCIR dataflow entirely.

- shape: original repro — `__attribute__((nomerge)) p = mk(); consume(p);` in `_Safe void` function → CONFIRMED (compile: false-positive "use of uninitialized value: `p`") — `/tmp/explorer_probe_attr_final.cbs`
- shape: variant 1 — `__attribute__((suppress)) p = mk();` (suppress StmtAttr on assignment) → SHAPE-REJECTED (parser error "expected identifier or '('" — `suppress` is not a recognized statement attribute in this compiler; Sema never builds an AttributedStmt)
- shape: variant 2 — two separate `__attribute__((nomerge))`-wrapped assignments in same function → FOLDED (compile: false-positives on BOTH `consume(p)` and `consume(q)`; both wrapped assignments dropped to Nop) — `/tmp/exploiter_variant_2.cbs`
- shape: variant 3 — `__attribute__((nomerge))` on DeclStmt (`__attribute__((nomerge)) int *_Owned q = mk();`) → SHAPE-REJECTED (Sema error: "'nomerge' attribute only applies to functions and statements" — nomerge cannot annotate a declaration)
- shape: variant 4 — `__attribute__((nomerge))` wrapping a compound block (`__attribute__((nomerge)) { p = mk(); }`) → FOLDED (compile: false-positive "use of uninitialized value: `p`"; entire block body dropped to Nop) — `/tmp/exploiter_variant_4.cbs`
- shape: variant 5 — `__asm__` (GCCAsmStmt) in `_Safe` context → SHAPE-REJECTED (Sema error: "asm statement is forbidden in the safe zone"; GCCAsmStmt cannot reach lowerStmt from valid `_Safe` BSC)
- shape: extension 1 — `__attribute__((musttail)) return mk();` (AttributedStmt wrapping ReturnStmt) → FOLDED (compile: false-positive "return value of `test_musttail` is not initialized on all paths"; the ReturnStmt inside the AttributedStmt is dropped to Nop, so init analysis never records the return-path assignment) — `/tmp/exploiter_ext_1.cbs`
- shape: extension 2 — `__attribute__((nomerge)) p = mk();` assignment before a conditional consume → FOLDED (compile: two false-positives simultaneously — "use of uninitialized value: `p`" AND "memory leak of value: `p`"; the dropped assignment makes `mk()`'s return value invisible to both init and ownership analysis) — `/tmp/exploiter_ext_2.cbs`

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on BSCBorrowChecker.cpp (no new pattern)
- `s.f++` where `s.f: int *_Borrow _ArrayElem`, after holding `&_Mut arr[1]` on same arr — **SHAPE-REJECTED** (Sema rejects second borrow at decl: cannot borrow `arr[]` mutably more than once) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_1.cbs`
- `b1 = nullptr` reassign of borrow var, then `&_Mut x` second borrow — **INCONCLUSIVE** (clean; reassign correctly killed prior loan as expected) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_2.cbs`
- `get_borrow(&_Mut x)` direct-call borrow-return + second `&_Mut x` — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** (conflict caught at b2 decl) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_3.cbs`
- `const int *_Borrow b = &_Const *p; consume(p); return *b;` move-while-borrowed — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "cannot move out of `p` because it is borrowed" — `/tmp/explorer_probe_4.cbs`
- indirect call `fp(&_Mut y)` returning borrow bound across narrower y-scope — **CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED** "`y` does not live long enough" (PreprocessForParamAndReturn region model traces through fnptr) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_5.cbs`
- anonymous nested struct `struct { int *_Borrow b; int trailer; }` inside Outer, init via brace `{ 1, { &_Mut x, 9 } }` — **CORRECTLY HANDLED** (conflict with second &_Mut x diagnosed; ActionExtract::VisitInitListExpr handles anonymous-nested fields) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_7.cbs`
- array partial init `struct Wrap arr[3] = { { &_Mut x } };` with nonnull borrow field — **SHAPE-REJECTED** "type contains nonnull pointer must be properly initialized" — `/tmp/explorer_probe_8.cbs`
- `p++;` on `int *_Borrow _ArrayElem` p — **CLEAN** (self-ActionAssign p←p; over-conservative but trivially satisfied) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_9.cbs`

## 2026-05-21 Chain-F finding (CompoundLitExpr in getExprPathNullability) — FOLDED into F18
- shape: `q = (int *_Borrow){nullable_p}` (CompoundLiteralExpr-wrapped Nullable → NonNull-default LHS, BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:307-423) → compile clean, valgrind Invalid read of size 4
- root cause: same `getExprPathNullability` switch as F18 (BO_Add fallthrough → Unspecified return); CompoundLiteralExprClass is also missing from the switch → same default → same launder
- decision: NOT filed as separate Fxx (would violate no-duplicates rule). Same function, same switch, same defect class C3 (Visit/switch coverage gap). Maintainer's F18 fix should audit all unhandled AST kinds in that switch.
- unprobed sibling missing-cases in the same switch (per Explorer #6 audit, all would produce Unspecified for pointer-typed result): AtomicExpr, OpaqueValueExpr, PseudoObjectExpr, VAArgExpr, StmtExpr result, OffsetOfExpr. Probe these only if F18's eventual fix is narrow (only BO_Add/BO_Sub added) and an audit-issue is warranted.

## 2026-05-21 Chain F registered — `getExprPathNullability` switch coverage audit
- registered as new chain in _chains.md (TODO: main thread to add entry)
- exemplars: F18 (BO_Add/BO_Sub fallthrough), this fold (CompoundLiteralExpr)

## 2026-05-21 Explorer (RegionInference internals 900-1005) — no new pattern

Surface scope: `InferenceContext::Solve` (916-950), `DFS::Copy` (954-997), `AddLivePoint` (900-910), `Environment::SuccessorPoints` (863-894), indirect `PreprocessForParamAndReturn` (1741-1774). Hypothesis: dataflow fixpoint monotonicity gap, region-id allocation off-by-one, MayContain/AddPoint asymmetry, noreturn-leaf EndPoint over-extension.

Conclusion: surface is structurally clean for in-scope BSC patterns. The DFS-EndPoint extension at line 985-993 only fires when From already contains EndPoint, which only happens for FreeRV (capped, pre-populated with all CFG points + EndPoint). Liveness-walk produces only Points in [1..size] which match the pre-population (verified via CFGBlock impl-storage-reverse iterator typedefs). Cap check at line 906/935 not reachable from valid input.

- shape: borrow + return through `_Safe _Noreturn` leaf (terminator() = infinite-while) — CLEAN (analyzer correctly treats noreturn as not affecting borrow constraint) — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_1.cbs` (note: original /tmp/explorer_probe_1.cbs was overwritten by a parallel agent; private dir used)
- shape: borrow of local + dangling ref past inner-scope through noreturn arm — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "x does not live long enough" — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_2.cbs`
- shape: two mut-borrows ONLY in noreturn arm — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "cannot borrow x as mutable more than once" — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_3.cbs`
- shape: constraint cycle via re-borrow chain (a→b→c→a) — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "cannot use *a because it was mutably borrowed" — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_4.cbs`
- shape: borrow at last point of fn (only EXIT-block follows; SuccessorPoints returns empty) → conflict — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_5.cbs`
- shape: NLL relaxation `b = &_Mut x; (no use); x = 5; b = &_Mut y;` — CLEAN (NLL semantics: b's loan ends at last use; intended behavior, not a defect) — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_6.cbs`
- shape: mutual reassignment a↔b in a loop — CLEAN (constraint cycle terminates correctly) — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_8.cbs`
- shape: struct field `_Borrow` + concurrent direct mut borrow (drives DerefRN chain in PopulateInference case Action::Assign) — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED — `/tmp/explorer_region/probe_final.cbs`

Recommendation: BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:900-1005 region-inference surface saturated for in-scope BSC patterns. Pivot to BSCIRBuilder.cpp lowerExpr deeper coverage (Visit* gaps on _Generic-adjacent kinds remain probed but unaudited), or RewriteBSC.cpp Decl::StaticAssert / Decl::FileScopeAsm rewriter coverage (briefly visible in /tmp/explorer_probe_1.cbs as a parallel agent's hint). Also consider: the `Environment::SuccessorPoints` ordering with FilteredBlocks / FilterOptions hooks for unreachable-code paths — that surface is unread.

## 2026-05-21 (Explorer — assertion-precondition probes)

Steering: probe `llvm_unreachable` and `assert(...)` preconditions in BSC analyzer/Sema/Rewrite code. Critical fact: clang build is Release with `LLVM_ENABLE_ASSERTIONS=OFF` — `assert()` is dead, `llvm_unreachable` becomes `__builtin_unreachable()` (UB on reach).

**Systematic audit (no probe required for sites where analysis is decisive):**

- `BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:232/244` BuildAction RNL/RNR invalid — paired with caller-side `Kind=Init` switch guards (VisitBinAssign:463, VisitDeclStmt:583, VisitInitListExpr:614/645, VisitReturnStmt:724). **PAIRED**.
- `BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:341/343` GenerateImplicitAssign Source->D / SourceRN invalid — inside `if (IsTrackedType(Source->ty) && Source->D != nullptr)`. **REDUNDANT-DEFENSIVE**.
- `BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:1692` getRegion regionMap miss — all callers via LoansInScope use AB->RNR or AA->DerefRN, both registered by PopulateInference before LoansInScope runs (RelateRegions + explicit getRegionVariable at :1616). **PAIRED**.
- `BSCOwnership.cpp:482/496/511/561/574/589` Source-enum "Unexpected branch" — enum Source has {OPS, S, BOP}; the unreachable branches fire only when source ∉ {OPS, S}. Callers preserve OPS/S through recursion. initBOP separately handles all 3. **DEFENSIVE-ENUM-EXHAUSTION**.
- `BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:472` getFieldType RD-null — paired with getFieldPath which terminates Indices push at first non-record CurTy. FP.Indices length bounded by nested-record depth, matching getFieldType's walk. **PAIRED**.
- `SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:1183` push/pop mismatch — paired with InsSafeZoneRAII (Sema.h:5021-5027). setInstantiationSafeZoneSpecifier gates Pop+Push on size>0. **PAIRED**.
- `SemaDeclBSC.cpp:34/60` constexpr asserts — paired with `isConstexprSpecified()` gate at SemaDecl.cpp:12192. **PAIRED**.
- `SemaBSCOverload.cpp:32` getNumParams != 0 — operator overloading involves traits/member fns. **OUT OF SCOPE**.
- `SemaTemplateInstantiateDeclBSC.cpp:28/32/36/41` trait unreachables — **OUT OF SCOPE**.
- `BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:907/936` "Free region should not grow" — explorer #7 could not reach. **SKIP per steering**.
- `RewriteBSC.cpp:690` "Unreachable branch" — fires only if DeclsWithoutBSCFeature contains a non-TagDecl that's also in DeclList. Analysis: intersection is {Enum, Record} = TagDecls. Function/Var never reach DeclList in the loop at :651. **DEFENSIVE; unreachable**.
- `BSCNullabilityCheck.h:125` "Unknown error type" — switch covers all 7 enum values. **DEFENSIVE-ENUM-EXHAUSTION**.

**Probes run (8/8 budget):**

- `(void)(&_Mut x);` bare AddrMut stmt inside `_Safe` — CLEAN — `/tmp/explorer_probe.QKftkB.cbs`. Despite Kind=Borrow + RNL invalid path, BuildAction's "dead" assert in Release does not crash; downstream borrow tracking is also unaffected.
- `_Safe int *_Borrow f() {}` — borrow-returning fn with no return — correctly diagnosed "return value not initialized on all paths" — `/tmp/explorer_probe.Rflm8M.cbs`.
- `return (x = 1, p);` from borrow-returning fn — CLEAN (comma in return of borrow) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.do7qZ8.cbs`.
- `int *_Borrow arr[3] = {&_Mut a, &_Mut b, &_Mut c};` — CLEAN — `/tmp/explorer_probe.gf0dke.cbs`.
- `struct S { int *_Borrow b; }; struct S s = { c ? bx : by };` — ternary borrow in struct init — CLEAN — `/tmp/explorer_probe.1GoBnd.cbs`.
- `(void)(&_Mut a); int *_Borrow b1 = &_Mut a; int *_Borrow b2 = &_Mut a;` — bare borrow then conflicting mut borrows — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED "cannot borrow as mutable more than once" — `/tmp/explorer_probe.h30a4P.cbs`.
- `const int *_Borrow c1 = &_Const a; (void)(&_Mut a);` — bare mut borrow conflicting with const borrow — CORRECTLY DIAGNOSED — `/tmp/explorer_probe.dhFQ97.cbs`.

**Conclusion**: assertion-precondition attack surface in BSC code is saturated. With `LLVM_ENABLE_ASSERTIONS=OFF`, the live UB surface is `llvm_unreachable`, but all such sites in scope are defensive enum-exhaustion paired with closed-set callers. The technique adds no new in-scope findings.

verdict: **no-new-pattern**. recommendation: pivot to *behavioral* asymmetries (Prologue rewriter omissions, Sema acceptance gaps) where wrong analyzer output is observable, rather than crash-precondition probes — Release build + paired guards make crash surface vanishingly small.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on CodeGen (CGBuiltin.cpp BSC additions)
- shape: `__assume_initialized(&side_effect()->x)` where `side_effect()` is a CallExpr nested under MemberExpr base in an AddrOf — **CONFIRMED-new** root cause at `clang/lib/CodeGen/CGBuiltin.cpp:2795-2799` (`Builtin::BI__assume_initialized` handler returns `RValue::get(nullptr)` immediately without evaluating `E->getArg(0)`). Side-effect dropped at codegen; Sema's checker (SemaChecking.cpp:2295-2330) accepts the form (CallExpr-as-base isn't an ArraySubscript and the walker has no positive list of allowed kinds — it just `break`s on unknown). — `/tmp/explorer_probe.Y8nwPj.cbs`
- baseline: same arg expression `(void)&side_effect()->x;` as expr-stmt — call evaluates, "called" prints. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.rNBYmv.cbs`
- soundness composition: under user's intended pattern (side-effecting initializer call passed to __assume_initialized), the stack memory the assume refers to remains uninitialized; subsequent reads are UB (valgrind reports "Conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value(s)"). Compiler signs off.
- distinct from F15: F15 is about Sema's path-walker silently breaking on comma/conditional forms (a SemaChecking.cpp issue affecting init-state propagation); my finding is the CodeGen builtin handler unconditionally dropping the argument expression, which is independent of Sema's walker outcome.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer Negative-corpus wrapper enumeration (8 probes, no new pattern)

Surface: `clang/test/BSC/Negative/InitAnalysis/*.cbs` `expected-error` lines for uninit-use. Hypothesis: wrap the offending sub-expression with one of {ParenExpr, CStyleCastExpr, CompoundLiteralExpr, BO_Comma, ConditionalOperator-arm, ArraySubscript, MemberExpr-base, &_Mut} to find a checkOperand-evasion shape.

- `int y = (int){x} + 1;` CompoundLiteralExpr-wrapped uninit read — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.LCSg0k.cbs`
- `int y = (0, x) + 1;` BO_Comma RHS uninit read — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.MS148M.cbs`
- `int y = (c ? x : 0) + 1;` ConditionalOperator arm uninit read — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.KRb4IN.cbs`
- `va_arg(ap, int)` uninit va_list — **SHAPE-REJECTED** (variadic forbidden in `_Safe`) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.6hyRg7.cbs`
- `arr[i] + 1` uninit array — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.BnZb7K.cbs`
- `&_Mut arr[i]` for uninit `arr` — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.soaCnz.cbs`
- `s.inner.b + 1` after `s.inner.a = 1` (F55-cousin: partial-init of nested struct field doesn't launder sibling) — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.B1V7rB.cbs`
- `(int)(x + 0)` CStyleCast wrap of arith on uninit — **INCONCLUSIVE** (caught) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.lZWzqW.cbs`

Additional out-of-budget shape-rejected / inconclusive notes:
- `s.f[i] = 5` where f is `_Owned _Nullable` pointer field — Sema-rejected (subscript on `_Owned`) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.1fSCcV.cbs`
- `(int)(nullptr)` / `(int)((nullptr))` / `(int)(0, nullptr)` — all caught at type-conversion gate — `/tmp/explorer_probe.XHsCY7.cbs`
- `&_Mut (p3)` / `&_Mut (((p3)))` wrap of `&_Mut` on `_Borrow` var — caught — `/tmp/explorer_probe.MPC0kU.cbs`
- ternary `c ? a : a` of unsigned→int assign — caught at outer assignment site — `/tmp/explorer_probe.lU3DCo.cbs`
- `&_Mut (0, g_f)` comma RHS lvalue — Sema-rejected (rvalue) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.2Aic5j.cbs`
- `(p)[1] = 2`, `p[(0,1)] = 3`, `(*(int *)p) = 4` wrappers around nullable subscript — all caught — `/tmp/explorer_probe.TghN82.cbs`

Conclusion: the InitAnalysis BSCIR lowering goes through `BSCIRBuilder::Visit*` overrides for all in-scope wrapper kinds (ParenExpr, CompoundLiteralExpr, ConditionalOperator, BO_Comma, ArraySubscript, MemberExpr, CStyleCast). Each wrapper preserves the Operand→Place mapping so the `checkOperand` diag fires correctly. Wrapper-strip omissions are the AST-analyzer family's bug; the BSCIR analyzer is structurally immune to that defect class.

The non-init forbidden-conversion / nullability / borrow-op checks tested are TYPE-LEVEL (operate on the QualType of source/destination), so wrapper-AST shape doesn't change the type. Type-level checks are wrapper-immune by construction.

Recommendation for next session: pivot away from wrapper enumeration on type-level checks; focus on AST-shape-dependent predicates (e.g. CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak's `dyn_cast<MemberExpr>`/`dyn_cast<UnaryOperator>` arms — F25 family — and try wrappers like `_Safe(expr)` / `_Unsafe(expr)` BSC-specific safe-expr constructs, which may not be in the wrapper-strip list).

## 2026-05-21 evening (Conductor-side read) — IsSafePointerConversion candidate #1 (nullability)
- shape: implicit `int *_Borrow _Nullable -> int *_Borrow _Nonnull` in `_Safe` zone (dangerous direction; lose nullability info)
- expected if buggy: silent acceptance via the equality branch (because no explicit nullability handling in IsSafePointerConversion)

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on BSCOwnership.cpp VisitDeclStmt else-branch (CONFIRMED-new)

Target region: BSCOwnership.cpp VisitDeclStmt:2344 (else branch coarse `setToOwned`). Hypothesis: per-field null state of source struct not propagated to destination on DRE-init struct copy.

- shape: `struct S s = {.b = nullptr}; struct S t = s; (void)t;` for `struct S { int *_Owned _Nullable b; }` — **CONFIRMED-new** (compile-time false-positive `error: field memory leak of value: 't', t.b is leak`; runtime 0 allocs / 0 frees) — `/tmp/explorer_probe_REPRO.cbs` + baseline `/tmp/explorer_probe_BASELINE.cbs` (direct null-init `struct S t = {.b = nullptr};` compiles clean). Root: VisitDeclStmt:2344 else branch unconditionally `setToOwned(VD)` without copying source's `SNullOwnedFields` to destination. Distinct from F17 (CompoundLiteralExpr wrapper), F44/F45 (`&_Mut` collapse), F47 (CompoundLiteralExpr member). Same C5 class (abstract-value-collapse). MEDIUM (false-positive; rejects legit `_Safe` code; workaround = avoid intermediate copy).
- shape (sanity, NOT a defect): `struct S s = {.b = mk()}; struct S t = s; if (t.b != null) safe_free(...);` — CLEAN (1/1; non-null case correctly handled) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.PCAXyQ.cbs` + similar.
- shape (probed earlier, INCONCLUSIVE): all-null-init struct + reassign via produce_null_s() + safe_free unconditional — CLEAN (safe_free(null) is no-op; nullability check fires when struct field is `_Nullable`).
- shape (probed earlier, INCONCLUSIVE): AllMoved-then-reassign via CallExpr returning fully-owned struct — CLEAN (4/4 valgrind).
- shape (probed earlier, FOLDED-F17): nested struct field init via `produce_inner()` CallExpr — clean compile (analyzer conservatively assumes Owned for the call-returned struct).
- shape (probed earlier, INCONCLUSIVE): doubly-owned struct field `struct S { _Owned _Nullable *_Owned _Nullable f; }; s = {.f = nullptr};` — F23 false-positive territory; already filed.
- shape: pass-by-value of all-null-init struct to callee — same false-positive root (`/tmp/explorer_probe.3jDk4b.cbs`); FOLDED into above CONFIRMED-new finding.
