## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:547)

- shape: `int *_Owned q = _Unsafe (s->f);` (s: struct *_Borrow with _Owned field) — **CONFIRMED-new** root cause at `clang/lib/Sema/BSC/SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:547-559`. `IgnoreParenCasts()` does not strip `SafeExpr` (BSC-specific AST class from `_Safe(...)` / `_Unsafe(...)` expression-form). The function's dispatch (`isa<UnaryOperator>` / `isa<MemberExpr>` after IgnoreParenCasts) misses SafeExpr → neither arm fires → `err_move_borrow` not emitted. Compile-clean; valgrind 1 alloc / 2 frees + Invalid free. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.88hd4X.cbs`
- baseline: same program WITHOUT `_Unsafe(...)` wrapper — correctly diagnosed at compile time with `error: _Borrow type does not allow move ownership`. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.SKxgMS.cbs`
- distinct from F25: F25 is ConditionalOperator-wrapper at the same function; its fold list (BO_Comma / BinaryConditional / Generic / ChooseExpr / StmtExpr) covers multi-arm expression kinds and the proposed fix is "recurse into arms." SafeExpr is a single-child wrapper requiring either (a) extending `IgnoreParenCasts` to strip SafeExpr (cross-cutting in `clang/include/clang/AST/IgnoreExpr.h`) or (b) per-site `if (auto SE = dyn_cast<SafeExpr>(E)) E = SE->getSubExpr();`. Different fix surface; F25's recurse-into-arms would not catch this shape.
- distinct from F21 (paren-deref base `(*s).f`): F21 is bug *inside* the MemberExpr arm's `getBase()->getType()` check; the SafeExpr bypass prevents the dispatch from finding any MemberExpr at all.
- defect class: **C1 (Ignore-asymmetry / wrapper-list incomplete)** — the same family as F14/F25, but the missing wrapper kind is the BSC-specific `SafeExpr` rather than a standard clang Expr kind. Blast-radius candidate: every other Sema/Ownership check using `IgnoreParenCasts()` (CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak, getMemberFullField, BuildUnaryOp guards) is potentially also bypassable by `_Safe(...)` / `_Unsafe(...)` wrappers — `IgnoreExpr.h` doesn't model SafeExpr at all.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on CodeGen (CGExprComplex.cpp / SafeExpr coverage)

- shape: `double _Complex c = _Safe(mk());` where `mk()` is a `_Safe` function returning `double _Complex` — **CONFIRMED-new** at `clang/lib/CodeGen/CGExprComplex.cpp` (`ComplexExprEmitter` at :45-395 has no `VisitSafeExpr` override; `_Safe(...)` wrapping a `_Complex`-typed expr falls to `VisitExpr` at :395-401 → `CGF.ErrorUnsupported(E, "complex expression")`). Scalar emitter (CGExprScalar.cpp:452) and lvalue switch (CGExpr.cpp:1368) DO handle SafeExpr. F60 fixed AggExprEmitter; this is the third sibling-emitter (ComplexExprEmitter) with the same gap. Codegen rejects what Sema accepts. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.cOFTWL.cbs`
- baseline: same program WITHOUT the `_Safe(...)` wrapper — compiles + runs cleanly, prints `r=3 i=4`. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.DUVdMA.cbs`
- sibling: `_Unsafe(c0)` over a `double _Complex` lvalue (no CallExpr, no function call) — same error. Confirms root cause is purely the SafeExpr→VisitExpr fallthrough independent of the SafeExpr's subexpression kind. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.cRElY7.cbs`
- distinct from F60: F60's root cause is in `AggExprEmitter` (CGExprAgg.cpp) for aggregate-typed results. This finding is in a different file (`CGExprComplex.cpp`), different visitor class (`ComplexExprEmitter`), different result-type category (`TEK_Complex` vs `TEK_Aggregate`). The fix is a separate `VisitSafeExpr` override on a separate class. F60 explicitly listed `CGExprComplex.cpp` in its "blast radius" but the campaign convention (see F62's "Broader blast radius — each may need separate filing" line) is that distinct fix-sites get separate filings.
- defect class: **C3** (Visit/switch coverage gap) — sibling-of-F60 in the parallel scalar/aggregate/complex CodeGen emitters trio.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp (IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast)

- shape: `outer_takes_unsafe_t fp = safe_outer;` where outer-fn type `void(*)(unsafe_cb_t)` differs from `safe_outer`'s type `_Safe void(safe_cb_t)` ONLY in the safe-zone qualifier of the *nested* fnptr param (`_Safe void(*)(int)` vs `void(*)(int)`) — **CONFIRMED-new** at `clang/lib/Sema/BSC/SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:486-579` (`IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast`). The function checks safe-zone specifier at lines 547-567 only for the OUTER LHSFuncType/RHSFuncType; never recurses into nested fnptr params/returns. `DoesFunctionPointerSatisfyConstraints` (430-455) does loop over params with strict comparison but the inner safe-zone variance slips through whatever Clang considers "compatible function pointer". Net effect: silent accept; runtime call from `_Safe` body invokes UNSAFE function. — `/tmp/explorer_repro_safe_zone_nested.cbs`
- baseline: same `unsafe_fn → safe_cb_t` conversion at the OUTER fnptr level (no nesting) — correctly REJECTED with "assigning an unsafe function pointer to a safe function pointer type is not allowed". — `/tmp/explorer_baseline_safe_zone_outer.cbs`
- distinct from F41/F53/F56/F57: those cover `_Owned/_Borrow/_Nullable` only-outer-level recursion in `CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType` / redecl predicates. This is the `_Safe/_Unsafe` dimension in `IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast` (different function, different file context, different dimension). Same shape of defect, separate fix surface.
- defect class: **C1 (Ignore-asymmetry / only-outer-level)** — fifth site in the family.

## 2026-05-21 evening (Conductor-side) — F62 blast-radius probe: CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak
- shape: `_Safe(mk());` as bare expression-statement, where `mk()` returns `_Owned`
- probe: compiles clean (only `-Wunused-value` warning), valgrind 1 alloc / 0 frees (definitely lost)
- baseline (no `_Safe` wrap): correctly rejected with `error: memory leak because temporary variable 'mk()' is _Owned or indirect _Owned type, please fix it`
- root cause: `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:534) has the same SafeExpr-strip gap as `CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak` (F62) — `IgnoreParenCasts()` doesn't peel SafeExpr.
- verdict: **FOLDED into F62**. F62's entry already enumerates `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` in its "Broader blast radius" section. Cross-cutting fix (add SafeExpr to `clang/include/clang/AST/IgnoreExpr.h`) closes BOTH predicates; per-call-site fix would leave each distinct.
- Filing decision: do NOT file as separate Fxx; the umbrella comment for F62 already documents this sibling. If maintainer chooses per-call-site fix, revisit and file as F63.

## 2026-05-21 evening (Explorer/WalkerBSC.h) — SafeFeatureFinder gap audit
- shape: caller(int *_Owned *pp) { consume(*pp); consume(*pp); } expecting use-after-move — **INCONCLUSIVE** — /tmp/explorer_probe.iUCDAb.cbs — bug exists (no diagnostic), but cause is BSCOwnership doesn't track *pp moves, NOT a SafeFeatureFinder skip (`PointerType::hasOwnedFields` recurses and catches `int *_Owned *`). With dummy local _Owned forcing walker on (/tmp/explorer_probe.Al8YJ8.cbs), STILL no diagnostic — confirms walker isn't the issue here.
- shape: caller(void (*cb)(int *_Owned), int *_Owned p) { cb(p); cb(p); } in _Safe — **SHAPE-REJECTED** — /tmp/explorer_probe.HWZ5D9.cbs — fn-ptr call forbidden in _Safe zone ("_Unsafe function call is forbidden in the safe zone").
- shape: same out of _Safe (just typed fn-ptr param) — **INCONCLUSIVE** for the walker gap — even though `SafeFeatureFinder::VisitQualType` on `void (*)(int *_Owned)` returns false (FunctionProtoType pointee bypassed by `Type::hasOwnedFields`'s RecordType/PointerType-only switch in TypeBSC.cpp:72-79), the body needed to exercise the gap requires the moved value `p` to be top-level _Owned somewhere, which triggers VisitVarDecl's QT check on the param and re-activates the walker. Cannot construct a body where walker misses AND borrow checker would diagnose.
- shape: global `int *_Owned g;` — **SHAPE-REJECTED** ("type of global variable cannot be qualified by '_Owned'") — /tmp/explorer_probe.C3O5gp.cbs — rules out the global-bypass route to hide _Owned from walker.
- net: SafeFeatureFinder has a real walker gap on `FunctionProtoType` pointee, but it appears observationally inert because every reachable BSC program that *uses* _Owned reach the walker via VarDecl/param/UnaryOperator. Recommend either widening to `BSCFeatureFinder::VisitDeclRefExpr` (different consumer = rewriter routing, like F59 — but the FunctionProtoType walk in `VisitType` already covers this) or moving to other surfaces.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp (no-new-pattern)

Target: `BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp` per-function audit (`init`/`invert`/`&=`/`|=`/`extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr`/`obliviateInfeasible`) + the `BlocksConditionStatusDPVD` single-pair-per-edge hint at `BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:738-741`/`970-975`.

Hypothesis tested: `BlocksConditionStatusDPVD[block][pred]` stores ONE `std::pair<DerefPathVD, NullabilityKind>` (not a map), so multi-deref-narrowing in one condition would overwrite. Hypothesis disproved by `if (*pp && *qq)` shape — CFG splits `&&` so each edge has exactly one trackable deref.

Probed shapes (all clean):
- `if (!(p && q)) return 0; return *p + *q;` — `/tmp/probe_basic_nullable.cbs` — clean.
- `(p && q) ? *p + *q : 0` — `/tmp/probe_cond_with_and.cbs` — clean.
- Double-deref `if (*pp && *qq) { **pp + **qq }` — `/tmp/probe_deref_and5.cbs` — clean.
- Field `if (s.f != nullptr) *s.f` — `/tmp/probe_field_narrow2.cbs` — clean.
- Comma RHS `if ((q, p)) *p` — `/tmp/probe_dr_comma_rhs.cbs` — clean.
- Comma + assign + `&&` `if ((p = foo(p), p) && q) *p+*q` — `/tmp/probe_comma_assign_in_and.cbs` — clean.
- Comma + `&&` for OWNERSHIP `if ((1, (a && b)))` — `/tmp/probe_comma_logop.cbs` — clean.
- For-step comma+`&&` `for (;;(i++, (a && b)))` — `/tmp/probe_for_logop_inc.cbs` — clean.

Conclusion: 8/8 PROBED-clean. The single-pair-per-edge structure is currently sound because CFG splitting + single-extract by `extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr` make the multi-narrow scenario unreachable from in-scope BSC condition shapes. All identified holes in `init()`/`invert()` (e.g. `!(p && q)` over-strong inference, `extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr` not handling ConditionalOperator) are conservatively shadowed by CFG splitting. No new root cause; nothing filed.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on BSCIRBuilder.cpp lowerExpr coverage audit (8 probes, no new pattern)

Surface: BSCIRBuilder.cpp's `Visit*` family for Expr nodes. Hypothesis: a BSC-relevant Expr kind missing from the explicit dispatch falls to `VisitStmt` (Nop + void Constant) which drops sub-expr side effects. Alternatively, a handled kind omits `shouldMove` propagation.

- `consume(c ? mk() : mk())` arg-position ternary, _Owned-returning arms — **INCONCLUSIVE** clean (1 alloc/1 free). `/tmp/explorer_probe.QdY06L.cbs`.
- `int *_Owned r = c ? mk() : mk(); consume(r)` ternary-init-of-local — **INCONCLUSIVE** clean. `/tmp/explorer_probe.Mlt7SI.cbs`.
- `(c ? mk() : mk());` discarded expr-stmt — runtime leak but **FOLDED into F22/F14** (Sema-level CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak's dyn_cast<CallExpr> doesn't see through ConditionalOperator wrapper; pre-BSCIR defect). `/tmp/explorer_probe.YJycXm.cbs`.
- `int *_Owned q = mk(); safe_free(q);` baseline check of VisitDeclStmt missing-shouldMove — **INCONCLUSIVE** clean (dataflow promotes Use(Copy) to Move). `/tmp/explorer_probe.dummy`.
- `consume((struct S){.f = mk()}.f)` CompoundLiteralExpr field access via lowerToPlace temp-duplication — **INCONCLUSIVE** clean. `/tmp/explorer_probe.QdY06L family`.
- `__atomic_exchange_n(&target, mk(), ORDER)` in `_Unsafe` — analyzer flags `newval` leak. **NOT a soundness bug**: VisitAtomicExpr (1162-1191) doesn't call shouldMove on sub-exprs (unlike VisitCallExpr 1013-1014 and VisitInitListExpr 1202-1203), so _Owned args remain Copy → analyzer treats as not-consumed. Conservative behavior; opaque builtin semantics; arguably correct in `_Unsafe`. **Not filed.** `/tmp/explorer_probe.A8jr8s.cbs`, `/tmp/explorer_probe.ZfwERX.cbs`.
- `struct S arr[2] = {{.f = mk(), ...}, ...};` — **SHAPE-REJECTED** (array of struct-with-_Owned). `/tmp/explorer_probe.J64skP.cbs`.

Notes:
- The lowerExpr dispatch covers ALL in-scope C Expr kinds. Falls-through-to-VisitStmt kinds (OffsetOfExpr, ConstantExpr, ExprWithCleanups) are all in contexts where side effects are either forbidden by C (constant context) or not BSC-reachable (C++ cleanups).
- The shouldMove-asymmetry in VisitAtomicExpr is real but exists in `_Unsafe`-only territory.
- `lowerIfStmt`'s EvaluateAsInt constant-fold path (line 368-376) cannot drop in-scope side effects: EvaluateAsInt fails on any expression with observable side effects (function calls, side-effecting builtins). Only `__builtin_constant_p`/`_Generic`-style selection could create a side-effect-bearing constant — and those are OOS.

Recommendation for next session: pivot to BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp / BSCOwnership.cpp transfer functions to find where Use(Copy(_Owned)) gets promoted to Move semantically. Any edge case in that promotion (e.g., aggregate copy with mixed _Owned/non-_Owned fields, or projection through a temp-duplicated Place) is a real defect class.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on SemaDeclBSC.cpp CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment (CONFIRMED-new)

Target: `CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment(QualType, QualType)` at SemaDeclBSC.cpp:156-186. This is the pointer-assignment nullability gate called from SemaExpr.cpp:10334.

- shape: `int *_Nonnull *p_nn = p_raw;` where `p_raw` has type `int **` (no annotation on inner pointee) — **CONFIRMED-new** root cause at SemaDeclBSC.cpp:171. The L171 short-circuit `if (LHSNullability && RHSNullability)` skips the compatibility check when RHS pointee has no nullability annotation (`getNullability` returns None). LHS demands NonNull on pointee; RHS pointee is unannotated → silent accept. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.3e8Cxc.cbs` (compile clean, run SIGSEGV)
- baseline: same shape, RHS pointee explicitly `_Nullable` — correctly rejected with `error: nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable pointer`. — `/tmp/explorer_baseline.u8zWAI.cbs`
- shape (3-level mismatch sanity, NOT a defect): `int *_Nonnull *_Nonnull *_Nonnull p3 = p3nul` where `p3nul` is `int *_Nullable *_Nonnull *_Nonnull` (deepest level mismatch with explicit annotation) — recursion DOES catch the deepest level when BOTH sides have annotations. CLEAN diagnostic. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.bLZ7HZ.cbs`. Confirms the bug is specifically the "missing annotation on RHS pointee" path, not the recursion mechanism.
- distinct from F56 (HasDiffNullabilityQualifiers, SemaDeclBSC.cpp:78-83): F56 is the function-redecl gate (compares two function declarations' type signatures) and the bug is missing-FunctionProtoType-recursion. This finding is in a DIFFERENT predicate (CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment), DIFFERENT caller (CheckAssignmentConstraints — runtime expression checking, not redecl), and DIFFERENT root cause (optional-pair short-circuit vs missing recursion). Both predicates would survive a fix to the other.
- distinct from F18/F26/F28/F29/F31/F48/F49/F50: all those are in BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp (analyzer-side) or other files. This is purely a SemaDecl-level type-compatibility gate that fires WITHOUT `-nullability-check=all`. Default-mode soundness gap.
- defect class: **C1 (Ignore-asymmetry / optional-pair short-circuit)** — new variant: where Clang's nullability uses Optional<NullabilityKind> and the gate requires BOTH options to be present, missing-on-one-side silently passes the check.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp PassConditionStatusToSuccBlocks (CONFIRMED-new)

- shape: `if (_Safe(p) == nullptr) return; *p;` where p is `int *_Borrow _Nullable` parameter — **CONFIRMED-new** false-positive `nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced` at the `*p` line. Root cause: `getVarDeclFromExpr` (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:164), `getMemberExprFromExpr` (:178), `getDerefPathVDFromExpr` (:191) all walk Paren/ImplicitCast wrappers but NONE strip `SafeExpr`. Combined with `getTrackablePtr` (BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp:100) which treats `SafeExpr(lvalue)` as a trackable pointer, the result is that `SetCFGBlocksByExpr` (:729) gets `SafeExpr(p)` and cannot resolve to a VarDecl → no narrowing entries written → post-return `*p` deref sees p as default-Nullable. — `/tmp/explorer_repro.YXctp7.cbs`
- baseline: same source without `_Safe(...)` wrapper compiles clean. — `/tmp/explorer_baseline.Os6sGe.cbs`
- folded sibling: `if (_Safe(p)) { *p; }` (bare condition, no equality op) — same root cause, same false positive — `/tmp/explorer_probe.d4y8wA.cbs`
- additional probes (this cycle, NOT defects):
  - `return p;` from _Borrow-returning fn (Nullable param) — correctly diagnosed — `/tmp/explorer_probe.q4XYtP.cbs`
  - `return (x = 1, p);` BO_Comma-wrapped Nullable return — correctly diagnosed (BO_Comma case at getExprPathNullability:373 handles RHS-recurse) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.5JnYgu.cbs`
  - `return c ? q : p;` ConditionalOperator return with Nullable arm — correctly diagnosed (ConditionalOperatorClass case at :335 propagates Nullable on either arm) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.N5qJ29.cbs`
  - typedef'd `_Borrow` return type `typedef int *_Borrow brw_t; return nullable_p;` — correctly diagnosed (typedef canonicalization preserves Nonnull-by-default for `_Borrow`) — `/tmp/explorer_probe.RcaAkh.cbs`
- distinction from F62 (SafeExpr in `CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak`, SemaBSCOwnership.cpp): different file (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp + BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp vs SemaBSCOwnership.cpp), different predicate (narrowing-flow helpers vs move-leak check), different invariant (path-sensitive nullability propagation vs Sema-time move-detection), different defect direction (FP precision loss vs FN soundness violation). F62's blast-radius list named CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak / getMemberFullField / BuildUnaryOp / CheckBorrowQualTypeCStyleCast but NOT the nullability narrowing path — this finding extends the SafeExpr-wrapper-list-incomplete defect class into a new code locus.

## 2026-05-21 Explorer cycle on BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp CheckInit / FindNonnull on IncompleteArrayType (CONFIRMED-new)

Target: `CheckInit` (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:478-576) and `FindNonnull` (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:251-277) — recursion into array element types for the implicit-zero-fill diagnostic.

- shape: `struct S { int n; struct WithNonnull arr[]; }` (FAM/IncompleteArrayType) with partial init `struct S s = {.n=0};` where `struct WithNonnull { int *_Nonnull p; };` — **CONFIRMED-new** false-positive `type contains nonnull pointer must be properly initialized`. Root cause: `FindNonnull` (line 262-265) treats IncompleteArrayType the same as ConstantArrayType — it recurses on the element type and returns true if the element contains _Nonnull. The caller at `CheckInit` line 484 fires `NonnullInitByDefault` for a FAM that has zero in-place elements. A FAM by C99 has no storage to initialize; nothing requires init. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.5T6EP3.cbs`
- baseline: same struct with `struct WithNullable { int *_Nullable p; }` — compiles clean. Confirms asymmetry is specifically about _Nonnull in FAM element type. — `/tmp/explorer_probe.r8F58P.cbs`
- folded siblings: ConstantArrayType[N] with same shape correctly diagnoses (real elements exist and need init) — NOT a defect. The defect is FAM-specific.
- additional probes (this cycle, all PROBED-clean — NOT defects):
  - `union U { int *_Nonnull p; int x; } arr[5] = { {.p=a}, {.p=b} };` partial array of union — correctly diagnoses trailing fill — `/tmp/explorer_probe.hbIiCw.cbs`
  - struct-of-struct-of-struct nested _Nonnull, outer partial init — correctly diagnoses deep recursion — same probe path reused
  - `union U u = {}` empty init — correctly diagnoses (Sema sets InitializedFieldInUnion to first member, not nullptr) — same probe reused
  - anonymous union inside struct, outer designated init bypasses union — correctly diagnoses
- defect class: **C1 (Ignore-asymmetry / type-discrimination missing)** — `FindNonnull`'s `isArrayType()` branch at line 262 unconditionally recurses without checking whether the array is incomplete (IncompleteArrayType) or constant (ConstantArrayType). Distinct from F19/F23/F44 (BSCOwnership.cpp, _Owned-field uninit FPs) — those are in a different analyzer with different state model. Distinct from F17 (compound-literal init wrapper FP) — that's a CompoundLiteralExpr wrapper miss, not an array-type-kind miss.

## 2026-05-22 DFS::Copy candidate #1 over-approximation — SHAPE-REJECTED
- Hypothesis: at a no-return leaf, `SuccessorPoints.empty()` extends `To` with EndPoint, over-approximating the borrow region.
- Probe: `int *_Borrow b1; if (c) { *b1; _Unsafe { die(); }} ...` (die() declared `_Noreturn`).
- Dump observation: the CFG block containing `die()` is given succs `[0]` (exit block) — BSC's CFG does NOT leave no-return calls as empty-successors leaves. Only the actual exit block (BB0) has empty succs.
- Result: `SuccessorPoints.empty()` branch only fires at BB0, where adding EndPoint is correct by construction. The hypothesized over-approximation does not trigger via no-return calls.
- SHAPE-REJECTED at the CFG-construction level. Move on.

- `int *_Owned *_Borrow b = &_Mut s.p; int *_Owned moved = _Unsafe(*b);` then double-free of `moved` + `s.p` → valgrind Invalid free (1 alloc, 2 frees).
- Same root cause as F62 (CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak IgnoreParenCasts misses SafeExpr) but the move-through site is `*b` for `b = &_Mut s.p` rather than direct `s->p`.

- FOLDED into F62 (IJP3AP umbrella entry).

## 2026-05-22 borrow-check direct-probe sweep — saturated
- arrow vs (*s) — correctly identified as same path.
- paren wraps: `(s)->f`, `(s->f)` — all correctly diagnosed.
- diamond merge `if(c){b1=&_Mut x;}else{b1=&_Mut x;} b2=&_Mut x;` — correctly diagnosed.
- diamond CFG with `_Noreturn` call leaf — DFS::Copy candidate #1 (empty-SuccessorPoints over-approx) SHAPE-REJECTED by CFG construction (no-return calls get synthetic edge to exit block 0).
- for-init borrow followed by post-loop borrow — correctly scoped.
- re-borrow chain `&_Const *mut` — correctly tracks original mut.
- borrow of struct field through `_Owned *_Owned p` — correctly diagnoses move-while-borrowed.
- borrow returned via fn (`get_a(s)`, `get_b(s)`) — conservatively over-approximates and rejects (precision limitation).
- `_Borrow _ArrayElem arr` + `arr + 1` — over-approximates and rejects.
- borrow-of-borrow (`int *_Borrow *_Borrow`) — Sema rejects (nested borrow not allowed).

Direct borrow-check surface is hardened. Remaining hot zones are at HANDOFFS:
- borrow ↔ ownership at struct field access through SafeExpr wraps (F62 family fold above)
- redecl-time fnptr nested borrow/owned qualifier (F53 family, already in bug_log)
- borrow ↔ init-analysis cross-check at function call points (chain C territory)

## 2026-05-22 IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:486-579) — RESTATUS
- Note had CONFIRMED-NEW status claiming the outer-only safe-zone check missed nested-fnptr variance.
- Re-probed both directions: `OuterUnsafe fp = caller_with_safe;` and `OuterSafe fp = caller_with_unsafe;` — BOTH correctly rejected.
- The rejection comes from a combination of BSC's `IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast` outer check (line 547-560) AND Clang's generic "incompatible function pointer types" check (because BSC's `getFunSafeZoneSpecifier()` is part of the canonical FunctionProtoType, so canonical equality reflects safe-zone difference).
- Status: appears FIXED/COVERED in current binary. Recommend updating SemaBSCSafeZone.md to mark FOLDED-into-FunctionProtoType-canonical-equality (which subsumes nested variance for direct typedef expansions).
- A laundering attack (cast through void* to bypass canonical type comparison) is rejected at the void* assignment point in safe zone, so the original CONFIRMED-NEW claim is not currently exploitable.

## 2026-05-22 lowerDoWhileStmt direct read — INCONCLUSIVE
- shape: `do { int *_Owned p = malloc; if(c) free(p); } while(0);` → analyzer correctly diags leak when `!c` path reachable. CORRECT (no bug).
- shape: do-while + early `return` with free → SHAPE-REJECTED at `c++` in cond (BSC safe zone forbids value-producing `++`).
- No new candidate surfaced. Function notes added to BSCIRBuilder.md.

## 2026-05-22 AreOwnedBorrowQualifiersCompatible (TypeBSC.cpp:154-181) — PROBED-confirmed-F57
- Probe: `_Unsafe void f(void (*cb)(int *_Owned)); _Safe void f(void (*cb)(int *));` → compiles clean (SAFE side drops nested `_Owned` qualifier without diag).
- Baseline (outer level, same direction): `_Unsafe void f(int *_Owned); _Safe void f(int *);` → correctly rejected with "function 'f' has incompatible _Safe and _Unsafe declarations".
- Exactly matches F57's symptom: nested `_Owned` in fnptr param dropped silently on SAFE/UNSAFE heterogeneous redecl.
- Direction matters: SAFE-has-qualifier→UNSAFE-drops-it is allowed by invariant (predicate correctly accepts); UNSAFE-has-qualifier→SAFE-drops-it is the soundness-violating direction (predicate misses at nested level).
- FOLDED-F57. Note in TypeBSC.md updates: candidate #1 status changes from UNPROBED to PROBED-confirmed-F57.

- `if (p || p) { *p; }` → compile clean (p narrowed via intersection). CORRECT.

- `if (p || !p) { *p; }` → diag (tautology produces empty intersection; p stays Nullable). CORRECT.

## 2026-05-22 F67 surface — CONFIRMED-new HIGH soundness
- `struct S { int *_Owned p; }; consume(s.p); int x = *s.p;` → compile clean, valgrind Invalid read.
- Baseline 1 (double-consume): correctly diags use-of-moved at second consume site → move IS tracked.

## 2026-05-22 F67 blast radius (Exploiter-style)
- `int x = *s.p;` (read) → no diag, runtime Invalid read. ← original confirmed shape
- `*s.p = 99;` (write) → no diag. SAME ROOT CAUSE (FOLDED into F67).
- `*s.p += 1;` (compound assign) → no diag. FOLDED.
- `(*s.p)++;` (postinc) → no diag. FOLDED.
- `&_Const *s.p` (re-borrow as const) → correctly diags use-of-moved. Different code path; borrow-creation check fires.
- `&_Mut *s.p` (mut-borrow) → correctly diags use-of-moved. Same as above.
- Gap is specifically: **deref-as-direct-operand of read/write/compound-op/inc-dec at site of `*s.p` for moved field**. Borrow-creation paths catch correctly.
- Fix surface narrows: the analyzer's UO_Deref handler when the sub-expression is a MemberExpr on an `_Owned` field — needs to consult `SOwnedOwnedFields[base_VD]` membership of the field before allowing the deref operation.

## 2026-05-22 narrowing chains — CORRECT
- `if (p && q) { *p + *q; }` → both narrowed; deref accepts.
- `if (!p || !q) return; *p + *q;` → De Morgan; both narrowed in fallthrough.
- `take(c ? a : b)` with a,b narrowed nonnull in scope → accepted at call site.

## 2026-05-22 F67 ROOT CAUSE TRACED
- Trace: `*s.p` → VisitUnaryOperator (BSCOwnership.cpp:2107) accumulates suffix="*" and calls VisitDeclRefExpr(DRE_for_s, "p*").
- VisitDeclRefExpr → HandleDREUse(DRE, "p*").
- HandleDREUse for SStatus (line 2550-2562): replaces trailing '*' with '.' → "p.", strips trailing '.', checks SAllOwnedFields[s].count("p") = TRUE → calls checkSFieldUse with fullFieldName="p." (with the '.').
- checkSFieldUse (line 1336-1343): checks `SAllOwnedFields[VD].count(fullFieldName)` where fullFieldName="p." — but SAllOwnedFields contains "p" NOT "p.". So this check is FALSE → diag doesn't fire.
- Same gap: also calls checkSFieldUse with fullFieldName="p*" — again "p*" ∉ SAllOwnedFields.
- Fix surface (precise): in checkSFieldUse line 1336-1343, when fullFieldName ends in '.' or '*', also check the BARE base name in SOwnedOwnedFields (and SNullOwnedFields). i.e., strip the trailing suffix character and re-test membership.
- Alternative fix: have HandleDREUse pass the BARE name "p" to checkSFieldUse (not "p." or "p*") for the deref/access-base case, plus a separate flag indicating "deref happens".

## 2026-05-22 F67 fold: struct-by-value move + deref
- `struct S s = {.p = malloc}; taker(s); int x = *s.p;` (where taker takes `struct S` by value) → compile clean; valgrind Invalid read.
- Baseline (same setup, but `safe_free((void *_Owned)s.p)` instead of `*s.p`): correctly diags "invalid cast of moved or uninitialized value: s.p".
- Same gap: any deref-via-MemberExpr (`*s.p`) after struct-level move bypasses the check; direct re-use as l-value-of-cast catches.
- FOLDED into F67.

## 2026-05-22 F67 boundary check: array of owned-field struct → SHAPE-REJECTED
- `struct S arr[2]` where S has `_Owned` field — Sema rejects with "type of array cannot be qualified by _Owned (even indirectly)". Can't probe this composition.

## 2026-05-22 F67 write-path also traced
- `*s.p = 99` (after consume(s.p)) goes through VisitBinaryOperator BO_Assign → VisitUnaryOperator (suffix='*') → VisitDeclRefExpr → HandleDREAssign(DRE, "p*").
- HandleDREAssign SStatus branch strips trailing '*' to '.', tests `SAllOwnedFields[VD].count("p")` (substr) = TRUE, calls checkSFieldAssign with "p." and then "p*".
- checkSFieldAssign (line 1419) parent-walk at line 1444-1466 starts with index = length("p.")-2 = 0, fails loop guard immediately. The base-name check is missed.
- Same gap as F67's read path: suffix-modified field name doesn't match the bare base name stored in SAllOwnedFields.
- Both read (checkSFieldUse) and write (checkSFieldAssign) have the SAME suffix-mismatch bug. Fix surface: either teach both check functions to also test the bare base name, OR have HandleDREUse/HandleDREAssign pass the bare name with a separate "has-deref-suffix" flag.

## 2026-05-22 deref null-init _Owned _Nullable field — CORRECT
- `struct S s = {.p = nullptr}; int x = *s.p;` → nullability analyzer diags "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced". Catches before ownership.
- The two analyzers are independent: nullability fires fast on Null state; ownership has the F67 gap on Moved state.

## 2026-05-22 F67 fold via struct-copy move
- `struct S s1 = {.p = malloc}; struct S s2 = s1; int x = *s1.p;` → compile clean; valgrind Invalid read.
- Baseline: `struct S s2 = s1; consume(s1.p);` → correctly diags "use of moved value: s1.p". So struct-copy DOES mark s1.p as moved.
- Only the deref site `*s1.p` misses the check. FOLDED into F67.

## 2026-05-22 isUnionStructFieldPath candidate #1 (out-of-range index) — SHAPE-REJECTED
- Candidate requires a malformed FieldPath with an index >= field count. Valid BSC AST won't produce this; the FieldPath is built from MemberExpr's getMemberDecl()->getFieldIndex() which is bounded. Not reachable from user code.

## 2026-05-22 owned-check dump tooling gap (NOT a soundness bug)
- Running `-dump-owned-check` on F67's repro shows only the WHOLE-VAR status `s: [Owned]` at all blocks.
- Per-field state (`SOwnedOwnedFields`, `SNullOwnedFields`, `SAllOwnedFields`) is NOT exposed in the YAML.
- For F67-class bugs (per-field state read-side gap), the dump can't reveal "field p was moved" — only the var-level status. Tooling improvement: surface per-field sets in the dump.
- The diag-correlation feature works (when a diag fires, we see kind/block/name). But for SILENT misses (F67), no diag → no clue from the dump.
- Recommendation for the dump-tool author: add per-field state lines under `struct: - var: "s"` showing `owned_fields: ["p"]` `null_fields: []` `all_owned_fields: ["p"]`. Would make F67-class bugs visible from the dump alone.

## 2026-05-22 F67 root cause confirmed across VisitArraySubscriptExpr too
- VisitArraySubscriptExpr (BSCOwnership.cpp:2045) builds `suffix += "*"` in a loop over nested subscripts, then dispatches to `VisitDeclRefExpr(DRE, memberField.second + suffix)` — SAME pattern as VisitUnaryOperator.
- So `arr[i]` on a moved owned-array-elem field goes through the same suffix-mismatch path: fullFieldName="p*" doesn't match SAllOwnedFields["p"], check misses.
- This generalizes F67: any DEREF-LIKE access path (unary-deref + array-subscript) through a MemberExpr base produces the suffix-mismatched name. F67 is therefore not just about `*s.p` — it's about ALL deref-like accesses of moved owned fields.
- Fix surface remains the SAME 2-line edit per F67 (suffix-strip and re-test bare name in checkSFieldUse/checkSFieldAssign).

## 2026-05-22 nullability narrow path identity arrow vs (*s).f — FOLDED-F33
- `if (s->f != nullptr) { *(*s).f; }` → diag "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" despite narrowing.
- `if ((*s).f != nullptr) { *s->f; }` → same diag in reverse direction.
- Root cause is F33: `VisitMEForFieldPath` (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:148-162) doesn't handle UnaryOperator UO_Deref bases. `(*s).f` paths get FieldPath{nullptr, ".f"} while `s->f` paths get FieldPath{s, ".f"} — different keys, narrowing doesn't bridge.
- FOLDED into F33 (in umbrella IJP3AP).

- F33's original repro (`repro/F33_fieldpath_nullptr_collision.cbs`) still compiles clean and valgrind still reports Invalid read of size 4.
- Bug NOT yet fixed in the current binary (despite F33 being on umbrella IJP3AP).
- VisitMEForFieldPath UnaryOperator UO_Deref handling still missing.
- Related F67-style fold (my just-tested arrow→dot-deref asymmetry) folds into this — same fix surface.

## 2026-05-22 F33 fold via CStyleCast wrapping
- `if (((struct S *_Borrow)s)->f != nullptr) { *s->f; }` → diag (narrow lost).
- Baseline: `if (((s))->f != nullptr) { *s->f; }` (ParenExpr wrap) → compile clean (narrowing preserved). ParenExpr IS in VisitMEForFieldPath; CStyleCastExpr is NOT.
- Same root cause as F33: VisitMEForFieldPath (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:149-163) handles only MemberExpr, DeclRefExpr, ImplicitCastExpr, ParenExpr. Any other AST kind in the recursive base position bails → FieldPath.first stays nullptr → collision / no-narrowing.
- Wrapper-list to expand for F33 fix: UnaryOperator (UO_Deref), CStyleCastExpr (this probe), likely ArraySubscriptExpr, ConditionalOperator, BinaryOperator (BO_Comma/BO_Assign).
- FOLDED into F33.

## 2026-05-22 F39 fold via chained array subscript
- `b[0] = 99; &_Mut arr[0][1];` — analyzer accepts both borrows due to F39's DefUse::VisitArraySubscriptExpr missing.
- Runtime safe (distinct memory locations) but analyzer's soundness rule is violated.
- FOLDED into F39.

## 2026-05-22 postinc-vs-compound-assign nullability asymmetry — CORRECT (not a bug, asymmetric in user's favor)
- `p += 1; *p;` → no diag (F48 launder via VisitBinaryOperator).
- `p++; *p;` → CORRECTLY diags. Postinc preserves nullable state.
- Asymmetry exists between compound-assign and postinc handling; postinc goes through VisitUnaryOperator (not the state-update at line 619). Postinc happens to NOT update CurrStatusVD, so the original Nullable narrowing persists.
- F48's fix (preserve CurrStatusVD on compound-assign) would close the asymmetry by aligning compound to the postinc-style behavior.

- Marked INCONCLUSIVE; would need a Cycle 14-style dive into the projection-slice logic to confirm.

## 2026-05-22 ActionExtract::VisitBinAssign paren-wrapped compound-lit RHS — CORRECT
- `s = ((struct B){.p = malloc});` (paren around RHS compound-lit) → IgnoreImpCasts doesn't strip ParenExpr; special-case at line 449-455 doesn't match. Falls to else branch (Kind=Assign).
- Runtime: 2 allocs/2 frees, no errors. Sound.
- The fallback (Kind=Assign with possible Init demotion) handles correctly.

## 2026-05-22 ActionExtract::VisitReturnStmt compound-lit return — CORRECT
- `return (struct B){.p = malloc};` from `_Safe struct B mk(void)` → 1 alloc / 1 free. Sound.
- VisitReturnStmt's "tracked return type" path (line 718-727) treats this as Assign with Dest=__ret, correctly handles the compound-literal RHS.

## 2026-05-22 F67 boundary confirmed: local owned var works correctly
- `wrap(p) { struct B b = {.p = p}; int x = *p; ...}` → correctly diags "use of moved value: p".
- Confirms F67 is SPECIFIC to struct-field deref. Local-owned-var deref-after-move catches via HandleDREUse's no-suffix path (fullFieldName="").
- F67 surface narrowed: gap is ONLY when MemberExpr-base feeds a deref (UnaryDeref or ArraySubscript). Direct local-var deref works.

## 2026-05-22 narrow via goto-on-null — CORRECT
- `if (p == nullptr) goto end; return *p;` → after goto, fallthrough has p narrowed Nonnull. Compiles clean.
- Standard early-return pattern via goto works as well as via return.

## 2026-05-22 inverted/double-inverted null checks — CORRECT
- `if (!(p == nullptr)) { *p; }` → compile clean (narrowing via NullCheckInfo::invert).
- `if (!!p) { *p; }` → compile clean (double-not idiom narrows).
- Both standard narrowing patterns work.

## 2026-05-22 loop narrow + reassign-to-null inside body — CORRECT
- `while (p != nullptr) { int x = *p; p = nullptr; }` → compile clean. Narrow holds inside body; reassign to null invalidates state at loop-back (cond re-evaluated).

## 2026-05-22 loop conditional free + break — CORRECT
- `while (c > 0) { if (c == 1) { safe_free(p); break; } c--; } return 0;` → correctly diags leak (the natural-exit path doesn't free).
- Loop-merge of break-path (Owned cleared) and natural-exit-path (Owned still) correctly identifies the leak risk.

## 2026-05-22 _Owned (NonNull) → _Owned _Nullable param move — CORRECT
- `take_nullable(p)` where p is `int *_Owned` (NonNull) and take_nullable takes `int *_Owned _Nullable` → compile clean. NonNull widens to Nullable on arg-passing; move semantics preserved.

## 2026-05-22 direct call _Owned _Nullable → _Owned param — CORRECT
- `take_nonnull(q)` where q is `_Owned _Nullable`, param is `_Owned` (NonNull) → diags "cannot pass nullable pointer argument". CORRECT.
- F28/F49 about INDIRECT calls bypassing this check; direct call works.

## 2026-05-22 _Borrow _ArrayElem of struct.arr[i] — CORRECT (NLL)
- `int *_Borrow _ArrayElem b = &_Mut s->arr[0]; *b = 99; return s->arr[0];` → compile clean.
- b's last use is `*b = 99`; b dead by `return`. NLL allows sibling read. Sound.

## 2026-05-22 ActionExtract::VisitInitListExpr union iteration potential out-of-bounds — SHAPE-REJECTED
- Function iterates ALL fields (line 603) but unions have only 1 explicit init in ILE.
- For non-trivial union initialization, getInit(Index) where Index > 0 would be implicit-value-init or out-of-bounds.
- However: BSC Sema rejects tracked types (_Borrow, _Owned, _ArrayElem) in union fields. So the union-with-tracked-fields case is unreachable.
- Untracked unions skip via `IsTrackedType(ILE->getType())` gate at line 596.
- SHAPE-REJECTED at Sema level.

## 2026-05-22 nested struct init — CORRECT
- Full nested init `{.inner={.p=malloc}, .v=5}` → compiles clean. Inner's owned field correctly tracked.
- Missing nested init `{.v=5}` (inner.p uninit) → correctly diags "type contains nonnull pointer must be properly initialized".

## 2026-05-22 obliviateInfeasible vs CFG narrowing — interaction
- `if (p && !p) { *p; }` — analyzer narrows p to NonNull in body (per dump).
- The CFG decomposes `&&` into nested ifs; outer narrows to NonNull, inner contradicts but doesn't override the outer (last-write-wins backwards via join).
- NullCheckInfo's obliviateInfeasible (called at NullCheckInfo construction, BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp:378) handles set-level contradictions, but CFG-based narrowing is a separate mechanism that takes precedence.
- Result: contradiction-in-condition body is accepted as if narrowed; dead code at runtime; not a soundness bug.
- Precision issue only.

## 2026-05-22 narrowing across fn-return — CORRECT
- `int *_Borrow _Nullable r = get(q);` where get returns _Nullable; r is Nullable per type. *r diags. CORRECT (narrowing doesn't survive opaque fn returns).

## 2026-05-22 forget<T>(_Owned) — BY DESIGN
- `forget<int *_Owned>(p)` consumes p without freeing — intentional leak.
- Analyzer treats p as moved (no leak diag); runtime leaks 1 alloc as expected.
- forget IS the documented "intentional leak" primitive. No bug.

## 2026-05-22 safe_swap<_Owned> — CORRECT
- swap two _Owned pointers via safe_swap then free both → 2 allocs/2 frees, sound.

## 2026-05-22 const-borrow + nullable field narrow — CORRECT
- `const struct S *_Borrow s` + `if (s->p != nullptr) { *s->p; }` → narrow correctly applied to const-borrowed struct's owned nullable field.

## 2026-05-22 narrow inside do-while body — CORRECT

## 2026-05-22 F45 root-cause refinement — broader trigger than originally documented
- F45 was filed with the chain: nullptr-init → reassign → move-out → `&_Mut s` (migration) → re-move → double-free.
- My probe today: same chain but with `&_Const s` (no isAddrMut migration). Still produces double-free.
- Root cause: the stale SNullOwnedFields["b"] entry (from F45's root cause A) ALONE fools `checkSFieldUse`'s line 1336-1343 check, which uses `OR` between SOwnedOwnedFields and SNullOwnedFields membership: `!(in_owned || in_null)`. Even when the field has been MOVED (not in owned), the stale Null entry makes the OR true, suppressing the use-of-moved diag.
- F45 doesn't actually require &_Mut to trigger — only requires the stale-Null entry from checkSFieldAssign + a subsequent move + subsequent re-use.
- The F44 isAddrMut migration is a SEPARATE bug that also leverages stale entries, but F45 itself can trigger via const borrow or any intermediate operation.
- Update to F45's documented trigger: NO &_Mut migration needed. Pure F45-A + checkSFieldUse's lenient check.
- This makes F45's reach broader than originally bug_log'd.

## 2026-05-22 F45 boundary: nested struct field does NOT inherit
- `struct Outer { struct Inner i; }; struct Outer o = {.i = {.b = nullptr}}; o.i.b = malloc; double-free` → analyzer correctly diags.
- F45's stale-Null gap does NOT propagate to NESTED field paths. HandleInitListExpr for nested struct init probably doesn't add "i.b" to SNullOwnedFields (only handles top-level null-fields).
- F45 is therefore limited to top-level _Owned _Nullable fields of a struct.

## 2026-05-22 F45 root-cause path: typed-cast (not void-cast)
- F45's trigger requires the move-out to be via TYPED cast `(int *_Owned)s.b` — these go through HandleDREAssign → checkSFieldUse (which has the LENIENT `count(SOwned)||count(SNull)` check at line 1336-1339).
- Void casts `(void *_Owned)s.b` go through VisitCStyleCastExpr → checkCastField, which uses the STRICT `!count(SOwned)` check at line 1849.
- So F45 specifically slips through the typed-cast path; void-cast path catches.
- Explicit `s.b = nullptr; s.b = malloc; safe_free; safe_free;` (void-cast double-free) is correctly diagnosed.
- F45's BROADER trigger documented earlier still requires typed-cast moves.

## 2026-05-22 F67 + F45 combo — same gap, accepted
- `struct S = {.b=nullptr}; s.b=malloc; consume(s.b); *s.b;` → compile clean (F67 gap; F45 stale Null doesn't matter at deref site since the suffix-mismatch already prevents the check).

## 2026-05-29 — safe-zone raw-pointer subscript bypass (Mode-1 read of DiagnoseInvalidUnaryExprInSafeZone)
- `int *_Nonnull q; q[i]` inside `_Safe` (read or `q[i]=v` write) — **CONFIRMED-F68** (HIGH; filed). Safe-zone raw-deref guard `DiagnoseInvalidUnaryExprInSafeZone` (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:1207 `case UO_Deref`) is UnaryOperator-only; ArraySubscriptExpr build path has no safe-zone deref hook → `q[i]` accepted while `*q` / `*(q+0)` rejected. Valgrind: Invalid write+read from `_Safe` code. `repro/F68_safe_zone_array_subscript_raw_deref.cbs`. Controls run: `q[5]` const-index (clean), `q[i]=v` runtime write (clean), `*(q+0)` (rejected), plain `int*_Nonnull` non-owned (same — ownership-independent). Distinct check from F36/F39.
- Folded into F68 (do not re-probe): `(void*_Owned)` cast of `p[0]` double-consume — the safe-zone subscript bypass is the same root; the move-not-tracked half folds into F64.
- BSCOwnership TransferFunctions::VisitArraySubscriptExpr (2045) / VisitMemberExpr (2069) / VisitBinaryOperator (2150, assign+nonassign) / VisitCStyleCastExpr void-owned (2237) read this cycle: candidates fold into F34 (IsTrackedType gap) / F46 (getMemberFullField paren / setToNull) / F64 (pointer-to-owned base untracked); arrays-of-owned Sema-forbidden (lines 36/198/303/395). Non-assign BinaryOperator branch (comma/comparison/additive→GetAddr) unreachable for owned operands. No new root cause in BSCOwnership this cycle.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — F68 blast-radius: sibling safe-zone guard arms
- `q += 1` / `q -= 2` on raw `int *_Nonnull` in `_Safe` — **SHAPE-REJECTED (not a soundness defect)**. Differential: `q++` REJECTED (`'++' operator is forbidden`), `q += 1`/`q -= 2` accepted (`/tmp/a1..a3.cbs`). BUT per bsc-safe-zone docs the forbidden pointer-op list is {`&`, raw deref, pointer category/cross-type casts, NULL}; raw pointer ARITHMETIC (`q+1`, `q+=1`) is NOT forbidden — only DEREFERENCE is. `q += 1` produces a pointer value, derefs nothing; the result can't be deref'd except via `q[i]` (= F68). So the `++`/`--` pointer prohibition's `+=` "bypass" doesn't cross a memory-safety line. Only F68 (subscript = raw deref) violates the documented "no raw pointer dereference" rule. Not filed (avoids F49/F38-style noise). The `q++` prohibition itself is a separate stylistic/UX restriction, not soundness.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — null-checker subscript narrowing (getExprPathNullability ArraySubscript case)
- `if (pp[i] != nullptr) *pp[i];` (pp: `int *_Nullable *_Nonnull`, `-nullability-check=all`) — **SHAPE-REJECTED / working-as-intended**. Rejected "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" while the deref-chain form `if (*pp != nullptr) **pp;` is clean (`/tmp/n1.cbs` vs `/tmp/n2.cbs`). Constant index `pp[0]` ALSO rejected (`/tmp/c0.cbs`). Temp-binding `int *_Nullable e = pp[0]; if (e != nullptr) *e;` is CLEAN (`/tmp/c2.cbs`, the intended workaround). **NOT a bug**: `getExprPathNullability` ArraySubscriptExprClass case (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:397-401) deliberately returns the STATIC element-type nullability with the comment "Builtin array elements cannot have independent path-sensitive state" — array elements intentionally get no flow-narrowed state (element aliasing). `getDerefPathVDFromExpr` (line 205-210) only models UO_Deref chains by the same design. Asymmetry deref-narrows / subscript-doesn't is INTENTIONAL. Folds into existing _probed:373 (`arr[0].f` narrowing filtered by containsArrayAccess). Not filed — filing against an explicit design comment would be noise.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — codegen SafeExpr coverage completion (ConstExprEmitter)
- `struct S g = _Safe((struct S){42,7});` static/file-scope aggregate init — **CONFIRMED-F69** (MEDIUM; filed). Sema clean (`run.sh` exit 0), codegen errors "cannot compile this static initializer yet" (CodeGenModule.cpp:4746). Root: ConstExprEmitter (CGExprConstant.cpp:1015) missing VisitSafeExpr; SafeExpr → VisitStmt → null → tryEmitForInitializer fails. `_Unsafe` same. SCALAR static `const int g = _Safe(5)` codegens fine (APValue fallback masks). Array `_Safe((int[3]){...})` = separate syntax rejection. Distinct emitter from F60 (AggExprEmitter runtime) / F63 (ComplexExprEmitter); F60 fix doesn't close it. `repro/F69_safeexpr_static_aggregate_initializer.cbs`.
- Codegen SafeExpr-unwrap audit now COMPLETE: Scalar(ok)/LValue(ok)/Agg(F60)/Complex(F63)/Const(F69). No further codegen VisitSafeExpr gaps.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — rewriter SafeExpr handling (closes cross-layer SafeExpr audit)
- `-rewrite-bsc` on `_Safe(a + b)` and `_Unsafe(x * 2)` — **CLEAN (no defect)**. Emits `(a + b)` / `(x * 2)`; keyword stripped, sub-expr preserved (no F09-style drop), zero literal `_Safe`/`_Unsafe` leaked to .c. `/tmp/rw.cbs` → `/tmp/rw.c`. RewriteBSC.cpp has no explicit SafeExpr handler but the source-rewriter strips the keyword tokens + keeps the parenthesized sub-expr correctly.
- **Cross-layer SafeExpr audit now COMPLETE**: Sema accepts; codegen Scalar(ok)/LValue(ok)/Agg(F60)/Complex(F63)/Const(F69); rewriter(ok). No remaining SafeExpr-unwrap gaps in any layer.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — init-analysis whole-struct copy + merge (transferStatement Assign :96-103)
- Whole-struct copy `struct S s2 = s1;` of a PARTIALLY-init s1 (`s1.a=5;` only) — **CORRECTLY FLAGGED** "use of uninitialized value: s1" (`/tmp/u1.cbs`). FN-safe.
- All-fields-init then whole read (flat `/tmp/u3`, nested `/tmp/u4`, pointer-field `/tmp/u5`) — **CLEAN**. tryPromoteParent correctly promotes all-fields-init → whole-init before a whole read. FP-safe.
- **Init-MERGE asymmetry** (`if(c){s1.b=6;}` then whole read of s1) — **CORRECTLY FLAGGED** "use of POSSIBLY uninitialized value: s1" (`/tmp/m1.cbs`); symmetric both-branch-init control clean (`/tmp/m2`); direct field read control flagged (`/tmp/m3`). The init dataflow meet is conservative — **NO F26/F35-style merge-asymmetry FN** in the init analyzer (unlike nullability mergeDPVD / ownership merge). 
- Conclusion: BSCIRInitAnalysis transferStatement Assign whole-local + tryPromoteParent + merge are sound in all probed directions. No bug. INCONCLUSIVE/clean.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — BorrowCheckerPrologue loop transforms (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:640-795)
- `for (int i=0; i<3; consume(p))` consuming `_Owned` p in the for-INCREMENT — **CORRECTLY FLAGGED** "use of moved value: `p`" (+ leak diag) (`/tmp/fi.cbs`). The loop back-edge re-move IS modeled: the Prologue wraps for/while/do cond+inc in a compiler-generated StmtExpr `({x=cond;x;})` keeping hoisted temps in-place (per-iteration), and the borrow checker's CFG models the back-edge. No FN.
- Conclusion: Prologue loop transforms (TransformForStmt/WhileStmt/DoStmt) are SOUND — StmtExpr-wrapping correctly avoids the eager-hoist for re-evaluated cond/inc. F40 (TransformBinaryOperator eager-hoist of &&/|| RHS) is the only Prologue defect; &&/||-in-loop-cond variants FOLD into F40 (same TransformBinaryOperator root). TransformConditionalOperator builds an IfStmt (conditional preserved, correct). INCONCLUSIVE/clean — no new bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — NullCheckInfo::invert() De-Morgan FN
- `if (!(p == nullptr && x > 0)) return *p;` under -nullability-check=all — **CONFIRMED-F70** (HIGH; filed). Compiles clean, runtime SIGSEGV (`probe(nullptr,0)` enters then-branch with p null). Root: NullCheckInfo::invert() (BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp:227) unconditionally swaps null↔present sets; for a compound `&&`-combined condition this is a broken De Morgan (`!(A&&B)`=`!A||!B`, a disjunction → no guarantee). Controls: `!(p==nullptr)` simple-negation correct; bare `*p` rejected. Distinct from F26 (mergeDPVD CFG-merge), F12 (ternary), F18/F48/F50 (arith/compound-assign laundering). repro/F70_nullcheck_invert_compound_demorgan.cbs. Note: runtime repro must NOT link bishengc_safety.cbs (not clean under the flag).
- OR-variant `!(p == nullptr || cond)` → init |= intersects null sets → {} → invert → {} → no over-claim (safe). `!(p != nullptr && cond)` → present={p} → invert → null={p} → over-claims NULL (FP/conservative, safe). The FN is specifically negation of `&&` containing a `==nullptr` conjunct.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — BorrowCheckerEpilogue::TransformSwitchStmt cond-restore asymmetry
- `switch (<substitutable cond>)` in a borrow-checked function — **PROBED-BENIGN (not filed)**. TransformSwitchStmt (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:1236-1237) does NOT restore the cond via replacedNodesMap (unlike TransformIfStmt:1177 / While:1249 / For:1150 / Do:1131 which DO) — a real C4 code asymmetry. But NO observable defect: `switch(sel+*b)`→100 200 999, `switch(getv(b))`→100, side-effecting `switch(tick()+*b)`→calls=1 (single eval), codegen clean (`/tmp/swr.cbs`, `/tmp/swc.cbs`, `/tmp/swe.cbs`). Benign: Prologue hoists `tmp=cond` + sets `switch(tmp)`; Epilogue leaving cond unrestored = `tmp=cond; switch(tmp)`, semantically identical/single-eval. Latent inconsistency worth a tidy, not a bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — short-circuit &&/|| narrowing within a condition (bounds F70)
- `if (p != nullptr && *p == 5)`, chained `pp != null && *pp != null && **pp`, and `if (p == nullptr || *p == 5)` under -nullability-check=all — **ALL CLEAN** (`/tmp/sc1..sc3.cbs`). Re-confirms prior _probed:318/362/363/440: short-circuit narrowing within a condition is SOUND (CFG-splitting gives each block its own pred narrowing). Combined with F70 (compound-negation `!(p==null && cond)` mis-narrows via invert()) and the simple-negation control (sound), this BOUNDS F70 precisely: the gap is ONLY negation of an &&/||-combined null-check via NullCheckInfo::invert()'s blind set-swap, not short-circuit narrowing or simple negation. No new bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — ensure_init callee-exit contract (BSCIRInitAnalysis checkEnsureInitAtReturn)
- `void f(int *__attribute__((ensure_init)) out, int c)` initializing `*out` only on the c-branch — **CORRECTLY FLAGGED** "'*out' may not be initialized on all paths at return" (`/tmp/ei1.cbs`). Controls: all-paths-init clean (`/tmp/ei2`), never-init → "not initialized at return" (`/tmp/ei3`), struct-pointee partial field (`out->a` only, `out->b` not) → "not initialized at return" (`/tmp/ei4`), struct-pointee full → clean (`/tmp/ei5`). All -uninit-check=all.
- Conclusion: ensure_init callee-exit contract is SOUND — conservative meetStates merge (BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:405) + field-level tracking. The EnsureInitDerefStates merge's Dst-only-entry asymmetry (:398-411, doesn't meet-with-Uninit unlike FieldStates) is UNREACHABLE (every ensure_init param seeded Uninitialized at entry :80, so both states always carry the entry). No FN. INCONCLUSIVE/clean.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr comparison-form extraction (BSCNullCheckInfo)
- `if(!p)return;*p` (UO_LNot via init invert) — CLEAN ✓; `if(p==0)return;*p` (0 null-const) — CLEAN ✓; `if(p!=q){*p}` (two nullable ptrs) — **correctly REJECTED** (p NOT narrowed; XNOR guard at :183 prevents p!=q from registering p) — no FN ✓; `if(nullptr==p)return;*p` reversed operands — CLEAN ✓. `/tmp/x1..x4.cbs`, -nullability-check=all.
- Conclusion: extraction (getTrackablePtr/extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr :100-195) is SOUND. BSCNullCheckInfo fully characterized this session: extraction sound, simple-negation sound (single-expr invert), short-circuit &&/|| sound (CFG-split), subscript intentionally not narrowed; the ONLY defect is F70 (compound-negation `!(A&&B)` via invert()'s blind set-swap). INCONCLUSIVE/clean — no new bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — IsSafeBuiltinTypeConversion matrix sign-flip cell
- `_Safe ... long long x = (unsigned long)u;` — **CONFIRMED-F71** (MEDIUM; filed). Matrix EnableToConvert[LongLong][ULong]=Y (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:116) allows 64-bit unsigned→signed sign flip; siblings ULong→Long and ULongLong→LongLong correctly rejected. Runtime: u=2^64-1 → ll=-1 (silent corruption). Distinct function from F51 (IsSafeConversion enum block); F51 writeup wrongly claimed this matrix handles same-width sign flips. repro/F71. Converse FP noted: UInt→Long ([10][4]=N) over-strict vs UInt→LongLong (Y) — not filed (rejects-valid, lower priority).

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — IsSafeBuiltinTypeConversion matrix full audit (F71 blast radius)
- Float-boundary cells: matrix [Float][Int]=Y and [Double][Long]=Y, BUT `int→float` and `long→double` implicit are REJECTED at runtime-of-check (`/tmp/f1,f2.cbs`) — a SEPARATE int↔float narrowing check overrides the matrix for float conversions (docs "int to float needs explicit" honored). double→float rejected ([12][13]=N), float→int rejected (even explicit). So the matrix's float-row Y cells are DEAD (overridden); no FN there.
- Integer↔integer same-width sign-flip scan: ONLY [LongLong=11][ULong=5]=Y is unsound (=F71). All other same-width unsigned↔signed cells ([Int][UInt], [Long][ULong], [LongLong][ULongLong], [UChar][SChar], etc.) = N (correct). So **F71 is the SOLE soundness FN** in IsSafeBuiltinTypeConversion. Converse over-strict FP: [Long=10][UInt=4]=N (UInt→Long IS value-preserving) vs [LongLong][UInt]=Y — rejects-valid, not filed (lower priority, F71's writeup mentions it). Matrix audit COMPLETE.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — IsSafeConstantValueConversion boundary audit
- Constant fit-check (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:152-226) — **PROBED-SOUND**. 8 boundaries exact: signed char 127 accept / 128 reject / -128 accept / -129 reject; unsigned char 255 accept / 256 reject; int→float 2^24 accept (exact) / 2^24+1 reject (not exactly representable). Bit-width+sign handling and exact float round-trip all correct. No off-by-one. INCONCLUSIVE/clean. Contrast: the safe-zone conversion BUGS (F51 enum block, F71 builtin matrix) are in the NON-constant paths; the constant-exemption path is sound.

But in this RELEASE build (asserts off): en1 64-vs-32 rejected cleanly, en3 dest-contains-(-1)-at-64bit allowed, en5 dest-lacks-(-1) rejected, en2 (-1→0xFFFFFFFF enum) allowed but CORRECT (bit-reinterpret = valid dest enumerator, no UB). - `&_Mut *X` (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:767) accesses Sources[0] without the empty-check that the const sibling VisitUnaryAddrConstDeref (:748) has — **SHAPE-REJECTED (unreachable, not filed)**. ## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — mixed-mode (_Safe + _Unsafe overload) × _Owned (PROGRESS.md row 139)
- Mixed-mode `_Unsafe void consume(int*p); _Safe void consume(int*_Owned p);` with owned tracking — **PROBED-CLEAN**. (A) double-consume in _Safe via owned overload → correctly "use of moved value: p"; (B) single consume → clean (no false leak); (C) un-consumed → correctly "memory leak of value: p"; runtime (safe+unsafe contexts both calling) → valgrind 2 allocs/2 frees, 0 errors. The owned overload is selected in safe context and the move is tracked; unsafe-context call also frees correctly. No FN/FP/double-free/leak. `/tmp/mm.cbs, mm2..mm4.cbs`. Mixed-mode + _Owned is sound.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast nested-fnptr safe variance — CORRECTION: SHAPE-REJECTED (NOT filed)
- Re-verified the 2026-05-21 "CONFIRMED-NEW" finding (1582-1584). **It was WRONG / premature.** `outer_takes_unsafe_t fp = safe_outer` (nested cb safety mismatch): inside a `_Safe` function → hard ERROR ("incompatible function pointer types", `/tmp/sctx.cbs`); in non-safe context → `-Wincompatible-function-pointer-types` WARNING (`/tmp/exploit.cbs`), NOT silent. Runtime SIGSEGV (unsafe `evil` run via `safe_outer`) only when non-safe code ignores the warning. Safety contract enforced (error in safe, warning outside) → NOT a soundness FN. Distinct from F41 (owned), which compiled SILENTLY clean in non-safe code (owned stripped → canonically identical → no warning) = real FN; the `_Safe` sugar difference here DOES trigger the C incompatibility diagnostic. **Do not file.** Important: prior "CONFIRMED-NEW" notes must be re-verified for safe-context behavior + warnings before filing.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — partial-move / drop order for plain owned-field structs (PROGRESS row 148)
- `struct S { int *_Owned p, *_Owned q; }`, `extract(struct S s) { return s.p; }` — analyzer correctly flags `s.q is leak` (no implicit owned-field destructor for plain structs; must explicitly dispose). Explicit `consume(s.q); return s.p;` → clean, valgrind 2 allocs/2 frees 0 errors. `consume(s.p); return s.p;` → correctly "use of moved value: s.p". `/tmp/drop,drop2,drop3.cbs`.
- Conclusion: for in-scope plain owned-field structs, disposal is explicit and fully move-tracked; NO implicit drops → no drop-order surface to mis-order. The "return-value vs argument drop order" concern (PROGRESS 148) only applies to `_Owned struct` destructors (OOS). PROBED-CLEAN, no bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — union with _Owned member (re-confirm)
- `union U { int *_Owned p; int x; }` — re-confirmed **SHAPE-REJECTED** at decl ("type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Owned'"), matching _probed 249/809/971/1913. Union+owned unreachable; F42 is the union-BORROW-aliasing path (plain int fields). No new surface.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — __attribute__((cleanup)) × _Owned
- `int *_Owned p __attribute__((cleanup(cleaner))) = mk(); consume(p);` — **CONFIRMED-F72** (MEDIUM; filed). Compile-clean, valgrind Invalid free 1/2 (consume + synthesized cleanup both free). Sole-cleanup variant (no consume) → FP "memory leak of value: p". Control double-consume (no cleanup) → correctly "use of moved value". Analyzer blind to cleanup-attr disposal. Novel (no test/bug_log). cleanup NOT in OUT_OF_SCOPE. repro/F72.

Clang CFG branch order consistent; narrowing applied to right branches across if/for/while/do-while. Duplicated FieldPath at :756/:761 is benign redundancy. `/tmp/w1,w2,w3.cbs`. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — _Owned × noreturn control flow
- `_Owned p; if(c){ panic(); } consume(p);` where panic is `_Noreturn` / `__attribute__((noreturn))` — **PROBED-CLEAN**. (A,B) noreturn branch with p unconsumed → NO false leak (dead path recognized via clang CFG, noreturn call has no successor); (C) ordinary-fn control clean; (D) noreturn branch + NO consume on fall-through → correctly "memory leak of value: p". `/tmp/nr1-4.cbs`. The ownership analyzer respects noreturn (CFG-level) — contrast with F72 where cleanup-attr disposal is NOT modeled. No bug.

The analyzer walks subscript-index sub-expressions for moves. Corroborates _probed:245 (`arr[sink(p)]` post-index use-after-move diagnosed). No bug — subscript index is a normal visited sub-expression position (unlike the control-flow-condition positions F02/F22).

- `struct S{int *_Owned _Nullable a;}`, a owned on one branch / null on other, merged, then `if(a!=null)free` — **PROBED-CLEAN**. Compile clean; valgrind f(0)+f(1) = 1 alloc/1 free, 0 errors. Owned|null discrimination preserved across the CFG-join merge. `/tmp/mg.cbs`.
- The line 261-262 map-vs-set emptiness smell (checks `statsA.SOwnedOwnedFields.empty()` whole-map, not `[VD]` set; else-branch :271-276 drops owned/null discrimination into SAllOwnedFields) is DOCUMENTED in BSCOwnership.md but NOT triggered — realistic partial-init-merge works; precise triggering needs statsA's owned/null maps globally empty while merging a field-bearing VD (contrived; likely F44/F45-adjacent if reachable). ## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — OwnershipImpl::merge line-261 smell trigger attempt
- owned field moved on c-branch / owned on !c-branch (only struct tracked, to empty the owned/null maps on the moved path) → `if(c){consume(s.a);}` then scope exit — analyzer **correctly flags** "field memory leak of value: s, s.a is leak" (the !c-path owned state survived the merge). `/tmp/m261.cbs`. The line-261 map-vs-set-emptiness smell does NOT produce an observable FN/FP here. Real code smell, not reachable into a defect in constructible scenarios. Not filed.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — GCC nullability attrs × BSC nullability checker
- `__attribute__((returns_nonnull)) int *_Nullable get(); *get();` under -nullability-check=all → **REJECTED** "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" (BSC goes by its own `_Nullable` qualifier; does NOT trust the GCC attr to narrow → no FN). `/tmp/rn1.cbs`.
- `__attribute__((nonnull(1))) void use(int *p); use(_Nullable q);` → **ACCEPTED** (BSC does not model the GCC `nonnull` PARAM attr; param is plain `int *` not `_Nonnull`). `/tmp/rn3.cbs`. By-design: BSC uses `_Nonnull`, not GCC attrs.
- **PROBED-CLEAN / no bug.** BSC nullability driven by its own qualifiers; ignores GCC nullability attrs. Soundness-critical direction safe (returns_nonnull doesn't override _Nullable). Contrast with F72: cleanup-attr is unmodeled AND a bug because it has a runtime EFFECT (synthesized free); nullability attrs are pure hints (no synthesized code) so ignoring them is benign. Distinguishes "unmodeled attribute with runtime effect" (fileable) from "unmodeled pure-annotation attribute" (benign).

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — CheckOwnedQualTypeCStyleCast (owned explicit-cast check)
- `(float *_Owned)int_owned` — REJECTED "incompatible _Owned types" (recursion :318 rejects mismatched owned pointees); `(void *_Owned)int_owned` — cast ALLOWED (void escape :316), subsequent "memory leak of value: q" correctly flagged (q now owns); owned↔borrow — REJECTED. `/tmp/oc1-3.cbs`. **PROBED-SOUND.** The owned C-style-cast check recurses into pointees properly (unlike F41's only-outer-level fnptr check); void escape + owned/borrow/raw rejection all correct. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — loop back-edge ownership dataflow
- owned overwritten in loop body (`for{ p = mk(); }`) — correctly "assign to _Owned value: p" (back-edge owned state modeled, overwrite flagged); owned consumed-then-reconsumed across iters (`for{ consume(p); }`) — correctly "use of moved value: p" + leak; loop-local alloc+consume — clean. `/tmp/be1-3.cbs`. **PROBED-CLEAN.** The ownership dataflow fixpoint propagates owned/moved state across loop back-edges soundly (catches overwrite-leak + use-after-move). Distinct from F35 (forward goto-skip merge); back-edge handling is sound. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — p++/++p nullability STATE path (re-confirm vs F48)
- `p++; *p;` and `++p; *p;` (p `_Nullable`, -nullability-check=all) — correctly REJECTED. Re-confirms _probed 250/1324/1858: VisitUnaryOperator (BSCNullabilityCheck) doesn't update CurrStatusVD for UO_Pre/PostInc/Dec → p stays Nullable → deref rejected (no state laundering, unlike F48's `p += 1` compound-assign state-update at :619 which IS the F48 FN, baseline D still reproduces). The `*(p++)` EXPRESSION-VALUE variant folds into F18 (getExprPathNullability UO switch hole). `/tmp/pp1-4.cbs`. No new bug — unary inc/dec STATE path sound; laundering fully mapped: F18 (arith/postinc expr-value), F48 (compound-assign state), F50 (compound-assign cond).

Not a new filing; strengthens F72 (cleanup-blindness double-frees via both consume+cleanup AND move+cleanup). `/tmp/clmv.cbs`.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — narrowing-extraction helpers getVarDeclFromExpr/getMemberExprFromExpr
- BSCNullabilityCheck SetCFGBlocksByExpr extraction chain — **PROBED-SOUND**. `if ((p = get()) != nullptr) *p;` assignment-condition narrows p (BO→LHS path, clean); `if ((g(), p) != nullptr) *p;` comma narrows RHS=p (handled upstream in extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr getTrackablePtr :104 comma→RHS, so getVarDeclFromExpr's unconditional BO→LHS never sees a raw comma); control `*p` rejected. getVarDeclFromExpr unwraps ParenExpr/ImplicitCast/BO→LHS; getMemberExprFromExpr unwraps Paren/ICE; SafeExpr handled upstream (F65). No wrong-var/missed-narrowing. `/tmp/gv1-3.cbs`. No bug.

- `void initit(int **__attribute__((ensure_init)) pp); int *q; initit(&q); return *q;` — **PROBED-CLEAN**: callee inits *pp (=q), analyzer marks q init, use clean (A). Bounds F73: ensure_init sound at redecl + direct-call + multi-level-pointer; ONLY nested-fnptr level (F73) slips. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — borrow-freeze × unevaluated (sizeof) context

Corroborates _probed 611/658 (sizeof+borrow clean). Borrow-checker read-detection respects unevaluated contexts. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — narrowing propagation into _Nonnull return
- `int *_Nonnull f(int *_Nullable p){ if(p==nullptr) return other(); return p; }` — narrowed p satisfies _Nonnull return (CLEAN, A); `if(p!=nullptr) return p;` also clean (C); un-narrowed `return p` correctly REJECTED "cannot return nullable pointer type" (B). `/tmp/nr1-3.cbs`. Narrowing propagates into the return-value nullability check (VisitReturnStmt consults flow-narrowed CurrStatusVD). Distinct from F12 (ternary-arm narrowing). No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — const × _Owned move/leak tracking
- `const int *_Owned p`: double-consume → "use of moved value: p" (co1); un-consumed → "memory leak of value: p" (co2). const pointee qualifier does NOT interfere with ownership move/leak tracking. `safe_malloc<const int>` separately errors "read-only variable is not assignable" (expected const-correctness, not ownership). `/tmp/co0-2.cbs`. const×owned ownership tracking sound (distinct from F27 const×borrow cast). No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — nullability narrowing invalidation on reassignment
- `if (p != nullptr) { p = get_nullable(); return *p; }` — reassign-to-nullable correctly INVALIDATES the narrowing → `*p` re-flagged "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" (A, no FN); reassign-to-nonnull `p = getnn()` keeps valid (C clean); no-reassign keeps narrowing (B clean). `/tmp/iv1-3.cbs`. Simple-assignment state-update (CurrStatusVD[p]=RHS nullability) correctly updates narrowed state. Counterpart to F48 (compound-assign laundering bug); simple-assign sound. No stale-narrowing FN. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — snapshot suite maintenance
- Ran check_probes.sh --repros-only: 61/61 prior repros matched (0 DIFFERS, 0 compile-fail) — no regressions, no upstream fixes since last run. The 6 new repros F68-F73 lacked baselines; created via --snapshot-only. Re-run: 67/67 matched, 0 no-snapshot. All filed-bug repros (F09-F73) now regression-tracked.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — _Borrow _ArrayElem iteration bounds
- `int *_Borrow _ArrayElem b = &_Mut single_var;` — **type-REJECTED** ("incompatible _Borrow types, cannot cast int *_Borrow to int *_Borrow _ArrayElem"). Can't synthesize _ArrayElem from a single-var borrow; it only arises from genuine array contexts (tight type control). `b++` past-end OOB would not be bound-checked (borrow checker does aliasing/lifetime, not bounds) — but that is BY DESIGN: _Borrow _ArrayElem is the trust-the-user iterator primitive (Slice<T> is the bounds-checked alternative); _probed 2142 confirmed iteration correct. Not a bug surface (cf. F68 which was RAW-pointer subscript bypassing the deref guard — a different, real hole). SHAPE-REJECTED/by-design.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — scope boundary: forget/safe_swap are generic (OOS) + nullptr→_Nonnull sanity
- `forget<T>(T t)` and `safe_swap<T>(T*_Borrow,T*_Borrow)` (bishengc_safety.hbs:29,85) are GENERIC → out of scope (generics). The libcbs ownership intrinsics (forget/safe_swap/Vec/etc.) are all generic; their ownership-tracking interactions can't be probed without generics. Future sessions: don't re-attempt forget/safe_swap as in-scope ownership probes.
- Sanity: `int *_Nonnull p = nullptr;` and `p = nullptr;` — both correctly REJECTED. nullptr→_Nonnull sound.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — _Safe(mk()) discarded temp leak — re-confirm FOLDED-F62
- `_Safe(mk());` / `_Unsafe(mk());` as discarded expr-statement (mk returns _Owned) — only "-Wunused-value" warning, NO leak error → silent leak. Control `mk();` correctly "memory leak because temporary variable 'mk()' is _Owned". **FOLDED-F62** (already documented _probed:1588-1592): CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:534) IgnoreParenCasts() doesn't peel SafeExpr — same cross-cutting IgnoreExpr.h gap as F62's CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak; one fix (add SafeExpr to IgnoreExpr.h) closes both predicates. Re-confirmed, not re-filed. `/tmp/se1-3.cbs`.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — SafeExpr bypass bounded: init analyzer is sound
- `int y = _Safe(x);` / `_Unsafe(x)` reading uninit x — **correctly FLAGGED** "use of uninitialized value: x" (A,C); control clean-flagged (B). The init analyzer does NOT get bypassed by SafeExpr. `/tmp/iu1-3.cbs`.
- **Bounds the SafeExpr-wrapper-bypass family**: it affects SEMA-AST-level IgnoreParenCasts-based checks (F62 ownership leak/move via IgnoreExpr.h; F65 nullability narrowing) but NOT the BSCIR-lowered analyses (init analysis, ownership DATAFLOW) — BSCIR lowering unwraps SafeExpr to a Use, so those see through it. So the SafeExpr-bypass is a Sema-AST-level phenomenon only. No init bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — getExprPathNullability switch coverage (bounds F18)
- Full switch (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:316-411) handles: Paren, SafeExpr(F65), ImplicitCast, Call(+__move_to_raw etc), Conditional(F12 is the narrowing-flow not this value-path), CStyleCast, UnaryOp(AddrOf/Deref/AddrMutDeref), BinaryOp(**Comma+Assign recurse RHS**:374), InitList, DRE, ArraySubscript(static), MemberExpr. `break`→default(Unspecified) only for: BinaryOp arithmetic (BO_Add/Sub = **F18**) and UnaryOp inc/dec (FOLDED-F18).
- Probed: `*((0,p))` comma → correctly REJECTED (recurses RHS=p nullable, sound — NOT a laundering gap); `*(p+0)` arith → accepted (F18 reconfirmed). `/tmp/cm1-2.cbs`. So F18 is the PRECISE gap (arithmetic only); comma/assign/conditional/initlist all sound. No new bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — borrow-through-nullable-deref nullability propagation
- `const int *_Borrow b = &_Const *p;` (p _Nullable, non-safe, -nullability-check=all) — REJECTED "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable pointer": the fused UO_AddrConstDeref propagates p's nullability (getExprPathNullability:367 → subexpr p), making the borrow nullable, caught at assignment to a nonnull-default borrow. Control `*p` flagged (B); narrowed `if(p!=nullptr) &_Const *p` clean (C). `/tmp/bn1-3.cbs`. (Note: &*p = address-of-deref = p, a reborrow — no actual null-deref crash; nullability tracked correctly.) Sound, no FN. In _Safe zone, raw `*p` is separately forbidden (F68 guard). No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — array-of-_Borrow allowed + element lifetime sound
- `int *_Borrow arr[2] = { &_Mut x, &_Mut y };` — ALLOWED (array-of-_Borrow is legal, unlike array-of-_Owned which is shape-rejected).
- Array-element borrow dangling: `arr[0] = &_Mut local;` (local in inner block) then `*arr[0]` after — correctly FLAGGED "`local` does not live long enough / dropped here while still borrowed" (matches scalar control). `/tmp/ad1,ad2.cbs`. So the LOAN LIFETIME of an array-element borrow IS tracked (use-after-free caught), even though arr[i]→*arr collapses for CONFLICT detection (_probed:50, over-conservative FP). No use-after-free FN. Array-of-_Borrow sound for lifetime. No bug.

- `__auto_type p = mk();` (mk returns _Owned): un-consumed → "memory leak of value: p" (A); double-consume → "use of moved value: p" (B). `/tmp/at1,at2.cbs`. **PROBED-CLEAN**: __auto_type deduction PRESERVES the _Owned qualifier — leak/move tracking apply normally, no qualifier-drop FN. (typeof was FOLDED-F37 for the unevaluated-deref case, _probed:633; that's a different concern.) __auto_type is in scope (not _Generic/StmtExpr-style OOS). No bug.

- **ensure_init fully characterized**: redecl mismatch (caught), direct-call (sound), multi-level pointer (sound), struct-pointee partial-field (caught), transitive (sound) — ALL SOUND. The ONLY ensure_init gap is F73 (nested-fnptr variance, silent FN). No new bug.
