## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — borrow-checker deep dump-driven audit (-dump-borrow-check)
Read the full RegionCheck core: CheckAction/CheckBorrows/CheckMove/CheckStorageDead,
FindLoansThatFreeze/Intersect, FrozenByBorrowOf, LoansInScope::{Compute,SimulateBlock,Walk},
InferenceContext::{Solve,AddLivePoint}, DFS::Copy, PopulateInference, PreprocessForParamAndReturn,
EnsureBorrowSource/RelateRegions, VisitReturnStmt, VisitIncrementDecrementOp. Used the new
`-Xclang -dump-borrow-check` (regions.points = NLL liveness extents, loans, actions) as the
soundness observable. ALL of the following PROBED-SOUND (no bug):
- mut/mut same var conflict; loan region ends at last use (NLL, not lexical). `/tmp/cal_borrow.cbs`
- return &_Mut of a no-param fn → Sema gate "no _Borrow param" (not borrow-ck). `/tmp/lt_ret.cbs`
- return &_Mut of LOCAL w/ a borrow param present → ForStorageDead, loan region forced to End('region_r). `/tmp/lt_local2.cbs`
- return struct-by-value whose _Borrow field points at a LOCAL → "x does not live long enough" (region flows thru h.f→h→__ret). `/tmp/lt_structret.cbs`
- loop-carried mut/mut across the back-edge → caught (loan region wraps BB5→BB4→BB3). `/tmp/lt_loop.cbs`
- array-elem var-index `&_Mut a[i]`/`&_Mut a[j]` → both collapse to path `*a` → conflict flagged (conservative-sound). `/tmp/lt_arr.cbs`
- INTERPROC: borrow-returning call result aliases ALL borrow args (Prologue emits `result = each_arg` at the call point) → using result after an arg's source dies → "y does not live long enough". `/tmp/ip_call.cbs`
- INTERPROC struct arg: result aliases a by-value struct arg's _Borrow field (result region flows h.f→h→result→r). `/tmp/ip_struct.cbs`
- NLL precision FPs: read-after-last-use-of-mutborrow, sequential two mut borrows, two shared borrows — all CLEAN (errors=0). The one flagged case had `(void)p` AFTER the read (legitimately extends p's life; `(void)p` IS a use, per CLAUDE.md pitfalls). `/tmp/np.cbs`
- pointer-arith × borrow: `&_Mut a[0]` then `a+=1`/`a++`/`*a=7` → "cannot use `a` because it was mutably borrowed" (element borrow of `*a` borrows the pointer `a` via supportingPrefixes; mutating a while borrowed forbidden — matches Rust). `/tmp/pa.cbs`
- type-level escape blocks: `_Borrow`-to-`_Borrow` forbidden; struct-with-_Borrow-field cannot be `_Borrow`-qualified even indirectly. So out-param borrow-of-local escapes are unreachable. `/tmp/lt_outparam.cbs,lt_field.cbs`

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — VisitUnaryAddrMutDeref empty-Sources (C1) — SHAPE-REJECTED
- BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:774 `VisitUnaryAddrMutDeref` indexes `Sources[0]` with NO empty-guard, while its const sibling :748 `VisitUnaryAddrConstDeref` guards `if (Sources.empty()){Kind=Init;return;}`. Confirmed asymmetry in code. Empty Sources arises from VisitCStyleCastExpr early-return (:482-486, subexpr is CK_NullToPointer CStyleCast) or a bare nullptr literal subexpr.
- **SHAPE-REJECTED**: cannot reach line 774 with empty Sources from valid _Safe BSC. 4 variants of `&_Mut *<null-ish borrow>` all compile clean (no ICE): `&_Mut *((int*_Borrow _Nullable)nullptr)`, double-cast, from-param, etc. Two blockers: (1) `_Borrow` defaults `_Nonnull` → null-cast hits nullability Sema gate; (2) `_Nullable` form compiles but Prologue HOISTS the `&_Mut *` operand into a temp DRE (`_borrowck_tmp_1 = *_borrowck_tmp_0; &mut *_borrowck_tmp_1`), so Visit(operand) always pushes a Source → never empty. Same load-bearing Prologue hoisting that shape-rejected the subscript-index candidate. `/tmp/crash_*.cbs,cz.cbs`. The const-sibling guard is defensive (or guards a historically-reachable shape). Not filable.
- **Borrow-checker core verdict: SATURATED-SOUND.** Regions/loans/liveness/conflict/interproc-lifetime/NLL-precision/pointer-arith all confirmed correct via dump. No upstream commit in 984b1f6..a9deb1b changed BSCBorrowChecker.cpp (the Chain-C reopen on this hop is driven by our own dump commit, not a behavior change).

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — borrow-checker CFG/owned-move/recursive-type sweep (all sound)
- owned-move-while-pointee-borrowed: `b=&_Mut *p; consume(p)` → "cannot move out of `p` because it is borrowed" (mut AND const borrow). NLL: move after borrow's last use → CLEAN. `/tmp/om.cbs`.
- interproc call-result-aliases-arg in ALL positions: `return id(&_Mut local)`, nested `id(id(&local))`, cross-scope `r=id(&local)` then use — all → "x does not live long enough"; `return id(param)` CLEAN. Prologue's `result=each_borrow_arg` emission is robust. `/tmp/cr.cbs`.
- recursive/self-referential types w/ _Borrow field: ALL type-rejected ("struct S cannot be qualified by _Borrow even indirectly", "&_Mut on _Borrow type not allowed"). The c6f845f/80c0359 coredump fixes were for OOS _Owned-struct/raw recursion; the _Safe-reachable shapes never reach RecursiveForFields. `/tmp/rc.cbs`.
- unusual CFG: **backward goto is FORBIDDEN in _Safe** ("jumping back to previous labels is not allowed") → borrow checker only ever sees REDUCIBLE CFGs. Forward-goto + switch borrow conflicts flagged correctly. `/tmp/cfg.cbs`.
- conditional-merge NLL precision: p dead-at-merge → q borrow CLEAN (no FP); p live-after-merge → flagged; p used in both branches but dead at merge → CLEAN. `/tmp/mg.cbs`.
- **FINAL VERDICT — borrow checker SATURATED-SOUND** (~29 probes, dump-driven). Reachable input space is tightly constrained by (1) aggressive type pre-filtering, (2) Prologue normalization (hoist complex subexprs, ?:→if, result=arg aliasing), (3) no backward goto (reducible CFG only). The region/loan/liveness/conflict core handles that space correctly + NLL-precise. No bug; no further borrow-checker probing warranted until upstream changes the file (currently unchanged by the rebase).

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — Prologue/Epilogue (Chain B) dump+rewrite audit (sound)
Used `-dump-prologue-epilogue` (seen_ast_kinds per phase) + `-cc1 -rewrite-bsc` (F09 detector:
surviving `_borrowck_tmp` in restored AST). Read all Prologue Transform methods (SemaDeclBSC.cpp
:797-980) + the hoisting helper.
- **Round-trip CLEAN** for every common construct: side-effecting CallExpr nested in array-subscript index, CStyleCast subexpr, InitList element, compound-literal, member base, binop operands, `?:` arms — ALL rewrite back to original source with ZERO surviving temps. Epilogue restoration is robust (the SeenKinds=[] in the epilogue dump is an instrumentation quirk, not a missed restore — rewrite proves restoration happened). `/tmp/rwt.cbs,pe_cal.cbs`.
- `?:` (TransformConditionalOperator :859) splits into IfStmt with per-branch assigns → conditional eval PRESERVED (rewrite restores `c ? t() : fl()`; not the F40 eager-hoist). `/tmp/tern.cbs`.
- **Escaping in-scope kinds NOT a hole**: `_Atomic`/`__c11_atomic_*` IS allowed in _Safe with `&_Mut` args. AtomicExpr escapes Prologue hoisting (no TransformAtomicExpr override) BUT a borrow conflict (`rd(&_Mut x)`) and an owned-move (`rdo(p)`) buried inside an atomic operand are STILL caught — borrow checker's generic VisitStmt child-iteration traverses atomic operands. `/tmp/ae.cbs`. **Reason it's sound**: AtomicExpr/VAArgExpr/OpaqueValueExpr/PseudoObjectExpr evaluate ALL operands → child-iteration is correct. The F16 root cause (walk UNSELECTED arms / drop SELECTED arm) needs CONDITIONAL eval, which only the OOS kinds (_Generic/ChooseExpr/GNU `?:`) have. So Chain B's open in-scope hops are sound-by-construction; VAArgExpr also shape-rejected (variadic forbidden in _Safe).
- **CHAIN B VERDICT (in-scope): SATURATED-SOUND.** Filed issues (F09/F16/F38) live only at OOS conditional kinds; F40 is the &&/|| eager-hoist analysis-FP; switch-cond Epilogue-restore gap is benign (semantically-equivalent hoisted form, _probed earlier). No new in-scope bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — divergence detector built + run (wrapper-invariance metamorphic)
Built `scripts/divergence.sh`: marks one subexpr with `<{ EXPR }>`, substitutes meaning-preserving
wrappers (paren, double-paren, `_Safe(...)`), and asserts the analyzer VERDICT is invariant. Refined
to split STRUCTURAL (parse/type → wrapper inapplicable here, skip) from ANALYSIS errors (borrow/owned/
null/init → the verdict compared). Runs with `-nullability-check=all` so all 4 analyzers participate.
- Ran ~14 base programs across borrow (mut/mut, subscript-base, ret-local), ownership (member-move,
  member-assign, move-while-borrowed), init (uninit deref), nullability (_Borrow _Nullable deref,
  narrowing, nullable→nonnull assign, De-Morgan compound cond). **NO wrapper-invariance divergence.**
- Confirms F30/F46/F62/F65 fixes are robust: e.g. `null_narrowed` with the `_Safe(p)` wrapper stays
  CLEAN (0 errors) — that is exactly the F65 scenario, which pre-fix gave a spurious nullable-deref.
- One false alarm caught + fixed in the detector itself: `_Safe(a)[0]` on a `_Borrow _ArrayElem`
  doesn't parse/typecheck (structural errors) → correctly classified INAPPLICABLE, not a divergence.
- **Tooling note**: the epilogue prologue/epilogue dump was fixed (llvmdup f66ac866) to log restore
  events (kind/from_temp/loc) + total_replacements; `restored < total` = an F09-class survivor. The
  divergence detector currently compares DIAGNOSTICS (wrapping changes temp-count so dump structure
  differs legitimately); a future RENAME/reorder metamorphic relation would compare the normalized
  dump directly. No new bug from this pass; detector is a reusable regression+hunting asset.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — LANE 1: runtime/valgrind on checker-blessed programs (sound)
Built `scripts/vg_probe.sh` (leak-sensitive: surfaces LEAK SUMMARY, unlike repro.sh -valgrind which
greps only ERROR SUMMARY → was blind to leaks). Positive control (raw malloc, no free) correctly shows
"definitely lost: 8 bytes" — harness validated. The static dumps are STRUCTURALLY BLIND to this class
(analyzer-says-safe-but-codegen-drops-it: F09/F58/F60/F63), so valgrind is the only oracle.
- **F60/F63 runtime-confirmed FIXED** by a9deb1b: F60 `_Safe(aggregate)` compiles+runs (`sum=5`, was
  the codegen rejection); F63 `_Safe(_Complex)` runs clean. Moved to FIXED.md.
- ~15 probes, ALL sound (rejected-by-analysis OR accepted-and-leak-clean):
  - owned move through `_Safe(id(a))` / `_Unsafe` — clean (ownership transferred, 1 free).
  - aggregate (struct w/ owned field) moved through `_Safe(s1)` — clean (a9deb1b emitter correct).
  - owned struct returned by value, field freed — clean; struct w/ 2 owned fields — clean; partial
    move (`consume(s.a)` + free s.b) — clean.
  - leak-on-a-path: early-return / conditional-consume / reassign-without-move — ALL correctly
    REJECTED ("memory leak of value" / "assign to _Owned value"). loop alloc+free — clean.
  - conditional move `consume(c?p:q)` — conservatively REJECTED ("memory leak of `q`"); comma move
    `consume((x=1,p))` — accepted + clean.
  - `__move_to_raw` in _Safe — forbidden (needs _Unsafe), expected.
- **LANE 1 VERDICT**: ownership-analysis × codegen boundary is SOUND on the reachable surface; no
  runtime leak/double-free/UAF from a checker-accepted program. `/tmp/vg_*.cbs`.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — LANE 2: dump-as-oracle MONOTONICITY detector (sound)
Built `scripts/monotone.sh`: appends a use at a /*A*/ marker and checks two monotonic relations the
DIAGNOSTIC alone can't express — (1) base conflict-set ⊆ base+use conflict-set; (2) no loan region
SHRINKS (keyed by stable creation point; appending at the end keeps prefix CFG numbering stable).
A region that shrinks when a use is added = region-engine bug invisible to diagnostics.
- Caught + fixed a DETECTOR false alarm: path-keyed join cross-producted multiple same-var loans
  ("x: 9->3"); re-keyed by creation point → correct.
- Ran extend / keep / shared / branch-merge: ALL monotonic (conflicts ⊆ and no region shrank).
  e.g. `extend`: appending use_b(p) grows p's region across q's borrow → 0→1 conflict (added, sound).
- **LANE 2 VERDICT**: the borrow region engine satisfies monotonicity under appended uses. No bug;
  reusable dump-based detector for future region-engine changes. `/tmp/mb_*.cbs`.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — LANE 3: cross-analyzer composition (sound for memory safety)
Probed the handoffs between ownership / borrow / init / nullability (run as separate passes;
-nullability-check=all so all participate). Hunt: an unsafe program where one analyzer's
state-tracking masks another's check (FN).
- use-after-move (deref OR copy) caught even when guarded by null-narrowing: `consume(p); if(p!=nullptr){*p}`,
  `if(p==nullptr){}else{*p}`, plain `if(c){*p}` — ALL "use of moved value". Narrowing does NOT mask the move.
- partial struct move + init: `struct{a,b owned}; s.a=q; take(s)` → BOTH "use of partially moved value: s.b"
  AND "use of uninitialized value: s" fire. Composition sound.
- nullability narrowing correctly INVALIDATED by reassignment: `if(p==nullptr)return; p=getn(); *p` →
  "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" (stale narrowing not reused). Sound.
- uninit `_Owned _Nullable` deref → both "uninitialized" + "nullable cannot be dereferenced" fire.
- **LOW (not filed, memory-safe)**: `consume(p); if(p==nullptr){}` (empty body) is ACCEPTED — a null-check
  in an if-CONDITION reads the moved p but isn't flagged as an ownership use (whereas `int b=(p==nullptr)`
  and `q=p` ARE flagged "use of moved value"). Memory-safe: comparing a moved pointer's bits to null is
  defined (no deref), and any deref in either branch is still caught (above). Logical move-discipline
  gap only; per scope (HIGH/MED memory-safety) → not filed. `/tmp/cmp_*.cbs`.
- **LANE 3 VERDICT**: analyzers compose correctly for memory safety; no FN. All 3 lanes (runtime,
  monotonicity, composition) confirm soundness on the reachable in-scope surface.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — LANE 1 volume + Chain F re-walk (rebase-reopened)
- **fuzz_oracle.py 600-program batch (seed 42): 520 accepted, ALL valgrind-clean, 80 legitimately
  rejected.** Combined with 30+200 runs: ~720 generated, ~720 accepted-clean, 0 runtime FN. The
  owned×codegen boundary is sound at volume.
- **Chain F re-walk** (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp touched by eadb9c5 "IgnoreParen*Safe helpers"): eadb9c5
  added SafeExpr peeling to getVarDeclFromExpr (:173), getMemberExprFromExpr (:188), and
  getDerefPathVDFromExpr (via IgnoreParenImpCastsSafe :199) — these fixed F65 (scalar SafeExpr null-
  narrowing). **VisitMEForFieldPath (:149-163) is the ONE helper NOT updated** — it peels ParenExpr/
  ImplicitCastExpr but not SafeExpr (nor CStyleCastExpr). Real asymmetry vs its 3 siblings.
- **PROBED-SHAPE-REJECTED**: a SafeExpr can NEVER be a member-access base — `_Safe(x).f`, `_Safe(x)->f`,
  `_Unsafe(x)->f` ALL parse-fail ("expected ';'/')'"; the SafeExpr grammar forbids trailing `.`/`->`).
  And `_Safe(s.f)` (whole-field wrap) is peeled by VisitMEForFieldPath's callers (lines 415/643/761/833
  pass an already-peeled MemberExpr). So VisitMEForFieldPath never sees a SafeExpr in its base-walk;
  field narrowing through `_Safe(s.f)` works (narrow_wrap/deref_wrap/both_wrap all clean). The siblings
  need SafeExpr peeling because they receive whole-expr wrappers; VisitMEForFieldPath does not. Latent
  smell, not filable. `/tmp/np_*.cbs`. **Chain F SafeExpr-peeling is complete for the reachable surface.**

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — Chain A re-walk (rebase-reopened): F14 still-live + sibling-fix asymmetry
- `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:534) vs `CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak` (:547): eadb9c5
  ("IgnoreParen*Safe helpers") changed CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak to strip via `IgnoreParenCastsSafe()`
  (fixing F62), but left CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak as bare `dyn_cast<CallExpr>(E)` — strips NOTHING.
- **F14 STILL REPRODUCES post-rebase** (vg_probe: `(mk());`/`(void)mk();`/`(0,mk());` each leak 4 bytes,
  compile clean). FOLDED new surface form: `_Safe(mk())`/`_Unsafe(mk())` (SafeExpr wrap) also leaks —
  same root (no-strip). Recorded under F14, NOT a separate finding (discipline: same dyn_cast hole).
- Cleanest fix pointer for the maintainer: "apply to :534 the same strip eadb9c5 applied to :547." The
  two sibling temp/move leak checks should share the wrapper-strip; the rebase fixed one, not the other.
- Filing deferred per user ("fix the bug and file later"); F14 is HIGH and pending.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — pointer ++/-- rework (97cf8c4, fresh) — PROBED-SOUND
Read 97cf8c4 (moved ++/-- out of DiagnoseInvalidUnaryExprInSafeZone into DiagnoseRawPtrIncDec/
DiagnoseBSCPtrIncDec + CheckIncrementDecrementOperand dispatch). Allow-list = ONLY `_Borrow _ArrayElem`.
- typedef'd plain `_Borrow` ++ : the sugar-vs-canonical smell (step1 `isNonArrayElemBorrowType(Op->getType())`
  sugar vs step2 canonical) is NOT exploitable — `isBorrowQualified()` canonicalizes, typedef borrow rejected
  ("++ not supported on 'BP' (aka 'int *_Borrow')"). REFUTED.
- `_Owned`, `_Owned _ArrayElem` ++ : rejected (correct — can't advance an owned array ptr). `_Borrow _ArrayElem`
  ++/-- allowed (the one legal case). plain `_Borrow`/raw ++/-- rejected with new category messages.
- void-result: `b = a++` rejected ("conversion from void") — void-override + its null-guard work.
- Removed DiagnoseOwnedPointerUnaryOp is safe: the unary ops it rejected on owned ptrs (+,-,~,etc.) are
  C-rejected anyway (arithmetic-operand-required); ++/-- now handled by the new dispatch. No FN. No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — IsSafePointerConversion (UNPROBED per INDEX) — PROBED-SOUND
Read SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:801 IsSafePointerConversion (the pointer-conversion matrix). Conservative
(fallback = identical-only → errs toward FP, not FN). Soundness-critical trivial-data void-borrow rule
(:821 `T*_Borrow → void*_Borrow` iff isTrivialDataType) verified: struct-with-raw-ptr-field and
struct-with-_Owned-field → both correctly FORBIDDEN; int / plain-struct → allowed. isTrivialDataType
correctly excludes pointer/owned-field structs — no soundness hole. Borrow→borrow add-const allowed,
drop-const/add-arrayelem/void-narrowing forbidden (matches spec). Owned branch only allows T*_Owned→
void*_Owned + identical (owned add-const may be a minor FP, not pursued — FP direction). No bug.

## 2026-05-29 (cont.) — BSCIRBuilder lowerStmt + lowerExpr dispatch — PROBED-COMPLETE (in-scope)
- lowerStmt (:257): handles SafeStmt/Compound/If/While/For/Do/Switch/Break/Continue/Goto/Label/Return/
  DeclStmt/NullStmt/AttributedStmt(F52 fix) + catch-all Expr→Visit. Fall-through-to-Nop (:345) only for
  OOS/exotic stmt kinds (inline asm, indirect goto, C++). Complete for in-scope C statements.
- lowerExpr/Visit (28 overrides): ArraySubscript/Atomic/BinaryOperator/Call/Cast/CompoundAssign/
  CompoundLiteral/Conditional/DeclRef/ImplicitCast/ImplicitValueInit/InitList/Member/Paren/Predefined/
  Return/SafeExpr/UnaryExprOrTypeTrait/UnaryOperator/VAArg + literals. VisitStmt fallback (:1311) drops
  to Nop+void only OOS kinds (StmtExpr/_Generic/ChooseExpr/GNU ?: /vector/blocks/AddrLabel) or
  effect-free (offsetof). No IN-SCOPE expression kind dropped — no init-analysis FN from a lowering hole.
- **Session resume verdict**: all freshly-rebased code (eadb9c5 nullability helpers, 97cf8c4 ++/--,
  95c83b5/20a2855/a9deb1b) + reopened chains (A→F14-still-live-folds, C→dump-only, F→shape-rejected) +
  IsSafePointerConversion + IR dispatch = SOUND for in-scope BSC. Standout open HIGH: F14 (temp leak via
  wrappers; fix = apply eadb9c5's :547 strip to :534). Fuzz oracle clean at 720 programs.

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain D dispatch-gate (pointer-to-function-pointer)
Traced the Sema Check* fnptr family. Built the (dimension × dispatcher × nesting) table:
owned-outer-ptr=CheckOwnedQualTypeAssignment(recurses,sound), borrow-outer=sound,
nullability-outer=CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment(recurses isPointerType, stops at FnProto),
owned-in-fnptr=CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType(F41 only-outer), borrow-in-fnptr=BorrowParamTypesMatch(sound),
nullability-in-fnptr=NONE(F29), ensure_init-in-fnptr=CheckEnsureInitFunctionPointerType(F73 only-outer).
NEW cell found: the DISPATCH GATE itself.
- shape: `fn_owned ofp = freer; fn_raw *rpp = &ofp; (*rpp)(&local);` (pointer-TO-fnptr, owned-param
  vs raw-param, implicit, no cast) → CONFIRMED-new. Compiles clean; valgrind Invalid free().
  Root: SemaExpr.cpp:10329 gates CheckBSCFunctionPointerType on OrigLHSType->isFunctionPointerType()
  which is FALSE for PointerType(PointerType(Fn)); the fnptr check is NEVER REACHED. Distinct from F41
  (that function reached-but-only-outer; a DIRECT _Owned param like here is caught by it — baseline proves).
- shape: single-level `fn_raw r = freer;` → REJECTED ("cannot cast 'void (int *_Owned)' to 'fn_raw'").
  Baseline confirms the asymmetry: one pointer level deeper suppresses the diagnostic.
- shape: explicit-cast variant `(fn_owned *)&rfp` → compiles (casts are permissive; not the finding).
- blast radius (UNPROBED but same gate): ptr-to-fnptr with nullability-param variance (peer of F29) and
  borrow-param variance (BorrowParamTypesMatch would catch if reached) similarly skip the dispatch.

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain E re-walk (CFG element granularity vs AST nesting): borrow + ownership coalescing
Method: mapped both CFG configs (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:273 `setAllAlwaysAdd()` for Nullability+Ownership on
RAW AST; :1494-1504 restricted 10-class list for BorrowCheck on Prologue-transformed AST). Confirmed
Prologue `TransformBinaryOperator` (:844) hoists EVERY BinaryOperator (incl. compound-assign via
TransformCompoundAssignOperator→TransformBinaryOperator delegation, comma, BinAssign) into a temp
DeclStmt, recursively decomposing operands; `TransformCallExpr`/`TransformUnaryOperator`(prvalue+inc/dec)
/`TransformConditionalOperator`(→IfStmt)/`TransformCStyleCastExpr`/`TransformCompoundLiteralExpr` all
hoist too. Ownership's `setAllAlwaysAdd()` linearizes ALL sub-expression statements (incl. CallExpr)
into their own CFG elements → moves never hidden.
- shape: `int *_Owned p; int b=(p && c); safe_free(p)` — owned ptr tested in `&&` operand → BSCIR
  VisitBinaryOperator &&/|| branch (:786-825) lowers operand as Move (no force-Copy unlike comparison
  :838) → **BENIGN**: no use-of-moved FP (init), leak still flagged if unfreed (ownership AST-based).
  `/tmp/explorer_{probe,baseline}.*` → INCONCLUSIVE (source asymmetry real, observable behavior sound).
- shape: const-borrow `m` used only inside `&&` RHS, mutate borrowee → conflict CAUGHT (borrow creation
  freezes borrowee for whole region) → INCONCLUSIVE (sound).
- shape: borrow after-use hidden in for-CONDITION (Prologue wraps cond in StmtExpr), mutate before →
  conflict CAUGHT → SHAPE-REJECTED (Prologue StmtExpr-wrap + VisitStmt child-iter sees the use).
- shape: borrow after-use in do-while COND / for-INCREMENT comma → conflict CAUGHT (Prologue recursively
  hoists comma operands into separate BinAssign DeclStmts; F11 survives only for comma VALUE-flow) →
  SHAPE-REJECTED.
- shape: pass `int *_Owned p` to `_Bool` param (ownership VisitCallExpr :2223 `isBooleanType()?GetAddr:Move`)
  → SHAPE-REJECTED (Sema forbids ptr→_Bool conversion of _Owned in safe zone before analyzer).
- shape: owned move hidden in array-subscript INDEX `arr[consume(p)]` then free (VisitArraySubscriptExpr
  :2044 returns early after base DRE, skips index) → move CAUGHT → SHAPE-REJECTED (all-add CFG linearizes
  the index CallExpr into its own element, so early-return doesn't lose it). `/tmp/explorer_*.3QiYzh,TBCBRm`.
- shape: owned move in `?:` true-arm only, then unconditional free (ownership VisitAbstractConditionalOperator
  :2187 merge) → maybe-moved CAUGHT (matches if-branch baseline) → SHAPE-REJECTED (merge is conservative).
- **VERDICT: Chain E borrow+ownership coalescing surface is SOUND for in-scope C.** Ownership's
  setAllAlwaysAdd + borrow's Prologue-recursive-hoist defend every reachable side-effect position. The
  only escapees remain comma value-flow (F11, filed) and conditional eval (F16 borrow / F25 Sema-temp,
  filed). No NEW root cause. The BSCIR &&/|| Move-vs-Copy asymmetry (:786 vs :838) is a real source
  smell but observably benign (init-only, no FP/FN) — logged in BSCIRBuilder.md for a future maintainer.

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — C5 ownership merge: field-set UNION is a double-free FN (NEW)
Chain: ownership CFG-join merge (OwnershipImpl::merge, BSCOwnership.cpp:254-276) for plain-struct
`_Owned` fields. The SOwnedOwnedFields set is UNION'd across predecessors (lines 262-265), not met.
Conservative for LEAK (union keeps a field owned if owned on any path → flagged) but UNSOUND for
MOVE/double-free: a field moved on one branch but owned on the other is RESTORED to owned by the union,
so a post-join re-consume is accepted → double-free on the moved path. The lattice has no "maybe-moved"
element; leak and double-free directions need different meets.
- shape: `struct S{int*_Owned p}; if(c){consume(s.p);} consume(s.p);` (move on ONE branch, then
  unconditional consume after join) → **CONFIRMED-new** (pending file). ACCEPTED; valgrind 1 alloc/2
  frees/Invalid free. `/tmp/explorer_probe.AR4NLu.cbs`.
- shape: `consume(s.p); consume(s.p);` straight-line double consume (BASELINE) → REJECTED "use of moved
  value: s.p". Asymmetry: the move IS tracked without a merge; the bug needs the merge. `/tmp/explorer_baseline.9k7D50.cbs`.
- shape: `if(c){consume(s.p);}else{consume(s.p);} consume(s.p);` move on BOTH branches + post-consume (B2)
  → REJECTED "use of moved value: s.p". Proves the union (one-path-owned) is the culprit. `/tmp/explorer_b2.D2dKNA.cbs`.
- shape: `if(c){consume(s.p);}else{consume(s.p);}` balanced 1-consume/path (B3) → CLEAN 1 alloc/1 free.
  Setup is legal. `/tmp/explorer_b3.SatfHq.cbs`.
- shape: `if(c){consume(s.p);} return;` asymmetric move + scope exit, NO post-consume (B4, == _probed:2307
  shape) → REJECTED "field memory leak ... s.p is leak". Confirms merge keeps field owned post-join; the
  ONLY delta from PROBE is the added post-join consume. `/tmp/explorer_b4.HXF91k.cbs`.
- DISTINCT from F67 (deref-site missing field-move check; straight-line, no merge), F45 (stale SNull via
  &_Mut), F61 (struct-copy null-field FP leak — opposite direction), F44 (Null→Owned collapse on &_Mut),
  and from _probed.md:2307 (which only checked the leak direction of the same union and stopped).
- root cause: OwnershipImpl::merge BSCOwnership.cpp:262-265 (SOwnedOwnedFields union, no meet). Defect class C5.

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Nullability MERGE chain (mergeVD/mergeFP/mergeDPVD + condition-status injection) deep audit
Chain Mode-2: traced who feeds the merges (runNullabilityCheck worklist :1193-1251 injects
BlocksConditionStatus{VD,FP,DPVD}[block][pred] into a COPY of each pred's end-status BEFORE
merge) and who consumes (runOnBlock→TF.Visit; deref-diag via getExprPathNullability DeclRef/Member/
UO_Deref :360-417). Goal: a DISTINCT-from-F26 join where narrowing leaks (FN) or is wrongly preserved.
KEY STRUCTURAL FINDINGS (re-confirm F26's distinction reasoning on current compiler 28656aa9):
 - mergeVD/mergeFP (:844-873) have the SAME absence-asymmetry CODE as mergeDPVD (:876-892) BUT are
   SOUND because initStatus (:813-842) pre-populates BlocksEndStatusVD/FP[entry] with Nullable for
   every Nullable VD/FieldDecl that appears as a top-level DeclRefExpr/MemberExpr (setAllAlwaysAdd
   linearizes even `*p`'s inner DeclRef into its own CFG elem → always pre-populated). CurrStatusVD/FP
   entries are NEVER erased (only InvalidateDeref* touches DPVD), so the key is present on ALL preds →
   absence-case never fires for VD/FP. CONFIRMS F26's claim; not a new bug.
 - meet(NonNull, Unspecified)=NonNull in merge* (:851/867/885 keep statusA when NK!=Nullable) is a real
   lattice weakness BUT UNREACHABLE for a pointer VD without first hitting F18/F48 (getDefNullability
   never returns Unspecified for a pointer; only the unhandled-switch default does → folds into F18/ChainF).
PROBES (8, hard budget):
- shape: F26 repro re-run on 28656aa9 → still FN (exit 0). Mechanism live.
- shape: bare `*p` param deref (no join) → DIAG (exit 1). p IS pre-populated. baseline SOUND.
- shape: meet(NonNull,Unspecified) join `if(c){if(p==null)ret;}else{p=q+0;} *p` → exit 0 BUT baseline
  (c-false alone `p=q+0;*p`) ALSO exit 0 → contaminated by F18 pointer-arith hole → FOLDED-F18 (not a
  clean merge asymmetry; the Unspecified comes from F18). /tmp/explorer_meet.JWePdX, explorer_meet_base.CIqsJJ
- shape: FP-merge analog of F26 (nullable FIELD `s.f` narrowed on c-true sibling, deref on join) → DIAG
  (exit 1). FP pre-populated → SOUND. /tmp/explorer_fp.hg0GQ9
- shape: nested FP `s.inner.f` merge analog → DIAG (exit 1). Deeper field path also pre-populated → SOUND.
  /tmp/explorer_fpnest.iF54up
- shape: DPVD loop back-edge `while(i<n){if(*p)**p;}` (no post-loop deref) → exit 0 (expected, no check site).
- shape: DPVD depth/assignment merge `if(c)*p=null; else if(*p)**p; return **p;` (both preds HAVE *p key) →
  DIAG (exit 1). when key present on BOTH preds meet correctly yields Nullable → SOUND (distinct-variant
  attempt FOLDS: F26 is strictly the ABSENT-key case). /tmp/explorer_depth.LsiebH
- shape: loop-cond narrows *p, body `*p=null` then `**p` → DIAG (exit 1). back-edge widens correctly. SOUND.
  /tmp/explorer_loopcond.zXrBBv
- shape: back-edge stale narrowing `while(i<n){**p; if(*p){} i++;}` → DIAG at body-top `**p` (exit 1).
  entry-edge default Nullable reaches the deref; narrowing does NOT leak across back-edge → SOUND.
  /tmp/explorer_backedge.qv24Xw
VERDICT: no-new-pattern. Every join shape (if/else sibling, FP, nested FP, loop back-edge, loop-cond,
assignment-created DPVD, both-preds-present) is either SOUND (VD/FP pre-population; both-preds meet) or
FOLDS into F26 (absent-DPVD-key) / F18 (Unspecified source). The merge family's only soundness hole is
F26's DPVD absence-asymmetry; mergeVD/mergeFP are provably protected by pre-population at this commit.
Recommendation: pivot away from the merge functions; the higher-yield nullability surface is the
condition-status PRODUCER side (extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr multi-extract / getDerefPathVDFromExpr
MemberExpr-root gap — `*s.f` untracked, conservative not a bug) or non-nullability chains.

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — C5 InitLattice MERGE across CFG joins (BSCIRInitAnalysis)
Mode-2 call-chain trace of the IR init-analysis merge (the F75 ownership-merge analog,
separate file/analysis). Hop: merge(:337) / meetStates(:329) / tryPromoteParent(:536) /
checkOperand cross-variant escape(:878-888) / transferStatement union over-promotion(:109-126).
- shape: union {struct{a,b} s; int x}; write u.s.a on if-arm only; read u.x after join (anon nested struct) → SHAPE-REJECTED (parser: "expected ';'", anon struct in union)
- shape: same with named `struct Inner s` variant, `_Safe int leak` → SHAPE-REJECTED (union field access requires _Unsafe block)
- shape: same, union access wrapped in `_Unsafe{}` blocks, non-_Safe fn → ACCEPTED + valgrind "use of uninitialised value" — BUT INCONCLUSIVE: init analysis does not diagnose non-_Safe fns at all (confounded)
- shape: T1/T2 plain uninit int read inside `_Unsafe` vs no-_Unsafe in non-safe fn → both ACCEPTED → init analysis silent on non-_Safe functions
- shape: T4 `_Safe` fn, uninit plain int read INSIDE `_Unsafe{}` block → ACCEPTED (init diag suppressed by _Unsafe); T5 same read OUTSIDE _Unsafe → REJECTED. ⇒ `_Unsafe` block fully suppresses the init-use diagnostic (by-design escape hatch, not a bug).
- shape: T6 union field read in `_Safe` without `_Unsafe` → SHAPE-REJECTED ("union field access requires _Unsafe block"). ⇒ union cross-variant reads NEVER reach the init diagnostic in safe code; the union-variant tracking + the checkOperand:886 presence-not-state cross-variant escape + the transferStatement:118-122 whole-local over-promotion on a single sub-field write are all DEAD code w.r.t. producing a diagnostic. The C5 over-approximation exists in the logic but is unreachable.
- shape: T7 `_Safe` fn, uninit STRUCT field read inside `_Unsafe{}` → ACCEPTED (confirms _Unsafe suppression is general, not union-specific).
- shape: T8 struct field `s.a` init inside while-loop body, read after loop → REJECTED "possibly uninitialized" (loop back-edge merge SOUND).
- shape: T9 struct field init in switch case 1, read in case 2 via fallthrough → REJECTED "possibly uninitialized" (switch/fallthrough merge SOUND).
CONCLUSION: the InitLattice merge (meetStates-based) is conservative on the REACHABLE
in-scope surface (plain structs, all join shapes: if/else, loop, switch-fallthrough — all
demote one-path-init fields to MaybeInit → use rejected). UNLIKE F75 (ownership merge UNIONs
the set), this IR init merge MEETs correctly. The only over-approximation (union whole-local /
cross-variant presence-based escape) is SHAPE-REJECTED: union field access mandates `_Unsafe`,
and `_Unsafe` suppresses the init diagnostic. No new root cause. NOT a fold of F75 (different
analysis) — it's the absence of F75's bug in the sibling analysis.

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain D: CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType nested-fnptr _Borrow at ASSIGNMENT time
- shape: BASELINE single-level `cb_borrow_t fp = use_raw;` (LHS fnptr param int *_Borrow, RHS raw) → REJECTED ("incompatible _Borrow function pointer types"). /tmp/explorer_baseline_FINAL.nqWpFe.cbs
- shape: PROBE nested-fnptr `outer_borrow_t fp = takes_raw_cb;` where outer fnptr's PARAM is itself a fnptr and the _Borrow mismatch is on the INNER fnptr's param (`inner_borrow_t`=int *_Borrow vs `inner_raw_t`=raw) → **CONFIRMED-new (pending file)**: compiles CLEAN (exit 0). /tmp/explorer_probe_FINAL.KGosHq.cbs
- shape: PROBE2 forced early-return guard FALSE by adding an outer-level `int *_Borrow extra` param alongside the nested mismatch → STILL clean (exit 0) — proves the FN is NOT only the hasBorrowRetOrParams early-return guard; BorrowParamTypesMatch / DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraintsImpl itself fails to detect a nested-fnptr inner-param _Borrow mismatch. /tmp/explorer_probe2.*.cbs
- shape: PROBE3 control — same 2-param shape but the OUTER `extra` param differs (int *_Borrow vs raw) → REJECTED, proving CheckBorrowFunctionPointerType IS running for this fnptr shape. /tmp/explorer_probe3.grqMtO.cbs
- shape: PROBE4 runtime via fp(raw_cb) → the CALL-argument check (correct, single-level) rejects passing a raw inner cb where inner_borrow_t is expected; runtime-via-call path blocked by a different/correct check. Type-level FN (assignment) is the genuine defect. /tmp/explorer_probe4.*.cbs
- shape: PROBE5 return-type nesting (`inner_ret_borrow_t` returns int *_Borrow) → SHAPE-REJECTED ("return type is not allowed to be _Borrow qualified" with no borrow param) — return-type borrow variant is out-of-grammar, NOT part of this FN's blast radius. /tmp/explorer_probe5.dPKCUM.cbs
- Root: BorrowParamTypesMatch lambda (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:888-908) -> DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraintsImpl (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:395-492). For a fnptr-typed param, AreBSCPointerQualifiersCompatible (:369) checks only the OUTER fnptr's owned/borrow, then line 482 compares pointees (FunctionProtoTypes) with getCanonicalType().getUnqualifiedType() equality, which does not distinguish `void(int *_Borrow)` from `void(int *)` here. No recursion into the nested fnptr's params.
- DISTINCT from: F53 (redecl gate HasDiffBorrorOrOwnedQualifiers, SemaDeclBSC — different function/caller, REDECL not ASSIGNMENT), F41 (CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType, OWNED dim, different function), F74 (call-site dispatch isFunctionPointerType ptr-to-fnptr), F29/F56 (nullability), F73 (ensure_init). Also DISTINCT from the 2026-05-20 INCONCLUSIVE probe (line ~201) which was a pointer-TO-borrow-pointer param (`int *_Borrow *`), not a fnptr-typed param — that case IS correctly rejected because the _Borrow is on the immediate pointee.
- defect class: C1 (only-deep-enough / peer-asymmetry: assignment-time borrow variance does not recurse into a fnptr-typed param the way it recurses into a plain pointer-typed param).

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — _Safe/_Unsafe BLOCK boundary vs ownership/borrow LEDGER (Mode-2 chain)
Chain-trace: does an ownership/borrow/init obligation crossing into/out of an `_Unsafe{}` block
nested in a `_Safe` function fall through a gap? STRUCTURAL: `BSCOwnership.cpp` + `BSCBorrowChecker.cpp`
have ZERO safe-zone gating (grep SafeZone|Unsafe|SZ_ = no logic). Only `BSCIRBuilder` (init IR) has
SafeZoneStack/currentSafeZone (:44-47). So ownership+borrow run over the WHOLE CFG, _Unsafe-transparent;
init-analysis IS _Unsafe-suppressed (prior explorer). Gating in SemaDeclBSC.cpp:279-401:
RequireBorrowCheck=FindSafeFeatures (SafeFeatureFinder, WalkerBSC.h:367 — NO VisitSafeStmt override, so
_Unsafe contents ARE traversed → ownership turned ON by owned/borrow inside _Unsafe). Ownership runs only
if 0 nullability errors; nullability(NC_SAFE) needs HasSafeZoneInFunction.
PROBES (8 distinct shapes, all SOUND — strong negative):
- P1 owned moved INSIDE _Unsafe{consume(p)} then used in outer _Safe → REJECT "use of moved value: p"
  (identical to B1 baseline move+use both outside). Ownership NOT suppressed. SOUND.
- P2 owned alloc, _Unsafe{} does nothing, scope exit → REJECT "memory leak of value: p". Leak ledger
  _Unsafe-transparent. SOUND.
- P3 free p inside _Unsafe{}, free again in outer _Safe → REJECT "invalid cast ... of moved or
  uninitialized value: p" (double-free caught). B3 (single free inside _Unsafe) → clean. SOUND.
- P4 mut-borrow inside _Unsafe held live, 2nd mut-borrow outside → REJECT "cannot borrow x as mutable
  more than once" (identical to B4 both-outside). Borrow exclusivity _Unsafe-transparent. SOUND.
  (note: borrow MUTABILITY is on the &_Mut operator, not the decl — `int *_Borrow b` decl + `&_Mut x`.)
- P5 _Owned p decl-uninit, init ONLY inside `if(c){_Unsafe{p=mk();}}`, free regardless → REJECT
  "use of possibly uninitialized value: p" + "invalid cast ... of uninit value: p" (identical to B5
  no-_Unsafe). Owned-init tracked across _Unsafe even though init-analysis suppressed there. SOUND.
- P6 owned FIELD s.f freed inside _Unsafe, re-freed outside → REJECT field double-free. P7 (single field
  free inside _Unsafe) → clean. Field-granular move ledger _Unsafe-transparent. SOUND.
- P8 raw-alias free of owned inside _Unsafe `(int*)p` → SHAPE-REJECTED "cannot cast between _Owned and
  raw pointer; use __move_to_raw/__take_from_raw" (even in _Unsafe; raw/owned cast always rejected).
- P9A canonical `_Unsafe{ free((void*)__move_to_raw(p)); }` (real destructor idiom) → clean, valgrind
  ERROR SUMMARY 0 / 0 lost / 0 invalid-ops. __move_to_raw consume tracked. P9B use p after move_to_raw →
  REJECT "invalid cast ... of moved or uninitialized value: p". SOUND.
VERDICT: no-new-pattern. The `_Safe`/`_Unsafe` BLOCK boundary is SOUND for the ownership + borrow ledgers:
both analyzers are deliberately safe-zone-AGNOSTIC (run on full CFG), so move/leak/double-free/borrow-
exclusivity/owned-init obligations crossing the boundary are tracked identically inside and outside
_Unsafe. The ONLY _Unsafe suppression is init-analysis (BSCIRBuilder, by design), and ownership's own
uninit-owned check (separate, CFG-based) is NOT suppressed → covers the owned case. The raw/owned cast
gate (P8) and the __move_to_raw/__take_from_raw transfer (P9) are the only ledger mutation paths inside
_Unsafe and both are correctly accounted. DISTINCT-confirm: not F62 (SafeExpr-strip in CheckMoveVar, a
Sema predicate — FIXED), not F14/F22 (temp/cond leak), not F75 (union owned-field merge). Recommendation:
the _Unsafe-block ledger surface is mined out; the remaining asymmetry worth a future look is the
ownership-runs-only-if-0-nullability-errors gate (SemaDeclBSC:395) — a nullability FALSE POSITIVE would
suppress the leak check, but that requires first finding a nullability FP (itself a separate bug).

## 2026-05-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Nullability narrowing PRODUCER side (init/invert/&=/|= reachability + VisitMEForFieldPath key collision)
Mode-2 call-chain: traced who PRODUCES a per-edge narrowing fact from a condition.
runOnBlock(:915 succ_size()==2 gate) → getLastCondition() → PassConditionStatusToSuccBlocks(:778)
→ NullCheckInfo(CondExpr)=init/&=/|=/invert (BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp) → extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr
→ SetCFGBlocksByExpr(:734) → getDerefPathVDFromExpr/getVarDeclFromExpr/getMemberExprFromExpr+VisitMEForFieldPath.
Goal: a condition shape that PRODUCES a present(NonNull) fact the condition does NOT prove → FN/SIGSEGV,
DISTINCT from F70 (`!(...&&...)` De-Morgan), F26, F18, F48, F49, F50, F65.
- shape: `!(c || p==nullptr)` (De-Morgan over OR) → p NOT narrowed → false-positive deref diag (exit 1).
  CONSERVATIVE/SOUND: `|=` intersects two distinct null-checks to {}, invert empty. OR-under-`!` never
  fabricates a present. /tmp/explorer_probe.u29nTJ.cbs
- shape: `if(p!=null && q!=null){*p;*q;}` plain split `&&` → clean (exit 0). CFG splits, both leaves narrow. SOUND.
- shape: `if((0,(p!=null && q!=null))){*p;*q;}` comma-wrapped `&&` → false-positive (exit 1). Comma defeats
  CFG-split; extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr(comma) recurses RHS to `&&` which it does NOT handle → null
  → NO narrowing. Comma is always the SAFE direction (kills facts). SOUND-conservative. /tmp/explorer_probe.p7kB2q.cbs
- shape: nullability diag fires OUTSIDE _Safe (`int h(int*_Nullable p){return *p;}` → exit 1) — unblocks
  raw-deref FieldPath-collision repros that the safe zone shape-rejects.
- shape: `if((*pp).f!=null) return *(*qq).f;` non-_Safe, raw ptrs → compiles clean, RUNTIME SIGSEGV (exit 139).
  Control `*(*qq).f` no-narrow → REJECTED (exit 1) ⇒ accept-in-branch is the collision. **FOLDED-F33**
  (VisitMEForFieldPath :149-163 omits UnaryOperator base → FP.first=nullptr → all `(*X).f` collide on
  key (nullptr,".f")). NOT new. /tmp/explorer_repro.PBJjvD.cbs, baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.AXFXnL.cbs
- shape: subscript-base `if(pp[0].f!=null) return *qq[0].f;` → REJECTED (exit 1), control also REJECTED.
  containsArrayAccess/getTrackablePtr reject subscript-containing exprs → `(pp[0].f!=null)` produces NO
  narrowing → conservative. F33 is specifically the deref-base case, NOT subscript. /tmp/explorer_probe.HLRggn.cbs
- shape (reasoned, not probed): `!(p==null && q==null)` both-null-checks under `!`-`&&` → `&=` unions
  null={p,q}, invert → present={p,q} → 2-ptr variant of F70. FOLD-F70.
VERDICT: no-new-pattern. The producer combinator (init/&=/|=/invert) has NO soundness hole beyond F70
(`!`-over-`&&`-with-`==nullptr`): CFG-split makes init's `&&`/`||` recursion dead except under `!`;
OR-under-`!` and comma-wrapping are conservative. The producer key-extractor collision IS F33
(VisitMEForFieldPath UnaryOperator-base gap), confirmed live at 28656aa9, subscript-base variant is
conservative. Recommendation next session: pivot to the DPVD producer (getDerefPathVDFromExpr) staleness
across a VD-narrowing that doesn't invalidate stale (VD,depth) entries — but that likely folds into F26;
or leave the nullability producer parked (only F70+F33 live) and pivot to a non-nullability chain.

- overwrite owned array ptr p = safe_malloc_array(...) without freeing old — SHAPE-REJECTED (rejects "assign to _Owned value: p") — /tmp/explorer_probe.poZBT0.cbs — 2026-05-30
- safe_free_array(p) inside while loop body (iter2 double-free) — SHAPE-REJECTED (rejects "invalid cast of moved value: p" on back-edge) — /tmp/explorer_probe.SWHa4p.cbs — 2026-05-30

- `const int*_Borrow _ArrayElem b = &_Const p[0]`, use, drop p without free (leak) — SHAPE-REJECTED (rejects "memory leak of value: p"; element borrow does NOT launder owner) — /tmp/explorer_probe.ausZty.cbs — 2026-05-30

## 2026-05-30 — cast borrow-drop candidate (explorer CONFIRMED-NEW) — main-thread verdict: NOT FILED (not a _Safe bug)
The cast-checks explorer flagged: C-style `(int**)pp` drops a nested `_Borrow` where the assignment
`int**raw = pp` is rejected (SemaCast.cpp:2927-2936 no hasBorrowInPtrChain peer; SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:655
CheckBorrowQualTypeCStyleCast returns true for the drop direction). RE-VALIDATED + SCRUTINIZED:
- **NOT _Safe-reachable**: the same cast inside a _Safe fn is REJECTED ("conversion ... forbidden in the
  safe zone" — the safe-zone pointer-category-cast ban catches it). `/tmp/c77_safe_cast.cbs`.
- Reachable ONLY in _Unsafe (default) context, and ONLY via an EXPLICIT C-style cast. The assignment form
  is rejected even in _Unsafe; the explicit cast accepting the drop is consistent with C's "explicit casts
  override implicit conversion checks" model (cf. `(int*)const_ptr` allowed, `int*=const_ptr` not).
- The laundered raw pointer is inert in _Safe (raw deref forbidden); the UAF the explorer showed needs
  _Unsafe deref. So NO _Safe memory-safety soundness FN — the BSC guarantee (memory safety in _Safe) holds.
- **VERDICT: NOT a fileable bug** (at most LOW _Unsafe cast-vs-assignment consistency, arguably intended).
  This is the same over-claim shape as the prior IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast "CONFIRMED-NEW" that was
  corrected — _Unsafe-only / explicit-escape findings are out of the _Safe-soundness bar. Distinct from F74
  (which WAS _Safe-reachable, no cast). Do NOT re-chase; do NOT file.
- Parallel _ArrayElem explorer: clean negative — _Owned/_Borrow _ArrayElem move/free/borrow tracking is
  well-guarded (8 control-flow shapes all correctly rejected); see code_notes/SemaChecking_ArrayElem.md.
  One unprobed surface noted: handleBSCRawTransferBuiltin (SemaChecking.cpp:248-269) cv/nullability laundering.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle (Mode 2, chain) — CodeGen owned-DROP emission free-count audit
Steered hunt: does codegen emit the _Owned free (safe_free call) the WRONG count vs
what the analyzer accepted -> runtime leak (missed free) or double-free (extra free) on
a CHECKER-ACCEPTED program? Oracle: scripts/vg_probe.sh. NEGATIVE — all SOUND.
Structural: BSC codegen synthesizes NO implicit owned-drop (no CGDeclBSC/CGExprBSC, no
EHScope cleanup for owned locals, no __destruct/Drop). Every free is an explicit user
safe_free CALL emitted once via standard C call codegen. So a codegen wrong-free-count
needs (a) codegen drop/dup of an explicit call (= F58 class, now Sema-gated) or (b) an
ANALYZER FN re-consume (F64/F67/F75) paired with codegen no-op. No codegen-ONLY divergence.
- owned consume baseline -> ACCEPTED, clean 1alloc/1free. /tmp/owned_base.cbs
- owned `?:`-into-var (c?mk():mk()) then consume -> ACCEPTED, clean (only chosen arm evaluated). /tmp/p3.cbs
- owned consume-then-`break` in while loop -> ACCEPTED, clean. /tmp/p4.cbs
- owned moved into struct field, struct consumed by-value -> ACCEPTED, clean. /tmp/p2.cbs
- owned struct returned by value (return-slot move) then consumed -> ACCEPTED, clean. /tmp/p5.cbs
- two-return-path owned struct, free the unreturned arm -> ACCEPTED, clean. /tmp/p6.cbs
- owned consumed on BOTH branches + forward `goto` convergence -> ACCEPTED, clean. /tmp/p7.cbs
- SafeExpr-wrapped aggregate owned-struct by-value arg drop_box(_Safe(b)) -> ACCEPTED, clean. /tmp/p9.cbs
- owned `?:` with consume in BOTH arms (c?consume(a):consume(b)) -> SHAPE-REJECTED ("use of moved value: b"). /tmp/p1.cbs
- SafeExpr-wrapped owned consume inside _Safe fn body -> SHAPE-REJECTED ("_Unsafe function call forbidden in safe zone"). /tmp/p8.cbs
Source read: CGBuiltin.cpp:5369-5382 ownership-transfer builtins (__move_to_raw/__take_from_raw/
__move_array_to_raw/__take_array_from_raw) — EmitScalarExpr(arg) once, pointer-cast, no drop/dup,
scalar-only args -> SOUND. CGBuiltin.cpp:2795-2797 __assume_initialized still returns nullptr w/o
emitting arg, but F58 fix Sema-gates side-effecting args -> closed. CGExprAgg.cpp:341-361 owned-struct
copy/move = standard memcpy/callCStruct*, no per-field free. SafeExpr VisitSafeExpr present in
scalar/complex/agg/lvalue emitters; MISSING in CGExprConstant.cpp = F69 (open, already filed).
Recommendation next session: the codegen owned-drop surface is closed-by-construction (no synthesized
drops). Pivot back to ANALYZER-side re-consume FNs (the only way to a double-free): widen the
OwnershipImpl::merge / field-move join (F75 family) to nested-field and array-of-owned-field joins,
or getDerefPathVD staleness across owned moves through pointer-to-pointer (F64 family) at deeper depth.

LAUNDER a pointee const/volatile, a _Nullable/_Nonnull nullability, or owned/borrow? Tested all 4

RESULT: CLEAN NEGATIVE — every dimension preserved correctly; nullability is dataflow-tracked, not
launderable via the static handler annotation.
- pointee `const` through __move_to_raw: `*r=99` on `const int*` raw result REJECTED ("read-only var not assignable"). PRESERVED — /tmp/explorer_probe.aMZkcd.cbs — 2026-05-30
- pointee `const` through __take_from_raw re-add (`const int*` raw -> `const int*_Owned`): `*q=99` REJECTED. PRESERVED — /tmp/explorer_probe.inM398.cbs — 2026-05-30
- pointee `volatile` / `const volatile` through __take_from_raw: ast-dump shows `volatile int*_Owned` / `const volatile int*_Owned`. PRESERVED — /tmp/explorer_probe.* (qv,qcv) — 2026-05-30
- __take_from_raw(plain raw) result nullability: ast-dump `int*_Owned`; under -nullability-check=all `int*_Owned q = __take_from_raw(raw)` REJECTED "nonnull cannot be assigned by nullable" => result is _Nullable (CONSERVATIVE/SOUND, raw default Nullable copied) — /tmp/explorer_probe.gK7KqO.cbs — 2026-05-30
- __take_from_raw narrowing-awareness: after `if(!raw){exit;}` the result assigns to `_Owned` (nonnull) clean under check=all; safe_malloc's `return __take_from_raw(addr)` into `T*_Owned` also clean. Dataflow narrows, not static type — SOUND — /tmp/explorer_probe.* — 2026-05-30
- __move_to_raw(_Owned p) -> raw is `int * _Nonnull` (explicit nonnull copied from owned default-nonnull). This is a TRUE claim (owned is non-null). NOT a launder: setting `r=NULL` then `*r` under check=all REJECTED "nullable cannot be dereferenced" (dataflow tracks the NULL assign, static _Nonnull does NOT suppress) — /tmp/explorer_probe.Ge5hkW.cbs — 2026-05-30
- __move_to_raw(_Owned _Nullable p) -> raw `int*` (no annotation = Nullable default). CORRECT (nullable preserved) — /tmp/explorer_probe.* (raw) — 2026-05-30
- __take_array_from_raw(plain) -> CallExpr type `int*_Owned _ArrayElem _Nullable`; __auto_type VarDecl pretty-prints DOUBLED `int*_Owned _ArrayElem  _Nullable _Owned _ArrayElem` — but explicit-typed assign `int*_Owned _ArrayElem a = ...` compiles clean (canonical MATCHES) and full safe_malloc_array round-trip is valgrind-clean (0 err / 0 lost). Doubled print is a COSMETIC AttributedType sugar artifact, not a canonical/move-tracking defect — /tmp/explorer_probe.* (aprobe) + /tmp/explorer_probe.* (safe_malloc_array vg) — 2026-05-30
VERDICT: NO new root cause in the raw-transfer builtin handler. The getUnqualifiedType (top-level only)
correctly leaves pointee cv inside the PointerType; the nullability copy seeds the static type but the
BSCNullabilityCheck dataflow (narrowing-aware) is the real authority and is robust to the static seed.
DISTINCT from F75 (owned-field merge UNION) and the _ArrayElem move/free negative (2026-05-30 above).

- CONTROL homogeneous nested _Owned drop (both unsafe): `void f(int *_Owned *p); void f(int **p);` -> correctly REJECTED ("conflicting types") => homogeneous path (HasDiffBorrowOrOwnedQualifiers) RECURSES PointerType; only the HETERO path has the hole. /tmp/explorer_homo.*

- DIMENSION borrow drop `int *_Borrow * -> int **` (hetero) -> ACCEPTED (BUG). /tmp/explorer_borrow.*
- DIMENSION arrayelem drop `int *_Owned _ArrayElem * -> int *_Owned *` (hetero) -> ACCEPTED (BUG). /tmp/explorer_ae.*

- ASYMMETRY BASELINE (internal): nested C-qual `const int ** -> int **` (hetero) -> correctly REJECTED ("incompatible parameter type") => typesAreCompatible DOES recurse C-level cv into pointee; only the BSC quals (stripped at :243-248) are dropped. Same nesting depth, C-qual caught / BSC-qual not. /tmp/explorer_arm.*

- RUNTIME: drop form double-free witness `/tmp/explorer_rt2.hRxh7F.cbs` -> vg_probe: ACCEPTED, ERROR SUMMARY 1, invalid-ops 1; valgrind "Invalid free() ... 1 allocs, 2 frees" = double free reachable via _Safe contract corruption.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle (Mode 2, Chain I) — Array-decay-to-_Borrow conversion matrix
Steered hunt (fresh code e79ba0f "destination-sensitive array decay to _Borrow"):
does the array T[N] -> _Borrow DECAY launder an element-type or pointee qualifier that the
pointer->pointer form rejects? Hops: GetSafeArrayDecayType (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:835) ->
Sema::isBorrowArrayDecayTypeMatch (SemaExpr.cpp:545-576) -> IsSafePointerConversion (:801).
KEY STRUCTURAL FINDING: when isBorrowArrayDecayTypeMatch returns TRUE, GetSafeArrayDecayType
returns DestPtrType verbatim, so IsSafeConversion's IsSafePointerConversion(SrcDecayed, Dest)
at :891-894 compares DestType vs DestType = TAUTOLOGY. All soundness rests on
isBorrowArrayDecayTypeMatch alone. Audited that predicate's full matrix:
- const int[N] -> int*_Borrow (drop const) -> REJECT (sound). /tmp probes 2026-05-30
- const int[N] -> int*_Borrow _ArrayElem (drop const into mutable) -> REJECT (sound)
- const int param(=const int*) -> int*_Borrow _ArrayElem -> REJECT (pointer path, sound)
- int[N] -> long*_Borrow (elem-type mismatch) -> REJECT (sound, hasSameType false)
- int[N] -> void*_Borrow (trivial->void) -> ACCEPT (correct)
- struct{int*}[N] -> void*_Borrow (non-trivial raw-ptr field) -> REJECT (sound, isTrivialDataType false)
- const int[N] -> void*_Borrow (non-const void) -> REJECT (sound, line 575)
- const int[N] -> const void*_Borrow -> ACCEPT (correct); int[N] -> const void*_Borrow -> ACCEPT (add const, correct)
- volatile int[N] -> int*_Borrow (drop volatile) -> REJECT (sound)
- int[N] -> volatile int*_Borrow (add volatile) -> REJECT by "incompatible _Borrow types" FIRST
  (array form's volatile/restrict-ADD strip at SemaExpr.cpp:561-564 is effectively DEAD CODE under
  the safe-zone borrow-compat check that fires earlier; benign — over-rejects the sound add direction)
- int[N] -> int*_Borrow _ArrayElem _Nonnull -> ACCEPT (array always non-null, correct)
- char[N] -> const char*_Borrow _ArrayElem (add const elem) -> ACCEPT (correct)
- int[2][3] -> int(*_Borrow _ArrayElem)[3] -> ACCEPT (matching ptr-to-array); -> void*_Borrow ACCEPT;
  -> int*_Borrow _ArrayElem (dimension-skip) -> REJECT (sound)
- int*_Owned a[N] -> any -> SHAPE-REJECT ("type of array cannot be qualified by _Owned"); array-of-owned/borrow nonexistent
- int[N] -> raw int* (safe) -> ACCEPT (no qual change); const int[N] -> raw int* -> REJECT (IsSafePointerConversion fallback)
- int[N] -> int*_Borrow then p[2] (plain borrow, no _ArrayElem) -> subscript REJECT ("subscript of _Borrow not allowed")
- NON-decay aside: two mut int*_Borrow _ArrayElem of same local array compile clean -> BUT explicit
  &_Mut a[0] twice ALSO clean -> _ArrayElem exclusivity property of borrow checker (F39 family), NOT a
  decay-type defect (decay produces identical &_Mut a[0] AST via MaybeDecayArrayToBorrowArrayElemPointer).
VERDICT: NO new root cause. Chain I decay TYPE matrix is SOUND across const/volatile/restrict/void/
nullability/multi-dim/element-mismatch/struct-with-pointer/raw dimensions. Recommend Chain I ->
SATURATED @ 28656aa9. Distinct surface from F51 (builtin narrowing) and IsSafePointerConversion audit.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer Chain-L — mixed-mode _Safe/_Unsafe overload SELECTION + fnptr-assign

Traced Chain L: `SelectDeclForHeterogeneousRedecl` (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:236-285),
`SelectFunctionDeclForPointerAssignment` (:537-562), the two callers
(SemaExpr.cpp:7427 direct-call lexical-zone; `IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast`:564-658
fnptr-assign dest-typed-context), constraint helpers `IsCallAssignmentCompatible`
(:341, outer-only `AreBSCPointerQualifiersCompatible`) and
`DoesFunctionPointerSatisfyConstraints` (:508, strict). Table
context{safe,unsafe} x decl-set{only-Safe,only-Unsafe,both} x target{direct call,
fnptr assign, fnptr arg, return}. RESULT: all OUTER-level cells SOUND; nested-qual
holes FOLD into F76; the unsafe-view "lie" is gated by the raw-cast rule. NO new root.

- hetero pair `void take(int*); _Safe void take(int*_Owned);` (safe ADDS owned), one shared body → COMPILES (INCONCLUSIVE/feature-baseline) — /tmp/explorer_probe.kvJTyO.cbs
- hetero with two DEFINITIONS → SHAPE-REJECTED ("redefinition") — one body only, two type-views
- `_Safe` decl that REMOVES owned (`int*_Owned`→`int*`) → SHAPE-REJECTED (F77 single-level path correctly rejects)
- safe-zone direct call `take(owned)` → selects safe(owned) view, consumes — INCONCLUSIVE (sound)
- safe-zone direct call `take(raw)` (safe view owned) → REJECTED err_bsc_no_matching_heterogeneous_function_call (sound)
- fnptr `safe_owned_fp = take` → ACCEPTED (matches safe view) ; `safe_raw_fp = take` → REJECTED (sound)
- fnptr `unsafe_raw_fp = take` / `unsafe_owned_fp = take` (safe zone) → ACCEPTED (selects matching view; unsafe-fnptr not safe-callable) — sound
- only-_Unsafe f → `safe_fp = f` (both zones) → REJECTED (unsafe→safe forbidden, sound)
- only-_Safe g → `unsafe_fp = g` (both zones) → ACCEPTED (safe→unsafe widening, sound)
- ownership-mismatch safe fnptr from non-hetero safe fn (owned/raw/borrow x raw/owned) → all REJECTED (sound, single-level)
- wrappers defeating selection DRE detection (paren / `1?take:take` ternary / `*&take`) → REJECTED; defeating selection makes RHSFuncType = default DRE type = SAFE view (MORE conservative, not less) — sound
- direct call `take(raw)` in safe zone, hetero unsafe=raw/safe=owned → REJECTED (arg-ownership mismatch caught) — sound
- pass hetero fn as `_Safe void(*)(int*)` (safe-raw) ARGUMENT `apply(take,r)` → REJECTED (call-arg fnptr path catches it) — sound
- unsafe-view "lie": `unsafe_raw_fp a=take; a((int*)q)` → SHAPE-REJECTED (raw cast of `_Owned` needs `__move_to_raw`; unsafe view can't be fed an owned alias) — by-design gated
- return-type hetero (unsafe raw-ret / safe owned-ret): `sof=get` ACCEPTED, `srf(safe raw-ret)=get` REJECTED, `urf(unsafe raw-ret)=get` ACCEPTED — all sound
- **NESTED-inner mismatch via selection**: `safe_rawinner_fp(_Safe void(int**)) = take` where safe view param is `int*_Owned*` → **ACCEPTED (silent)** → **FOLDED-F76** (routes through `DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraintsImpl:482` canonical-unqualified pointee compare — same root as F76; selection is just a new CALLER of the same buggy compare) — /tmp/explorer_probe.afhR3m.cbs

VERDICT: no-new-pattern. Chain-L selection logic is SOUND at outer-qualifier level
in every reachable cell. The two distinct outer-only-compare roots (F76 assign-path,
F77 redecl-merge) already cover the nested holes; selection adds a third CALLER but
not a third root. Recommendation: register sub-chain "selection routes nested
qualifier compare through F76's DoPointerTypesSatisfyAssignmentConstraintsImpl" so a
future F76 fix is verified to also close the heterogeneous-selection caller.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain K (two-CFG-build asymmetry: setAllAlwaysAdd raw-AST ownership/null CFG vs 10-class Prologue borrow CFG)
Hunt: a soundness FN where ownership/nullability (raw AST, full linearization, runOnBlock visits only
{DeclStmt, CallExpr, assign-BinaryOperator, inc/dec-UnaryOperator, ReturnStmt} — BSCOwnership.cpp:2615-2620)
reach a DIFFERENT element boundary than the borrow Prologue CFG, hiding a move/borrow.
- shape: top-level `c ? consume(p) : consume(p);` (ConditionalOperator NOT in the 5 visited kinds) → INCONCLUSIVE/SOUND. CFG dump shows full-linearization gives each nested `consume(p)` its OWN CallExpr element → runOnBlock visits it via SetHandlingCallExpr. Both arms tracked Moved; merge keeps Moved.
- shape: move nested in IF condition `if (consume_ret(p)) { consume_ret(p); }` (IfStmt NOT in 5 kinds) → SOUND. Second consume REJECTED "use of moved value: p". The branch-condition element is linearized + visited.
- shape: `*p = consume_ret(p);` (LHS deref-use of pointer moved by RHS, one assignment) → SOUND. REJECTED "use of moved value: p" (RHS visited Move before LHS Assign).
- shape: `c && consume_ret(p); consume_ret(p);` and `c || consume_ret(p); consume_ret(p);` (short-circuit move then unconditional consume) → SOUND. Both REJECTED — ownership merges to MaybeMoved, post-consume rejected. (Confirms Chain E note that &&/|| operand moves are caught.)
- shape: `int r = use_borrow(&_Const *p) + consume_ret(p);` (borrow into p's pointee AND move p in one stmt) → ACCEPTED, runtime clean (no double-free; `+` evaluated borrow-read before consume). Benign; not a defect.
- shape: mut-mut borrow conflict buried in one call `two(&_Mut x, &_Mut x)` → SOUND, REJECTED "cannot borrow x as mutable more than once". Prologue hoists both into temps; borrow checker sees both live.
- shape: mut-mut conflict with first borrow via COMMA `two((c, &_Mut x), &_Mut x)` AND `int *_Borrow b1=(c,&_Mut x); int *_Borrow b2=&_Mut x;` → ACCEPTED (should reject) → **FOLDED-F11**. The comma's `&_Mut x` is invisible to the borrow checker's ActionExtract/DefUse::VisitBinaryOperator (BO_Comma falls through, no operand visit). This is F11's exact root (single-build borrow-checker coalescing), NOT a two-CFG-build divergence. /tmp/explorer_kprobe.*.cbs
- shape: mut-mut conflict with first borrow via TERNARY `c ? &_Mut x : &_Mut x` and via `&&`+ternary → SOUND, REJECTED. Prologue correctly hoists ?: and && so the borrow checker sees those borrows.
NEGATIVE: Chain K yields NO new root cause. Ownership/nullability's `setAllAlwaysAdd` full linearization is a strict SUPERSET of the borrow Prologue CFG's element set for move/borrow effects — every nested side-effecting subexpr (call, ?:, &&, if-cond, assign-LHS/RHS) becomes its own CFG element and runOnBlock's 5-kind filter still reaches the nested CallExpr/DeclStmt moves. The ONLY laundering construct is the comma operator, and that is a borrow-checker `VisitBinaryOperator` coalescing (F11), already filed — it manifests on the borrow build's single VisitBinaryOperator, not on a divergence between the two builds. Ownership special-cases BO_Comma correctly (BSCOwnership.cpp:2177), so the comma move-flow is sound; only borrow is broken (= F11). Chain K should be SATURATED @ 28656aa9 (both builds sound; comma = F11; ?:/&& hoisted correctly; full-linearization superset confirmed via -dump-owned-check).

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle — CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak field-TYPE predicate asymmetry (SemaBSCDestructor/OwnedStruct scan)
Task: scan SemaBSCDestructor.cpp + SemaBSCOwnedStruct.cpp for IN-SCOPE code paths.
GUARD TABLE finding: SemaBSCDestructor.cpp is PURELY OOS — every internal path gates on
`IsVarDeclWithOwnedStructureType` == `isOwnedStructureType()` (= `_Owned struct`, OOS).
`DesugarDestructorCall` runs on all FDs but does nothing for plain `_Owned`/struct-with-owned-field.
SemaBSCOwnedStruct.cpp `CheckExprMemoryLeak::VisitMemberExpr` also OOS-gated (base `isOwnedStructureType()`).
PIVOT to the co-located peer at the SAME return-stmt call site (SemaStmt.cpp:3938):
`CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak` (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:547-559) is NOT OOS-gated.
- shape: move nested move-semantic field `s->inner` (struct Inner{int*_Owned p} as field of Outer) out of
  `_Borrow struct Outer*` via DeclStmt-init → ACCEPTED (should reject like `s->f`) → runtime double-free
  (valgrind 1 alloc/2 frees, Invalid free) → **CONFIRMED-new** (pending file). /tmp/explorer_repro.XpqyWI.cbs
- baseline: direct owned-qual field `s->f` move through borrow → REJECTED err_move_borrow (predicate :556
  `ME->getType().isOwnedQualified()` catches it). /tmp/explorer_baseline.UUpjCA.cbs
- control: nested field `struct Inner{int v}` (no owned) → clean compile + clean run (0/0). Legal value copy.
- variant A: call-arg `sink(s->inner)` (SemaExpr.cpp call site) → ACCEPTED (same root, blast radius).
- variant B: plain non-Safe fn DeclStmt init → ACCEPTED (root is Sema, not zone-gated).
- variant C: UO_Deref arm `*s` where `*s` is move-semantic struct (Inner via `_Borrow Inner*`) → ACCEPTED;
  baseline `*s` owned-qualified (`int*_Owned *_Borrow`) → REJECTED. Confirms BOTH arms (:551 UO_Deref + :556
  MemberExpr) share the single-predicate asymmetry (`isOwnedQualified()` missing `|| isMoveSemanticType()`).
ROOT: CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak tests `isOwnedQualified()` only; sibling CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak (:537)
tests `isOwnedQualified() || isMoveSemanticType()`. Field whose TYPE is a struct-with-owned-fields is
move-semantic but not owned-qualified → move-out-through-borrow undiagnosed → double-free.
DISTINCT from F21 (getBase() walk gap for paren-deref base-type — this is field-TYPE), F62 (SafeExpr wrapper),
F34 (WRITE direction `s->f=mk()` at dataflow IsTrackedType — this is READ/move-OUT at Sema), F61/F67/F75
(dataflow null/double-free of owned fields, BSCOwnership.cpp). Defect class C1 (predicate asymmetry).

- shape: compound-literal cast-type `(int *_Borrow _ArrayElem){arr}` sole-ish feature → rewrite clean, decl-var catches it → INCONCLUSIVE-then-SOUND (no leak)

- shape: `typedef int *_Owned T`, fnptr typedef, struct-with-fnptr-field, nested-typedef-owned through rewriter → all SOUND (qualifiers stripped, plain-c compiles)
- shape: local `typedef int *_Owned OT` sole-feature → SOUND (init type catches, clean)
- shape: array-of-`_Owned` (`int *_Owned x[N]`) field/local/param → SHAPE-REJECTED (Sema: array cannot be `_Owned`-qualified)
- shape: borrow lifetime/two-mut violation with `_Borrow` nested behind raw pointer `int *_Borrow *` → DETECTED via `hasBorrowFields()` recursion (TypeBSC.cpp:81-90 descends pointee) → SafeFeatureFinder catches it; hypothesis REJECTED
- shape: SafeFeatureFinder gate — owned/borrow buried ONLY in fnptr signature `void(*)(int *_Owned)` / `int *_Owned(*)(void)` → gate `FindSafeFeatures` returns FALSE (hasOwnedFields doesn't recurse into FunctionProtoType, SemaDeclBSC.cpp:279) → CFG ownership+borrow SKIPPED, BUT UNOBSERVABLE: no body-level CFG obligation arises from a fnptr-signature-buried qualifier alone; owned-temp leak caught by gate-independent Sema `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` → SOUND-but-smelly NEGATIVE (logged in RewriteBSC.md, not filed)

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain O: owned/borrow asymmetry at the indirect global/union/array decl gate
Hunt: a DISTINCT predicate in the "does this TYPE carry a nested owned/borrow obligation" family
(F77/F79/F57 exemplars) with an OBSERVABLE gap. Built the predicate × nesting × dimension table:
  - isMoveSemanticType (TypeBSC.cpp:341-367): walks RecordType fields ONLY; does NOT walk a field's
    pointer-pointee chain. basic-test = isOwnedQualified().
  - hasOwnedFields (TypeBSC.cpp:423-454): WALKS the field pointee chain (:438-444) + BFS into records.
  - hasBorrowFields (TypeBSC.cpp:456-488): WALKS the field pointee chain (:471-478) + BFS into records.
Gate callers (SemaDecl.cpp:8527/8528 global, :18687/18688 union field; SemaType.cpp:5202 array):
  CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:122) gates the INDIRECT case on isMoveSemanticType()
  — the NON-pointee-walking predicate. Its BORROW peer CheckBorrowOrIndirectBorrowType (:980) uses
  hasBorrowFields() — the pointee-WALKING predicate. Wrong predicate chosen for the owned dimension.
- shape: global `struct O { int *_Owned * f; } go;` → **ACCEPTED (exit 0)** → **CONFIRMED-new** (pending file).
  /tmp/explorer_repro.FXJxBp.cbs
- baseline: byte-identical `struct B { int *_Borrow * f; } gb;` → **REJECTED** ("contains '_Borrow' type even
  indirectly") — proves the gate's intent + that the owned predicate choice is the gap. /tmp/explorer_baseline.iYMlpa.cbs
- control: single-level `struct O2 { int *_Owned f; } go2;` → **REJECTED** ("contains '_Owned' type even
  indirectly") — isMoveSemanticType DOES catch a direct owned FIELD; proves it's specifically the
  pointer-NESTING (int *_Owned *) that slips, not "owned-in-global-struct allowed". /tmp/explorer_control.WEfBhJ.cbs
- blast: union field `union U { struct O o; int x; };` (nested-owned struct) → **ACCEPTED (bug)**; the
  _Borrow analog `union U { struct B b; }` → **REJECTED**. Same predicate, second call site (SemaDecl.cpp:18687).
  /tmp/explorer_union.*.cbs /tmp/explorer_unionb.*.cbs
- informational: direct `int *_Owned * gp;` (not wrapped in a struct) → ACCEPTED (isMoveSemanticType on the
  bare type: outer raw ptr not owned-qualified, no record → false). Same root predicate gap, sibling shape.
- runtime: heap routed solely through the global's buried _Owned field, all transfer in _Unsafe so no
  CFG-tracked local survives → `2 allocs, 0 frees, 12 bytes` (never freed). Valgrind classifies "still
  reachable" (not "definitely lost") ONLY because the global root is alive at exit — standard valgrind
  behavior for global-rooted allocations; the leak (0 frees, no drop) is real. /tmp/explorer_rt.xJHM9O.cbs
  (NB: a function-LOCAL `int *_Owned cell` instead trips the function-CFG leak check — /tmp/explorer_rt.zrnJS8.cbs
   REJECTED "memory leak of value: cell" — which is WHY the type-decl gate is the right layer and its hole matters.)
ROOT: CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType (SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:122) uses isMoveSemanticType() (no pointee walk)
where its borrow peer uses hasBorrowFields() (pointee walk). DISTINCT from F77 (AreOwnedBorrowQualifiersCompatible
hetero-redecl outer-only), F79 (CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak move-out gate isOwnedQualified-only — DIFFERENT function),
F57 (no FunctionProtoType recursion), F41/F76 (fnptr-variance ASSIGNMENT). Defect class C1 (owned/borrow
sibling-predicate asymmetry — wrong recursion-depth predicate selected for the owned global/union/array gate).
Fix: change the indirect arm from isMoveSemanticType() to hasOwnedFields(), mirroring the borrow peer.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain O: distinct owned-detection predicate hunt (post-F80)
Goal: find a DISTINCT predicate in the indirect/nested owned-borrow TYPE-detection family with an
OBSERVABLE soundness gap, NOT re-probing F77/F79/F80/F57/F41. Built the full recursion-depth table
(predicate x basic-test x pointee-walk x struct-field-walk x fnptr-param-walk):
  - isMoveSemanticType (TypeBSC.cpp:341-367): basic=isOwnedQualified; pointee-walk=NO; field-walk=YES(records); fnptr=NO. [F80 root]
  - hasOwnedFields (TypeBSC.cpp:57-79): basic=isOwnedQualified; pointee-walk=YES; field-walk=YES; fnptr=NO.
  - hasBorrowFields (TypeBSC.cpp:81-99): basic=isBorrowQualified; pointee-walk=YES; field-walk=YES; fnptr=NO.
  - hasOwnedRetOrParams (TypeBSC.cpp:109-119): basic=isOwnedQualified OUTER-ONLY; pointee=NO; field=NO. [F41 gate]
  - hasBorrowRetOrParams (TypeBSC.cpp:121-130): basic=hasBorrow()=>hasBorrowFields; pointee=YES; field=YES. [richer than owned peer — but the only caller (:462/:883) is F41's CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType; folds into F41]
  - CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak (SemaBSCOwnership:547): isOwnedQualified-only. [F79]
  - CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType (SemaBSCOwnership:122): isMoveSemanticType. [F80]
  - dataflow Ownership IsTrackedType (BSCOwnership.cpp:59): ptr=isOwnedQualified-outer; record=isMoveSemanticType.
  - dataflow Borrow IsTrackedTypeImpl (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:25-51): owned-ptr recurses pointee; borrow at any level; ALL fields. RICHER.
  - SafeFeatureFinder::VisitQualType (WalkerBSC.h:90-92): isOwnedQualified||isBorrowQualified||hasOwnedFields||hasBorrowFields + VisitType FunctionProtoType param recursion (:73-82). ROBUST gate — catches fnptr-buried owned in a param/var/field. No observable gap (prior 2026-05-30 probe + re-read).
  - isNestedBorrow (SemaBSCOwnership:584): multi-level-borrow detector; NO owned analog (CheckNestedOwnedType absent) — but that's BY DESIGN (ptr-to-owned is legal; borrow-of-borrow is not). Not a bug.
Probes:
- shape: function-LOCAL `struct O { int *_Owned * f; }` (owned 1 ptr deep), no free → ACCEPTED (no leak diag); control DIRECT owned field `struct O { int *_Owned f; }` → REJECTED "field memory leak of value: o, o.f is leak". Dataflow IsTrackedType case-3 (isMoveSemanticType) misses pointee-nested owned. /tmp/explorer_probe.7hw7eg.cbs /tmp/explorer_ctl.ihSxTZ.cbs → **FOLDED-into-F80** (same isMoveSemanticType helper, different call-site; F80 fix-option "deepen isMoveSemanticTypeImpl" closes it). Logged BSCOwnership.md IsTrackedType note.
- shape: runtime heap-holder leak of nested-owned-field struct local (malloc int*_Owned* cell, *cell owns int, orphaned) → ACCEPTED, valgrind definitely-lost 8 + indirectly-lost 4 bytes (silent leak FN). /tmp/explorer_rt.DHSUH2.cbs → **FOLDED-into-F80** (observable confirmation only).
- shape: `struct O{int*_Owned* f;} a = b;` assignment move-tracking (would-be double-free if Copy not Move) → contaminated: the inner `int *_Owned p` local trips its OWN leak diag first; can't isolate a clean pure-_Safe double-free for the nested case. /tmp/explorer_probeC.iC3Xh3.cbs INCONCLUSIVE (and would fold into F80 anyway — same helper at BSCOwnership:2156).
- shape: `&inner` into struct field with `int *_Owned inner` local → the direct owned local is itself tracked & rejected ("memory leak of value: inner") — confirms you cannot route the obligation SOLELY through the nested field without a heap holder. /tmp/explorer_probe.7f4rWx.cbs SHAPE-NOTE.
VERDICT: no DISTINCT new root cause. Every observable owned-detection gap in this family traces to one of
the four already-filed roots: isMoveSemanticType-no-pointee-walk (F80, incl. the dataflow IsTrackedType
co-located peer), hasOwnedRetOrParams/CheckOwnedFunctionPointerType outer-only (F41), CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak
isOwnedQualified-only (F79), AreOwnedBorrowQualifiersCompatible outer-only/no-fnptr-recursion (F77/F57).
The dataflow IsTrackedType (BSCOwnership.cpp:59) is the strongest NEAR-miss: distinct file/function/observable
but shared helper → fold. Flagged in BSCOwnership.md as an F80 fix-review co-located peer.
Recommendation next session: pivot OFF the owned-detection family (saturated at 28656aa9 modulo F80's eventual
fix shape) — try Chain Q (borrow-compare/reborrow qualifier laundering) or Chain R (CanBeUninitializedInSafeZone
type-classification FN), which are untraced and use DIFFERENT predicate families.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain R: CanBeUninitializedInSafeZone type-classification (TRACED, NEGATIVE)

Predicate: SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:1131-1175. Caller: ParseDecl.cpp:2630
(`InitKind::Uninitialized` → `err_unsafe_action "uninitialized declarator"` iff
`!CanBeUninitializedInSafeZone`). The predicate ONLY rejects `isOwnedStructureType()`
(top-level + recursive over struct/union fields + array elems); EVERYTHING else
(raw/_Owned/_Borrow POINTERS, structs WITH pointer fields) returns true=can-be-uninit.
So the decl-gate is FAR laxer than bsc-safe-zone §3 — BUT this is the already-filed
F01/F07 LOW "doc gap": uninit ptr/_Owned/_Borrow DECL is intentionally accepted, misuse
caught at USE. Chain-R target = a type where NEITHER decl-gate NOR use-time check fires.
Probed the use-time backstop (default UC_SAFE init analysis, runs for any _Safe fn):
- shape: plain `struct{int *p;}` uninit, return s.p (raw field read) → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: s.p" (init analysis catches). NOT a hole.
- shape: nested `struct{Inner in;}` uninit, return o.in.p (deep raw field read) → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: o.in.p". Init recurses deep. NOT a hole.
- shape: `int *arr[4];` uninit, return arr[i] → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: arr". Array-of-ptr read caught. NOT a hole.
- shape: `union{int *p; long n;}` uninit, return u.p → REJECTED by DIFFERENT rule "access to union field is _Unsafe" — unions independently protected, never reach uninit-read path.
- shape: whole-struct value read while uninit (`return s;`) → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: s". Whole-value read caught.
- shape: partial-init (init sibling int `s.a`, leave raw ptr `p` uninit) then `return s;` → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: s". Partial-init whole-read caught.
- shape: copy uninit struct to `S *_Borrow dst` (`*dst = s;`) → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: s". Struct-copy read caught.
- shape: `struct{int *arr[3]; int a;}` uninit, init s.a, `return s.arr[i]` → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: s.arr". Struct-embedded array-of-ptr read caught.
- shape: init s.arr[0] (borrow), read s.arr[1] → hit borrow→raw conversion rule first (forbidden in safe), can't isolate; INCONCLUSIVE-shape-blocked.
VERDICT: no DISTINCT new root cause via the decl-gate FN angle. The decl-gate laxity is
fully backstopped by the use-time init analysis (BSCIRInitAnalysis), which tracks raw/nested/
array/whole-struct/partial-init pointer-field reads and fires "use of uninitialized value".
Unions are protected by the union-access rule before reaching the uninit-read path. The
decl-gate-laxity-alone direction is F01/F07 (already filed LOW). Chain R → recommend
SATURATED @ 28656aa9 for the FN angle; reopen if BSCIRInitAnalysis field-tracking or
CanBeUninitializedInSafeZone changes. Note written to SemaBSCSafeZone.md.
Recommendation next session: Chain R's complementary FP angle (decl-gate WRONGLY rejecting
a valid uninit type) is unprobed but low-yield (gate is lax, not strict). Higher yield: Chain S
(IsSafeBuiltinTypeConversion matrix — F71 was one wrong cell, more cells likely) or Chain T
(homogeneous redecl HasDiff* nesting gaps mirroring F77).

- shape: implicit `long double -> double` narrowing → SOUND (rejected). /tmp/explorer_ld2d.*

- shape: explicit `(enum E)floatvar` — float→enum bypassing the float→int prohibition (line 903 guards isIntegerType() which is false for enum) → SOUND (rejected; the enum block at :983 else defaults IsSafeBehavior=false and nothing re-accepts a float source). /tmp/explorer_flt2enum.*
- shape: explicit `(int)floatvar` baseline → SOUND (rejected, rule "float→int forbidden even explicit"). /tmp/explorer_flt2int.*
- shape: `unsigned long long -> unsigned long` (same-width unsigned) → SOUND (accepted via fallback, not a FP). /tmp/explorer_ull2ul.*
- shape: `unsigned long -> unsigned long long` widen → SOUND (accepted, [ULongLong][ULong]=Y). /tmp/explorer_ul2ull.*
- shape: `short -> signed char` narrowing → SOUND (rejected). /tmp/explorer_s2c.*
RESULT: NO DISTINCT wrong cell found. The implicit-narrowing/sign-flip surface is
SATURATED at F71 (only unsound accept) and the enum→int surface at F51 (only
unsound accept). All 32-bit and same-width sign-flips OTHER than the 64-bit
[LongLong][ULong] are correctly rejected (value-range + signedness checks). The
float dimension, the value-range escape hatch (GetExprRange), and the
constant-fit path (prior PROBED-SOUND) all hold. Chain S → recommend SATURATED
@ 28656aa9 for unsound accepts; reopen only if the matrix/fallback/value-range is edited.
