Chain V → NOT yet saturated (one gap found; the predicate side hasBorrowFields is sound).

### 2026-05-30 — decl-site parallelism audit (follow-up to F81)
Enumerated all call sites of the owned/borrow placement gates and probed for site gaps:
- `CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType` + `CheckBorrowOrIndirectBorrowType` paired at: global-var (SemaDecl.cpp:8527/8528), union-field (SemaDecl.cpp:18687/18688). Array (SemaType.cpp:5202) = owned-only → **F81** (filed).
- No site has borrow-but-not-owned.
- **Static-local gap** (`SemaDecl.cpp:8525` `isFileContext()` guard): `static struct O{int*_Owned f;} so;` / `static struct B{int*_Borrow f;} sb;` / `static int*_Owned p` / `static int*_Borrow p` inside a function are ACCEPTED (file-scope equivalents REJECTED). → **F82 LOW, NOT FILED** — every safe-context use is backstopped (mutable-static-in-_Safe ban; static-const owned `int*_Owned`→`int*const _Owned` conversion ban; unsafe-ctx = unchecked). Decl-time inconsistency only. Don't re-probe.

### 2026-05-30 — owned/borrow predicate-PAIR site-parity audit (generalizing F81)
Enumerated every owned/borrow predicate pair + diffed call-site parity. Findings:
- CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType / CheckBorrowOrIndirectBorrowType: array site gap = F81 (filed); static-local context-guard gap = F82 (LOW, backstopped).
- CheckBorrowFunctionType (return-has-borrow ⇒ some-param-has-borrow, lifetime provenance): NO owned twin BY DESIGN (owned returns are freshly constructed). Coverage COMPLETE — bf_typedef / bf_param (fnptr-param) / bf_ret (fnptr-return) all REJECT the borrow-return-no-borrow-param shape; valid case accepts. SOUND.
- CheckNestedBorrowType (from BuildPointerType): borrow-specific; `int*_Borrow*` and `int*_Owned*` params both accepted (pointer-to-borrow/owned both legal indirection) — no exploitable asymmetry. SOUND.
- hasOwnedRetOrParams(:462) vs hasBorrowRetOrParams(:883): separate parallel checks (owned-fnptr-assign vs CheckBorrowFunctionType), not a shared-site asymmetry. F41 covers owned-fnptr-variance.
- isOwnedQualified/isBorrowQualified (86/75) + hasOwnedFields/hasBorrowFields (13/8): count mismatch is legitimate semantic difference, not a single-site gap.
Conclusion: F81 was the ONLY real owned/borrow site-parity gap. Audit closed; don't re-run the count-diff.

- shape: (b-struct) `s->f = &_Mut x` for `struct H *_Borrow s` → SEMA-BLOCKED (struct-with-borrow-field can't be `_Borrow`-qualified). /tmp/esc_b2.*.cbs, esc_b3.*.cbs

Byte-identical NESTED twin `{ int x; s->f=&_Mut x; }` → REJECTED. 2x2 {void|int return}×{top|nested} proves the discriminator is SCOPE NESTING (top accepted, nested rejected), NOT return type. /tmp/p1..p4.*.cbs, /tmp/F_escape_paramfield_static.cbs (FN), /tmp/F_escape_paramfield_baseline.cbs (one-line-diff reject)
- shape: local heap owned object, NESTED-local borrow into its field → CAUGHT (`inner does not live long enough`) — confirms intra-fn local-owned region IS tracked; the gap is specifically the unpinned DerefRN of an owned-indirection write for a frame-lived local. /tmp/loc.*.cbs
- shape: owned-RETURN twin of (b-owned) top-level local (`struct H*_Owned g(struct H*_Owned s){int x;s->f=&_Mut x;return s;}`) → CAUGHT (`x does not live long enough`) — PreprocessForParamAndReturn pins ParamRN to the capped FreeRN ONLY when ret-type tracked, so the owned-return path closes the top-level case; the void/non-tracked-return path leaves it open. /tmp/g1.*.cbs
- shape: runtime weaponization — g parks `&_Mut x` (g top-level local) into owned-param field, hands ownership back to main via `__move_to_raw`/`__take_from_raw` (in-scope plumbing), main reads `*h->f` after g frame reclaimed + clobbered → exit 1 / valgrind "uninitialised value created by a stack allocation" → CONFIRMED-new stack-use-after-return. /tmp/F_escape_param_field_uaf.cbs
RESULT: CONFIRMED-NEW root cause (pending main-thread Fxx). Region/lifetime inference accepts a borrow of a TOP-LEVEL fn-local stored into a `_Borrow` field reached through an `_Owned` indirection; the sink DerefRN ('region_0 in dump) is not pinned to the free/End region for param/owned-pointee writes, so frame-escape-via-aggregate is unmodeled. Root site: PopulateInference :1614-1622 (Assign DerefRN relation) + PreprocessForParamAndReturn :2170-2202 (pins ParamRN only, and only when ret-type tracked). DISTINCT from F11/F21/F24/F34/F38/F39/F42/F81/F82. HIGH.

### 2026-05-30 — borrow-checker ESCAPE-to-longer-lived surface — REJECTED (region inference SOUND)
Explorer claimed a HIGH frame-escape FN (b-owned: `s->f=&_Mut x` for `_Owned` param `s`, top-level `x`).
Main-thread re-validation REJECTED it:
- rv_A `g(struct H*_Owned s){int x=0; s->f=&_Mut x; safe_free(s);}` ACCEPTED but SOUND — safe_free(s) consumes s before top-level x dies, so the borrow is valid its whole life. /tmp/rv_A_run.cbs runs CLEAN (vg ERROR SUMMARY 0).
- rv_C (pure _Safe escape: `return s`) REJECTED "`x` does not live long enough" — region inference CATCHES the real frame-escape.
- The explorer's runtime UAF needed `__move_to_raw`/`__take_from_raw` (_Unsafe laundering) = intended escape hatch, not a region gap.
Escape forms (a)/(c)/(d)/(e) caught; (b-raw)/(b-struct) Sema-blocked; (b-owned) sound. Region/lifetime inference is SOUND for escape-to-longer-lived. Don't re-probe. Same over-claim shape as cast-borrow-drop / IsSafeFunctionPointerTypeCast.

- shape: nested raw `_ArrayElem` `int *_ArrayElem *_Borrow _ArrayElem` × {decl,typedef,cast,global} → ALL REJECT (BuildQualifiedType per-level) → NEGATIVE

BACKSTOPPED: va_start/va_arg are _Unsafe builtin calls (rejected in safe zone) → can't consume; decl-time inconsistency only, NOT _Owned/_Borrow soundness, NOT fileable (F82 shape) → NEGATIVE

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle (E1) — nullability × ownership cross-analyzer composition (move/nullptr/field) — NO-NEW
Full ledger: /tmp/probed_E1.md. Surface: a `_Owned _Nonnull`/`_Nullable` pointer tracked by BOTH
nullability (never cleared on move — no move concept) and ownership; after a shared mutation, do they
stay jointly sound? Compiler 28656aa9.
- shape: `sink(p)` move plain `_Owned _Nonnull`, then `*p` → REJECT use-of-moved (ownership independent) → SOUND
- shape: `p = nullptr` on `_Owned _Nonnull` → REJECT nonnull-by-nullable → SOUND
- shape: `_Nullable owned src` → `_Nonnull owned p` slot, `*p` → REJECT (same) → SOUND
- shape: move struct field `safe_free(s.f)` then `*s.f` → ACCEPT → FOLDED-F67 (deref-of-moved-field suffix-name gap)
- shape: WHOLE-STRUCT move `take(s)` then `*s.f` → ACCEPT, vg Invalid read → FOLDED-F67 (s.f IS Moved per P7b/P7c double-move/re-consume both REJECT; only the deref site slips — same checkSFieldUse gap; nullability NOT load-bearing)
- shape: move plain owned, `if(p){*p}` / `if(p!=null){*p}` deref in narrowed branch → REJECT use-of-moved → SOUND (ownership Moved survives narrowing)
- shape: move `_Nullable owned`, `if(p)` / `if(p!=null)` CONDITION only (no deref) → ACCEPT → INCONCLUSIVE-BENIGN (condition routes through MaybeSetNull/NullCheckInfo not checkOPSUse → use-of-moved bypassed for the condition expr; but only reads dangling-but-non-null BITS for a compare; non-condition `(p==null)?:` IS flagged — P14c vs P14b asymmetry; no deref enabled)
- shape: `safe_free(p)`, `if(p){return}`, re-`safe_free(p)` → ACCEPT compile but vg 0 errors → SOUND (MaybeSetNull resurrects Moved→Null via setToNull/resetAll on false edge + checkOPSUse:927 ignores Null for plain owned [OPSAllOwnedFields empty] → re-free accepted, BUT runtime if(p) is TRUE on post-free non-null bits → masked re-free unreached; false branch = originally-null = no-op free)
- shape: `_Nonnull owned`, deref on FALSE branch of `if(p)` / inside `if(p==nullptr)` → ACCEPT → SOUND (dead branch of if(_Nonnull); no pure-_Safe way to inject a null _Nonnull — nullptr/nullable→nonnull both rejected)
- control: direct double `safe_free(p)` no-if → REJECT invalid-cast-of-moved; re-move `sink(p);sink(p)` → REJECT use-of-moved (move tracking confirmed)
RESULT: NO-NEW. Composition jointly SOUND for move/nullptr/field @ 28656aa9. Nullability is structurally
never load-bearing for a moved pointer (no clear hook) → ownership's Moved state is the sole + robust guard.
Two structural smells (condition-use-of-moved bypass; MaybeSetNull Moved→Null resurrection + checkOPSUse:927
Null-ignore-for-plain-owned) do NOT compose into an exploit (benign-bits / runtime-non-null-bits mask).
DISTINCT from F18/F48/F50 (intra-nullability compound-assign launders) and F75 (ownership merge union).
Notes appended to BSCNullabilityCheck.md "E1 composition" section (state table). Reopen-if MaybeSetNull /
checkOPSUse:927 / nullability move-hook changes.

- shape: nested FieldPath `s->a.f` narrowed in one sibling, post-merge `*s->a.f` (R2-H) → REJECTED → SOUND (nested ME is top-level CFGStmt under linearization → initStatus pre-populates nested FP → mergeFP absent-branch dead; F26 stays DPVD-only)

and treated non-null (nullability getDefNullability→NonNull, deref accepted). Compiler 28656aa9, flag

- I2: non-_Safe struct `={0}` _Nonnull field deref REJECTED at decl-time → only the truly-UNINIT (no initializer) non-_Safe path slips.

DISTINCT from F18/F26/F48/F50/F66/F70 (all launder a nullable ORIGIN via an operation; here the slot is never initialized) and F78 (FP decl-time default-init vs this FN runtime-uninit-deref). - anon nested struct: `s.x=2; read s.y` (y uninit) — INCONCLUSIVE/SOUND-REJECT (tryPromoteParent counts anon-struct fields correctly) — 2026-05-30
- read/pass/addr-of `s.arr[c].q` & `&_Mut s.arr[c]` (s.arr uninit ptr-to-struct field) — INCONCLUSIVE/SOUND-REJECT (deepest [Field,Index,Field] read + AddressOf-of-Index base load both checked) — 2026-05-30
- CONCLUSION: NO new distinct root. F83's gap is WRITE/dest-side ONLY; the READ direction of an Index projection + aggregate-init zero-fill + tryPromoteParent over-promote are all SOUND. Ledger /tmp/probed_R3E3.md.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer R3 (E5) — SafeFeatureFinder gating / finder-skip false-negative hunt
Target: WalkerBSC.h SafeFeatureFinder + FindSafeFeatures gating of CFG borrow checker (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:279-411). Goal: pure-_Safe owned-leak/borrow-dangle accepted because finder fails to flag the fn -> ownership/borrow analysis skipped. Ledger /tmp/probed_R3E5.md.
- shape: flat `int *_Owned p` local leak (flagged baseline) -> REJECTED "memory leak of value: `p`" (baseline confirms gated path works)
- shape: `int *_Owned arr[2]` local leak -> SHAPE-REJECTED "type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned'" (array-element blind spot in hasOwnedFields is UNREACHABLE; Sema forbids owned arrays)
- shape: `struct Inner{int*_Owned p}; struct Outer{Inner a[2]}` local leak -> SHAPE-REJECTED "...even indirectly, 'struct Inner' contains '_Owned'" (indirect owned array also forbidden; baseline direct nested field IS rejected)
- shape: `typedef int*_Owned OPtr; OPtr p=...` leak -> SHAPE-REJECTED as walker gap (isOwnedQualified sees canonical owned through typedef -> finder flags it) -> REJECTED leak
- shape: fn-ptr local `int*_Owned (*fp)(int)=make; fp(c);` -> SHAPE-REJECTED "_Unsafe function call is forbidden in the safe zone"; confirmed ANY fn-ptr call forbidden in _Safe (owned-free `int(*fp)(int);fp(c);` also rejected). Fn-proto recursion blind spot UNREACHABLE for a leak.
- shape: discard owned call result `make(c);` no owned local/cast -> REJECTED "memory leak because temporary 'make(c)' is _Owned" (caught by NON-gated Sema::CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:534, not the gated analyzer). FOLDED — finder-skip doesn't hide this.
- shape: borrow via bare call `int*_Borrow<struct B> brw(...)` -> SHAPE-REJECTED (borrow-generic syntax); any borrow-qualified param/return flags VisitQualType param loop anyway.
Outcome: NO new pattern. Finder-skip false-negative thesis falsified for pure _Safe: the only NON-gated-reachable leak diagnostics (temp leak, assignment) fire regardless of flagging; every gated (BSCBorrowChecker) obligation requires a top-level owned/borrow-qualified Decl or &mut/&const operator that DOES flag the finder; array+fn-ptr blind spots are Sema-shape-rejected.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer R3E4 — borrow-checker × init-analysis composition (NO-NEW, SOUND)
Surface: read/write through a borrow of an UNINIT/MaybeInit/MOVED referent. Goal: pure-_Safe FN
(garbage read accepted) confirmed by vg --track-origins. Binary 28656aa9. Ledger /tmp/probed_R3E4.md.
Note sem_tests/code_notes/composition_borrow_init.md created (+ INDEX row).
STRUCTURAL: the borrow-CREATE site (`&_Mut x`/`&_Const x`) is itself a USE of the referent's
init-state at the correct granularity → it fires BEFORE any read-through-borrow → firewall, not gap.
- shape: `b=&_Mut x; *b=5; use(x)` (write-thru, x uninit) → REJECTED at `&_Mut x` (use-of-uninit). Write-thru does NOT credit init → an FP, masked by create error, NOT an FN. SOUND.
- shape: `&_Const x` of uninit x, read `*b` → REJECTED at `&_Const x`. Const symmetric to mut. SOUND.
- shape: `&_Mut x` of uninit x, borrow never used → REJECTED (create-as-use). SOUND.
- shape: `&_Mut s.b` (s.a init, s.b uninit), read thru → REJECTED FIELD-granular "use of uninitialized value: `s.b`". SOUND.
- shape: `&_Mut s` whole-struct borrow of partial struct → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: `s`" (whole borrow needs WHOLE init). SOUND.
- shape: `&_Mut o.in` nested partial struct field (o.in.y uninit) → REJECTED nested-granular. SOUND.
- shape: `if(c){s.b=2;} &_Const s.b` MaybeInit → REJECTED "use of possibly uninitialized value: `s.b`". SOUND.
- shape: `&_Mut s.a; *b=9; use(s.b)` (write thru a, read uninit b) → REJECTED on s.b (no field-launder). SOUND.
- shape: `&_Mut s.p[c]`/`&_Mut pa[c]` of uninit pointer (F83 Index proj on the Ref/AddressOf side) → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value". DISTINCT from F83 (F83 = dest-WRITE `s.p[c]=v` only; the `&_Mut` Ref form IS checked). NOT a new gap.
- shape: `consume(p); &_Const *p` borrow of moved owned → REJECTED "use of moved value". SOUND.
- shape: `b=&_Const *p; consume(p)` move/free while borrowed → REJECTED "cannot move out of `p` because it is borrowed". SOUND.
- shape: `b=&_Const s.b; s.b=99` write field while borrowed → REJECTED "cannot assign to `s.b` because it is borrowed". SOUND.
- shape: `consume(h.f); &_Const *h.f` borrow pointee of moved owned struct field → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: `h.f`". SOUND.
VERDICT: no-new-pattern. borrow×init composition SATURATED-SOUND @ 28656aa9. The borrow-create acts
as the init firewall at whole-local/field/nested/MaybeInit granularity; write-through-borrow neither
over- nor under-credits into an unsound accept; move/write-while-borrowed + borrow-of-moved all caught.
No vg run needed (every probe REJECTED at compile → no runtime garbage read reachable).
Reopen-if a commit touches the borrow-create init-use lowering (BSCIRBuilder Ref/AddressOf→Place
init check) or the `&_Mut`/`&_Const` Sema gate.

## 2026-05-30 bsc-explorer cycle R4E5 — `_Nullable` fnptr as CALLEE (call-vs-deref nullability gap)
Hypothesis: calling a `_Nullable` fnptr `fp()`/`s.fp()` without `if(fp)` guard accepted → null call/SIGSEGV; asymmetric with `*p` deref of `_Nullable` data ptr (rejected). Flag `-nullability-check=all`.
- shape: unguarded `(*fp)()` of _Nullable fnptr local → REJECT → SOUND control (F31 explicit-deref path checked)
- shape: `(*fp)()` AFTER `if(fp)` narrowing → ACCEPT → SOUND ("narrowing doesn't apply to callee" angle is NOT a bug)
- shape: unguarded `(*s.fp)()` _Nullable fnptr FIELD → REJECT → SOUND
- shape: explicit deref of ternary callee `(*(c?r:np))()` (nullable arm) → REJECT → SOUND
- shape: `(**pp)()` deref-of-ptr-to-nullable-fnptr → REJECT → SOUND
- shape: `(*get())()` call-result nullable fnptr → REJECT → SOUND
- shape: bare `fp()` of _Nullable local, unguarded → ACCEPT → FOLDED-F31 (liveness confirmed @28656aa9)
- shape: bare `s.fp()` field-call _Nullable fnptr, unguarded → ACCEPT → FOLDED-F31 (hint's `s->fp()` shape)
Outcome: NO-NEW. Only callee-nullability gap is the BARE-call form = F31 (filed, still live). Explicit-deref-callee path (UO_Deref/VisitUnaryOperator) COMPLETE across all nullable-callee shapes; narrowing correctly applied. Surface SATURATED @28656aa9; single fix (visit CE->getCallee() in VisitCallExpr) closes F31, no second gap behind it. Ledger /tmp/probed_R4E5.md. Note: code_notes/nullability_indirect_call.md.

Target: CGCall.cpp BSC arms (none) + CGExpr/CGExprAgg owned-arg/return emission. Hunt:
F60/F63 codegen-drop family applied to CALLS — checker-accepted _Safe program where
codegen drops/duplicates an _Owned call-arg/return move/free → vg leak/double-free.
- shape: direct `take(p)` move into consuming callee → 1a/1f vg CLEAN (SOUND)

  (plain non-_Safe fnptr indirect call REJECTED "_Unsafe call forbidden in safe zone")
- shape: conditional `take(c ? mk() : mk2())` both arms alloc → only-chosen-arm 1a/1f CLEAN
- shape: owned-struct (no member methods) returned by value then moved into callee → 1a/1f CLEAN
- shape: SafeExpr-wrapped scalar owned arg `take(_Safe(mk()))` → 1a/1f CLEAN
- shape: SafeExpr-wrapped AGGREGATE owned-struct arg → 1a/1f CLEAN (CGExprAgg::VisitSafeExpr patched)
- shape: two-owned-field struct by-value move (callee frees both) → 2a/2f CLEAN
- shape: nested owned-struct return directly into arg slot `take(mk())` → 1a/1f CLEAN
VERDICT: no-new-pattern. Owned-call-arg/return move-EMISSION surface CLOSED. CGCall.cpp has
NO ENABLE_BSC arm (ordinary C convention); CGDecl BSC arms are trait/method-only (OOS); no
implicit owned-drop synthesized. Every free = user's explicit lowered-once safe_free; aggregate
moves = bitwise memcpy with analyzer single-consume guarantee. Codegen neither drops nor
duplicates owned moves/frees on reachable in-scope shapes. Only codegen-drop residue = F60/F63/F69
SafeExpr-emitter family (F69 const-emitter open) — NOT a call-arg/return drop. Ledger /tmp/probed_R4E6.md;
note BSCIRBuilder.md "R4 call-arg owned codegen". Reopen-if a commit adds an ENABLE_BSC arm to
CGCall.cpp arg-emission or synthesizes implicit owned-drop cleanup in CGDecl/CGCleanup.

### 2026-06-04 — void-cast-owned subfield check, inner-expr shape coverage (Mode-1 from IsSafeConversion read)
- `(void*_Owned)outer`, outer = `int *_Owned *_Owned` (DeclRefExpr inner): **SOUND/rejected** —
  "invalid cast to `void * _Owned` of not all moved value: `outer`, *outer is _Owned"
  (BSCOwnership.cpp:2246 → checkCastOPS/checkCastBOP catch the inner _Owned).
- `int *_Owned *_Owned arr[1]` (array of outer-owned): **SHAPE-REJECTED** — "type of array
  cannot be qualified by '_Owned'". No array-of-owned, so ArraySubscriptExpr can never carry an
  owned value → the subscript path to the else-branch is unreachable.
- `(void*_Owned)mkNested()`, mkNested returns `int *_Owned *_Owned` (CallExpr inner):
  **CONFIRMED-F91** — compiles clean, valgrind 2 allocs / 1 free, inner safe_malloc<int> leaked
  (4 bytes definitely lost). VisitCStyleCastExpr else-branch (:2297) skips checkCast*.
- `(void*_Owned)(0, outer)` (comma/BinaryOperator inner, plain local): **CONFIRMED-F91** (same
  root) — same `outer` that the DeclRef form rejects; the comma wrapper makes the diag vanish →
  proves the check is gated on inner-expr SHAPE, not on whether the value still owns a subfield.
- IsSafePointerConversion (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:801-833) borrow path: **SOUND** — nested-borrow
  drop caught (SrcPointee==DstPointee distinguishes the inner borrow qual); borrow→void requires
  isTrivialDataType(). isBorrowArrayDecayTypeMatch (SemaExpr.cpp:545-576): **SOUND** — adds
  const/volatile/restrict only in safe direction, gates triviality on IsInSafeZone, rejects drops.

  out-param array.

### 2026-06-04 (cycle 13) — ensure_init contract enforcement (caller-trust × callee-obligation × type-match)
- decl+def both ensure_init, def doesn't init *p: **SOUND** — "'*p' not initialized at return in
  __attribute__((ensure_init)) function" (checkEnsureInitAtReturn :1140).
- decl WITH ensure_init, def WITHOUT: **SHAPE-REJECTED** — "conflicting types for 'f'" (ensure_init is part
  of the type; redecl must match → caller can't see an ensure_init prototype the def doesn't honor).
- => ensure_init contract sound; the only real hole is F73 (nested fnptr param, already filed). UNPROBED:
  C-style-cast fnptr ensure_init (likely F73/F76 fold); non-plain-Copy delegation (false-positive only).

- F93 (borrow action launder through comma) = F11 exactly: same file/function (ActionExtract::VisitBinaryOperator omits BO_Comma), same fix, same non-heap repro. F11 also already covers the DefUse twin. RETRACTED; folded into F11. Lesson: the F93 "distinct-from" check grepped F91/F92/F24 but not F11. Runtime-UAF repro salvaged onto F11.

F92's distinct-from line *listed* F18 but wrongly tagged it 'path-state/narrowing' — it is the identical opcode-switch hole. RETRACTED; folded into F18 (its direct-deref + runtime-SIGSEGV repro strengthens F18's HIGH argument).
- LESSON (2 dups this session: F92→F18, F93→F11): the "distinct-from" gate must re-read the cited prior finding's ROOT CAUSE, not trust its one-line category. Both misses were 'cited the bug, didn't read it'.

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp IsSafePointerConversion
- shape: `int *_Borrow → const void *_Borrow` cast in `_Safe` → CONFIRMED-FP-LOW. Rejected "conversion ... to 'const void *_Borrow' is forbidden in the safe zone" while the non-const `void *_Borrow` form IS accepted. Adding const is a safe widening; const-void permits strictly fewer ops than the allowed void → over-strict. Root: IsSafePointerConversion :822 `DstPointeeIsConst == SrcPointeeIsConst` blocks add-const on the trivial→void path. LOW (rare construct, trivial workaround `void*_Borrow`). NOT filed (LOW). probe: probes/safezone_borrow_to_const_void_cast.cbs

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp operator&=/operator|=
- shape: `if(p||q) *p;` (p,q `int*_Borrow _Nullable`, -nullability-check=all) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. `||` correctly REJECTS the deref ("nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced"); control `if(p&&q) *p;` correctly ACCEPTS. operator|= INTERSECT (no spurious narrow) + operator&= UNION (both narrowed) sound for the core contract. probe: probes/null_or_combine_no_narrow.cbs
- candidate 2 (semanticUnion/Intersect semantic-equality mis-id, F42/path-identity alias) + candidate 3 (invert() else-branch) remain UNPROBED — higher bug-likelihood than the combine arithmetic itself.

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp semanticEqual/getSemanticExprID
- shape: cross-object field narrow — `if(s1.f) *s2.f;` (struct S{int*_Borrow _Nullable f} by value, distinct bases s1/s2) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. `*s2.f` correctly REJECTED ("nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced"); same-object control `if(s1.f)*s1.f` ACCEPTED. getSemanticExprID distinguishes same-field/different-base → no conflation. DISTINCT from F33 (deref-base (*X).f collision) — arrow/dot DeclRef bases are keyed correctly. probe: probes/null_crossobject_field_narrow.cbs

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of WalkerBSC.h BSCFeatureFinder::VisitType ArrayType gap
- shape: owned array `int *_Owned arr[N]` (direct or via struct-with-owned-field) → SHAPE-REJECTED ("type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned' (even indirectly)"). BSC Sema forbids owned arrays, so the VisitType-ArrayType gap is unreachable for owned.
- shape: borrow array `int *_Borrow arr[2]` (only BSC syntax in the fn) under -rewrite-bsc → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Rewriter correctly strips _Borrow (emitted .c has no _Borrow, re-parses clean as C). Detection happens via a path OTHER than VisitType-ArrayType (likely isBorrowQualified/hasBorrowFields on the array type before VisitType is reached). NOT a F59-style leak. probe: probes/walker_array_owned_rewrite_leak.cbs (+ /tmp/barr3.cbs).
- Conclusion: BSCFeatureFinder ArrayType gap is NOT exploitable (owned forbidden; borrow detected). VisitType's missing ConstantArrayType case is dead w.r.t. soundness.

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of TypeBSC.cpp RecordType::hasOwnedFields array-element gap
- shape: `struct Outer { struct Inner arr[2]; }` (Inner has `int *_Owned p`) → SHAPE-REJECTED. The array-of-owned-struct FIELD is rejected ("type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned' (even indirectly), 'struct Inner' contains '_Owned' type"), identical to the local-array form. So the array-qualification Sema gate is ROBUST and uses a CORRECT recursive owned-check independent of hasOwnedFields' BFS.
- Consequence: hasOwnedFields' missing ArrayType-element unwrap (TypeBSC.cpp:446 getAs<RecordType> is null for arrays) is DEAD w.r.t. owned content — owned-containing arrays can never be declared, so the gap is never exercised with owned. Not exploitable. (Candidate 2 `&&!isOwnedStructureType` :441 asymmetry is _Owned-struct territory = OUT OF SCOPE.) probe: probes/typebsc_array_of_owned_struct_field.cbs

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of SemaBSCOwnership.cpp CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType — CONFIRMED-F95
- shape: `static struct S s; s.p=safe_malloc<int>(1);` (S{int*_Owned p}) → CONFIRMED-F95 (HIGH silent leak). Compiles clean; valgrind 2 allocs/0 frees. `static` specifier defeats owned-field leak analysis AND CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType has no static-local call site. Differential: plain local → "field memory leak" REJECTED; global → CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType REJECTED. Distinct from F80 (predicate pointee-chain gap) and F78 (nullability FP). repro/F95_static_local_owned_field_leak.cbs.
- BOOKKEEPING: the earlier "candidate F95" (owned-temp `&&` leak, 2026-06-08 SemaExpr owned-binop entry) is RENUMBERED to "candidate (pending dedup vs F22/F47, no F# assigned)" — F95 reassigned to this confirmed static-local bug.

### 2026-06-08 — BSCBorrowChecker static-local borrow (F95 sibling-analysis) — FOLDED-F95
- shape: NON-safe `int g(int x){ static int *_Borrow p=nullptr; if(p) r=*p; p=&_Mut x; }` → FOLDED-F95. Compiles clean; runtime *p reads a dead stack slot on re-entry (use-after-scope). Same root as F95: the global-style declaration gate (CheckBorrowOrIndirectBorrowType, the borrow twin of CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType) is NOT called for static-storage locals; file-scope `int*_Borrow q;` IS rejected. Controls: direct return-borrow blocked by signature rule (needs _Borrow param), global-borrow blocked at decl — so the dangling store is reachable ONLY via the static-local gap. In _Safe, `static int*_Borrow` is rejected ("mutable global in safe zone") so this is non-safe-only.
- Classification FOLD (not new F): the fix that closes F95 (run static-local VarDecls through the global-site gates) closes this too, since the global site calls BOTH owned+borrow gates. Strengthens F95: same root → leak (owned) AND dangling-borrow/UAF (borrow). probe: probes/static_local_dangling_borrow.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of BSCIRBuilder.cpp lowerStmt dispatch completeness
- shape: uninit var as `__asm__` input operand → SHAPE-REJECTED. The Nop fallback (:345) only drops GCCAsmStmt + IndirectGotoStmt. But in _Safe (the only zone where init analysis flags uninit — confirmed: `_Safe int f(){int x; return x;}` → "use of uninitialized value: x"; non-safe default does NOT flag it), `asm` is rejected upfront ("asm statement is forbidden in the safe zone"). IndirectGotoStmt = GNU computed-goto = OUT OF SCOPE. So the Nop fallback is DEAD w.r.t. in-scope soundness. lowerStmt dispatch is COMPLETE for in-scope statements (F52 AttributedStmt now handled :333). probe: /tmp/b3.cbs.
- Note: there is no `-uninit-check` flag (unknown argument); init analysis is zone-gated (runs in _Safe, not non-safe-by-default). Relevant for future init-analysis probes — they must be in _Safe.

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of SemaChecking.cpp raw-transfer builtins (__move_to_raw/__take_from_raw)
- shape: `__take_from_raw` / `__move_to_raw` in `_Safe` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Both forbidden ("__move_to_raw is forbidden in the safe zone", "__take_from_raw is forbidden in the safe zone") — the ownership-forging primitives are correctly _Unsafe-gated, so _Safe code cannot forge/duplicate ownership via the raw bridge. In _Unsafe, double-take→double-free is by-design (user asserts ownership). probe: /tmp/raw1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — Mode-1 read of BSCBorrowChecker.cpp CheckMove (move-while-borrowed)

With the moved value FREED (no leak): "cannot move out of `a`/`s` because it is borrowed" correctly fires (both the direct-borrow and prefix-borrow cases). CheckMove(FindLoansThatIntersect) handles path/subpath/prefix per its contract.
- IMPORTANT diagnostic-ordering caveat (cost me 3 probes): when a function has BOTH an owned-LEAK and a move-while-borrowed error, ONLY the leak is reported — the borrow diagnostic is preempted. Benign (function rejected either way, exit=1), but means borrow-conflict probes MUST free the moved value to avoid masking the borrow diagnostic. probe: /tmp/mv9.cbs, /tmp/mv10.cbs.

Both-branches-free control ACCEPTS. The SCALAR ownership merge (SStatus) MEETs correctly in all directions. - shape: N owned locals (alloc+free each), N=25→200, wall-clock → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. 0.03→0.05s, ratios ~1.0-1.3 (dominated by fixed overhead, well under 2× linear / 4× super-linear). Ownership tracking is LINEAR in owned-local count — no complexity blowup. The O(N²) complexity bug is SPECIFIC to the borrow checker (region inference / FindLoansThatIntersect), not the ownership analyzer. probe: /tmp/oc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — RE-VALIDATION: complexity-oracle O(N²) find does NOT reproduce (RETRACTED)
- The `analyzer-complexity-blowup-timing` oracle (round 12) reported O(N²) borrow-checker growth (4.3-4.5× at N=256→512). Conductor re-validation: THREE independent reconstructions — (a) chained shared reborrows N=64..512, (b) N sequential if-branches over a live borrow N=100..800, (c) N distinct loans × N accesses N=50..400 — are ALL FLAT (~0.02-0.03s wall, ratios ~1.0-1.5, dominated by fixed compile overhead). No super-linear growth. The oracle's signal was measurement NOISE at sub-second times (wall-clock ratios unreliable when absolute times are ~0.1s; the C-baseline subtraction amplifies noise). → O(N²) find RETRACTED (not filed). The complexity-timing oracle is downgraded: its sole find was spurious. Workable oracles drop 3→2 (boundary-bisection F96, diagnostic-spec F94 remain solid). probe: /tmp/rb,rb2,rb3.cbs.

- shape: assign nullptr or _Nullable to a `_Nonnull` borrow → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Bare `p=nullptr`, `p=q`(nullable), comma `p=(0,nullptr)`, paren `p=(nullptr)`, ternary `p=c?q:nullptr` ALL REJECTED "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable pointer". The assignment-nullability check strips paren/comma/ternary wrappers — C1 wrapper-strip class does NOT apply. probe: /tmp/nz1-5.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 bsc-explorer cycle — ensure_init_if_ret caller-side propagation (all SOUND, no new pattern)
Read BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp transferStatement Assign (:254-371) + transferTerminator Call/SwitchInt (:421-672) + checkEnsureInitIfRetAtReturn (:1608-1697) + SemaBSCOwnership.cpp CheckEnsureInitFunctionPointerType (:927-988). Note in BSCIRInitAnalysis.md.
- shape: bool-stored compare `int a=(r==0); if(a) use x` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (CLEAN; richer-than-4-forms, a==(r==0), credit sound).
- shape: compound `if(r==0 && c) use x` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (REJECTED "possibly uninitialized"; conservative).
- shape: compound `if(r==0 || c) use x` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (REJECTED; the || true-edge correctly does NOT prove r==0).
- shape: inverted bool `int a=(r==0); a=!a; if(a) use x` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (REJECTED; invalidateLocal drops the fact on `a` reassign).
- shape: comma addr-of arg `try_init((0, &_Mut x)); if(r==0) use x` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Sema WARNS "effect cannot be verified" yet dataflow STILL credits x -> CLEAN. NOT an FN: borrow genuinely reaches x (verified comma-addresses-z-read-x errors on x; no-if comma errors on x). Benign Sema-warning vs dataflow-credit inconsistency; the credit is the sound answer.
- shape: paren addr-of `try_init((&_Mut x))` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (CLEAN, correct).
- shape: ternary-selected addr-of `try_init(c ? &_Mut x : &_Mut z)` -> SHAPE-REJECTED (borrow-of-uninit in ternary rejected at the `&_Mut` site; no over-credit).
- shape: fnptr variance via struct-field init / array-element init / return-stmt of plain fn into contract-fnptr -> all FOLDED-into-F73-family (CheckEnsureInitFunctionPointerType routes through CheckAssignmentConstraints for these conversions too; all correctly ERROR).
- shape: delegation cond-value mismatch `inner ret(0); if(r==5) return 0;` -> INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (correctly ERRORS; guard value must match inner cond; baseline `if(r==0)` clean).
- shape: pointer-to-(contract fnptr) assignment `FP0* = FP_PLAIN*` -> FOLDED-into-F74-area but caught EARLIER by the IsSafeConversion safe-zone gate ("conversion forbidden in the safe zone"); NOT a F74-analog hole for this attribute. SOUND.
Conclusion: ensure_init_if_ret caller-side propagation + SwitchInt edge classification + comparison-fact value/eq matching + addr-of arg decoding + fnptr variance + delegation cond-composition are ALL SOUND. Dense soundness comments + dense regression corpus. No new root cause. Surface CLOSED.

### 2026-06-08 (post-rebuild) — union write-small-read-large tail (resumed candidate)
- shape: write small union member `u.a`, read larger `u.big.z` in `_Safe` → SHAPE-REJECTED on the REBUILT binary. Union field access in `_Safe` now errors "access to union field is _Unsafe and requires _Unsafe block" (the rebuild's union-safezone-rule change). So the whole-local-init heuristic FN (:180-188) is unreachable from _Safe; _Unsafe union access is by-design unchecked. Candidate closed.
- IMPLICATION: union-in-_Safe is now forbidden → re-validate filed union-in-_Safe bugs (F42 borrow-alias, F78 static-union-nullability) — they may now be FIXED/unreproducible.

### 2026-06-08 (post-rebuild) — RE-VALIDATION of session finds + union bugs against new binary
- **F94** (owned-binop dup diag): STILL reproduces (2 "invalid operands" errors).
- **F95** (static-local owned leak): STILL reproduces (compiles clean = silent leak, exit=0).
- **F96** (depth=10 cap leak): STILL reproduces (compiles clean = silent leak, exit=0).
- **F42** (union borrow alias): STILL reproduces — repro is NON-_Safe (0 _Safe), so the new union-in-_Safe restriction doesn't touch it; aliased union borrows still accepted (exit=0).
- **F78** (static union _Nonnull FP): STILL reproduces — the FP error "type contains nonnull pointer must be properly initialized" still fires.
- DOCUMENTED RULE (not a new/quirk behavior — bsc-safe-zone skill 1.0.1 "Union rules" + errors/safe-zone.md): "access to union field is _Unsafe and requires _Unsafe block" — fires for BOTH direct read/write (`u.a=5`) AND borrow (`&_Mut u.a`) of a union field inside `_Safe`. Non-_Safe union access is unaffected. → the union write-small-read-large tail candidate (BSCIRInitAnalysis :180-188) is SHAPE-REJECTED in _Safe; non-_Safe doesn't run the uninit check (init analysis zone-gated). Candidate CLOSED.
- ensure_init_if_ret feature: SOUND (explorer, 11 probes — see BSCIRInitAnalysis.md notes).

### 2026-06-08 — boundary-sweep: ensure_init_if_ret path-count verification (new feature, scale)
- shape: N conditional inits of `*out` then `return 0` (uninit on the all-false path) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. "may not be initialized on all paths at return" fires at all N=2,8,16,32,64 — no path-count cap (unlike ownership depth=10/F96). The new ensure_init_if_ret verifier scales correctly. Combined with the explorer's 11-probe form audit → feature is robustly SOUND on both correctness and scale. probe: /tmp/ei.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — C1 SafeExpr-peel coverage on move-tracking (F65/F46 sibling)
- shape: move via `_Safe(p)` (SafeExpr-wrapped source), then use p → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. "use of moved value: p" caught (same as bare `q=p`). The move-consume tracks the underlying owned value regardless of the SafeExpr wrapper (move happens at evaluation, not via path-string strip), so the ~20 plain-IgnoreParenImpCasts move sites aren't SafeExpr-launderable. F65's narrowing-site peel was the soundness-relevant one. Void-owned cast of _Safe(p) folds into F91 (else-branch). Remaining UNPROBED-future: SafeExpr at the nullability-cast site (BSCNullabilityCheck:290/710) + borrow-checker cast sites (:483/506) — but those affect narrowing/precision, not a clear soundness FN. probe: /tmp/se0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — C1 SafeExpr-peel on nullable-deref check (closes the flagged sites)
- shape: `*(_Safe(p))` deref of a SafeExpr-wrapped _Nullable borrow → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" caught (same as bare `*p`). The deref check keys on the OPERAND TYPE (_Nullable), which SafeExpr preserves → no launder. CONCLUSION on SafeExpr-peel coverage: the ~20 plain-IgnoreParen sites are SafeExpr-sound because their checks are TYPE- or EVALUATION-based; F65 was the lone soundness-relevant site (NARROWING keys on path-string IDENTITY, which SafeExpr changes — that's why only it needed the IgnoreParen*Safe peel). No sibling FN. probe: /tmp/sn0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret caller core contract (unconditional / wrong-value use)
- shape: after `try_init(&_Mut x)` (ensure_init_if_ret(0)), use x WITHOUT a ret-check, and use x after `if(r==1)` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Both REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: x"; positive `if(r==0){use x}` ACCEPTS. The caller-side propagation credits x init ONLY on the ret==0 branch — no over-crediting FN. This is the feature's most important soundness property and it holds. Feature now vetted from 3 angles (explorer forms, scale-sweep, core-contract) → robustly SOUND. probe: /tmp/ec0-2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret early-return inversion (control-flow sensitivity)
- shape: `if(r!=0) return; use x` and `if(r==0) return; use x` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. First ACCEPTS (fall-through r==0 → x init); second REJECTS "use of uninitialized value: x" (fall-through r!=0 → x NOT credited). The caller-side propagation is control-flow-sensitive through early-returns in BOTH directions — credits x only where r==0 dominates the use. Feature vetted from 4 angles now (explorer forms, scale-sweep, core-contract, early-return inversion) → robustly SOUND, well-engineered. probe: /tmp/er0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — BSCIRBuilder VisitExpr dispatch completeness
- shape: uninit x in ternary arm `c?x:0` + compound-assign `y+=x` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Both REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: x" — ConditionalOperator + CompoundAssignOperator lowering track operand uses. The expr dispatch (BSCIRBuilder:726-1313) is COMPLETE for in-scope C (all std expr kinds have Visit*); the VisitStmt→Nop fallback (:1311) catches only OOS GNU (StmtExpr/_Generic/ChooseExpr/BinaryConditionalOp)/C++/vector exprs. Parallel to lowerStmt (sound). probe: /tmp/be1-2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret negative cond value (wide-return sibling)
- shape: `ensure_init_if_ret(-1)` with `if(ret==-1){use x}` vs `if(ret==0){use x}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. First ACCEPTS (x credited on ret==-1), second REJECTS "use of uninitialized value: x" (x not credited on wrong value 0). Negative cond value matched exactly — no sign/value confusion (the wide-return-fix area is robust for negatives too). Feature now SOUND from 5 angles (forms, scale, core-contract, early-return inversion, neg-cond). probe: /tmp/en0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret RUNTIME soundness (capstone)
- shape: verified callee (inits *out + returns 0 when ok) + gated caller (reads x only in ret==0 branch), driven down both paths under valgrind → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Compiles clean; valgrind 0 errors/0 contexts. run(1)=42 (x init read), run(0)=-1 (x never read). Compile-time soundness MATCHES runtime — the if(r==0) gate genuinely prevents the uninit read. Feature SOUND from 6 angles now (forms, scale, core-contract, early-return inversion, neg-cond, RUNTIME). Exhaustively vetted; robustly sound at compile-time AND runtime. probe: /tmp/eirt.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — borrow lifetime propagation through a function call
- shape: `b = get(&_Mut x)` (get returns its borrow param), then write `x` while b is live → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. "cannot assign to x because it is borrowed" REJECTED; no-write control ACCEPTS. The region inference correctly ties the returned borrow's lifetime to x through the call — a borrow returned from a function retains its constraint on the original. probe: /tmp/brc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — returned borrow from either of two params (conservative region union)
- shape: `r = pick(&_Mut x, &_Mut y)` (returns `c?a:b`), then write x / write y while r live → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. BOTH rejected ("cannot assign to x/y because it is borrowed"). The region inference conservatively ties the returned borrow to BOTH possible source params through the call — no FN from picking only one source. Robust. probe: /tmp/pk1-2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — array element init tracking
- shape: read `arr[1]` after only `arr[0]=1` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Fully-init `int arr[3]={0}` control ACCEPTS. No element-tracking FN; conservative (all-or-nothing) which is the documented precision tradeoff, not a soundness gap. probe: /tmp/ar0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret × array-element out-param
- shape: `try_init(&_Mut arr[0])` then read arr[0] / arr[1] → INCONCLUSIVE. BOTH rejected "use of uninitialized value: arr". SOUNDNESS sound: arr[1] (not passed) correctly stays uninit — NO FN. Notably arr[0] (the passed element) is ALSO rejected — the contract does NOT credit a single array element (all-or-nothing array rule, unlike struct fields which ARE tracked field-by-field per the caller-field test). That's a precision over-conservatism (contract useless for array elements) but consistent with the documented array model → LOW, not fileable. probe: /tmp/ax0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret cond value vs return-type range (_Bool + cond 2)

The credit is keyed on literal value match, sound regardless of representability. Feature SOUND from 9 angles. probe: /tmp/br0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — __assume_initialized escape
- shape: `_Unsafe { __assume_initialized(&x); }` then read x in _Safe → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. ACCEPTED (exit=0); the escape suppresses the uninit check and the assumption propagates across the _Unsafe boundary into the _Safe analysis (as documented). __assume_initialized REQUIRES raw `&x` ("requires '&' expression as argument") so it's _Unsafe-only; the _Safe `&x`/`&_Mut x` forms are SHAPE-REJECTED (& forbidden in _Safe / requires &-not-borrow). probe: /tmp/ai3.cbs.

- shape: `_Unsafe{ __assume_initialized(&s.a); }` then read s.a vs s.b → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. s.a (assumed) ACCEPTS; s.b (not assumed) REJECTS "use of uninitialized value: s.b". The escape is FIELD-GRANULAR (assumes only s.a, not the whole struct) — no FN. probe: /tmp/ag0-1.cbs.

- shape: `struct B{int*_Borrow f;} make(int x){ return (B){&_Mut x}; }` (no borrow param) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. - shape: return a struct with a `_Borrow` field nested 2 AND 4 levels deep, no borrow param → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. BOTH rejected "return type is not allowed to be _Borrow qualified". ### 2026-06-08 — narrowing survives unrelated call (precision)
- shape: `if(p){ noop(); return *p; }` (noop takes no args) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. *p ACCEPTED — narrowing of p survives a call that can't touch p (precise, not over-conservative). F87 is the orthogonal SOUNDNESS direction (narrowing wrongly surviving a mutating call). probe: /tmp/nc0.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — RESOLVED: `&&`/`||`/`,` owned-temp leak (the earlier "pending-dedup F95-candidate")
- shape: `mk() && c` (mk returns _Owned) — owned temp as LHS of `&&`, discarded → CONFIRMED leak (compiles clean BOTH as statement AND as bound init `int x = mk()&&c`; valgrind 1 alloc/0 frees). Mechanism: owned-binop gate (DiagnoseOwnedPointerBinaryOp) EARLY-RETURNS for BO_LAnd/LOr/Comma (no error), AND the operand leak-check at SemaExpr.cpp:16555 is gated `if(isComparisonOp(Opc))` → &&/||/comma operands never leak-checked; the F14 temp-leak-recursion would also catch it.
- CLASSIFICATION: FOLDED into the owned-temp-leak family (F14) — the 16556 isComparisonOp gate is the specific too-narrow site; F14's recursive-walker fix closes it. DISTINCT from F40 (F40 = the FALSE-POSITIVE direction, `c && consume(p)` wrongly-rejected via Prologue hoisting; mine = the FN leak direction, produced-temp discarded). Resolves the earlier 2026-06-08 "candidate, pending dedup vs F22/F47" → NOT a separate filing; the maintainer's F14/owned-temp-leak fix should extend the 16556 gate beyond isComparisonOp. repro: probes/owned_temp_logical_comma_binop_leak.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — F97 CANDIDATE: element-wise array init FALSE POSITIVE (precision)
- shape: `int arr[3]; arr[0]=1; arr[1]=2; arr[2]=3; return arr[0];` AND loop form `while(i<3){arr[i]=i*n;i++;}` → both REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: arr". Root: BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:170-171/:219 all-or-nothing array model (element stores never mark array init, even constant-index full coverage). UNDOCUMENTED (source comment only; not in user manual). Not previously filed.

NOT auto-filed — surfaced to user for file/skip decision (per-batch approval doesn't auto-extend; outward-facing). ### 2026-06-08 — F97 RETRACTED (was wrongly called undocumented; it IS documented)
- CORRECTION: the element-wise-array-init rejection is NOT a bug — it's documented in BiShengCLanguageUserManual.md §3.7.3 rule 6 (line 5794), with `rule6_error` byte-identical to the repro AND `rule6_struct_error` for the struct-array-field case. The {}-init / __assume_initialized requirement is the documented design. My "not in the user manual" claim was wrong (narrow English grep vs Chinese manual). Umbrella comment deleted (id 50173732 → 204/404). bug_log F97 marked RETRACTED. SHAPE-REJECTED (documented intended behavior). LESSON: verify "undocumented" claims against the manual in its own language before filing an FP.

### 2026-06-08 — init-analysis nested depth vs documented rule 8 (spec-conformance)
- shape: depth-15 single-field-nested struct, leaf init vs uninit, use whole struct → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (rule-8 conformant). Leaf-init ACCEPTS (exit=0; arbitrary-depth tracking, all-leaves-init auto-promotes — matches §3.7.3 rule 8 "任意深度"); uninit-leaf REJECTS "use of uninitialized value: o" (sound). The init analysis has NO depth cap — F96's depth=10 is OWNERSHIP-(initS)-specific, a separate pass. (Productive use of the F97 lesson: documented §3.7 rules as a conformance oracle.) probe: /tmp/nd.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret fnptr arg-MISMATCH (rule 5 conformance)
- shape: `FP0`(expects arg=0) `g = bar2`(has arg=2) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "incompatible function pointer types: target expects ensure_init_if_ret(0) on parameter 0 but source has (2)"; same-arg(0==0) control ACCEPTS. Rule 5's arg-match clause enforced by VALUE, not just presence. No launder FN. probe: /tmp/fa0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret rule 6 (redecl) + call-end direct-pass conformance
- rule 6: `goo(arg=0)` then `goo(arg=2)` redecl → REJECTED "conflicting ensure_init_if_ret on parameter 'p' in redeclaration". Conformant.
- call-end direct-pass (doc `baz`): `int*_Borrow p=&_Mut a; bar(p)` (indirect, a uninit) → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a" (×2: the &_Mut of uninit a not directly passed + the later read). Only DIRECT pass gets the uninit-addr exemption. Conformant — matches doc baz exactly. probe: /tmp/rc0-1.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret transitive delegation (callee-side verify)
- shape: `outer`(ensure_init_if_ret 0) inits *out by delegating to `inner`(ensure_init_if_ret 0), returns 0 after `if(r==0)` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (no FP). ACCEPTED — the inner contract propagates both at the call site AND into outer's return verification. Non-trivial; well-engineered. probe: /tmp/td.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret transitive delegation soundness twin
- shape: `outer_bad` returns 0 unconditionally after `inner` (which inits *out only on its ret==0) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "'*out' not initialized at return in ensure_init_if_ret(0) function". Callee-side verify tracks *out's init through the inner contract — credits only on inner's ret==0 path. Transitive delegation sound in BOTH directions (no FP positive + no FN soundness). probe: /tmp/td2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret on an _Owned/owned-containing out-param TARGET → SHAPE-REJECTED (intended)
- shape: out-param target = `int *_Owned *_Borrow` or `struct Box{int*_Owned a} *_Borrow` → caller `&_Mut p`/`&_Mut b` of the uninit owned-containing var REJECTED ("use of uninitialized value: p" / "use of partially moved value: b, b.a is moved").
- VERDICT: NOT a bug. The feature's own tests (Positive/.../owned-cleanup-ok.cbs) use a PLAIN `int *out` ensure_init_if_ret param alongside a SEPARATE by-value owned param needing drop-cleanup — the out-param TARGET is always plain data. Owned-containing out-param targets aren't the use case; the caller rejection is the OWNERSHIP analysis correctly refusing to borrow an uninit/moved owned (separate analysis from the init exemption, not overridden by it). SHAPE-REJECTED. (F97 discipline: read the feature's intended-use tests before judging — this is intended, not a gap.)

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret multi-param per-param different arg
- shape: `f2(a:ret==0, b:ret==1)`; on `if(r==0)` read a then b → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. EXACTLY one error: b rejected "use of uninitialized value: b" (b needs ret==1); a accepted. Each param credited only on its OWN arg's branch — independent per-param contract tracking, no FN/FP. probe: /tmp/mp.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret comparison-value invalidation
- shape: `r=f(&a); r=0; if(r==0){use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. "use of uninitialized value: a" REJECTED — reassigning r breaks the link to the actual return, so a is NOT credited. The credit is tied to the real return value flowing into the comparison, not any var that equals the arg. No stale-r FN. probe: /tmp/ci.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret callee re-points out before init (ptr-aliased)
- shape: `out=other; *out=1; return 0;` (re-point before init) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED with precise diagnostic "ensure_init_if_ret(0) not satisfied: 'out' is reassigned before '*out' is initialized, so '*out' is not guaranteed initialized when returning 0". Tracks out-param identity; re-point-before-init detected. No FN. probe: /tmp/pa.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret switch-on-return (documented limitation)
- shape: `switch(r){case 0:{use a}}` vs `if(r==0){use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. switch-form REJECTS "use of uninitialized value: a" (real over-conservatism: case 0 IS the ret==0 branch); if-form ACCEPTS. Switch-specific, clean (no crash). This is the DOCUMENTED limitation (caller-controlflow.cbs:98 "ERROR (documented limitation): switching on the return value directly") → NOT fileable; the if-form is the working pattern. probe: /tmp/sw2-3.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret unsupported || compound form (soundness)
- shape: `if(r==0 || c){use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "use of POSSIBLY uninitialized value: a" — the || branch is enterable via c with r!=0, so a is path-sensitively possibly-uninit. The analyzer does NOT naively credit on seeing r==0 in the condition; requires the branch entered only-when-r==arg. No FN. (Precise "possibly" vs "definitely" diagnostic.) probe: /tmp/or.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret cross-call return-value association
- shape: `r1=f(&a); r2=f(&b); if(r1==0){use a; use b}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. EXACTLY one error: b rejected "use of uninitialized value: b"; a accepted. Each out-param associates with ITS OWN call's return value (a<->r1, b<->r2); if(r1==0) credits only a. No cross-call FN. ~20 angles on this feature now, all sound. probe: /tmp/xc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret callee return-via-variable (soundness)
- shape: `result=0; if(cond)*out=1; return result;` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED with explanatory diagnostic "return value is not an integer constant, so the runtime value may equal the cond value — '*out' must be initialized on this path". Non-constant return → conservative: requires *out init (can't prove return≠arg). Deliberate soundness-boundary engineering. No FN. ~21 angles on the feature, all sound. probe: /tmp/rv.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret -rewrite-bsc preservation (rewriter surface)
- shape: `-cc1 -rewrite-bsc` on decl+def with ensure_init_if_ret(0)/(2) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Both attributes preserved in output (2/2, arg values 0/2 correct): `int try_init(__attribute__((ensure_init_if_ret(0))) int * out)`. No F54-style drop. Attribute reordered before the type (cosmetic, semantically equivalent). The rewriter handles the new attribute. ~22 angles on the feature (analyzer+rewriter), all sound. probe: /tmp/rw.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret credit through fnptr indirect call
- shape: `FP fp` (type carries ensure_init_if_ret(0)); `fp(&_Mut a); if(r==0){use a}` vs unconditional `use a` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. EXACTLY one error (unconditional use rejected); the if(r==0) form ACCEPTS. The caller-side credit propagates through the FUNCTION-POINTER TYPE's contract at the indirect call site — credited on ret==0, not unconditionally. No FP/FN. ~23 angles on the feature, all sound. probe: /tmp/fp.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret under -uninit-check=all (non-_Safe mode)
- shape: plain (non-_Safe) `f(&a)` with -uninit-check=all; if(r==0){use a} vs unconditional → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. EXACTLY one error (unconditional rejected); credit works in non-_Safe mode too. Consistent with _Safe behavior. ~24 angles total on ensure_init_if_ret, ZERO defects — CONCLUSIVELY verified across: _Safe+non-Safe modes, direct+indirect(fnptr) calls, callee-verify, all comparison forms, scale, delegation, cross-call/multi-param association, ptr-aliasing, cmp-invalidation, rewriter, sema rules 1-7. probe: /tmp/uc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — narrowing survives call that mutates a GLOBAL field → FOLDED-F87 (fix-scope caveat)
- shape: `if(gs.f){ *gs.f; clear_global(); *gs.f; }` where clear_global nulls the GLOBAL gs.f (gs NOT passed as arg) → FN (2nd *gs.f accepted, exit=0). Pinning: INLINE `gs.f=0` control IS rejected (global field-write invalidates), so the gap is CALL-SPECIFIC = same root as F87 (VisitCallExpr:654-668 invalidates no narrowing maps). F87 baseline (local+_Borrow arg) also reproduces (control B).
- CLASSIFICATION: FOLDED-F87 (identical VisitCallExpr-no-invalidation root). FIX CAVEAT (important): F87's stated fix "invalidate narrowing keyed on _Borrow ARGS" is INCOMPLETE — a call can null a GLOBAL's field without taking it as an arg, so VisitCallExpr must ALSO clear narrowing on globals (conservatively, any global FieldPath/Deref narrowing on any call). The unified InvalidateAllStatusForVar fix must include globals, not just args. probe: /tmp/gl.cbs (+gl2 inline control, gl3 F87 baseline).

### 2026-06-08 — mergeVD/mergeFP one-sided-narrowing at join (F26 sibling investigation)
- shape: `if(cond){ if(!X) return; } *X;` for X = scalar var p (mergeVD), field s->f (mergeFP), deeper s->f->g (mergeFP deep) → ALL INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (all rejected "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced"). The mergeVD/mergeFP copy-on-absent code-structure LOOKS like F26's mergeDPVD bug, but is MASKED by initStatus pre-population (Nullable for every nullable VarDecl/FieldDecl in source — INCLUDING deeper field paths, newly confirmed). F26 is genuinely DerefPath-specific (DPVD not pre-populated). No new bug; F26's documented "masked, defense-in-depth" analysis holds even for deeper paths. (F97 discipline: buggy-LOOKING merge code probed SOUND — did not file on code-reading.) probes: /tmp/mv.cbs, /tmp/mf.cbs, /tmp/md.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — F86 blast-radius: array-subscript site also unchecked (FOLD-F86)
- shape: uninit `int *_Nonnull p; p[0]` (non-_Safe, -nullability-check=all) → FOLDED-F86. Accepted (exit=0), same as the `*p` baseline. The ArraySubscript check (VisitArraySubscriptExpr:688) gates on `getExprPathNullability(base)==Nullable` exactly like UO_Deref (:677, F86) — a _Nonnull base returns NonNull → check skipped → uninit _Nonnull subscript unchecked. Same root, sibling site. F86's fix must cover BOTH UO_Deref AND ArraySubscript. Not a separate filing. probe: /tmp/n1.cbs,/tmp/n2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — F86 blast-radius COMPLETE: all 3 deref-like sites unchecked for uninit _Nonnull
- shape: uninit `struct S *_Nonnull p; p->a` → FOLDED-F86 (accepted, exit=0). VisitMemberExpr-arrow (:698) shares the `getExprPathNullability(base)==Nullable` gate with UO_Deref (:677) and ArraySubscript (:688). ALL THREE deref-like ops (`*p`, `p[i]`, `p->a`) skip the uninit/flow check when the base is _Nonnull. (The :708 cast site is a DIFFERENT check — nullable→nonnull cast, not a deref.) F86's fix must cover all 3 dispatch sites. F86 blast-radius fully mapped. probes: /tmp/n1,n2,n3.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — IsSafePointerConversion const-to-void borrow (composition gap) → INCONCLUSIVE (likely intended, NOT filed)
- shape: `int*_Borrow` → `const void*_Borrow` (add const + erase to void in one step). _Safe REJECTS "forbidden in the safe zone" (IsSafePointerConversion :821 `DstConst==SrcConst`); the two atomic steps each work (`int*`→`const int*` add-const; `const int*`→`const void*` to-void). LOOKS like a composition FP.
- BUT: non-_Safe ALSO rejects it ("incompatible _Borrow types" — a DIFFERENT check, TypeBSC borrow-compat), while `int*_Borrow`→`void*_Borrow` (non-const) works in both zones. CONSISTENT cross-zone rejection by two independent checks → deliberate borrow-type rule, not a safe-zone over-restriction. Doc 3679 allows only `T*_Borrow`→`void*_Borrow` (non-const), silent on const-void. Clean workaround (add const first). VERDICT: likely-intended composition limitation; NOT filed (F97 discipline — borderline FP, consistent + not doc-contradicted). Note for future: if the borrow type system is meant to compose these, BOTH checks share the gap. probes: /tmp/pc1-7.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — SafeFeatureFinder::VisitQualType missing recursion (RequireBorrowCheck gate) → INCONCLUSIVE (latent, unreachable)
- shape: SafeFeatureFinder::VisitQualType (WalkerBSC.h:374, self-TODO'd) checks only direct owned/borrow qual + owned/borrow FIELDS, no VisitType recursion → misses array-of-owned / pointer-to-owned / fnptr-signature-owned. Gates RequireBorrowCheck (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:356) = whether borrow/ownership check runs.
- VERDICT: real incompleteness but NOT exploitable. (1) array-of-owned `int*_Owned arr[N]` is SHAPE-REJECTED at decl ("type of array cannot be qualified by _Owned"). (2) The finder also visits the whole body; EVERY actual owned/borrow violation involves an owned/borrow-typed expr/decl (leak⇒owned value, borrow-conflict⇒&_Mut/&_Const expr) which the body-visit catches → RequireBorrowCheck correctly true. Scalar control `int*_Owned p` leak IS caught. The missing recursion only drops feature-in-signature-only cases (no value ⇒ no violation). Latent-unreachable, analogous to F26's masked mergeVD/FP. Resolves INDEX UNPROBED entry. probes: /tmp/sf1.cbs (caught), /tmp/sf2.cbs (shape-rejected).

### 2026-06-08 — init-analysis Rvalue operand-check C3 completeness
- shape: code-audit of transferStatement Rvalue switch (1988-2028) vs Rvalue::Kind enum (BSCIR.h:435) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. All 10 Rvalue kinds handled (Use/Ref/AddressOf/BinaryOp/UnaryOp/Aggregate/Array/Cast checked; NullPtr/SizeOf no-op = correct, no operand read). Exhaustive switch, no default-fall-through FN. Dest Deref-projection prefixes checked too. F83 (dest Index) + F88 (callee operand) are the known filed gaps at OTHER sites. No new C3 gap in this region.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret callee loop-conditional init (RESUMED per user)
- shape: callee inits `*out` only inside `for(i<n)` body, `return 0` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "'*out' may not be initialized on all paths at return" — the verify handles loop-body-may-not-run (n==0 ⇒ *out uninit). No FN. 25th angle.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret callee goto-skips-init (26th angle)
- shape: `if(c) goto done; *out=1; done: return 0;` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "'*out' may not be initialized on all paths at return" — at-return check inspects the goto path (c-true skips init). No FN.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret combined-return-values (27th angle, adversarial)
- shape: `int r = f(&a) + g(&b); if(r==0){use a; use b}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. BOTH a and b REJECTED "use of uninitialized value" — the analyzer does NOT credit when the return flows into an arithmetic combination (sum==0 ≠ either ret==0). Credit requires the comparison on the DIRECT un-combined return of the specific call. Strong precision result, no FN. probe: /tmp/eic.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret return-var r++ before compare (28th angle)
- shape: `r=f(&a); r++; if(r==0){use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a" — increment invalidates r's return-link (like reassignment); r==0 after ++ ≠ contract. Mutation-invalidation covers ++ not just =. No FN. probe: /tmp/eii.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret constant-expression comparison (29th angle, precision boundary)
- shape: `if(r == 2-2){use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE (conservative FP, sound, NOT filed). REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a" — the caller comparison recognizer is LITERAL-ONLY; it does NOT constant-fold `2-2`→0, so a is not credited. SOUND (no FN — rejecting is safe). LOW severity (non-idiomatic code; nobody writes 2-2 for 0) + consistent with the attribute's "arg must be integer literal" theme → likely intended. Characterizes the recognizer boundary: only literal arg-value comparisons credit. probe: /tmp/eik.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret bare-truthiness if(r) (30th angle) → DOCUMENTED limitation
- shape: `if(r) return; use a;` → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a". NOT a bug: the documented recognizer supports exactly FOUR forms (cmp-forms-ok.cbs header): `e == value`, `e != value`, `value == e`, `value != e` (e = return-expr or copy-propagated var; value = INTEGER LITERAL). Bare `if(r)` has no ==/!= operator → outside the four forms → intended. SOUND (conservative, no FN).
- RETROACTIVE: the `2-2` case (29th angle) is ALSO outside the four forms (value must be a literal, 2-2 is a constant-expr) → intended, not just "likely intended". Both confirmed against the documented spec. The recognizer boundary = the four explicit-operator literal-value forms; bare truthiness + constant-expr values are deliberately not recognized.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret copy-propagation (31-32 angles, documented form)
- shape: `int s=r; if(s==0){use a}` ACCEPTS (copy-prop works, conformant); 2-level `int s=r; int t=s; if(t==0)` ALSO accepts (chain tracked, beyond doc's singular). probes: /tmp/ecp.cbs, /tmp/ecp2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret copy-then-mutate soundness (33rd angle)
- shape: `int s=r; s=0; if(s==0){use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a" — mutating the copy breaks the credit link (s no longer ==r). Copy-prop sound both directions. probe: /tmp/ecm.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret _Bool contract + idiomatic if(r) → CANDIDATE FP (user decision)
- shape: `_Bool g(...ensure_init_if_ret(1)); _Bool r = g(&a); if(r){use a}` → REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a". Explicit `if(r==1)` (a four-form) ACCEPTS. So the IDIOMATIC _Bool success check `if(r)` is not credited.
- WHY POSSIBLY FILEABLE (distinct from F97): _Bool return is doc-SUPPORTED (rule 3); `if(r)` is THE canonical _Bool idiom (`if(r==1)` is discouraged style). The four-forms doc describes CURRENT recognizer support, NOT an intentional-rejection spec (unlike F97's rule6_error which documents the rejection as designed). Canonical-idiom rejection = MEDIUM per campaign FP policy.
- WHY MAYBE NOT: trivial workaround (`if(r==1)`/`if(r!=0)`); the bare-truthiness limitation is uniform (int+_Bool) and arguably an accepted recognizer simplification.
- STATUS: surfaced to user for file/no-file (post-F97: don't auto-file borderline FPs). SOUND (no FN). probe: /tmp/eib.cbs (reject), /tmp/eib2.cbs (explicit accepts).

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret value-vs-contract matching (soundness of the _Bool angle)
- shape: contract(1) `if(r != 0) return; use a` → REJECTED (fall-through r==0 ≠ contract 1, a uninit); contract(0) same form → ACCEPTED (r==0 == 0). INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. The recognizer ties credit to the CONTRACT value, not the comparison literal — no FN on value mismatch. CONCLUSION: the _Bool `if(r)` issue (candidate FP) is PURELY precision (sound, no soundness angle). Clean MEDIUM-precision-FP file/no-file decision. probes: /tmp/evm.cbs, /tmp/evm2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret retry pattern (35th angle)
- shape: `r1=f(&a); if(r1!=0){ r2=f(&a); if(r2!=0)return; use a } else { use a }` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. exit=0, both gated uses credited; re-pass of &_Mut a on failure uses uninit-addr exemption. No FP/FN. probe: /tmp/ery.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret caller goto early-exit (36th angle)
- shape: `if(r!=0) goto fail; use a; ... fail:` (non-_Safe, -uninit-check=all) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. exit=0, a credited on the r==0 fall-through — recognizer handles goto-based early-exit, not just return. probe: /tmp/egc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret do-while retry-until-success (37th angle)
- shape: `do{ r=f(&a); }while(r!=0); use a` (non-_Safe, -uninit-check=all) → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. exit=0, a credited after the loop — the analyzer propagates the credit through the do-while EXIT condition (r==0 at exit ⟹ a init). Non-trivial loop-exit-condition dataflow, precise. probe: /tmp/edw.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret contract value range [-32768,32767] (38th angle, boundary)
- shape: sweep contract value at int16_t boundary → INCONCLUSIVE (intended limit, exact). 32767/-32768 ACCEPTED; 32768/-32769 REJECTED "outside the range [-32768, 32767] supported in function pointer types". Exact boundary, no off-by-one, clear diagnostic. INTENDED (16-bit value storage in function-type ExtInfo for fnptr compatibility). Minor: doc rule 4 says "integer literal (can be negative)" without stating the range — doc-incompleteness, not a compiler bug.
- DIAGNOSTIC-QUALITY CONTRAST (informs the _Bool if(r) decision): the out-of-range case has a CLEAR diagnostic ("outside the range supported"); the _Bool if(r) FP gives a MISLEADING "use of uninitialized value: a" (implies the var is genuinely uninit, not "use an explicit comparison form"). The poor diagnostic slightly strengthens the _Bool FP's fileability.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret + __assume_initialized composition (39th angle)
- shape: unchecked `f(&a)` + `_Unsafe{__assume_initialized(&a)}` then use a → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. With assume: ACCEPTED (escape hatch overrides un-credited contract); without: REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a". Features compose correctly, no FP/FN. probe: /tmp/eai.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret multi-iteration runtime failure-path (40th angle)
- shape: gated caller `if(r==0){read v}` driven 100× alternating success/failure under valgrind → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Compiles clean; valgrind 0 errors/0 contexts, no uninitialised-read/conditional-jump. The gate prevents the uninit read on the failure path at runtime across many iterations. probe: /tmp/ert.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret switch-on-copy (41st angle)
- shape: `int s=r; switch(s){case 0: use a}` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: a" — the documented switch-on-return limitation applies to switch-on-COPY too (switch isn't a recognized form regardless of return-vs-copy). Consistent, conservative, no FN. probe: /tmp/esc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret recursive self-delegation (42nd angle)
- shape: recursive `f`(ensure_init_if_ret 0): base inits *out; recursive `return f(out,n-1)` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Self-delegation ACCEPTED (modular: recursive call uses f's own signature/contract); discard-return twin REJECTED "'*out' not initialized at return". Recursion sound both directions, no FP/FN. probes: /tmp/erc.cbs, /tmp/erc2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret on variadic function (43rd angle)
- shape: `int f(int *out[contract], int n, ...)`; gated use vs unchecked use → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. EXACTLY one error (unchecked b rejected); gated a accepted. Contract on a fixed param works despite `...`. No FP/FN. probe: /tmp/evar.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret callee reads *out before init (44th angle)
- shape: `int tmp = *out; *out = tmp+1; return 0;` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. REJECTED "use of uninitialized '*out' in ensure_init_if_ret function" — callee can't read *out before writing (caller may pass uninit location). No FN. probe: /tmp/erd.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret position-independence + ternary init (45-46 angles)
- contract on 2nd-of-3 params → ACCEPTED (position-independent). callee `*out = c?1:2; return 0` → ACCEPTED (verify recognizes both ternary arms init *out). Both INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. probes: /tmp/epp.cbs, /tmp/etn.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret + _Nonnull composition (47th angle)
- shape: `int *_Nonnull __attribute__((ensure_init_if_ret(0))) out` → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. Compiles; contract works (gated a credited, unchecked b rejected — exactly one error). Both attributes compose. probe: /tmp/enn.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret -rewrite-bsc roundtrip (48th angle)
- shape: contract+violation → rewrite → recompile output. Rewrite preserves attribute (1/1); recompiling output as .c MISSES the violation (BSC analysis doesn't run in C mode — false alarm, language-mode), but as .cbs CATCHES it ("use of uninitialized value: a"). Roundtrip preserves contract semantics. INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. probe: /tmp/erw_roundtrip.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ensure_init_if_ret high param-index (49th angle)
- shape: contract on the 35th param of a 35-param function → INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND. exactly one error (unchecked b); gated a credited. Param-index encoding handles high indices, no FN/FP. probe: /tmp/epx.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — CORRECTION: _Bool if(r) FP is a DELIBERATE code-documented limitation (NOT fileable)
- Read the implementation: BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:669-672 explicitly handles the no-ComparisonFact path with the comment: "Patterns without a recognised ComparisonFact (e.g. `if (ok)`, `if (!ok)`) are not one of the four spec-supported forms — *out stays in its incoming state on every edge." The implementers NAME `if(ok)`/`if(!ok)` as intentionally unsupported.
- VERDICT FLIP: the _Bool if(r) rejection is INTENDED (deliberate four-forms simplification, documented in BOTH cmp-forms-ok.cbs AND this code comment). Same shape as F97 (documented intended behavior). DO NOT FILE. My earlier "file as MEDIUM" lean was premature — should have read the source first (F97 lesson). At most it's an ENHANCEMENT request (extend recognizer to bare-truthiness for _Bool), not a bug. The misleading diagnostic ("use of uninitialized value") is the only mild critique, but the rejection itself is by-design.

### 2026-06-08 — CANDIDATE (noted, not pursued): const-add for _Owned pointer rejected
- `const int*_Owned q = p` (owned add-const) → REJECTED "forbidden in safe zone"; BORROW add-const `const int*_Borrow cb = b` WORKS (exit=0). Asymmetry. IsSafePointerConversion (827-832) handles `void*owned <- T*owned` + borrow add-const but NOT owned add-const → fallback rejects. Adding const is a safe widening (ownership on pointer not pointee; safe_free casts to void*_Owned dropping const). LIKELY a precision FP.
- STRENGTHENED 2026-06-08: `const int*_Owned` is a USABLE, FREEABLE type — `_Safe void consume(const int*_Owned p){ safe_free((void*_Owned)p); }` compiles clean. So the type is legitimate, but the natural way to obtain one (add-const from non-const owned) is blocked, while borrow add-const works. Clear inconsistency → real precision FP (MEDIUM).
- VERDICT: STRONG candidate, surfaced to user for file decision. Unlike F101 (explicit doc rules 13/6), no EXPLICIT doc rule states owned add-const is allowed — only "_Owned rules similar to const" (manual:2271). The borrow-vs-owned asymmetry is strong evidence but not doc-conclusive. Post-F97 discipline: do not file a borderline FP without clear spec support. Flag for user/maintainer: is `const T*_Owned <- T*_Owned` meant to be allowed (consistency with borrow add-const)? If yes, fix = add the owned add-const case to IsSafePointerConversion. probes: /tmp/oc6.cbs, /tmp/bc.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — F101 owned-vs-borrow assign-check asymmetry (refines fix surface)
- CheckBorrowQualTypeAssignment (SemaBSCOwnership:677-711) has NO `_Bool <- borrow` special case (unlike CheckOwnedQualTypeAssignment:356-360 which has `_Bool <- T*_Owned`). So:

  - borrow→_Bool: CheckBorrowQualTypeAssignment REJECTS (no case), IsSafeConversion BLOCKS → consistent rejection, contradicts only doc rule 6.
- Primary root for BOTH = IsSafeConversion:895-900 (value-context pointer→_Bool reject). Fix: allow owned/borrow→_Bool there. Secondary: add `_Bool <- borrow` to CheckBorrowQualTypeAssignment to match the owned check (the assign-check asymmetry is itself a small inconsistency). F101 (filed) fix surface refined.

- `int*_Borrow → const void*_Borrow` (to-void add-const) rejected by CheckBorrowQualTypeAssignment void* const-match (:700) — a const-variance cousin of F102. Per "stop probing variants of confirmed defects" discipline, folds into F102's class (const-variance incompletely implemented across the conversion checks); same fix surface. Not a separate finding.

### 2026-06-08 — range-inbound implicit conversion (0dafa404) soundness boundary
- `x&0x7F`→char [0,127] ALLOWED; `x&0xFF`→char [0,255] REJECTED; `x&0xFF`→uchar ALLOWED; `(x&0x7F)+1`→char [1,128] $(see exit); `(x&0x3F)+64`→char [64,127]. Range arithmetic + signed/unsigned target tracked. INCONCLUSIVE-SOUND (boundary correct). probes: /tmp/rg5-9.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — range-conversion: addition conservative (SOUND, not a bug)
- `x&0x3F` [0,63] allowed; `(x&0x3F)+64` [64,127] REJECTED (conservative on `+` — addition range not tracked). `(x&0x3F)|0x40` [64,127] → see exit (OR tracking). Conservative-on-addition is SOUND (rejects safe code = precision limitation, not unsoundness; not a canonical idiom → not filed). Key soundness property HOLDS: no out-of-range value accepted. Feature 0dafa404 sound.

### 2026-06-08 — pointer ++/-- rework (97cf8c4b): single-obj vs ArrayElem
- `int*_Borrow p=&_Mut x; p++` (single-obj) → see exit; `int*_Borrow _ArrayElem p=&_Mut a[0]; p++` (array) → see exit. Tests recently-reworked ++/-- diagnostics. probes: /tmp/pp.cbs, /tmp/pp2.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — OPEN LEAD (not a finding): array-elem runtime bounds unclear
- `int a[3]; int*_Borrow _ArrayElem p=&_Mut a[0]; p++ x3; *p` → compiles clean, valgrind 0 errors (stack over-read into valid mem — inconclusive). Heap version blocked (owned ptr no subscript). _ArrayElem doc (manual:3890-3892) frames it as TYPE-LEVEL (prevents arithmetic on single-object ptrs — confirmed `int*_Borrow p++` rejected), NOT an explicit runtime-bounds promise. So array-elem `p++` past bound + deref may be a silent OOB (C-like) — UNCLEAR if intended (type-level prevention) or a spatial-safety FN.
- RESOLVED 2026-06-08: clean OOB CONFIRMED — `static int g[3]; const int*_Borrow _ArrayElem p=&_Const g[0]; p+=100000; *p` compiles clean, valgrind "Invalid read of size 4". BUT doc (manual:3890-3892) frames _ArrayElem as TYPE-LEVEL only: it prevents arithmetic on single-object pointers ("阻止用户误用指针运算" = prevent MISUSE), NOT a runtime-bounds guarantee on legitimate array-pointer arithmetic. So array-pointer OOB is C-like programmer responsibility — LIKELY INTENDED, NOT a soundness FN per the doc's actual claims. NOT FILED (F97 discipline — doc doesn't promise the violated property). Note the goal/mechanism tension (line 2178 lists OOB as a safety goal; _ArrayElem mechanism is type-level) — a design observation for the user, not a bug. (Construction notes: owned ptr no subscript; mutable global borrow rejected in _Safe; const global borrow works for read OOB.)

### 2026-06-08 — array-elem SUBSCRIPT far-OOB (vs arithmetic) — consistency check
- `p[100000]` (subscript) far-OOB → ALSO unchecked (compiles clean, valgrind 'Invalid read of size 4'), SAME as `p+=100000;*p`. So array-elem OOB is CONSISTENTLY unchecked across BOTH subscript and arithmetic — no asymmetry. Confirms the type-level design (no runtime bounds for either path) is deliberate/internally-consistent → INTENDED, not a bug. Array-elem bounds question fully resolved (not filed). probe: /tmp/sub.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — F69 blast radius (which constant contexts hit ConstExprEmitter gap)
- F69 blast radius BOUNDED: scalar `int g=_Safe(5)` → FOLDS, codegen ok (escapes the gap — _Safe(5) constant-folds to 5, no ConstExprEmitter aggregate path). struct `struct S g=_Safe((struct S){..})` → FAILS (F69). array-of-struct `struct S arr[1]={_Safe((struct S){42,7})}` → WORKS (codegen ok!) — the InitListExpr/brace-list path peels/folds SafeExpr elements via a different constant-eval path. So F69's blast radius is NARROW: only the DIRECT top-level aggregate static init (`struct S g = _Safe(...)`) hits the ConstExprEmitter gap; list-wrapped aggregate elements escape. So F69 = AGGREGATE constant initializers (non-foldable); scalar constants fold and escape. Fix (add VisitSafeExpr to ConstExprEmitter) covers all aggregate constant contexts. probes: /tmp/f69a-c.cbs.

### 2026-06-08 — ConstantLValueEmitter SafeExpr (F69-sibling check)
- ConstStructBuilder (CGExprConstant:557) + ConstantLValueEmitter (:1786) also lack VisitSafeExpr (like F69's ConstExprEmitter). Probed constant-lvalue contexts: `static FP fp = g` (fnptr const), `static int *gp = &gv` (global addr) → see exits. Both work (codegen ok) → no SafeExpr reaches the const-lvalue path in constructible cases (fnptr/global-addr constants not SafeExpr-wrapped). So ConstantLValueEmitter/ConstStructBuilder gaps appear UNREACHABLE; F69 (ConstExprEmitter direct aggregate) is the ONE reachable SafeExpr-constant gap. No F69-sibling. CODEGEN (direction b) explored: F69 the gap, no new finding. probes: /tmp/cl.cbs, /tmp/cl2.cbs.
