## BSC GENERICS × safe-zone/ownership/nullability/init (2026-06-17 — first BSC-`<T>` campaign, G-series numbering)
NOTE: these are BSC function/struct TEMPLATES `foo<T>`, NOT the C11 `_Generic` keyword (OOS) above.
USER allowed generics for this session; user wants G-series finding numbers (not F). RESULT: NO finding —
the area is robustly implemented (deferral is DEFER not SILENCE; instantiation re-runs every check).
All probes /tmp/gen/*.cbs. Every generic matched its byte-identical non-generic twin.
- narrowing return `_Safe int f<T>(T x){return x;}` + `f<double>(...)` → diag AT INSTANTIATION — **SOUND** (G1/MIX1/MIX2/MIX4)
- const-fit `char c=(T)300` / const-generic `f<300>()` overflow → diag at instantiation, value-sensitive (f<100> clean) — **SOUND** (CO/NTV/MIX3)
- raw deref `*p` in generic _Safe → '*' forbidden at instantiation — **SOUND** (G2/ND)
- use-after-move/double-free + owned leak in instantiated generic — **SOUND** (UAM2/UAM3/LK2); UNINSTANTIATED not analyzed (standard)
- uninit read in generic — **SOUND** (UI)
- ensure_init_if_ret on generic, body returns sentinel w/o init *out → caught at instantiation — **SOUND** (EI/EIfull)
- nullability: nonnull-return-null (NB3i), nullable→nonnull arg (NB1/NB2), nullable-return-deref (NN3) → all caught at instantiation — **SOUND**
- _Safe BLOCK in non-safe generic fn narrowing → diag at instantiation — **SOUND** (A4); float→int explicit still forbidden (A4ok, correct)
- borrow/ptr cast `(T2*_Borrow)(void*_Borrow)p` in generic — **SOUND** (A2/A2u)
- generic STRUCT owned-field leak `struct Box<int*_Owned>` assign-not-freed → "field memory leak" — **SOUND** (OF1/OF2)
- NLL: generic returning *_Borrow with no _Borrow param → substitution-rejected; valid borrow-tied-to-param clean — **SOUND** (NLL/NLLok)
- deduced calls `f(c)`, `pass(3.5)`→int sink → narrowing caught — **SOUND** (AC3/AC4)
- per-specialization analysis: valid+invalid insts both orders → invalid always caught, no caching FN — **SOUND** (MIX1/2/4)
- -rewrite-bsc monomorphization (single/multi-type/owned-move) → clean C, one free emitted — **SOUND** (RW/RW2/RW3)
- runtime: generic alloc/borrow-deref/free under valgrind → 0 leaks, 0 errors — **SOUND** (RT)
- crash hunt: dependent-pointee p++/a-b/p+i/sizeof(T) in uninstantiated + instantiated templates → no getTypeSize assert — **SOUND** (X1-X4)
DO-NOT-REFILE: uninstantiated-template-not-analyzed is standard C++ template semantics (ill-formed-NDR); not a soundness bug.
- header/cross-file generic `#include genh.hbs; hnarrow<double>(3.5)` → narrowing caught at instantiation — **SOUND** (TU); valid hnarrow<int> clean (TUok)
- generic type-alias narrowing → caught at instantiation (TA, partial parse); generic union field access in _Safe → "requires _Unsafe block" (UN, correctly gated)

## 2026-06-17 — G01 CONFIRMED: conditional type alias drops nullability sugar
- `conditional<1,int*_Nonnull,int*_Nullable>` return/init of nullptr accepted (FN); direct/alias forms rejected. Root: ConditionalType::desugar→canonical strips _Nonnull sugar (TypeBSC.cpp:529 / ASTContext.cpp:5727). repro/G01_*.cbs. owned/borrow survive canonicalization (CT4 leak caught) → nullability-sugar-specific.

## 2026-06-17 — G02 CONFIRMED (CRASH): const-generic arg `(call())` crashes parser
- `struct A<(foo())>` / `g<(foo())>()` → Parser::ConsumeToken assertion (Parser.h:492) via ParseTemplateArgumentList on assert builds; "expected '>'" misparse on release dup. Documented-valid (`Array<int,(constexpr_fn())>`). Triggers: single-paren CallExpr only. Clean: (sz+1), (sizeof(int)), bare sz, ((foo())). repro/G02_*.cbs. ASSERT builds: ~/bsd/llvm-project.
- G02 trigger BROADER than calls: also `(arr[0])` subscript, `(gv++)` postfix-inc, `(obj.fp())` member-call, `(g(1,2))` — any parenthesized POSTFIX expr ending in `)`/`]`/`++`. FOLDED (same Parser.h ConsumeToken assertion / ParseTemplateArgumentList). NON-triggers: `(1+1)`,`(1>=0)`,`(1?2:3)`,`((int)x)`,`(0,5)`,`(+1)`. One fix.
- G02 ROOT CAUSE refined: ParseTemplateArgument's tentative type-id disambiguation (isCXXTypeId) + IsBSCStaticMemberFunctionCallInTemplateArgumentList (ParseExprBSC.cpp:73, scans LookAhead but no paren-depth) mutate the token stream on a parenthesized IDENTIFIER/postfix arg (`(E)`,`(sz+1)`,`(foo())`), leaving a special token → ConsumeToken assert in ParseTemplateArgumentList. Literals `(1)` skip annotation (no type-name lookup) → clean. Sibling IsBSCStaticMemberFunctionCall (ParseExprBSC.cpp:116-118) has a comment fixing EXACTLY this token-mutation crash class → area is historically crash-prone. ~90 fuzz cases (parser/sema/codegen/opt/rewrite/debuginfo) all fold to this one root.

## 2026-06-17 — G03 CONFIRMED (CRASH, distinct from G02): generic-struct specialization body
- `struct W<T>{T v;}; struct W<int>{int v;};` → emits "template specialization requires 'template<>'" then SIGSEGV in Sema::ActOnTagFinishDefinition (via ParseStructUnionBody/ParseClassSpecifier). Crashes RELEASE (canonical-dup) + assert builds. Minimal 2-line. m6 (method body) same root. NON-crash: primary redef, non-generic redef, bodyless _Impl. repro/G03_*.cbs. Found by hammering heavyweight generics after user pushed "are you sure G02 is the only one". DETECTOR LESSON: grep for `Assertion \`` (backtick, LLVM format) + `Stack dump`/`PLEASE submit`, NOT bare "assertion" (matches _Static_assert diagnostics → false positive, e.g. Option<int*_Owned>).

## 2026-06-17 — G04 CONFIRMED (CRASH, distinct): generic-alias-as-struct-spec
- `typedef A<T> = int; struct A<int>{int v;};` → "isa<> used on a null pointer" (Casting.h:109) in Parser::ParseClassSpecifier; SIGSEGV on release. Also nested same-name `struct W<T>{ struct W<int>{...} inner; }`. Minimal 2-line. repro/G04_*.cbs.
- G03 FAMILY (ActOnTagFinishDefinition, all FOLDED into G03): generic struct spec body (m1/m6), UNION spec (sp/p1), forward-decl+spec (sp/p4), const-generic spec `W<3>` (sp/p5), spec-fwd-decl+body (sp/p11), nested-type-arg spec `Outer<Outer<int>>` (sp/p12). Broad trigger, one root.

## 2026-06-17 — G05 CONFIRMED (assertion, assert-only): template-id `::` member-def C++ path
- `A<int>::foo(void){return;}` → "No class names in C!" assert in Sema::getCurrentClass (SemaDeclCXX.cpp:2431), via ParseDeclarationSpecifiers. Release recovers (rc=0). `<int>` essential; non-generic `A::foo` clean. Minimal 1-line. repro/G05_*.cbs.
- FOLDED into G04: `struct Box<int,double>{...}` (wrong arg-count struct spec, mf/f10) → same isa<> null in ParseClassSpecifier.

## 2026-06-17 — SATURATION CONFIRMED: generic crash surface = G02-G05 (4 distinct roots)
- Randomized fuzzer: 400 malformed generic decls (struct/union/enum/typedef/_Trait/_Impl × params × specs × bodies × member-defs) → 29 crashed → only 2 distinct signatures, BOTH known (ParseClassSpecifier=G04, getCurrentClass=G05). Plus G03 (ActOnTagFinishDefinition) + G02 (ConsumeToken/template-arg). No new distinct stack.
- Well-formed generics ROBUST across ALL features: functions, structs, unions, const-generics, type aliases, conditional aliases, member fns, traits, operators, COROUTINES (_Async/_Await/Future), stdlib (Option/Vec/Rc/Result/Cell), nested/recursive instantiation, codegen/-O1/-O2/-g, -rewrite-bsc, runtime/valgrind. ~600 total invocations.
- Crash cluster = BSC generic struct/union/alias SPECIALIZATION & DEFINITION + member-fn-style defs (template-id::method). Unsupported-but-plausible (C++ habits) syntax crashes instead of diagnosing; G03/G04 crash the SHIPPING build.

## 2026-06-17 — G06 CONFIRMED (HIGH, COREDUMP): &_Mut-escape doesn't invalidate nullability narrowing

## 2026-06-17 — nested-field borrow precision — SOUND (nested-granular)
- borrow s.inner.x, write disjoint s.y (NFB1) / sibling-nested s.inner.z (NFB3) clean; write same s.inner.x (NFB2) → "cannot assign...borrowed". Nested-field-granular precision. probes /tmp/gen/nfb/NFB1-3.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — nested-owned-field partial-move precision — SOUND
- move s.inner.p, free disjoint s.q (NOM1) clean; double-free s.inner.p (NOM2) → "moved or uninitialized: s.inner.p". Ownership nested-field-precise (matches borrow checker). probes /tmp/gen/nom/NOM1-NOM2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — nested-field init merge — SOUND
- nested s.inner.x init one-branch (NIM1) → "possibly uninitialized: s.inner.x"; both-branches (NIM2) clean. Init nested-field-precise. probes /tmp/gen/nim/NIM1-NIM2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — holistic generic+owned+borrow runtime — SOUND
- HO1 (mk<T>/get<T>/put<T>/thru<T>/del<T> + &_Mut*a borrow + owned move-through): compiles, runs correct (run=0), valgrind 0 leaks/0 errors. Codegen holistically sound for complex multi-feature programs. probe /tmp/gen/ho/HO1.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — nested-field nullability narrowing — SOUND
- if(s->inner.p) needs(s->inner.p) (NFN1) clean; unnarrowed (NFN2) → "cannot pass nullable pointer argument". Field-path narrowing extends to nested fields. (Gaps remain: G06 escape/global, G07 array-subscript.) probes /tmp/gen/nfn/NFN1-2.cbs.

rewriter (generic+borrow), constexpr, overload/redecl, unions, pointer-cmp, const-correctness, variadic,
raw-libc-rejection. FOLDED: F58/F91/F101. Niche-LOW-not-filed: mixed-qualifier-ternary-borrow→raw,
int->_Bool-implicit-var-init. Productive frontier comprehensively EXHAUSTED for this scope+oracle set.

## 2026-06-17 — owned move inside loop — SOUND
- move p in loop body (LM1) → 2nd-iter "use of moved value: p"; move+break with 0-iter path (LM2) → "memory leak: p" (n<=0 path leaks). Loop-carried ownership precise. probes /tmp/gen/lm/LM1-LM2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — recursive-function ownership — SOUND
- move p into recursive call, base frees (REC1) clean; free-then-recurse-with-moved-p (REC2) → "use of moved value: p". Ownership tracks across recursion. probes /tmp/gen/rec/REC1-REC2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — &&/|| short-circuit narrowing (RHS) — SOUND
- if(p && probe(p)) (SC1) narrows p in && RHS; if(!p || probe(p)) (SC2) narrows in || RHS. Both clean. probes /tmp/gen/sc/SC1-SC2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — local-var direct-reassign narrowing invalidation — SOUND (sharpens G06)
- reassign p=getn() to nullable after if(p) (DR1) → "cannot pass nullable" (invalidated); reassign p=nonnull (DR2) → clean (re-narrowed). Direct assignment to the tracked key DOES invalidate (locals AND fields per WR). So G06 root is PRECISELY: &_Mut-escape (address-taken, callee/alias write) fails to invalidate, while direct assignment correctly does. probes /tmp/gen/dr/DR1-DR2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — bitfield assignment — standard clang warning (defensible, not safe-zone-specific)
- 100 to 3-bit field (BF1) → standard -Wbitfield-constant-conversion warning (100→-4); 5 to 4-bit (BF2) clean. Bit-width truncation handled by standard clang warning, outside the safe-zone type-conversion (int->char) model. Defensible. probes /tmp/gen/bf/BF1-BF2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — arithmetic-UB ops (shift-overflow) — standard clang warning (defensible pattern)
- x<<40 (SH1) → -Wshift-count-overflow. Consistent pattern: safe zone relies on standard clang warnings for arithmetic UB (div-zero/shift/bitfield), doesn't hard-error. Design choice (safe-zone=memory/type/nullability safety, not arith-UB-freedom). Not a bug. probe /tmp/gen/sh/SH1.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — unary-minus result narrowing — LOW/niche value-range FN (NOT filed)
- `char f(char c){ return -c; }` (CR2) CLEAN but LOSSY for c=-128 (-(-128)=128 → narrowed to char = -128, runtime-confirmed wrong). Binary c+0/c*1/c+c (CR6/T1) correctly ERROR. ~c (CR4) clean is SOUND (~c range [-128,127] fits char). Root: DoesExprValueRangeFitInType→GetExprRange (standard-clang IntRange) computes unary-minus range as fitting char, missing the INT_MIN-negation overflow. LOW (one edge value, standard-clang IntRange imprecision not BSC-specific, wrong-value-not-memory-unsafety). Per HIGH/MED-only rule: NOT filed. A future session could file as a minor safe-zone narrowing-FN if desired. probes /tmp/gen/cr2/CR2-CR6,T1-T2.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — value-range permissive path (masks/modulo) — SOUND & PRECISE
- a&0xFF->signed char [0,255]vs[-128,127] (VR1) correctly FORBIDDEN; a&0x7F (VR2) clean; a%256 (VR3) forbidden; a&0xFF->uchar (VR4) clean. Value-range precise for masks/modulo; CR2 unary-minus is an ISOLATED GetExprRange imprecision (niche LOW), not a broad hole. probes /tmp/gen/vr/VR1-VR4.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — value-range offset-mask — VR5 conservative FP (LOW, not filed)
- (a&0xFF)-128 range exactly [-128,127] fits signed char but REJECTED (VR5, conservative FP — range not propagated through subtraction); a>>25 (VR6) clean. Value-range path (GetExprRange/std-clang IntRange) has BIDIRECTIONAL edge imprecision: FP here (VR5, conservative-safe), FN at unary-minus (CR2). Both LOW/niche/std-clang-rooted, NOT filed (campaign HIGH/MED-only; FP is safe-direction). probes /tmp/gen/vr2/VR5-VR6.cbs.

- a.data[3]=9; return a.data[3] (FC1) and plain int a[4];a[3]=9;return a[3] (F3) → "use of uninitialized: a" (array tracked at WHOLE-array granularity; writing one element ≠ initialized). Consistent w/ array-element-precision theme (G07 narrowing, array ownership/borrow collapse). LOW, plain+generic (NOT generic-specific), defensible — NOT filed. probes /tmp/gen/fc2/F1-F3.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — IsSafePointerConversion candidate 1 (nullability laundering) — PROBED-SOUND
- SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:819. IsSafePointerConversion compares POINTEES only, never the
  pointer-level _Nonnull/_Nullable → it waves `_Nonnull*←_Nullable*` through. Probed
  whether the SEPARATE nullability gate backstops it. All 3 rejected:
  - pc1 implicit `int*_Nonnull q = (int*_Nullable)p;` → "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable pointer"
  - pc2 explicit `(int*_Nonnull)p` → "cannot cast nullable pointer to nonnull type"
  - pc3 ptr-to-ptr `int*_Nonnull*_Borrow ← int*_Nullable*_Borrow` → rejected (deeper level too)
  Layered defense intact (CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment, SemaDeclBSC F66 area).
  Candidate 2 (deep const) verified SOUND by reading (full QualType== in fallback).
  IsSafePointerConversion is SOUND. probes /tmp/gen/cf/pc{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — Chain D: IsSafeConversion call-site coverage (return/argument) — PROBED-SOUND
- IsSafeConversion has only 2 real call sites: SemaExpr.cpp:10795 (CheckAssignmentConstraints,
  behind `getLangOpts().BSC && Diagnose`) + SemaCast.cpp:2911 (explicit cast). Hypothesis:
  return-narrowing and call-argument-narrowing might call CheckAssignmentConstraints with
  Diagnose=false → bypass the gate (would be a DISTINCT site from G08's RMW gap).
- REJECTED. All 4 narrowing forms correctly rejected ("implicit conversion ... forbidden in
  the safe zone"):
  - rc1 `return d;` (double→int)  rc2 `g(d)` arg (double→int)
  - rc3 `return n;` (int→char)    rc4 `g(n)` arg (int→char)
  matching the assignment baseline rcB. CheckAssignmentConstraints IS reached with Diagnose=true
  on the return + argument paths. So IsSafeConversion coverage is COMPLETE across
  assignment/init/return/argument/explicit-cast — the LONE remaining hole is compound-assign/
  RMW (G08, CompoundAssignOperator path that never reaches CheckAssignmentConstraints).
  Confirms G08 is the single gap, not one of a family. probes /tmp/gen/cf/rc{1,2,3,4,B}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — CheckCStyleCast candidate 1 (deferred cast re-check at instantiation) — PROBED-SOUND
- SemaCast.cpp:2874 early-returns CK_Dependent for a type-dependent cast in a generic body

    type 'double' to 'int' is forbidden in the safe zone" (explicit float→int forbidden).

    generic-body line → the deferred cast IS re-checked at instantiation. NOT silenced.
  - cc_ctrl `bad<int>` (legal (int)int) → clean.

  BSC block SOUND. probes /tmp/gen/cf/cc_{base,gen,ctrl}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — CheckOwnedQualTypeCStyleCast void+owned re-typing move semantics — PROBED-SOUND
- SemaBSCOwnership.cpp:273. Gate forbids owned↔raw (:304) + owned↔borrow (:300) (anti-launder),

## 2026-06-17 — CheckInit getInitializedFieldInUnion null-deref (BSCNullabilityCheck.md cand1) — SHAPE-REJECTED
- BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:537-540. Hypothesis: `union U u = {}` (NumInits==0) → union branch
  `FD = getInitializedFieldInUnion()` may be null → `FD->getType()` (:540) null-deref crash.
- NOT REACHABLE (7 shapes u1-u7, release + assert builds, -nullability-check=all):
  - u1/u2/u3/u7 (nonnull member, empty/array init) → NonnullInitByDefault fires FIRST ("type
    contains nonnull pointer must be properly initialized") → CheckInit recursion never reached.
  - u4/u5 (non-pointer first member / pure non-pointer union, `{}`) → rc=0 CLEAN → getInitialized-
    FieldInUnion() returns the FIRST declared field for empty braces (not null) → FD->getType() safe.
- So FD is never null at :540 in a reachable path: empty-braces yields first-field, and the
  uninit-nonnull case is intercepted upstream. Candidate closed. probes /tmp/gen/cf/u{1..7}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — init merge LocalStates asymmetry (C5) — SHAPE-REJECTED; merge SOUND
- BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:688 merge. LocalStates (:692) lacks the "present in Dst, missing in
  Src → meet Uninit" loop that FieldStates (:724) has → in theory a local Init-in-Dst,
  absent-in-Src would wrongly stay Init (FN). DOUBLY UNREACHABLE:
  (1) entryState (:128) pre-populates EVERY local (Init/Uninit); merge never drops keys → local
      key-set invariant = all-locals on every path → "missing in Src" never happens.
  (2) the trigger (decl in top-level switch block, jump-past-init) is Sema-forbidden in _Safe:
      m1 → "variable declaration in the top-level switch block is forbidden in the safe zone".
- meetStates SOUND, confirmed: m3 if-only-init `return x` → "use of possibly uninitialized
  value: x" (Init meet Uninit = MaybeInit, diagnosed); m2 both-paths-init → clean.
  FieldStates both-sides meet correct (lazy keys). probes /tmp/gen/cf/m{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — LoansInScope dataflow merge (C5) — PROBED-SOUND

    `struct S gs=((struct S){.a=1,.b=2});`; return-aggregate + local-aggregate likewise. 0 leftover; rc=0.

## 2026-06-17 — VisitArraySubscriptExpr index-skip (C3) — SHAPE-REJECTED; + F97 re-confirm (intended)
- BSCOwnership.cpp:2044 VisitArraySubscriptExpr visits only the base, never the index. Hypothesis:
  a move in index position `arr[consume(p)]` is invisible → missed move / double-free.
- SHAPE-REJECTED: the CFG/BSCIR LINEARIZES the expression — consume(p) is its own CFGStmt element
  visited by VisitCallExpr regardless of VisitArraySubscriptExpr skipping the index. as2 confirms:
  a second `safe_free(p)` after `arr[consume(p)]` correctly errors "invalid cast to void*_Owned of
  moved or uninitialized value: p" → the move WAS tracked. No double-free FN. (Index-skip in the
  AST visitor is moot because dataflow runs over linearized CFG elements.)
- SIDE OBSERVATION: `int arr[3]; arr[0]=10;arr[1]=20;arr[2]=30; arr[i]` (or const `arr[1]`) →
  "use of uninitialized value: arr" = F97, which is RETRACTED/INTENDED (manual §3.7.3 rule 6:
  element-wise array assignment does NOT mark array init; use {}/__assume_initialized). NOT a bug.
  Do not re-probe. probes /tmp/gen/cf/as{1,2,3}.cbs,ar{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — BSCIRBuilder VisitStmt expr-fallback drops children (C3) — SHAPE-REJECTED (in-scope)

  handled + recurse: comma (:830 lowers LHS for side-effects), ternary (:1258), &&/|| (:788),
  assign/compound (:763), call/member/subscript/cast/init-list/compound-literal.

  - cm2 consumed-in-comma, no reuse → clean (no false leak).

  out of scope (scope gate); F16-family covers the borrow-checker Prologue dead-arm angle. SHAPE-REJECTED.

  probes /tmp/gen/cf/cm{1,2}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — VisitReturnStmt × ternary conditional-return-of-owned leak — PROBED-SOUND
- BSCOwnership.cpp:2404 VisitReturnStmt (op=Move for owned return). Candidate: `return c ? p : q;`
  (both _Owned) might mark BOTH arms moved → miss the non-returned arm's leak (FN). SOUND:
  - rt1 `return c ? p : q;` → "memory leak of value: q" — analyzer is PATH-SENSITIVE (CFG splits
    ternary; only taken arm moved per path; non-taken arm flagged leak). Program correctly rejected.
  - rt2 single owned return → clean (moved out); rt3 free-one+return-other → clean.
  VisitReturnStmt + ternary tracks per-path ownership correctly. No leak FN. probes /tmp/gen/cf/rt{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — borrow-escape / dangling-return (syntactic gate + region) — PROBED-SOUND
- Two-layer protection. Syntactic gate: _Borrow return requires _Borrow param (be1/be2/be3 rejected
  "no _Borrow qualified type in parameters" — even be3 returning borrow-of-global, conservative).
  Region analysis (gate passes): be5 `return &_Mut x` (local) + be6 local-borrow-var + be8 ternary
  `c?p:&_Mut x` → "`x` does not live long enough" (path-sensitive). be4/be7 return-param → clean.
  No dangling-reference FN. probes /tmp/gen/cf/be{1..8}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — borrow-escape via store into longer-lived location — SHAPE-REJECTED (type-level prevention)
- Tested storing a borrow of a local into a longer-lived borrow location (escape past lifetime).
  UNCONSTRUCTIBLE — BSC forbids the enabling types:
  - es1 `int *_Borrow *_Borrow pp` → "type of 'int *_Borrow' cannot be qualified by '_Borrow'"
    (no borrow-to-borrow — can't have a borrow whose pointee is a borrow).
  - es2/es3 `struct H{int *_Borrow b;}` taken by `*_Borrow` → "struct H cannot be qualified by
    '_Borrow'(even indirectly), contains '_Borrow' type" (can't borrow a struct w/ borrow field).
  => No reachable longer-lived borrow location to store a local's borrow into. Combined with the
  return-escape soundness (be1-be8), borrow escape is fully prevented (type-level + region + syntactic
  gate). probes /tmp/gen/cf/es{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — addr-of safe-zone guard form-completeness — PROBED-SOUND; + F68 re-validated LIVE
- DiagnoseInvalidUnaryExprInSafeZone UO_AddrOf (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:1237): type-based — `&` forbidden
  in _Safe unless operand is a FUNCTION type. NO form-bypass (addr-of is ALWAYS UnaryOperator, no
  alternate AST path like deref's `*p` vs `p[i]`). Confirmed: ad1 `&x`,`&arr[0]`,`&s.f`,`&*pb` ALL →
  "'&' operator is forbidden in the safe zone". SOUND (contrast F68 deref which HAS a subscript bypass).
- F68 RE-VALIDATION: `q[0]` on raw `int *_Nonnull` in _Safe → rc=0 CLEAN (still bypasses raw-deref
  guard). F68 (HIGH, filed) STILL REPRODUCES on rebuilt compiler; NOT in FIXED.md → still live.
  The subscript build path still has no safe-zone deref hook (ArraySubscriptExpr ≠ UnaryOperator UO_Deref).
  probes /tmp/gen/cf/ad1.cbs,f68.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — loop-carried owned move (back-edge move propagation) — PROBED-SOUND
- consume(p) inside a loop body moves p; iteration 2 must see p moved (else runtime double-free).
  SOUND on both for + while:
  - lm1 for / lm2 while: "use of moved value: p" at loop-body consume (back-edge brings moved-p to
    loop head) + "memory leak of value: p" (zero-iteration path: p never freed). Both correct.
  - lm3 consume-once control → clean.
  Ownership dataflow loop handling (moved-state across back-edge + zero-trip leak path) SOUND.
  No double-free FN. probes /tmp/gen/cf/lm{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — raw pointer arithmetic forms in _Safe — RE-CONFIRMS SHAPE-REJECTED (_probed:2263)
- Re-validated on rebuilt compiler: pa1 `q++` REJECTED ("use of '++' on raw pointer not supported
  in safe zone"); pa2 `q += 1` + pa3 `int*_Nonnull r = q + 1` ACCEPTED. Same as the prior
  SHAPE-REJECTED analysis: arithmetic produces a pointer value (derefs nothing); the only deref
  path is `q[i]` = F68. The `++`/`--` prohibition is stylistic/UX, not soundness. Not a new defect;
  the `+=`/`+` "bypass" crosses no memory-safety line (checked prior coverage first → known). 
  probes /tmp/gen/cf/pa{1,2,3}.cbs.

  "use of uninitialized value: o.in.y" (PRECISELY pinpoints the nested field).
  Init analysis tracks nested struct fields precisely at depth 2 (full→complete, partial→rejected,
  missing pinpointed). No nesting-too-shallow here (F77/F79/F80/F19 = deeper recursion-cap / ownership
  analyzer, distinct). probes /tmp/gen/cf/ns{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — conditional move inside loop (MaybeMoved across back-edge) — PROBED-SOUND
- cml1 `for{ if(i==0) consume(p); } safe_free(p)` → "use of moved value: p" at loop-body consume
  (back-edge carries moved state) + "invalid cast ... moved or uninitialized value: p" at post-loop free
  (conditional double-free prevented). cml2 (no free) → loop-carried move + "memory leak of value: p"
  (maybe-not-moved path). MaybeMoved tracked across loop back-edge; no double-free FN. SOUND.
  Completes loop×ownership: unconditional move (lm1/lm2) + conditional move (cml1/cml2) both sound.
  probes /tmp/gen/cf/cml{1,2}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — array of owned pointers (int *_Owned arr[3]) — SHAPE-REJECTED (type-level forbidden)
- oa1/oa2 `int *_Owned arr[3]` → "type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned'". BSC forbids arrays of
  owned pointers at the type level → no per-element ownership tracking needed (sidestepped by construction).
  Consistent with: global borrows forbidden, borrow-of-struct-with-borrow-field forbidden, borrow-to-borrow
  forbidden — BSC uses TYPE-LEVEL restrictions to prevent hard ownership/borrow tracking scenarios. SOUND.
  probes /tmp/gen/cf/oa{1,2}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — CONFIRMED-G10: generic type alias bypasses owned-array restriction → silent leak
- `typedef Arr<T>=T[N]; Arr<int*_Owned> a;` ACCEPTED (rc=0) while direct `int*_Owned a[3]`, typedef
  `OwnPtr a[3]`, and non-generic `typedef OArr=int*_Owned[3]; OArr a` are ALL REJECTED ("array cannot be
  qualified by '_Owned'"). Runtime valgrind: a[0]=safe_malloc unfreed → "definitely lost 4 bytes", no diag.
- ROOT: CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType(...,"array") (SemaType.cpp:5202) runs only on the DECLARATOR path;
  generic-alias instantiation builds the array via template substitution → check NOT re-invoked. DISTINCT
  from F80 (gap inside the check) + G01 (conditional-alias nullability). Extends to struct field (d2).
  MEDIUM leak FN. Repro repro/G10_generic_alias_owned_array_bypass_leak.cbs. probes /tmp/gen/cf/tl2*,d{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — G10 blast radius: other type-restrictions via generic alias — BOUNDED (G10 is localized)
- Tested whether the generic-alias substitution-path-skip is systemic: br1 global-borrow `Id<int*_Borrow> g`
  → REJECTED; br2 borrow-to-borrow `Ptr<int*_Borrow>` → REJECTED; br3 owned-global `Id<int*_Owned> g` →
  REJECTED. Only the OWNED-ARRAY check (G10) is bypassed by a generic alias.
- => G10 is NOT a systemic substitution-path-skip; it is SPECIFICALLY the owned-array-element check
  (CheckOwnedOrIndirectOwnedType on "array", SemaType.cpp:5202) being declarator-path-only. The
  global/owned-global/borrow-to-borrow checks run on a path that DOES see alias-substituted types.
  G10 fix surface = re-run the owned-array check after array-type substitution. probes /tmp/gen/cf/br{1,2,3,4}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — G10 severity bound: double-free escalation BLOCKED by F97 — stays MEDIUM
- g10df attempt (alias a[1]=a[0], free both) in _Safe → ALL element reads rejected "use of uninitialized
  value: a" (F97: element-wise array init never completes → can't READ owned-array elements in _Safe).
- So G10's harm ceiling is the LEAK (write a[0]=malloc + scope-exit, pure _Safe, no read needed). A
  double-free would require _Unsafe reads (user-responsibility, not a compiler soundness violation).
  G10 stays MEDIUM (leak FN), not HIGH. F97's element-read restriction incidentally caps G10's severity.
  probe /tmp/gen/cf/g10df.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — generic union bypasses "union field cannot be _Owned" — LOW (backstopped, NOT a soundness FN)
- un1 direct `union U{int*_Owned v;}` → REJECTED "type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Owned'".
  un2 generic `union U<T>{T v;...}; U<int*_Owned>` → only the general union-access rule fires (type-check
  BYPASSED, like G10's array case).
- BUT unlk: `U<int*_Owned> u; _Unsafe{u.v=safe_malloc;}` unfreed → "field memory leak of value: u, u.v is
  leak" CAUGHT. Unions/struct-fields have PER-FIELD ownership tracking (cf. fw/ns) → the owned union member
  leak IS backstopped. CONTRAST G10 (array: whole-granularity → NOT tracked → silent leak FN, HARMFUL).
- So generic-union-owned bypass = HARMLESS missed-diagnostic (inconsistency: direct rejected, generic
  accepted) — NOT a soundness FN. LOW, not filed. The array-vs-union asymmetry is the key: G10 harmful
  (array untracked), union backstopped (per-field tracked). probes /tmp/gen/cf/un{1,2},unlk.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — SafeExpr (_Safe/_Unsafe expr) side-effect eval-count — PROBED-SOUND
- se_eval: `_Unsafe(incr())` + `_Safe(incr())` each evaluate the side-effecting subexpr EXACTLY ONCE
  → counter==2, exit=0 at BOTH -O0 and -O2. No double-eval, no drop. Scalar VisitSafeExpr is a
  transparent single-eval passthrough. (F60/F62/G09 were SafeExpr codegen/sema gaps; the eval-count
  itself is sound.) probe /tmp/gen/cf/se_eval.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — borrow-conflict across generic function call — PROBED-SOUND

## 2026-06-17 — array-of-borrow (G10 borrow analog) — PROBED-SOUND, no sibling
- ba1 direct `int *_Borrow arr[3]` ACCEPTED; ba2 via generic alias ACCEPTED — array-of-borrow is LEGAL
  both ways (NO "array cannot be _Borrow" restriction, unlike owned-array). ba3 store &_Mut block-local
  into arr[0], use after scope → "`x` does not live long enough" (region/lifetime tracks borrow
  per-element-store, dangling caught).

  safe & allowed); OWNED uses LOCATION-BASED whole-array leak-granularity (can't track per-element → array-
  of-owned RESTRICTED; G10 = bypass of that restriction → silent leak). No G10-borrow-sibling. probes ba{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-17 — F68 boundary: subscript-only (arrow + star deref guarded) — bounded
- ar1 `q->a` raw struct *_Nonnull in _Safe → REJECTED "'->' operator used by raw pointer type is forbidden"
  (member-access guard fires). ar2 `*q` → REJECTED "'*' operator forbidden" (unary-deref guard). ar3 `q[0]`
  subscript → ACCEPTED (F68 gap). So F68 is SUBSCRIPT-ONLY: `*` (UnaryOperator) + `->` (MemberExpr) both
  guarded; only ArraySubscriptExpr build path lacks the raw-deref check. Bounds F68 fix surface precisely.
  probes /tmp/gen/cf/ar{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-18 — disjoint-field mutable borrow precision (split borrows) — PROBED-SOUND + PRECISE
- df1 `&_Mut s.a` + `&_Mut s.b` simultaneous → ALLOWED (no false conflict; field-granular loans). df2
  `&_Mut s.a` twice → "cannot borrow s.a as mutable more than once" (same-field conflict caught). df3
  nested disjoint `&_Mut s.p.x` + `&_Mut s.q.y` → ALLOWED (precise at depth 2).
  Borrow checker = PRECISE field-granular tracking (split borrows work; no FP, no FN). probes /tmp/gen/cf/df{1,2,3}.cbs.

## 2026-06-22 — BSCIRBuilder lowerStmt/lowerExpr dispatch + drop/cleanup sequence — RE-AUDIT SOUND (nothing-found)
- Target: clang/lib/Analysis/BSC/BSCIRBuilder.cpp. Re-read lowerStmt dispatch (:257-346), all Visit* expr overrides (:728-1317), lowerToPlace fallback (:101-165), emitScopeCleanup/emitLocalsCleanup (:219-251), lowerReturnStmt param-drop (:703-722), and Body::simplify (BSCIR.cpp:225-395: collapseGotoChains/mergeBlocks/simplifyBranches/removeDeadBlocks). Goal: a Visit*/lower*-kind that drops a side-effecting subexpr (move/consume), or a Drop terminator lost to simplify.
- Probes (7, all SOUND or fold-to-filed):
  1. `return p.a` from a move-semantic struct `struct Pair{int*_Owned a; int*_Owned b;}` with `safe_free(p.b)` first + caller frees return → rc=0, vg 2a/2f/0err. Field-move into `_0` + struct-drop does NOT double-free the moved field. `/tmp/wf/p.NXoOf8.cbs`.
  2. `int*_Owned q = c ? p.a : p.b;` ternary-move of owned field → correctly diag'd `use of moved value: q` on return + leak of p.a,p.b. VisitConditionalOperator's MISSING shouldMove-upgrade (:1258-1289, cf. VisitCallExpr:1020/VisitInitListExpr:1209) is BENIGN because VisitMemberExpr (:1131-1139) already returns Move for owned-typed members, which propagates through the ternary arm into the result temp. `/tmp/wf/p.jhk8Xd.cbs`. (confirms 2026-05-21 explorer probe family.)
  3. `((struct S){.f=mk(),.v=7}).v;` compound-literal field access → FOLDED-F20 (compound-literal-with-owned-field temp leak; CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak doesn't track CompoundLiteralExpr). `/tmp/wf/p.sCbHqz.cbs`.
  4. `return (int*_Owned)mk_void();` void*_Owned→int*_Owned cast in return position → REJECTED "conversion forbidden in safe zone" (shape-rejected in _Safe; matches probe_owned_to_voidptr/owned_void_arbitrary prior probes). `/tmp/wf/p.PWpOVL.cbs`.
  5. `return p;` where `p` is an `_Owned` PARAMETER → rc=0, vg 1a/1f/0err. lowerReturnStmt:716 param-drop (DropOnly) is correctly a no-op for a moved parameter (ownership analysis sees Moved at drop). `/tmp/wf/p.LdLXdj.cbs`.
  6. setup probe `_Safe struct Pair mkpair` construction (field-assign two owned fields + return-by-value) → rc=0 SOUND. `/tmp/wf/p_setup.cbs`.
  7. attempted compound-literal-lvalue write-back via lowerToPlace temp-fallback (:158-164) — folded into #3/F20 (compound literal is a temp; "lossy write-back" is the F20 leak, not a distinct root).
- CONCLUSION: BSCIRBuilder dispatch + drop/cleanup is SOUND for in-scope shapes. lowerStmt Nop-fallback only reaches OOS kinds (GCCAsmStmt/IndirectGotoStmt); VisitStmt-default-Nop only catches OOS exprs (Choose/Generic/GNU-?:/StmtExpr) + side-effect-free constants. shouldMove propagation gap in VisitConditionalOperator/VisitAtomicExpr is benign (member-expr/call-result already Move or in _Unsafe). Scope-depth bookkeeping (prescanLabels vs lowerForStmt/lowerSwitchStmt) is consistent. simplify (collapseGotoChains/mergeBlocks/removeDeadBlocks) does NOT drop Drop terminators: collapseGotoChains stops at non-Goto/non-empty blocks; mergeBlocks only merges Goto→single-pred-target (Drops are terminators not statements, never merged away); removeDeadBlocks only removes truly-unreachable empty DeadBBs after jumps. No NEW defect class. Re-affirms the 2026-05-21 / 2026-05-30 R3E6 audits.

## 2026-06-22 — INCIDENT: G16 RETRACTED (false finding) + lessons
- WHAT: GLM explorer reported borrow-checker FN (write-while-mut-borrowed accepted on a noreturn leaf). Conductor validated repro/baseline/dump/dup/scope and FILED G16 (MEDIUM). User challenged it.
- VERDICT: NOT A BUG. BSC borrow checker is NLL/liveness-based; on the noreturn path `b` is DEAD at the write (die() aborts before `*b`) → no conflict → compiler correct. DFS::Copy excluding the noreturn leaf is correct. Retracted: bug_log G16 marked RETRACTED; umbrella comment 50294709 PATCHed to a retraction banner.
- ROOT OF THE MISS: validated that the finding REPRODUCED + DIFFERED from a baseline, but accepted the worker's claimed INVARIANT ("reject regardless of control flow") without checking it against the analyzer's actual (NLL) semantics. Asymmetry = correct liveness, not a defect.
- LESSONS (now in memory feedback_validate_invariant + BSCBorrowChecker.md NLL note): (1) repro+baseline-diff is necessary, NOT sufficient — verify the behavior violates the INTENDED semantic model; (2) know the model (BSC borrow = NLL/liveness); (3) agent confidence + real evidence can carry a wrong interpretation; (4) RED FLAG: a proposed "fix" that would reject valid code ⇒ finding likely inverted; (5) for FP/FN, build the minimal semantic test of the rule first. Second reproduces-but-misanalyzed retraction (cf. F97) → the validation gate needs an explicit "is this actually wrong?" step.

- GLM-5.2: runOnBlock (BSCOwnership.cpp:2613-2626) allowlist visits only DeclStmt/CallExpr/assignment-BO/inc-dec-UO/ReturnStmt; a bare pure-expr stmt (`*p;` UO_Deref, `(void)*p;` cast) after `safe_free(p)` is skipped → HandleDREUse never runs → use-after-move READ accepted in _Safe. Conductor re-validated: `*p;` rc=0 (FN), `sink=*p;`/`int x=*p;` rc=1 (caught), allowlist confirmed in source.
- CONDUCTOR dup-call: FOLD into F20 (HIGH). F20's entry ALREADY documents this exact runOnBlock allowlist (BSCOwnership.cpp:2615-2620) skipping a bare CompoundLiteralExpr stmt. owntransfer = a DIFFERENT statement kind (UO_Deref) hitting the SAME allowlist hole — same root + fix surface (visit all stmts in runOnBlock fixes both). GLM argued distinct-by-symptom (leak vs use-after-move-read) but root/fix is shared. NOT filed.
- NEW INSIGHT (amends F20 / reopens Chain-K): the runOnBlock allowlist breaks not just LEAK detection (F20) but also use-after-move-READ detection (HandleDREUse) for pure-expr statements. Chain-K's "runOnBlock SOUND" conclusion (BSCOwnership.md:418) is amended: sound for the move/leak-hoist side, NOT for pure-expr reads of moved values.

## 2026-06-22 — fresh-rewrite probes (origin 18111bd2 / built 34e6f26e), Mode-1 read→probe
Triggered by "have you probed?" — first real bug-hunting since the rebuild. Read the
−1492-line analyzer rewrite (the 7 new BSC commits) and probed two soundness hypotheses:

- **Struct-status over-promotion FN** (from `18111bd2` "Reconcile struct status after _Owned
  field assignment", which transitions the whole struct to Owned once all _Owned fields are
  owned). Hypothesis: a struct {int *_Owned p; int n;} with only `b.p` assigned gets marked
  Owned → whole-struct use reads uninit plain `b.n` (FN). **REFUTED**: `struct B c = b;` after
  only `b.p=src` is correctly rejected "use of uninitialized value: `b`" — the independent
  init-analysis pass backstops the ownership over-promotion. (probe: /tmp/p_probe.cbs)
- **`*p` vs `p[]` mutable-borrow aliasing FN** (from `53bc93dd` "[borrowchk] differentiate *p
  and p[] for _Borrow _ArrayElem", the −435-line BorrowChecker rework). Hypothesis: borrowing
  the same array element via `&_Mut arr[1]` then `&_Mut p[0]` (==arr[1]) is allowed → two live
  mutable aliases (FN). **REFUTED**: rejected "cannot assign to `*p` because it is borrowed" —
  the checker separates *p/p[] for liveness but still detects the aliasing conflict. Control
  (two `&_Mut arr[1]`) correctly rejected. (probe: /tmp/ae_probe.cbs)

Also scanned the BorrowChecker rewrite diff for REMOVED diagnostic/conflict emissions
(potential lost checks) — none; the −435 is the _ArrayElem liveness rework, not dropped checks.
Net: fresh rewrite SOUND on these surfaces. Next: deeper read of BSCNullabilityCheck (−256)
+ BSCIRInitAnalysis (−267) rewrites, or resume the GLM fleet when keys free.

## 2026-06-22 — solo hunt on the init-analysis (−267) + nullability (−256) rewrites
"yesyesyes" → focused Mode-1 read+probe of the two unread rewrite halves. ALL SOUND, no new bug.

- **Uninit coverage battery (init-analysis)**: read an uninit scalar `int u` in 12 positions —
  if-cond, while-cond, for-cond, ternary-cond, return, array-index, binop, call-arg, array-init
  elem, comma-rhs, unary, shift-count. **ALL 12 caught** ("use of uninitialized value: u"). The
  use-check (`checkOperand` over IR operands; if/while lower to bool-`SwitchInt`, discriminant
  checked at BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:2089) is comprehensive. No coverage gap.
- **Aggregate field-wise init (FP hunt)**: nested-struct full field-wise init, depth-3 nested,
  all CLEAN (struct promotion tracked correctly). **Array element-wise full init**
  (`int a[3]; a[0..2]=…; use`) is REJECTED — but this is DOCUMENTED INTENDED behavior (User
  Manual §3.7.3 rule 6: element-wise array assignment does not mark the array initialized; use
  init-list / `__assume_initialized`), source-commented (BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:170,218,240), and
  already RETRACTED in a prior session (bug_log:1500). FOLD, not a bug. (dedup discipline caught it.)
- **Nullability rewrite (−256)**: nullable deref at all three sites `*p` / `p[0]` / `p->x` →
  all REJECTED; narrow-in-branch-then-deref-after-join (C7) → REJECTED (narrowing correctly does
  not persist past the branch). Sound.

NET for the whole 2026-06-22 fresh-rewrite hunt (ownership + borrow + init + nullability):
the −1492-line rewrite is SOUND on every surface probed. It added regression tests and fixed 17
of our findings; the remaining open findings (F105/F64/F14/F09…) are vg-confirmed still-open.
Next new-bug surfaces (unprobed): union cross-variant reads, ensure_init contracts on nested
aggregates, _Borrow _ArrayElem index-precision, or breadth via the GLM fleet (paused).

## 2026-06-22 bsc-explorer cycle — union write-small-read-WHOLE tail (init-analysis FN)
Focus: BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp union handling, cross-variant reads in _Safe.
RE-OPENS the 2026-06-08 "write-small-read-large tail" candidate that was closed
SHAPE-REJECTED on ONLY the direct-field-read reach path. The whole-union read
path (copy/return/pass-by-value) needs no _Unsafe and is a LIVE FN.
- shape: union with _Owned member decl → SHAPE-REJECTED ("type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Owned'"). Forge-ownership-via-union surface dead at decl.
- shape: union field = struct-with-_Owned-field decl → SHAPE-REJECTED ("...'_Owned'(even indirectly)..."). Indirect owned union field dead at decl.
- shape: union with _Borrow member decl → SHAPE-REJECTED ("...'_Borrow'"). Borrow union member dead at decl.
- shape: plain-_Safe union FIELD access (read/write `u.a`) → SHAPE-REJECTED ("access to union field is _Unsafe and requires _Unsafe block") — confirms 06-08 finding, union field access still gated.
- shape: _Unsafe block suppresses init-use diag (uninit `int` read inside _Unsafe) → CONFIRMED (accepted; baseline plain-_Safe read rejected). _Unsafe is the suppression boundary.
- shape: whole-uninit-union pass-by-value `sink(u)` / `return u` / copy `union U v=u;` in plain _Safe (NO _Unsafe) → CORRECTLY REJECTED "use of uninitialized value: u". Whole-union reads ARE checked in plain _Safe — the reach path the 06-08 probe missed.
- shape: write small `u.a` (4B, in _Unsafe), then whole-union COPY `union U v=u;` + RETURN in plain _Safe; caller reads `r.b` (8B long) and branches → CONFIRMED-new (filed as Fxx pending). ACCEPTED; valgrind "Conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value(s)". Root: transferStatement :180-187 marks WHOLE union local Init on a partial (smaller) variant write; tail bytes stay uninit but whole-local-Init laundered through the plain-_Safe whole-union read. Asymmetry: no-write→REJECT, small-write→ACCEPT(FN), full-write→ACCEPT(correct).
- Repro /tmp/F_union_write_small_read_whole_tail_FN.cbs ; baseline /tmp/F_union_write_small_read_whole_tail_BASELINE.cbs.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — _Borrow _ArrayElem index PRECISION (post-rewrite 53bc93dd/908ddef2 "differentiate *p and p[]", bin 34e6f26e / src 18111bd2)
Focus: does the BorrowChecker distinguish constant indices (disjoint→accept), conservatively handle variable indices (alias→reject), and keep *p vs p[] collapsed for conflict detection. Source read: Path::to_string/structurallyEquals (BSCBorrowChecker.h:228-252 — flag UsesArraySubscriptNotation is DISPLAY-ONLY; index value NEVER stored in Path), VisitArraySubscriptExprOrUnaryDeref (:808-846), FrozenByBorrowOf (:1571-1598), FindLoansThatIntersect (:1542). F39 confirmed FIXED (DefUse::VisitArraySubscriptExpr now at :189 visits base+idx as Use).
- shape: `&_Mut arr[0]` + `&_Mut arr[1]` both live (DISJOINT constants) → REJECTED ("cannot borrow `arr[]` more than once"). Over-conservative FP, but this is DOCUMENTED `_ArrayElem` unified-element-borrow INTENT (structurallyEquals collapses all elements to one `*arr` path; index value never tracked). NOT a filable bug. → SHAPE/INTENT. Repro /tmp/explorer_probe.YHQYto.cbs, baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.WtkoSh.cbs
- shape: `&_Mut arr[i]` + `&_Mut arr[j]` variable indices both live → REJECTED (conservative; i could == j). SOUND (no aliasing FN). /tmp/explorer_probe.PytLWu.cbs
- shape: reborrow `b=&_Mut *a` (deref) then write `a[0]` while b live → REJECTED ("cannot assign to `a[]` because it is borrowed"). SOUND (*-vs-[] collapse on conflict side). /tmp/explorer_probe.1K8f2f.cbs
- shape: array-of-_Borrow-ptrs init-list `{ &_Mut x, &_Mut x }` (two mut of same x) → REJECTED ("borrow `x` more than once"); baseline `{&_Mut x,&_Mut y}` disjoint → ACCEPTED. SOUND (init-list numeric-index paths arr.[0]/arr.[1] at :664 don't break referent-aliasing detection). /tmp/explorer_probe.oBODnX.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.enYCrz.cbs
- shape: `&_Mut arr[0]` then write DIFFERENT element `arr[2]` while a live → REJECTED ("cannot assign to `arr[]`"); baseline write-after-last-use → ACCEPTED (NLL). SOUND (direct array write collapses onto element borrow). /tmp/explorer_probe.Kxwzkn.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.91Teh6.cbs
- shape: borrow element of OWNED array `&_Mut a[0]` then `safe_free_array(a)` while borrow live → REJECTED ("cannot move out of `a` because it is borrowed"; note `*a` borrowed). FrozenByBorrowOf (:1585) continues through owned pointer. SOUND (no UAF FN; F39's UAF surface is fixed). /tmp/explorer_probe.zCo7e1.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.z3ydyk.cbs
- shape: deref-form loan `b=&_Mut *a` (flag=false) vs subscript-form write `a[1]` (dest flag=true) while b live → REJECTED. structurallyEquals (:230) ignores UsesArraySubscriptNotation → the *-vs-[] display split does NOT cause a miscompare. SOUND (no aliasing FN across the flag boundary). /tmp/explorer_probe.HG8TZK.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.pi22rg.cbs
- NET: the −435-line "differentiate *p and p[]" rework is DISPLAY-ONLY; all conflict comparators use structurallyEquals which never inspects the index value or the array-subscript flag. Result: all element borrows of one base collapse to a single path → conservatively SOUND in every direction (constant-disjoint over-rejected = documented intent; variable-index/free/overwrite/cross-notation all correctly rejected). No new root cause. Recommendation next: nested 2-D `arr[i][j]` element-of-element borrow paths, or the init-list numeric-index path (:664) interaction with a subsequent SUBSCRIPT-form move of a specific element (does arr.[0] init-path match a *arr-collapsed move?).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — VisitCStyleCastExpr inner-expr shape siblings of F91 (MemberExpr/UnaryOp branches), bin 34e6f26e / src 18111bd2
Focus: the cast/else-branch skip in VisitCStyleCastExpr (:2273-2337). F91 = top-level `else` (:2334) for non-DeclRef/Member/UnaryOp InnerE. This cycle hunts the OTHER two skips: (a) MemberExpr branch (:2300) where getMemberFullField returns a non-DeclRef base; (b) UnaryOperator deref-chain branch (:2313) whose own walker bottoms out on a MemberExpr base.
- shape: `(void*_Owned)mkS().f` (MemberExpr base = CallExpr, single struct temp) → SHAPE-REJECTED: a DIFFERENT check fires first — "memory leak because temporary variable 'mkS()' is _Owned or indirect _Owned type" (CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak / F14 family on the whole struct temp). The `.f` member access doesn't consume the temp. /tmp/explorer_probe.eDqXpW.cbs
- shape: `(void*_Owned)(*p).f` (p = `_Borrow` of local struct, nested `int*_Owned*_Owned` field) single free → leak FP "field memory leak of value: `s`, s.f, *s.f are leak" (move-through-(*p).f untracked, scope-end net catches s.f). Compile ERROR, not clean FN. /tmp/explorer_probe.dmZAAW.cbs
- shape: `(void*_Owned)(*p).f` then `(void*_Owned)s.f` DOUBLE-FREE, nested field → leak FP "*s.f is leak" (second free marks s.f moved, inner *s.f net catches). Compile ERROR. /tmp/explorer_probe.Q5TkoN.cbs
- shape: `(void*_Owned)(*p).f` then `(void*_Owned)s.f` DOUBLE-FREE, **single-level `int*_Owned f`** (no inner subfield net) → **COMPILES CLEAN (exit 0)**; valgrind: 1 alloc / **2 frees, Invalid free()**. Asymmetry baseline (both frees via `s.f` DeclRef-member) → REJECTED "invalid cast to `void * _Owned` of moved or uninitialized value: `s.f`". /tmp/explorer_probe.uWUjJu.cbs (probe) /tmp/explorer_baseline.JLJJac.cbs (baseline). ROOT: getMemberFullField (:115-131) returns a UnaryOperator(deref) base for `(*p).f` → `dyn_cast<DeclRefExpr>(memberField.first)` fails → checkCastField skipped → move of s.f untracked. **FOLDED into F30 family** (getMemberFullField wrapper-list incomplete) — BUT F30 was marked FIXED by 95c83b5 which added only the ParenExpr arm, NOT the UnaryDeref arm. This `(*p).f` UnaryDeref variant STILL reproduces post-fix (was pre-noted at _probed.md:248 as F30-folded; the ParenExpr-only fix left it live). Same FUNCTION/invariant as F30 → fold per discipline, but it's an INCOMPLETE-FIX survivor (the fix added ParenExpr peeling; `(*p).f`=`p->f` needs UnaryDeref-of-pointer rebasing, a different arm). Flag to main thread as "F30 fix incomplete: UnaryDeref base of MemberExpr still skipped → double-free FN".
- shape: `(void*_Owned)*s.pp` (UnaryOp(Deref, MemberExpr) → UnaryOperator branch :2313, its own walker peels `*`, base=MemberExpr, dyn_cast<DeclRefExpr> fails → checkCastField skipped) then `(void*_Owned)s.pp`, nested `int*_Owned*_Owned` → leak FP "*s.pp is leak" (move of *s.pp untracked, scope-end net catches inner). Compile ERROR, not clean FN. Distinct CODE PATH from getMemberFullField (the UnaryOperator branch has its OWN deref-walker), but manifests as FP not FN on this shape. /tmp/explorer_probe.mmXeiL.cbs
- NET: The MemberExpr-branch `(*p).f` skip is a LIVE double-free FN but FOLDS into F30's getMemberFullField wrapper-list family (F30 FIXED only the ParenExpr arm; UnaryDeref survives = incomplete fix, not a new root cause). The UnaryOperator-branch `*s.pp` skip is a distinct code path but produces only a leak-FP (scope-end net masks it), not exploitable as an FN. No NEW root cause found this cycle. Recommend main thread: consider re-opening/extending F30 to cover the UnaryDeref base (the `(*p).f`→double-free is a more-severe symptom than F30's originally-noted FP, and the merged fix left it live).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — nullability laundering through CASTS (VisitCStyleCastExpr :708 + getExprPathNullability CStyleCast arm :353-355). bin 34e6f26e / src 18111bd2.
Hypothesis: casting a _Nullable pointer to _Nonnull (direct / void* round-trip / return-position / field / nested / reborrow / var-assign) silently drops the nullable check so a later deref/use is accepted (FN). RESULT: SOUND across all routes. The cast→nonnull check (VisitCStyleCastExpr :708) fires on the cast itself, AND the Sema-level diags ("cannot cast nullable pointer to nonnull type" DiagnosticBSCSemaKinds.td:368, "cannot return nullable pointer type" :366, "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable pointer" :372) backstop wherever the analyzer runs (_Safe OR -nullability-check=all). The cast variant behaves IDENTICALLY to the uncast variant at every zone level → no cast-specific launder.
- shape: `*( (int*_Borrow _Nonnull)p )` deref of nonnull-cast of nullable, _Safe → REJECTED "cannot cast nullable pointer to nonnull type" (cast diag masks; not silent). /tmp/explorer_probe.UWrwyj.cbs vs baseline plain `*p` /tmp/explorer_baseline.r0Ewma.cbs
- shape: void* round-trip `void*raw=(void*)p; *((int*_Nonnull)raw)` in _Safe → SHAPE-REJECTED ("conversion to/from void* forbidden in safe zone"). /tmp/explorer_probe.NyYW2E.cbs
- shape: `return (int*_Borrow _Nonnull)p;` against _Nonnull return, NON-safe DEFAULT → ACCEPTED — but this is the standard SZ gate (the UNCAST `return p;` is ALSO accepted in non-safe default). With -nullability-check=all OR _Safe → REJECTED ("cannot cast nullable...") IDENTICAL to uncast ("cannot return nullable..."). NOT a launder; FOLD into SZ-gate (cf F86 non-_Safe caveat). /tmp/explorer_probe.cdTIk5.cbs, _Safe-cast /tmp/explorer_probe.7e2sfi.cbs, _Safe-uncast /tmp/explorer_baseline.pGRBZd.cbs, NC_ALL-cast /tmp/explorer_probe.Qs6n0d.cbs
- shape: nested cast `*((int*_Borrow _Nonnull)(int*_Borrow)p)` _Safe → REJECTED (cast diag fires; IgnoreParenImpCasts does NOT strip the inner explicit cast). /tmp/explorer_probe.xHzcYK.cbs
- shape: field cast `*((int*_Borrow _Nonnull)s->b)` (nullable field) NC_ALL → REJECTED (cast diag + "cannot access member through nullable pointer"). /tmp/explorer_probe.JAYjv9.cbs
- shape: cast-as-_Nonnull-call-arg → parser quirk on `_Safe void` (macro), inconclusive; uncast baseline correctly REJECTED "cannot pass nullable pointer argument". /tmp/explorer_baseline.EF69om.cbs
- shape: cast of reborrow `(int*_Borrow _Nonnull)(&_Mut *p)` then deref, _Safe → REJECTED "cannot cast nullable..." ; uncast `&_Mut *p` to _Nonnull → REJECTED "nonnull cannot be assigned by nullable". /tmp/explorer_probe.pXUrwO.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.H81pIt.cbs
- shape: typedef-hidden `(NN)p` where `typedef int*_Borrow _Nonnull NN`, _Safe → REJECTED "cannot cast nullable..." (Sema resolves typedef; no written-vs-canonical gap). /tmp/explorer_probe.Ct9gcC.cbs
- shape: cast→_Nonnull var→deref-var `int*_Nonnull q=(int*_Nonnull)p; *q;` — matrix: default(no flag)=ACCEPTED (== uncast default, SZ gate), NC_ALL=REJECTED "cannot cast nullable..." (== uncast NC_ALL "nonnull assigned by nullable"). IDENTICAL cast-vs-uncast. /tmp/explorer_probe.Myh7On.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.7QjWNi.cbs
- KEY FINDING: the three nullable-cast/return/assign diags are SEMA-LEVEL (DiagnosticBSCSemaKinds.td), gated by safe-zone, firing identically for cast and uncast. The analyzer's getExprPathNullability CStyleCast arm (:354 returns getDefNullability(destType)) IS a launder of the value's path-state, but it is backstopped at every consumer (VisitCStyleCastExpr :708 self-check + Sema). bug_log.md:1412 already documents ":708 CStyleCast site is a different check — nullable→nonnull cast, not a deref" — surface KNOWN.
- NET: no-new-pattern. Cast-launder surface SOUND. The only accept is non-_Safe/non-NC_ALL which is the documented SZ gate (folds with F86's caveat), symmetric for cast and uncast. Recommend next: the getDefNullability owned/borrow default (:246-249 returns NonNull for unannotated owned/borrow) interacting with __move_to_raw/__take_from_raw builtins (getExprPathNullability :331-336 forwards through them) — does a nullable owned moved-to-raw then taken-back launder? OR the FindNonnull recursion (:256-282) on a struct/array DECL-init default-init path.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — nested/2-D `_Borrow _ArrayElem` element-of-element + row-vs-element overlap (post-rewrite bin 34e6f26e / src 18111bd2)
Focus: with `int m[2][2]`, does the BorrowChecker get the right conflict decision for row-vs-element overlap (`&_Mut m[0]` row + `&_Mut m[0][0]` element), both nesting orders, write-while-borrowed of a sibling element, 2-D init-list referent aliasing, and the 2-D-owned/whole-array pointer-borrow shapes. Source read: Path build VisitArraySubscriptExprOrUnaryDeref (:808-846) — `m[0]`→"m[]", `m[0][0]`→"m[][]" (m[] is structural prefix); Path::to_string/structurallyEquals/prefixes (BSCBorrowChecker.h:163-252 — index value never stored, UsesArraySubscriptNotation display-only, prefixes() walks base chain). Continuation of the prior cycle's explicit recommendation (line 6291).
- shape: `&_Mut m[0]` whole-row borrow constructible? (`int(*_Borrow _ArrayElem row)[2]=&_Mut m[0]`) → ACCEPTED with full aggregate init `{{1,2},{3,4}}`; with element-wise init only → FP "use of uninitialized value: m" (separate init-analysis array quirk, not this surface). /tmp/explorer_probe.wfqjKK.cbs
- shape: ROW m[0] (m[]) + ELEMENT m[0][0] (m[][]) overlap, both mut+live → REJECTED "cannot borrow `m[][]` more than once"; baseline element of SEPARATE array n → ACCEPTED. SOUND (prefix-overlap detected). /tmp/explorer_probe.HLbJF3.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.* 
- shape: REVERSE nesting — element m[0][0] FIRST then row m[0] (prefix borrowed after longer path) → REJECTED "cannot borrow `m[]` more than once"; baseline row of separate 2-D array → ACCEPTED. SOUND (prefix walk works both directions). /tmp/explorer_probe.jjcQYR.cbs
- shape: borrow m[0][0], then DIRECT-write sibling m[0][1] (collapses to m[][]) while borrow live → REJECTED "cannot assign to `m[][]` because borrowed"; NLL baseline (write after last use) → ACCEPTED. SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.1xwkwY.cbs
- shape: 2-D OWNED heap array element borrow + free-whole-array → SHAPE-REJECTED (safe_malloc_array is generic `<T>` 2-arg; `int(*)[2]` element rejects the `void*_Owned _ArrayElem` cast in safe_free_array — generic/OOS territory, not constructible). /tmp/explorer_probe.mJBQJb.cbs
- shape: whole-2-D-array pointer-borrow (`&_Mut m`→int(*)[2][2]) + element borrow overlap → SHAPE-REJECTED twice (plain `_Borrow` subscript disallowed; and `int(*_Borrow)[2][2]`→`_ArrayElem` slot is "forbidden conversion in safe zone"). Whole-array pointer-borrow not reachable in safe code. /tmp/explorer_probe.hvKYCE.cbs
- shape: 2-D init-list of mut borrow pointers `{{&_Mut x,&_Mut y},{&_Mut x,&_Mut y}}` (x,y each borrowed twice) → REJECTED "cannot borrow `x`/`y` more than once"; baseline 4 distinct referents → ACCEPTED. SOUND (nested init-list numeric-index paths preserve referent-aliasing detection at 2-D). /tmp/explorer_probe.C9Ky80.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.* 
- NET: nested/2-D `_Borrow _ArrayElem` borrow precision is SOUND in every reachable direction. Row-vs-element overlap is caught via Path prefix relationship (m[] prefix of m[][]) in BOTH nesting orders; write-while-borrowed + NLL correct; 2-D init-list referent-aliasing correct. Over-collapse of disjoint constant elements (m[0][0] vs m[1][0] both "m[][]") = documented unified-element intent (FP, not filable, mirror of 1-D). Whole-array pointer-borrow + 2-D-owned-heap shapes are Sema-shape-rejected before reaching the borrow checker. No new root cause. Recommendation next: leave 2-D ArrayElem (saturated-sound); pivot to the init-analysis array quirk noted above (element-wise init `m[0][0]=..` does NOT mark `m` initialized for a whole-row `&_Mut m[0]` borrow → "use of uninitialized value" FP) — likely a documented array limitation but worth one differential vs a struct-field analogue.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-exploiter blast-radius for BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:819-825 (ActionExtract::VisitArraySubscriptExprOrUnaryDeref LHS branch) + :1211-1226 (LoansKilledByWriteTo) + :1168-1176 (Use re-gen)
Pattern: reassigning a _Borrow ARRAY ELEMENT via subscript (arr[0]=&_Mut y) does NOT kill the loan the element held → fresh borrow of original source wrongly REJECTED ("cannot borrow X as mutable more than once") — FP/NLL counterpart of F39 (FN). defect_class=C7, severity=MEDIUM. Original repro /tmp/explorer_repro.RYOFTW.cbs reproduces (exit=1, line-17 "cannot borrow x"); baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.P2icOz.cbs CLEAN (exit=0). Dedup vs F39: distinct (F39=FN direction, this=FP direction).
- shape: subscript-create + subscript-kill, NO init-list (arr[0]=&_Mut x; arr[0]=&_Mut y; &_Mut x; use arr[0]) — FP reproduces at line 15 (note → subscript-CREATE, same as original). EXTRA secondary error "use of uninitialized value: arr" at line 17 (init-analysis quirk for subscript-written array elements under _Safe) — does NOT change borrow fix surface. → FOLDED. /tmp/exploiter_variant.ILlKx6.cbs
- shape: shared &_Const loan reassigned-then-reused (arr[0]=&_Const x; arr[0]=&_Const y; &_Const x) — CLEAN (exit=0). Shared borrows are allowed to overlap by design, so clean compile is expected regardless of the bug; probe did not separate signal. → INCONCLUSIVE. /tmp/exploiter_variant.cffmPa.cbs
- shape: multi-element arr[i] reuse reviving arr[j] loan (arr[2]={&_Mut x,&_Mut y}; arr[1]=&_Mut z; &_Mut y) — FP reproduces at line 16 (note → init-list &_Mut y). Confirms not index-specific; reassigning a DIFFERENT index (arr[1]) still fails to kill the loan on the init-list-established element. → FOLDED. /tmp/exploiter_variant.57yait.cbs
- shape: scalar-pointer-to-array-element (scalar _Borrow p; p=&_Mut dat[0]; p=&_Mut dat[1]; &_Mut dat[0]) — FP reproduces but via a DIFFERENT mechanism: LHS of reassigning BinAssign is a plain scalar DeclRefExpr `p` (NOT a subscript), so the :819-825 LHS-subscript collapse branch is never entered; the subscript `dat[1]` is on the RHS borrow source and `dat` is a real int[] (not borrow-qualified) so ArrayElemSubscriptNotation=false. Control A (scalar holder, scalar borrowed values x/y) compiles CLEAN — only difference is borrowed-lvalue is scalar vs array element. Error name is `dat[]` (whole-array region) vs original's `x` (scalar). Fix surface (LoansKilledByWriteTo path-prefix / array-region granularity for element-borrow loans) DIVERGES from the conductor's named :819-825 LHS-collapse site. → DISTINCT-ROOT-CAUSE (escalate as new Explorer-tier finding). /tmp/exploiter_variant.IviRnD.cbs ; control A /tmp/ctrlA.cbs (CLEAN) ; control B /tmp/ctrlB.cbs (errs, == variant 4) ; control C /tmp/ctrlC.cbs (errs, expected conflict).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-exploiter (then main-thread) — ExprBSC.cpp isNullExpr blast-radius + file
Pattern: Expr::isNullExpr (ExprBSC.cpp:26-55) dyn_cast ladder missing DeclRefExpr/ConditionalOperator arms → null-owned-var copy mis-classified non-null → ownership setToOwned → checkMemoryLeak FP leak. defect_class=C2+C7, severity=MEDIUM.

Baseline `N` alone CLEAN (isNullExpr handles nullptr literal); `p = nullptr` literal CLEAN. → CONFIRMED-F108 (distinct root from F61 struct-copy else-branch :2381; F108 is pointer-branch :2357 + ExprBSC isNullExpr DeclRef arm missing). repro sem_tests/repro/F108_owned_nullvar_copy_false_leak.cbs
- shape: assign-path `p = nullptr; p = N;` reassign (BSCOwnership.cpp:2201 gate) — same FP leak. → FOLDED-F108 (same isNullExpr-DeclRef-arm-missing root; both :2358 init and :2201 assign gates share the predicate).
- shape: conditional `p = (c ? N : N);` both branches null var — FP leak (ConditionalOperator also has no isNullExpr arm). → FOLDED-F108 (minimal proof is the plain copy above; conditional is a secondary manifestation of the same missing-arm root).
- shape: `_Owned p = N` (non-_Nullable destination) — REJECTED "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable pointer" (correct; nullability gate works). → confirms the bug is ownership-isNullExpr-gate-only, nullability gate is sound here.
- shape: `(c ? nullptr : nullptr)` literal ternary — REJECTED by Sema "non-pointer operand type nullptr_t incompatible with nullptr" (needs a typed-null branch). → SHAPE-REJECTED (Sema forbids nullptr_t in ternary; use typed null var N as in the folded conditional above).
- dangling-borrow escape (return &_Const local; out-param store): SHAPE-REJECTED — returning/escaping a
  _Borrow to a local doesn't parse (illegal global-_Borrow / syntax). Construct not reachable. No finding.
- generic fn taking _Owned, caller use-after-move (consume<int>(p); free(p)): PROBED-SOUND — move-tracking
  SURVIVES monomorphization; the post-call free is rejected "invalid cast ... of moved value: p" (both
  _Safe consume signatures). No finding.
- value assigned only inside for-loop body, used after (n<=0 path uninit): PROBED-SOUND — "use of
  possibly uninitialized value: x" correctly flagged; control (x=0 before loop) clean. No finding.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — _Borrow of _Owned struct FIELD overlap (sibling/whole-struct/const-vs-mut/move) on fresh binary 34e6f26e / src 18111bd2
Focus (per directive): take immutable/mutable _Borrow of an _Owned struct FIELD while that field (or a sibling) is moved/borrowed — does the borrow checker reject overlapping access correctly? Variants: &_Const of s.f while s.f borrowed _Mut; borrow s.f then move s.f; borrow whole s while a field is borrowed. Mapped FindLoansThatIntersect/FindLoansThatFreeze/FrozenByBorrowOf (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:1501-1598) + Path::prefixes/supportingPrefixes/structurallyEquals (header :188-236).
- shape: borrow s.f _Mut (owned field) then MOVE disjoint sibling s.g → ACCEPTED clean (SOUND; siblings disjoint, no prefix overlap). Baseline: borrow s.f then move SAME s.f → REJECTED ("field memory leak of s, s.f is leak" — caught via ownership not borrow-conflict, but still rejected). /tmp/explorer_probe.H0e1Ts.cbs, baseline /tmp/explorer_probe.PzsJLj.cbs
- shape: _Const s.f then _Mut SAME s.f (plain int field) → REJECTED "cannot borrow s.f as mutable because also borrowed as immutable" (SOUND). /tmp/explorer_probe.9zyVPz.cbs
- shape: _Mut s.f then _Const SAME s.f → REJECTED "cannot borrow s.f as immutable because also borrowed as mutable" (SOUND; symmetric). /tmp/explorer_probe.ETP40n.cbs
- shape: _Const s.f + _Const s.g (two shared disjoint siblings) → ACCEPTED clean (SOUND; shared loans coexist). /tmp/explorer_probe.MSPDSi.cbs
- shape: borrow s.f _Mut (owned) then MOVE whole struct s (take_whole(s)) → REJECTED "cannot move out of s because it is borrowed" note "s.f is borrowed here" (SOUND; field-borrow freezes whole-struct move via prefix overlap). /tmp/explorer_probe.Gw5o4s.cbs
- shape: borrow WHOLE s _Mut then move sibling field s.g → REJECTED "cannot move out of s.g because it is borrowed" note "s is borrowed here" (SOUND; whole-struct mut loan is prefix of every field). /tmp/explorer_probe.y3AuaL.cbs
- shape: borrow s.f _Mut then borrow whole s _Const → REJECTED "cannot borrow s as immutable because also borrowed as mutable" (SOUND). /tmp/explorer_probe.wSRVA8.cbs
- shape: borrow whole s _Const then borrow s.f _Mut (inverse) → REJECTED "cannot borrow s.f as mutable because also borrowed as immutable" (SOUND; symmetric). /tmp/explorer_probe.yuxmIo.cbs
- shape: borrow (*p).f _Mut where p is _Borrow pointer, reassign p to s2, use *bf → ACCEPTED clean. NOT a bug: bf captured the concrete address of s.f at borrow time; reassigning the non-owning _Borrow view p does NOT move s.f, so bf stays valid. FrozenByBorrowOf line 1585 explicitly stops at non-owned pointer bases FOR THIS reason (sound). Baseline (bf to s.f directly) also ACCEPTED. /tmp/explorer_probe.Ygl5Tu.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline.3u4Uhf.cbs
- shape: OWNED pointer p, borrow (*p).f _Mut then MOVE p (consume_owned(p)) → REJECTED "cannot move out of p because it is borrowed" note "(*p).f is borrowed here" (SOUND; FrozenByBorrowOf does NOT stop for owned-pointer base → owned-ptr move IS frozen, unlike _Borrow-ptr reassign). Confirms line-1585 owned-vs-non-owned distinction is correct. /tmp/explorer_probe.aaLzAa.cbs
- shape: borrow s.f _Mut then call writer(&_Mut s) whose body writes (*ps).f → REJECTED "cannot borrow s as mutable more than once" (the &_Mut s arg call-site trips whole-vs-field overlap; SOUND). Call-body-write blindness moot (call site already blocked). /tmp/explorer_probe.PKS5iN.cbs
- NET: no-new-pattern. The _Owned-struct-FIELD borrow overlap surface is SOUND in every reachable direction on 34e6f26e/18111bd2: sibling-disjoint access correctly allowed; same-field const-vs-mut both directions rejected; field-vs-whole-struct overlap (move whole s, borrow whole s, both const/mut directions) correctly rejected via prefix/supportingPrefixes intersection; the FrozenByBorrowOf line-1585 owned-vs-non-owned-pointer-base distinction is sound (owned-ptr move frozen, _Borrow/raw-ptr reassign not — correct, since reassigning a non-owning view doesn't free memory). The focus area is saturated-sound. Recommend next: widen OFF this surface — the ONE adjacent untested angle is whether a CALL that writes to s.f through a _Borrow arg is caught when the call does NOT itself take &_Mut s at the call site (e.g. a _Borrow arg already in scope re-used) — but that requires an already-borrowed s, which itself conflicts, so likely unreachable; better pivot to BSCOwnership move-vs-borrow-state interaction (the "field memory leak" diagnostic on same-field move-while-borrowed suggests the borrow-conflict diag may be masked by the leak check — worth one diff to see if the borrow-conflict diag fires FIRST or is suppressed).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — nullability × init: uninit _Nonnull slot (struct field / local / ensure_init)
Focus (per directive): a struct field `int*_Nonnull` left uninitialized then read/dereffed, or a
`_Nonnull` local read before assignment — does `_Safe` catch the uninit nonnull? Distinct from F81.
Binary 34e6f26e / src 18111bd2. Init analysis is FULLY nullability-agnostic (0 `nullab`/`nonnull`
refs in BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp) — inside `_Safe`, field-granular init analysis is the SOLE uninit guard;
nullability's `getDefNullability` returns NonNull for `_Nonnull` and suppresses its own deref check
(F86). So the composition question is whether init catches every uninit `_Nonnull` READ before
nullability's NonNull-assumption is relied upon.

- shape: `_Borrow _Nonnull` field uninit, `*s.p` deref in `_Safe` → REJECT "use of uninitialized: `s.p`" (init guard fires; SOUND). /tmp/expl_borrow_nn.cbs
- shape: `_Borrow _Nonnull` field DEFAULT-ZEROED via `{0}` → REJECT "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable" (nullability CheckInit/FindNonnull fires at decl; the F78 fix; SOUND). /tmp/expl_borrow_nn_zero.cbs
- shape: raw `int*_Nonnull` field uninit, read into `int*_Nonnull q = s.p` in `_Safe` (no contract) → REJECT "use of uninitialized: `s`/`s.p`" (init guard; SOUND). /tmp/expl_base_no_contract.cbs
- shape: raw `int*_Nonnull` field, `_Safe` def with `__attribute__((ensure_init))` writes only `.x` → REJECT "'*out' not initialized at return" (callee-side checkEnsureInitAtReturn fires; SOUND). /tmp/expl_base_safe_def.cbs
- shape: return struct by value with uninit `_Nonnull` field → REJECT (init checks return operand whole-struct; SOUND). /tmp/expl_return_struct.cbs
- shape: pass struct by value with uninit `_Nonnull` field → REJECT "use of uninitialized: `s`" (SOUND). /tmp/expl_pass_struct.cbs
- shape: `_Nullable`-field → `_Nonnull`-field copy `n.p = u.p` → REJECT "nonnull cannot be assigned by nullable" (nullability assignment check; SOUND). /tmp/expl_struct_copy.cbs
- shape: `_Nonnull` field written `(int*)0` in ensure_init callee → REJECT "nonnull pointer cannot be assigned by nullable" (SOUND). /tmp/expl_nn_field_write.cbs
- shape: **hetero ensure_init (UNCONDITIONAL), _Unsafe def FIRST (writes .x, never .p) + _Safe decl AFTER adds `ensure_init` + _Safe caller reads `s.p`** → **ACCEPTED clean (exit 0)** + valgrind ERROR SUMMARY 1 (use of uninit value in `if(q==0)` branch). /tmp/expl_hetero_runtime.cbs
  - asymmetry (3-way, all on same bin): (A) `_Safe` def with ensure_init → REJECT "'*out' not initialized at return" (checkEnsureInitAtReturn); (B) hetero def that WRITES .p → CLEAN (genuinely init); (C) hetero NO ensure_init contract → REJECT "use of uninitialized: `s`/`s.p`". Only the hetero+ensure_init+def-first cell launders.
  - **FOLD into F104.** F104's blast-radius note (2026-06-08) explicitly states: "affects BOTH init contracts — `ensure_init` (unconditional) is laundered the same way (unsafe def first + safe decl adds `ensure_init` → caller reads uninit `*out`, valgrind-confirmed), not just `ensure_init_if_ret`. Same retroactive-non-enforcement root; one fix covers both." My probe is exactly that documented unconditional-`ensure_init` variant (F104 was filed on the `ensure_init_if_ret` form; the unconditional form is its documented blast radius). Same root (CheckBSCEnsureInitIfRetRedecl cross-safety one-directional + order-dependent retroactive non-enforcement), same fix surface. NOT a new filing. Note: `.fresh_verdict.tsv` row `F104 fixed` is a mis-mark — the F104 repro STILL compiles clean (exit 0) on 34e6f26e (the FN persists); the verdict's "ensure_init non-_Safe FP-fix" note refers to a DIFFERENT ensure_init non-_Safe FP, not the F104 launder. Both `ensure_init_if_ret` (F104 repro) and unconditional `ensure_init` (this probe) still launder.
- shape: `__assume_initialized(&_Mut s)` whole-struct credit of uninit `_Nonnull .p` → SHAPE-REJECTED ("requires '&' expression" — `__assume_initialized` only accepts a plain `&`, banned in `_Safe`; by-design trust builtin, F15 territory). /tmp/expl_assume_init2.cbs

NET: no-new-pattern. The nullability×init composition for uninit `_Nonnull` is SOUND inside `_Safe`
on every basic shape (uninit field read/deref, default-zero, struct copy, return/pass-by-value,
null-write-to-nonnull) — init analysis (field-granular) and nullability CheckInit (decl-time) jointly
backstop. The ONE laundering cell (hetero ensure_init, def-first, unconditional) is the documented
F104 blast-radius variant (FOLD-F104), not new. F86 already owns the non-`_Safe` direction. Recommend
next: widen OFF this surface — the ensure_init × `_Nonnull` POINTNEE-FIELD candidate (callee partial-
inits a struct pointee, caller credits whole pointee via markAllFieldsInit) is backstopped by
checkEnsureInitAtReturn on `_Safe` defs and by F104 on hetero defs; saturated. Better pivot: the
BSCIRInitAnalysis `markAllFieldsInit` recursion on a pointee with a NESTED union/empty-field (F106
cousin) under ensure_init — does an empty field in the pointee block promotion (FP) or launder (FN)?

NOT F64 (F64 outer RAW/unowned → untracked; here outer _Owned → tracked, double-consume baseline proves move recorded). ## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — bare-statement use-after-move READ (runOnBlock 5-kind filter, BSCOwnership.cpp:2652-2657)
Focus (per directive): _Owned ownership transfer via RETURN values / dropped returns / use-after-move reads. Read VisitReturnStmt (:2441, op=Move for non-int return), runOnBlock (:2641-2675, 5-kind CFG-element allowlist: DeclStmt|CallExpr|assign-BO|inc-dec-UO|ReturnStmt), HandleDREUse (:2504+). Candidate: a bare expression statement whose top-level Expr is NOT in the 5-kind allowlist (UnaryOperator UO_Deref, CStyleCastExpr, non-assign BinaryOperator) is skipped by runOnBlock → HandleDREUse never runs → a use-after-move READ in that bare statement is ACCEPTED in _Safe.
- shape: `int *_Owned p = safe_malloc<int>(1); ...; consume_owned(p); *p;` (bare UO_Deref statement after move) → ACCEPTED clean (exit 0, only -Wunused-value). Baseline `int x = *p;` (same deref bound to DeclStmt) → REJECTED "use of moved value: `p`" (exit 1). Asymmetry PROVES it is the bare-statement-kind filter, not the move-tracking (the move IS recorded — the bound form is caught). /tmp/explorer_probe.ZkSU4s.cbs / baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.DJhM6A.cbs
- shape: `(void)*p;` bare CStyleCast statement after move → ACCEPTED clean (exit 0). Confirms the gap is NOT UO_Deref-specific — ANY bare-statement kind outside the 5-kind allowlist slips. /tmp/explorer_probe.LEuDuT.cbs (renamed PA)
- shape: `*p = 99;` bare assignment statement after move → REJECTED "use of moved value: `p`" (exit 1). CONFIRMS the filter boundary: assign-BO IS in the allowlist (visited) → caught; bare READ forms are NOT → slip. The asymmetry pins the root to runOnBlock:2652-2657. /tmp/explorer_probe.I1eB9f.cbs
- shape: `(*p, 0);` bare comma (non-assign BO) statement after move → ACCEPTED clean (exit 0). Ownership's VisitBinaryOperator DOES special-case BO_Comma (:2214) — but only WHEN VISITED; runOnBlock never visits a non-assign BO, so the comma handler never runs. Same filter root. /tmp/explorer_probe.MKEbSw.cbs-area (PC)
- runtime: the bare `*p;` load IS emitted in LLVM IR (`%6 = load i32, ptr %5` after the free call) BUT is dead-code-eliminated by the backend at -O0 (disasm: `callq consume_owned` → `retq`, no load) because the result is unused → valgrind reports 0 invalid-reads. So the minimal repro is a COMPILE-TIME FN (static-_Safe-contract violation), not a runtime UAF. The read of freed memory is real in the IR but elided in the binary.
- NET: CONFIRMED-new (compile-time FN; MEDIUM — static-contract violation, minimal repro has no runtime UB due to DCE). Root: BSCOwnership.cpp:2652-2657 runOnBlock 5-kind filter excludes bare UnaryOperator(UO_Deref)/CStyleCastExpr/non-assign-BinaryOperator statements → HandleDREUse never runs → use-after-move READ accepted in _Safe. DISTINCT from F64 (F64 = IsTrackedType doesn't recognize `int*_Owned*`; here the type IS tracked `int*_Owned` — the bound form catches the move, proving the move IS recorded; the slip is purely the statement-kind filter). DISTINCT from F98 (F98 = BSCIR/init analysis brace-less-switch BODY blindness; here = OWNERSHIP analyzer CFG-element filter, different analyzer). DISTINCT from F02/F03 (F02/F03 = move not RECORDED when consume is in a control-flow CONDITION; here the move IS recorded, the bug is the subsequent bare-statement READ being skipped). DISTINCT from F11 (F11 = borrow-checker DefUse comma; here = ownership runOnBlock filter, and ownership's BO_Comma handler is sound WHEN visited — the issue is non-visitation). DISTINCT from F91 (F91 = void-cast else-branch subfield-owned check; different function VisitCStyleCastExpr). Scope-gate clean (no OOS keywords; standard C + _Owned/_Safe/safe_malloc/safe_free). In scope. Repro /tmp/explorer_probe.ZkSU4s.cbs; baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.DJhM6A.cbs. Defect class C3 (Visit*/CFG-element coverage gap — a bare-statement shape the runOnBlock filter doesn't visit). Severity MEDIUM.
