DEDUP = FOLDED-F22: F22's catalog explicitly defines its surface as "wherever a leaking-temp can appear without going through ActOnExprStmt AND without triggering the comparison-op branch at SemaExpr.cpp:16557" — the `&&`/`||` operand position is exactly on the non-comparison-op side. F22's fix (CheckFullExpressionLeak helper / invoke from each expression-acceptor) covers it. NOT a new root cause. /tmp/explorer_repro_land.63iey4.cbs (&& leak), /tmp/explorer_base_land.MZPxxY.cbs (== diag control), /tmp/explorer_lor.GR1F2A.cbs (|| leak), /tmp/explorer_diff.WOpJS2.cbs (combined diff)
- read: CheckReturnStmtMemoryLeak (SemaBSCOwnedStruct.cpp:37) → CheckExprMemoryLeak::VisitMemberExpr (:24) dyn_cast<DeclRefExpr>(ME->getBase()) w/o IgnoreParenCasts on base → C1 ignore-asymmetry (paren-wrap of base bypasses). BUT trigger gated on isOwnedStructureType() = `_Owned struct` = OUT OF SCOPE per CLAUDE.md. No in-scope reproducible path. OOS-gated; not filed. (Confirms prior explorer's "OOS-gated" conclusion.)
NET: no-new-pattern. The CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak (F21/F25/F62-fixed/F79) and CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak (F14/F22/F47/F20) wrapper-bypass surfaces are SATURATED on 34e6f26e: every probed wrapper/inner-expr class either folds into an existing filed root or is shape-rejected as unreachable / OOS-gated. Recommend next: pivot OFF Sema move/temp-leak — the function-reading Mode-1 frontier (BSCIRBuilder lowerExpr dispatch, BSCBorrowChecker Loans/Liveness unread functions) still has unwritten invariants.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — BORROW checker F111-analog (bare exprstmt using _Borrow)
- shape: heap _Owned o; `int *_Borrow b = &_Mut *o; safe_free((void*_Owned)o); *b;` (bare UO_Deref
  exprstmt USE of b after the referent should be dead) → REJECTED "cannot move out of `o` because
  it is borrowed" (rc=1). Baseline (no use of b at all) → ACCEPTED (rc=0). Asymmetry PROVES the
  bare `*b;` is recorded as a use (loan stays live → conflict caught). **INCONCLUSIVE→FOLDED
  (no gap)**: the borrow checker has NO F111-analog block-level allowlist. Source-confirmed:
  Liveness::SimulateBlock (:1069-1093) and LoansInScope::SimulateBlock (:1239-1276) iterate EVERY
  CFGStmt (no top-level-kind filter, unlike ownership runOnBlock :2647-2657 / F111); DefUse::VisitUnaryDeref
  (:173, Action=Use → Gen b) and ActionExtract::VisitUnaryDeref (:849→:808, Kind=Noop→Use, pushes
  Source `*b`) BOTH handle the bare deref. DISTINCT from F111 (different analyzer — ownership's
  block-level filter; the gap that exists there does NOT exist here). Folds into the prior
  bare-borrow probes (_probed.md:1558-1564, all correctly diagnosed) + the E4 retraction reasoning
  (a discarded borrow read is correctly modeled by NLL; loan ends at last use if it's bare).
- shape: `(void)*b;` (CStyleCastExpr-wrapped bare deref, after the free) → REJECTED (same move-while-
  -borrowed error). Confirms no gap on the cast-wrapper bare shape either.
- shape: `(b == b);` (non-assign BinaryOperator bare exprstmt, after the free) → REJECTED (same
  error). Confirms no gap on the comparison bare shape either.
  All three F111-named bare shapes (F111 breadth = 4 excluded kinds) are CAUGHT here — the inverse
  of F111, where each was skipped. Files: /tmp/explorer_probe.4ICTub.cbs (+baseline .SIK23D.cbs).
  Note appended to code_notes/BSCBorrowChecker.md (SimulateBlock CFG-element iteration entry).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — BSCIRInitAnalysis ARRAY element-granularity vs whole-array read (array-analog-of-F107 hypothesis, binary 34e6f26e)
Focus (per directive): array-ELEMENT / designated-hole granularity in init-analysis. HYPOTHESIS: writing one array element then a WHOLE-array read (copy / pass-by-value / return) launders uninit elements (array analog of F107 union-variant-width over-credit). Also array-of-struct partial element init then whole use; and designated `{[0]=1}` holes then whole-array use. Baselines: fully-init array used = clean; no-init array used = rejected. Dedup vs F106 (empty-struct-field), F107 (union variant width), F83 (indexed-write-dest uninit), and RETRACTED F97 (element-wise array init FP).
- shape (BASE1): `int a[2]={1,2}; sink_arr(a); return a[0];` → CLEAN (exit 0). positive control. /tmp/explorer_baseline.eaKeEW.cbs
- shape (BASE2): `int a[2]; sink_arr(a);` → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: a". negative control. /tmp/explorer_baseline.3oRPWi.cbs
- shape: `int a[2]; a[0]=1; return a[1];` (read uninit element after partial elem write) → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: a". SOUND — element writes do NOT mark the array Init (getFieldPath None / :172 break); whole-local read rejected. /tmp/explorer_probe.L4ANrS.cbs
- shape: `int a[2]; a[0]=1; sink_arr(a);` (whole-array pass-by-value after partial elem write) → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: a". SOUND (Call terminator CD.Args checked). /tmp/explorer_probe.TLw0tr.cbs
- shape: `struct Pair a[2]; a[0].x=1; return a[0];` (whole-element return of partially-init element) → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: a". SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.x5WB1h.cbs
- shape: `struct S a[2]; a[0].x=1; return a[0].y;` (read uninit sibling field of element) → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: a". SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe.uEQOWL.cbs
- shape: `int a[3]={[0]=1}; return a[2];` (designated-init WITH HOLES then whole-array read) → ACCEPT (exit 0). SOUND — C zero-fills the holes to a DEFINED value (cf R3 aggregate-init :411-418); not a launder. /tmp/explorer_probe.sM7wBY.cbs
- shape: `struct W{int a[2];int q;} w; w.a[0]=1; w.a[1]=2; w.q=3; return w;` (array FIELD, all elements + scalar written, whole-struct return) → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: w". FP — array field 'a' can never be markFieldInit'd (:172 isArrayType break) → tryPromoteParent sibling-loop never sees 'a' Init → w never promotes. **FOLDED into RETRACTED F97**: this is the EXACT documented `rule6_struct_error` case (BiShengCLanguageUserManual.md:5839-5845, §3.7.3 rule 6 line 5811 "此规则同样适用于结构体中的数组字段——对数组元素的逐个写入不会将该字段标记为已初始化"). F97 was RETRACTED 2026-06-08 because this is documented intended behavior. Control: the {}-init form `struct W w={{1,2},3}; return w;` (documented rule6_struct_ok) → CLEAN + valgrind ERROR SUMMARY 0 → proves PRECISION FP, no soundness hole. /tmp/explorer_probe.AYaM6v.cbs (FP), /tmp/<probe6ok> ({}-init control CLEAN+vg-clean)
NET: no-new-pattern. The array-element-granularity gap hypothesized (array analog of F107) does NOT exist — arrays do NOT get the F107 whole-local-Init over-promotion on element writes; element writes leave the array Uninit and ALL whole-array reads (copy/pass-by-value/return) are correctly REJECTED. The one FP (array-field-in-struct blocking parent promotion) is the documented §3.7.3 rule 6 `rule6_struct_error` (FOLDED into retracted F97 — documented intended behavior, precision not soundness). The designated-init-with-holes case is SOUND (C zero-fill). Recommend next: pivot OFF the array-element init surface (saturated; the all-or-nothing array model is documented intended). The remaining open BSCIRInitAnalysis surfaces are the ensure_init×_Nonnull pointee composition (composition_init_null.md) and the union write-small-read-large tail is F107 (filed).

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — plain generic-alias qualifier-survival (Owned<T>=T*_Owned etc., bin 34e6f26e)
Focus (per directive): GENERIC TYPE ALIAS adding an ownership/borrow/nullability QUALIFIER.
HYPOTHESIS: G01 found conditional<> drops nullability; the PLAIN generic-alias analogue may drop
_Owned/_Borrow/_Nonnull on substitution. PROBE the plain alias and compare EACH to the direct form.
Dedup: G01 (conditional desugar→canonical strips _Nonnull sugar), G10 (Arr<T>=T[N] owned-ARRAY check
declarator-path-only), G12 (generic-FN typearg strips nullability sugar). Distinct target = plain
generic-alias qualifier-survival for _Owned/_Borrow/_Nonnull on a LOCAL SCALAR (not conditional,
not array-alias, not generic-fn typearg).
- shape #1: `typedef Owned<T>=T*_Owned; Owned<int> p=safe_malloc<int>(1);` (no free) → REJECTED
  "memory leak of value: p" (rc=1), IDENTICAL to direct `int*_Owned p=safe_malloc`. _Owned QUALIFIER
  SURVIVES the generic-alias substitution into the leak-checker path (cf. G10 where the array CHECK is
  bypassed — here the qualifier itself survives; distinct). probe /tmp/explorer_probe.r4u2yQ.cbs
  baseline /tmp/explorer_baseline.f35d1o.cbs (direct form, same diag). SOUND.
- shape #2: `typedef Ref<T>=T*_Borrow; Ref<int> a=&_Mut x; Ref<int> b=&_Mut x;` (double mut borrow) →
  REJECTED "cannot borrow x as mutable more than once" (rc=1). _Borrow rules ENFORCED through alias,
  parity with direct form. /tmp/expl_ref_double.7WUAlZ.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #3: `typedef NN<T>=T*_Nonnull; NN<int> p=nullptr;` → REJECTED (raw-deref `*p` guard fires first;
  null-init parity confirmed per G01: alias `NN<T>` correctly rejects like direct). /tmp/expl_nn_null.pzBpRV.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #4: `typedef Owned<T>=T*_Owned; consume(p); *p=9;` (use-after-move via alias) → REJECTED
  "use of moved value: p" (rc=1), IDENTICAL to direct form. Move-tracking survives alias substitution.
  /tmp/expl_owned_move.SbS7Ac.cbs baseline /tmp/expl_owned_move_base.0D6Sky.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #5: `struct S{ Owned<int> f; }; s.f=safe_malloc;` (owned field via alias, no free) → REJECTED
  "field memory leak of value: s, s.f is leak" (rc=1). Field-leak tracking survives alias. /tmp/expl_field_alias.qg7juX.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #6b: `Owned<Owned<int>> bop = safe_malloc<int*_Owned>(inner);` (2-level nested alias → BOP
  (int*_Owned)*_Owned, neither freed) → REJECTED with TWO diags ("memory leak of value: bop" +
  "field memory leak ... *bop"). Nested-alias qualifier survival sound at depth 2. /tmp/expl_nested_alias2.GYVvIO.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #7: two alias-owned locals `Box<int> p,q` neither freed → REJECTED with two leak diags. /tmp/expl_twoparam_alias.nOSPa4.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #8: `typedef OB<T>=T*_Owned _Borrow; OB<int> p=&_Mut x;` (alias combines owned+borrow) →
  REJECTED "incompatible _Borrow types, cannot cast 'int *_Borrow' to 'int *_Owned _Borrow'" (3 errors).
  Combined-qualifier substitution seen by the type-checker (no launder). /tmp/expl_owned_borrow_alias.SzENwq.cbs. SOUND.
- shape #9: `typedef NNO<T>=T*_Owned _Nonnull; NNO<int> p=nullptr;` → REJECTED "nonnull pointer cannot
  be assigned by nullable pointer". Owned+nonnull combined qualifier survives; nullability gate fires. /tmp/expl_nn_owned_alias.obMrvS.cbs. SOUND.
NET: no-new-pattern. The plain generic-alias qualifier-survival surface is SATURATED-SOUND on 34e6f26e:
every qualifier (_Owned/_Borrow/_Nonnull) and every analyzer path (leak / borrow-conflict / move-tracking /
field-leak / nested-BOP / combined-qualifier / nullability-gate) treats the alias-substituted type
IDENTICALLY to the direct form. The qualifier survives template substitution into the type the analyzers
see (unlike G10, where the owned-array RESTRICTION CHECK is declarator-path-only and bypassed on the
substitution path — a DIFFERENT mechanism: G10 = a check not re-run; here = the qualifier itself survives).
DISTINCT from G01 (conditional desugar→canonical strips _Nonnull SUGAR; here plain alias has no
desugar-to-canonical step that strips qualifiers), G12 (generic-FN typearg substitution strips nullability
sugar; here it's a type-ALIAS not a generic-fn typearg). Recommend next: the plain-generic-alias surface
is exhausted — widen OFF it. Candidate un-probed direction: the generic-alias × MANGLE path (G14 found
generic-STRUCT mangle collision drops _Owned/_Borrow from the record name; the generic-TYPE-ALIAS mangle
path may have an analogous drop, distinct from G14's struct-record path) — check TypePrinter getBSCTemplateArgsName
on a type-alias instantiation used in a -rewrite-bsc context.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — const-generic ARRAY MEMBER × init-analysis (BSCIRInitAnalysis tryPromoteParent/getNumFields, array-field sibling)
Focus (per directive): a const-generic `struct Array<T,int N>{T data[N];}` instantiated `Array<int,3>` (and `Array<int,0>`), field-init'd element-wise then used WHOLE (return/copy/pass-by-value) in `_Safe` — is the uninit array tail caught? Hypothesis: array-member init granularity gap cf F106 (empty-struct-blocks-promotion FP), F107 (union-variant-width FN), F83 (indexed-write-dest). Binary 34e6f26e / src.
- shape: `Array<int,3> a; a.data[0]=1; return a;` (return-by-value whole-struct read) → REJECT "use of uninitialized value: a" (FP). /tmp/explorer_probe.f3aXjv.cbs
- shape: ALL 3 elements written `a.data[0..2]=..; return a;` → STILL REJECT (no element-wise workaround; the array field is never markFieldInit'd). /tmp/explorer_probe.LZlCzr.cbs
- shape: `Array<int,0>` empty array `return a;` → REJECT (zero-length array field ALSO blocks promotion; does NOT launder). /tmp/explorer_probe.QGkngm.cbs
- shape: NON-generic `struct W{int a[2];int q;}; w.a[0..1]=..; w.q=..; return w;` → REJECT (NOT generic-specific). /tmp/explorer_probe.qNvX42.cbs
- baseline A2: single-scalar-field struct `Scalar<int>{int v;} s.v=1; return s;` → ACCEPT (isolates the ARRAY-typed field as the blocker). /tmp/explorer_baseline.PnIfR7.cbs
- baseline B (workaround): `Array<int,3> a = {0}; a.data[0]=1; return a;` → ACCEPT ({}-aggregate-init promotes the whole local directly; the FP has a {}-workaround like F106). /tmp/explorer_baseline.cjsbwh.cbs
- soundness control C: `Array<T,int N>{T data[N]; int r;}`; array elems written, `r` UNWRITTEN, `return a;` → REJECT (genuinely-uninit scalar sibling caught → this is UNDER-promotion FP, NOT an F107-style over-credit FN; directive's F107-laundering hypothesis REFUTED). /tmp/explorer_baseline.Tht8Do.cbs
- discriminator D: known documented local-array FP `int a[3]; a[0..2]=..; return a[0];` → REJECT (same symptom as the struct-array-field case; the struct-array-field case = the documented array-element-init limitation (BSCIRInitAnalysis.md:507-510) wrapped in a struct, NOT a distinct root). /tmp/explorer_baseline.XtiPQo.cbs
- discriminator E: F106 EXACT repro `struct Empty{}; struct W{Empty e;int q;}; w.q=3; return w;` → REJECT on 34e6f26e (F106 STILL OPEN; the array-field case is a sibling variant of the SAME open root, not a re-open). /tmp/explorer_baseline.2FZjPT.cbs
- runtime: {}-workaround binary runs valgrind-clean (ERROR SUMMARY 0; `{0}` genuinely zero-fills the array → the FP rejects valid+provably-safe code). /tmp/explorer_probe.kNwREm.cbs
- NET: **FOLDED into F106 (no new filing).** Root identical: `getNumFields` (BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:834-842) counts an unmarkable sibling (empty-struct in F106; array-typed field here, never markFieldInit'd due to `transferStatement:155 isArrayType break`) → `tryPromoteParent` (:970-977) all-siblings-Init test fails → parent never promotes → whole-struct read flagged uninit. The directive's F107-style laundering (FN) hypothesis is REFUTED by control C (genuinely-uninit case IS rejected → under-promotion, not over-credit). The directive's F83 (indexed-write-dest) hypothesis is REFUTED (opposite direction/symptom). The fix surface (skip array-typed siblings in the promotion count, OR permit whole-array-field marking on {}-init) differs in detail from F106's "skip zero-field siblings" but is the SAME family/root — F106's blast-radius note already covers generic structs + unmarkable siblings; the array field is a blast-radius variant. The const-generic `Array<int,N>` wrapping makes it a FIRST-CLASS canonical-idiom FP (the generics-guide `Array<T,int N>{T data[N]}` example), strengthening F106's severity rationale, but does NOT make it a distinct filing. Recommend Conductor: append the array-field-sibling case to F106's blast-radius note (the ensure_init-pointee variant already noted there). The DIRECTIVE's distinct-root target (const-generic array-member FN) was NOT found — the surface is an FP fold, not the hypothesized laundering.

## 2026-06-23 bsc-explorer cycle — nullability path-MERGE granularity across CFG joins (defect C7, distinct-from-F26) on fresh binary 34e6f26e
Focus (per directive): null-checked in ONLY ONE branch of if/else (or one switch case), dereffed AFTER the join where a path did NOT guarantee non-null (FN); mirror = _Nonnull narrowing lost at back-edge join (FP). Dedup F65/F50/F48/G01; F26 is the filed exemplar for this surface (distinct = path-MERGE granularity). Read mergeVD/mergeFP/mergeDPVD (:844-892), runNullabilityCheck worklist+condition-injection (:1193-1251), initStatus (:813-841), SetCFGBlocksByExpr (:734-774), getExprPathNullability (:312-428). Used the new -dump-nullability-check (cc1) as the per-block state oracle.
- shape: scalar-VD one-path `if(c){if(p==nullptr)return;} *p` + 3-way (all/none/one) → PROBED-SOUND. all=CLEAN, none=REJECT, one=REJECT. mergeVD takes Nullable from unchecked pred. /tmp/explorer_baseline_all.31mQXv.cbs, /tmp/explorer_baseline_none.QfL4G6.cbs, /tmp/explorer_probe.cccCC8.cbs
- shape: VD "p never referenced bare" (only in if(p==nullptr)+*p) → PROBED-SOUND. Dump shows p Nullable at entry in every block (initStatus pre-populates despite no bare DRE). /tmp/explorer_probe_vd2.hotNjQ.cbs
- shape: flat-FP one-path `s(_Nonnull)->f(_Nullable)`, `if(c){if(!s->f)return;} *s->f` under -nullability-check=all → PROBED-SOUND (3-way: all CLEAN/none REJECT/one REJECT). /tmp/explorer_probe_fp3.qqm9kW.cbs + baselines. NOTE: must use -nullability-check=all NOT _Safe (the _Safe zone raw-`*`-forbidden gate masks the nullability test — `*s->f` rejected by "'*' operator is forbidden in the safe zone" regardless of narrowing).
- shape: deeper-FP one-path `s->f->g` (both _Nullable) → PROBED-SOUND. Dump confirms s..f AND s..f.g seeded Nullable at entry, stay Nullable through join. /tmp/explorer_probe_deep.u8z3ha.cbs
- shape: DPVD one-path = F26 RE-VALIDATED on 34e6f26e: STILL REPRODUCES (exit 0 FN). Rewrite did NOT touch mergeDPVD. sem_tests/repro/F26_dpvd_merge_asymmetry.cbs
- shape: loop back-edge FP (narrow before loop, *p after) → PROBED-SOUND. /tmp/explorer_probe_loop.*.cbs
- shape: loop body narrow + back-edge drop (narrow inside while body, *p after) → PROBED-SOUND (zero-iteration path REJECTs). /tmp/explorer_probe_lb.*.cbs
- shape: && short-circuit `if(c && p){...} *p` → PROBED-SOUND (c-false path didn't eval p → after-join REJECT). /tmp/explorer_probe_sc.*.cbs
NET: no-new-pattern. Path-MERGE granularity (mergeVD/mergeFP/mergeDPVD + condition-injection) is SOUND across scalar-VD/flat-FP/deeper-FP/loop-back-edge-both-dir/&&-short-circuit on 34e6f26e. Only live merge unsoundness = F26 (DPVD absent-key), UNCHANGED by rewrite. initStatus pre-population protects VD/FP at every depth probed. SATURATED-sound @ 34e6f26e for the path-merge nullability surface. Recommend next: pivot OFF nullability merges — the BSCIRInitAnalysis merge (meetStates) or BSCOwnership merge (F75 family) on the rewritten binary, OR unread functions in BSCIRBuilder.cpp lowerExpr dispatch.

## F112 — struct-FIELD borrow-holder reassign loan-not-killed FP (BSCBorrowChecker field-liveness) [2026-06-23, GLM bxj0z9qxm → Conductor, FILED]
- SHAPE: `struct B{int*_Borrow f;}; s.f=&_Mut x; (void)*s.f; s.f=&_Mut y; int*_Borrow q=&_Mut x;` → FP "cannot borrow x more than once". Scalar twin (`p=&_Mut x;...p=&_Mut y;&_Mut x`) CLEAN.
- ROOT: holder-reassign loan-kill is LIVENESS-driven (not LoansKilledByWriteTo, which kills sub-paths of WRITTEN path; x-loan keyed on x). LiveRegions whole-variable granularity (:1129) → whole-struct s stays live → x-loan region extends past s.f reassign. DISTINCT from F109 (subscript path collapse; repro has NO subscript so F109 fix can't apply) + F39. C7. FILED F112.

## array-field whole-struct promotion FP — RESOLVED as documented §3.7.3 (NOT F106, NOT filed) [2026-06-23, Conductor; resolves bqawxyzrn vs brne3bbkk disagreement]
- SHAPE: `struct S{int arr[2]; int n;}; s.arr[0]=1; s.arr[1]=2; s.n=5; sink(s);` → FP "use of uninitialized value: s". `{}`-init workaround CLEAN; scalar-only control CLEAN.
- VERDICT: documented all-or-nothing array model (§3.7.3 rule 6) — element writes never mark the array field Init; this is the SAME documented behavior F97 was RETRACTED for. NOT F106 (F106 = empty-struct field that CANNOT be init at all, no workaround; array field HAS the {}-init workaround). bqawxyzrn (array specialist) correct = intended; brne3bbkk (const-gen) mis-folded into F106. Do NOT enrich F106, do NOT file.

## F112 — RETRACTED 2026-06-23 (NOT A BUG, confirmed LIMITATION by user)
- SHAPE: struct-field borrow holder reassign (`s.f=&_Mut x; s.f=&_Mut y; &_Mut x`) → FP "cannot borrow x more than once". Scalar twin clean.
- VERDICT: by-design whole-variable (whole-struct) loan-region liveness; field/sub-place liveness not modeled = KNOWN LIMITATION of the NLL impl, not a fixable defect (same spirit as array all-or-nothing §3.7.3). Comment removed from IJP3AP (204). DO NOT re-probe field/nested/reborrow loan-region-granularity FPs as bugs.

## F113 — ensure_init(_if_ret) on non-param = warn-not-error (spec-rule-must-reject oracle r11) [2026-06-24, FILED]
- SWEEP: 7+ placement rules probed. ALL hard-error EXCEPT ensure_init/ensure_init_if_ret on non-param (local+global) = -Wignored-attributes WARN + silent drop, rc=0.
- ROBUST (correctly error): _Owned on int/double/struct-var/union-member/array-member ("type of X cannot be qualified by '_Owned'"); __assume_initialized non-&-arg + arg-with-subscript.
- ROOT: BSC init attrs use default clang warn-and-ignore subject check, not a dedicated err_ (every sibling ensure_init rule IS err_). FIX: err_ensure_init_not_on_parameter. FILED F113 MEDIUM.

## FULL spec-verifier sweep (oracle spec-rule-must-reject) — 2026-06-24
- TOOL: sem_tests/scripts/spec_verifier.py — extracts every fenced code block w/ a `// error:`/`// 错误:` marker from the manual, compiles in BSC mode, classifies ERROR / WARN-ONLY / ACCEPT (rc=0 = hit). NOTE limitation: the manual usually COMMENTS OUT the violating line (`// code; // error: ...`) so the example compiles → harness sees inert → false ACCEPT. Every ACCEPT must be uncomment-verified by hand.
- COVERAGE BUG FIXED: original blocks() matched bare ``` as an opener → desynced on the 103 bare fences → silently skipped ownership/borrowing/nonnull/generic (only safe-zone+init ran). Rewrote to toggle-state parse (a ``` flips in/out; capture c/C/cbs/bsc/bare lang). Now 75 in-scope error-blocks: ownership 18, borrowing 19, nonnull 6, safe-zone 15, init 14, generic 3.
- VERDICT: COMPLIANT everywhere EXCEPT F113. All manual `// error:` rules enforced — every ACCEPT hit was a commented-out violation OR an intended `// ok` control, uncomment-verified to ERROR: init:314 same-safety ensure_init redecl (→ "conflicting types"); init:334 fnptr ensure_init mismatch (→ "incompatible function pointer types"); init:395/425 __assume_initialized arg rules (→ errors); ownership:730 __move_to_raw order-2-after-inner-freed (→ "use of all moved value") + order-1-inner-still-owning (→ "invalid cast ... not all moved value"). PLACEMENT MATRIX all hard-error: _Owned×5, _Borrow×2, _Nonnull/_Nullable×4, __assume_initialized×2. LONE GAP = ensure_init/ensure_init_if_ret on non-param = warn-not-error → F113 (filed).

## NEGATION-COMPLETENESS CORRECTION (2026-06-24, user challenged "how do you do spec negation / check all !1")
- The full-sweep "COMPLIANT except F113" verdict above is OVERCLAIMED. It only checked (a) the manual's OWN `// error:` examples (a biased sample — authors show violations they know are caught; survivorship bias hides under-enforced cells) and (b) a placement matrix SEEDED by the already-known F113 (confirmation, not enumeration). It did NOT do true negation: for each POSITIVE rule, enumerate + probe the COMPLETE violation space.
- Proof the sampling missed cells — an EXPANDED negation pass (attribute×subject, qualifier×non-pointer-subject) immediately found more:
  - F113 BROADER: ensure_init / ensure_init_if_ret warn-only also on STRUCT FIELD / FUNCTION decl / TYPEDEF / RETURN-TYPE (folds into F113; bug_log breadth updated). (union-field, array-var hard-error.)

Both obscure constructs, no soundness path → LOW, recorded not filed.

## 2026-06-24 — ensure_init nested-struct partial-init over-promotion (composition #1 / DeclBSC #3) — SOUND
- Hypothesis (RANK HIGH): tryPromoteParent (BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:929) over-promotes a partially-init
  NESTED struct → ensure_init callee whole-credit → checkEnsureInitAtReturn skipped → caller reads uninit
  nested field (soundness FN).

  writes `out->inner.x` + `out->z` but NOT `out->inner.y`. /tmp/claude-998/nest.cbs.

  The sibling-init loop (:970-977 `if getFieldInitState(Sibling)!=Initialized return`) correctly refuses to
  promote `inner` while `inner.y` is uninit → parent pointee stays non-Init → contract fires. Control `full`
  (writes all 3) compiles clean. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no bug; this is the FP-direction (like F106)
  and it's correctly enforced, NOT over-promoted.
- Marks: composition_init_null.md cand #1 nested-partial sub-case → PROBED-SOUND; DeclBSC.md classifyEnsureInit
  cand #3 → PROBED-SOUND. (Union over-promo variant already filed F107; nested-STRUCT partial is sound.)

## 2026-06-24 — switch-fallthrough owned double-consume (CFG merge soundness) — SOUND
- Hypothesis: at a `case` reachable by fall-through (p Moved) AND directly (p Owned), if merge(Moved,Owned)
  is optimistically Owned, a consume in that case slips on the fall-through path → runtime double-free.
- Probe: switch `case 0: consume(p); /*fallthrough*/ case 1: consume(p); break;`; main calls test(0).
  /tmp/claude-998/sw.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED at compile — `error: use of moved value: \`p\`` at the case-1 consume. OwnershipImpl::merge
  preserves the fall-through Moved state at the case-entry join → double-consume caught. OUTCOME:
  SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no bug. (Also note: consume() is _Unsafe → callable only from non-_Safe fns;
  move-checking still runs on plain functions.)
- FRONTIER NOTE: 5 consecutive SOUND probes on hardened paths (borrow operators ×5, ensure_init nested,
  switch-merge) vs 1 FIND (F114) from the spec-conformance D2 method. The productive frontier this session is
  the SPEC-CONFORMANCE oracle (D1 prose-rule must-reject / D2 must-accept), NOT re-probing the well-trodden
  CFG-ownership/borrow-operator surfaces. Bias next cycles toward spec-text-seeded probes.

## 2026-06-24 — nullable→nonnull cast in non-safe zone (manual §3.3:342, flag-gated) — SOUND + METHOD GAP
- Manual: with `-nullability-check=all`, non-safe-zone `(int *_Nonnull)p` on a nullable `p` is forbidden.
- Probe /tmp/claude-998/nncast2.cbs (isolated raw line, no owned/borrow noise):
  default flags rc=0 (accepted, correct — check is flag-gated); `-nullability-check=all` rc=1
  `error: cannot cast nullable pointer to nonnull type` (correctly rejected). OUTCOME: SOUND — rule conformant.
- **METHOD GAP A (flag-gating)**: spec_verifier.py D1 sweep runs `-fsyntax-only` ONLY (no
  `-nullability-check=all`), so every flag-gated `// error:` block is under-exercised. The r11 "75 blocks
  COMPLIANT" has this blind spot.
- **METHOD GAP B (multi-line masking)**: the §3.3:342 block has 3 `// error:` lines; the `_Owned`/`_Borrow`
  lines error for UNRELATED ownership-cast reasons even without the flag → block-level rc=1 → spec_verifier
  marks it ERROR/conformant while the ANNOTATED nullability rule (line 4) was never the firing error. A
  multi-line `// error:` block can mask an under-enforced rule. Per-line attribution would be the real fix.
- ACTION: patch spec_verifier.py to also run a `-nullability-check=all` pass (Gap A); Gap B logged as a
  known limitation (per-line attribution = future work).

## 2026-06-24 — borrow checker `?:` conditional-borrow loan tracking (no VisitConditionalOperator) — SOUND
- Gap: DefUse + ActionExtract lack VisitConditionalOperator → `?:` falls to VisitStmt (:755) which overwrites
  Sources/Kind/BK per child → IF a whole `?:` were one ActionExtract pass, only the last arm's loan survives.
- Probe: `int *_Borrow p = c ? &_Mut x : &_Mut y; int *_Borrow q = &_Mut x; *p=7; *q=8;`. /tmp/claude-998/cond_borrow.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: cannot borrow \`x\` as mutable more than once at a time` + note `first mut borrow
  occurs here` pointing at the `?:` (line 3). Both arms' loans ARE captured. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND —
  the missing VisitConditionalOperator is LATENT-UNREACHABLE: clang CFG splits `?:` into per-arm blocks so each
  arm's borrow is extracted in its own CFG element, never as one whole-`?:` ActionExtract pass (same
  CFG-split-mitigates-visitor-gap pattern as WalkerBSC SafeFeatureFinder). Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

- Handler (BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:432-470): `__assume_initialized(&out)` on an ensure_init param marks BOTH

  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED — the _Safe gate on `&` blocks the launder from the safe zone; in _Unsafe,

  #1 → SHAPE-REJECTED. Handler properly gated; no soundness hole reachable from _Safe.

## 2026-06-24 — owned assignment as a SUB-EXPRESSION double-visit FP — CONFIRMED-F115 (FILED)
- HandleDREAssign/VisitBinaryOperator read → hypothesis: a nested owned assignment is double-processed.
- Decomposition (5 forms): (A) split `b=mk(); a=b; consume(a)` CLEAN; (C) `int x=y=5` chained-int CLEAN;
  (E) `a=b` standalone-move CLEAN; (B) `a=(b=mk())` FP; (D) `consume(b=mk())` FP — both reject
  `assign to _Owned value: b` + `memory leak of b` on a FRESH b.
- ROOT CAUSE (source): VisitBinaryOperator (BSCOwnership.cpp:2186-2205) descends full AST per CFG element;
  clang emits the side-effecting `b=mk()` as its OWN element AND as a sub-expr of the parent CallExpr/assign
  → `b=mk()` visited TWICE → 2nd visit sees b already Owned → spurious assign-to-owned + value never moved →
  leak FP. Defect class E (CFG-element vs AST-nesting double-visit). The "assign to _Owned value" on a
  freshly-declared b is itself proof of double-processing.
- FP not soundness (assignment-line hard error blocks compile → no runtime double-free). Dedup clean.
  DISTINCT from F112 (intended NLL whole-var liveness — here no design rationale, standalone works, diag wrong).
- FILED F115 on umbrella IJP3AP. repro/F115_owned_assign_subexpr_double_visit_fp.cbs.

## 2026-06-24 — F115 blast-radius bound: borrow-checker sub-expr assign — SOUND (ownership-specific)
- Tested the F115 double-visit (sub-expr assignment) in the BORROW checker (distinct analyzer = would be a
  distinct root cause if buggy): `int *_Borrow p = (q = &_Mut x);` and `use(q = &_Mut x)` (call arg) and split
  control — ALL CLEAN rc=0. /tmp/claude-998/bsub.cbs, bcall.cbs, bsplit.cbs.
- CONCLUSION: F115 defect-class-E double-visit is OWNERSHIP-SPECIFIC. The ownership "assign to _Owned value"
  check is NON-MONOTONIC (fires when the PRE-state of the LHS is Owned), so a re-visit of an already-owned `b`
  spuriously fires + the leak check trips. The borrow checker (NLL, reassignment-tolerant) and init-analysis
  (monotonic Init state) are IMMUNE — a re-visit just re-establishes the same loan / re-marks init, no error.
  So F115 has NO borrow/init sibling; the fix surface is the single VisitBinaryOperator/CFG-element
  double-visit in BSCOwnership. Bounds the blast radius — no separate filing.

- RESULT: FLAGGED — `error: nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced`. getExprPathNullability's ArraySubscript
  branch correctly yields the declared element nullability (Nullable), deref check fires. Control (direct
  `*p`) flagged too. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no FN. The "array access untrackable" rule (3-nonnull:69)
  only blocks NARROWING (can't null-check-narrow an element), NOT the declared-nullability deref check —
  consistent. VisitArraySubscriptExpr candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

  SOUND — no FN. Short-circuit conditional init candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — comma-statement discarded owned temp `(c, mk());` runtime leak — FOLDED-F111 (severity-relevant)
- Same root cause as F111: `runOnBlock` (BSCOwnership.cpp:2647-2657) only dispatches top-level DeclStmt/CallExpr/
  assign-BinOp/incdec-UnaryOp/ReturnStmt. A bare comma statement `(c, mk());` is top-level BinaryOperator(comma,
  non-assign) → NOT dispatched → the discarded owned temp `mk()` is never leak-checked.
- Probe /tmp/claude-998/commaleak.cbs: compile rc=0 (only -Wunused-value) = FN; valgrind "definitely lost:
  4 bytes in 1 blocks, 1 allocs / 0 frees" = CONFIRMED RUNTIME LEAK.
- FOLD-F111 (same runOnBlock filter root + same fix = widen the filter). DISTINCT CONSEQUENCE from F111's
  minimal repro (`*p;` use-after-move, DCE'd, no runtime effect → MEDIUM): this is a real RUNTIME MEMORY LEAK,
  so the filter gap's practical severity reaches HIGH. Not a separate filing (same root/fix); recorded as a
  severity-relevant runtime manifestation of F111. Also note F22/F14 (discarded owned temp via for-inc/comma-
  in-init) are RELATED but their root is the Sema leak-check / Prologue; this one is specifically the
  runOnBlock dataflow filter (F111's surface), reached because the comma stmt is never visited.

## 2026-06-24 — `(void*_Owned)(c ? bop : bop)` nested-owned conditional cast — FOLDED-F91
- VisitCStyleCastExpr (BSCOwnership.cpp:2273-2336): the `(void*_Owned)` handler dispatches DRE/MemberExpr/
  UnaryDeref-chain inner; a ConditionalOperator inner falls to `else → Visit(subExpr)` (:2335) which moves the
  outer via op=Move but SKIPS the checkCast* subfield-owned ("not all moved") check.
- Probe /tmp/claude-998/condcast.cbs: `int *_Owned *_Owned bop` (nested); `safe_free((void*_Owned)(c?bop:bop))`
  → compile rc=0 (clean) + valgrind "definitely lost: 4 bytes, 2 allocs/1 free" = inner pointee LEAKED.
- FOLD-F91 (same root: checkCast* skipped for non-DRE/ME/UO inner; same fix: recurse the subfield check into
  the inner expr). REFINES the 2026-05-20 note (line ~354 "conditional inner SOUND"): that held for SINGLE-LEVEL
  owned (per-arm move-tracking via HandleDREUse suffices); for NESTED `_Owned *_Owned` the subfield-owned check
  is what's skipped → leak. So F91's blast radius (CallExpr/comma inner) ALSO includes the ConditionalOperator
  inner. No separate filing. (F43 = the same handler's checkCastField Null-state gap, distinct leg.)

## 2026-06-24 — array element write→read init `arr[0]=5; return arr[0];` — SHAPE-REJECTED (intended §3.7.3)
- Probe: `int arr[3]; arr[0]=5; return arr[0];` (read just-written elem) AND `return arr[1];` (read unwritten),
  -uninit-check=all. /tmp/claude-998/arrelem.cbs, arrunwr.cbs.
- RESULT: BOTH flagged `error: use of uninitialized value: \`arr\``. Array element writes don't mark even the
  WRITTEN element as init (no element-granular init tracking); the whole array stays Uninit until {}/__assume_
  initialized. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED — this is the documented ARRAY ALL-OR-NOTHING init limitation (§3.7.3),
  the SAME intended conservative limitation the user cited when retracting F112 (whole-variable granularity).
  Reading the written element is an over-conservative FP BY DESIGN, not a filable bug. Candidate #1 →
  intended-limitation. (cf [[feedback_validate_invariant]]: borrow/init FPs can be intended conservative
  limitations — confirm against intended granularity before filing.)

## 2026-06-24 — ownership move-tracking across _Safe/_Unsafe block boundary — SOUND
- Probe (a): `_Safe f` allocates p, moves it in `_Unsafe { consume(p); }`, no use after → CLEAN rc=0 (move
  tracked, no spurious leak FP). (b): use p in a later `_Unsafe { peek(p); }` after the move → FLAGGED
  `error: use of moved value: \`p\`` (rc=1). /tmp/claude-998/unsafemove2.cbs, unsafeuam2.cbs.
- OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — the ownership dataflow runs on the whole-function CFG; the safe-zone is a
  Sema concept, NOT a dataflow boundary, so moves inside _Unsafe blocks are tracked into the _Safe region (no
  leak FP, no use-after-move FN across the boundary). Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — ensure_init_if_ret(0) conditional-credit branch polarity — SOUND
- Probe: `inner(c, &_Mut x)` [eiir(0)] then `if (r!=0) return x; return x;`. -uninit-check=all. /tmp/claude-998/eiir.cbs.
- RESULT: r!=0 branch `return x` FLAGGED `error: use of uninitialized value: \`x\`` (line 10); r==0 branch
  `return x` CLEAN (credited). The PendingCondInit credit fires ONLY on the matching return-value edge
  (retval==CondValue), respecting branch polarity per the source comment (:502-505 "unconditional marking is
  unsound"). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no FN. Distinct edge from F104 (redecl launder, separate leg);
  the core conditional-credit branch logic is sound. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — BSCIRBuilder lowerExpr coverage audit + designated-init owned lowering — SOUND
- Audited the Visit* dispatch (BSCIRBuilder.cpp): every IN-SCOPE standard-C side-effecting Expr kind has a
  lowering (Call/Member/ArraySubscript/Binary/Unary/Conditional/Cast/InitList/CompoundLiteral/CompoundAssign/
  Paren/VAArg/sizeof/literals/DeclRef). Only OOS kinds (StmtExpr, _Generic/GenericSelectionExpr, ChooseExpr,
  BinaryConditionalOperator ?:, vector/C++ exprs) fall to VisitStmt→createNop (:1313) → no in-scope side-effect
  is lost in lowering.
- Probe (DesignatedInitExpr, the one plausible in-scope gap): `struct S{int*_Owned f,*_Owned g;} s =
  {.g=mk(),.f=mk2()};` (out-of-order) then free both → compile rc=0; valgrind 2 allocs/2 frees, 0 errors.
  Both owned moves tracked (designators resolve to the semantic positional InitList → VisitInitListExpr; no
  Nop fall-through). /tmp/claude-998/desig.cbs. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no IR-lowering coverage FN
  reachable from in-scope code. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — CodeGen SafeExpr coverage (CGExprConstant gap) — SOUND; F60/F63 re-confirmed fixed
- All runtime emitters now handle SafeExpr: CGExprScalar:452, CGExprAgg:129, CGExprComplex:116, CGExpr(lvalue):
  1368. F60(Agg)/F63(Complex) FIXED (commit a9deb1b, already in FIXED.md + .fresh_verdict vg-confirmed; this
  re-confirms). CGExprConstant.cpp has NO SafeExpr handler.
- Probe: `_Safe f` with `static const int arr[3]={10,20,30}; static const int k=5+7; return arr[1]+k;` →
  runs, prints 32 (correct). /tmp/claude-998/safeconst.cbs. The const-EVALUATOR (ExprConstant) strips SafeExpr
  before the codegen constant emitter, so CGExprConstant never sees a raw SafeExpr → its missing handler is
  UNREACHABLE. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no constant-emitter SafeExpr-drop bug. Candidate → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — return borrow-to-local from fn with borrow param (region inference) — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Borrow bad(int *_Borrow p){ int local=5; return &_Mut local; }`. /tmp/claude-998/retlocal.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: \`local\` does not live long enough` + note `dropped here while still borrowed`.
  Control `return &_Mut *p` (reborrow of param referent) CLEAN rc=0. Region inference correctly ties the
  returned borrow's region to the param and rejects a local's escaping borrow (no dangling-borrow-return FN).
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — scalar narrowing invalidation across loop back-edge — SOUND
- Probe: `_Safe f(c, p _Nullable){ if(p){ while(c){ *p=1; p=getnull(p); } } }`, -nullability-check=all.
  /tmp/claude-998/loopnarrow2.cbs.
- RESULT: top-of-loop `*p=1` FLAGGED `error: nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced` (back-edge merges the
  post-reassign Nullable state of p → narrowing invalidated on iter 2+). Control (no reassign) CLEAN rc=0.
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — scalar narrowing is correctly invalidated on reassign across the loop
  back-edge. F84 (FieldPath-not-invalidated-on-reassign) is the FIELD-specific gap; the SCALAR case is sound.
  Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — designated borrow-field struct init loan tracking (ActionExtract::VisitInitListExpr) — SOUND
- Probe: `struct S{int a; int *_Borrow b;}; struct S s={.b=&_Mut x}; int *_Borrow q=&_Mut x; *s.b=2; *q=3;`.

- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: cannot borrow \`x\` as mutable more than once` + note `first mut borrow occurs
  here` pointing at the designated init `{.b=&_Mut x}` (line 4). The loan from s.b's designated initializer
  IS recorded; field/init index alignment holds (semantic init-list fills field a → loop indexes align).
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no loan-loss FN in the borrow-field init path. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — union borrow-field init OOB (VisitInitListExpr union path) — SHAPE-REJECTED (unreachable)
- Candidate: a tracked UNION enters the struct field-loop (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:629-646) which iterates ALL
  fields() indexing getInit(Index); a union init has 1 init → getInit(Index>=1) potential OOB/crash.
- Probe: `union U { int *_Borrow b; long a; }; union U u = {.b=&_Mut x};`. /tmp/claude-998/bunion.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED at type level — `error: type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Borrow'` (rc=1, NO
  segv). Borrow-in-union is forbidden → no tracked union exists → the union field-loop is UNREACHABLE → the
  getInit OOB cannot fire. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED — not a bug, latent-unreachable. Candidate #2 → SHAPE-REJECTED.
  (Ownership analyzer's VisitInitListExpr (BSCOwnership:2433) iterates inits() not fields(), so no analogous OOB.)

## 2026-06-24 — crash-hunt via assertion-enabled build (in-scope boundary constructs) — no assert found
- Used the COMPATIBLE assert build ~/bsd/llvm-project/build/bin/clang (ziruichen12138 @88c4eedd — the
  session-start `ninja clang` rebuild finished; was @7b963eaa) with ITS OWN libcbs header tree.
- CAVEAT discovered: a separate enterprise fork (do-not-use) is INCOMPATIBLE with the dup tree's
  bishengc_safety.hbs (8 header parse errors: __take_from_raw undeclared, empty struct = "incomplete type",
  etc.) → mixing trees fabricates false divergences (the earlier c2 "assert-rc=1 vs release-rc=0" was this
  noise, NOT a bug). Memory [[reference_assert_builds]] updated with the per-tree-header rule.
- Boundary batch on the compatible build vs release: empty-struct-field+borrow (k1), 4-deep nested ternary
  borrow (k2), borrow-of-borrow-struct `*(*pa).b` (k3), borrow-to-borrow `int*_Borrow*_Borrow` (k4) — ALL
  assert-rc==release-rc, NO Assertion/UNREACHABLE/SIGSEGV. OUTCOME: no compiler-robustness crash on these
  in-scope edges; analyzers robust. No new bug.

## 2026-06-24 — targeted crash-hunt (deep-nest owned, nested union) on COMPATIBLE assert build — no crash; DROPPED
- m1 (4-level `int*_Owned*_Owned*_Owned*_Owned d`, `consume(***d); ****d;`): both builds rc=1, NO assert —
  `use of moved value: ****d` correctly caught (deep use-after-move; CONFIRMS F110's root is ONLY the 1-char
  parent "*" boundary, deep parents ARE checked). m2 (nested union owned): both rc=1, `type of union field
  cannot be qualified by '_Owned'` (owned-in-union forbidden, like borrow-in-union). No crash/divergence.
- CONCLUSION: crash-hunt over in-scope boundaries finds no asserts (analyzers robust); cross-fork build mgmt
  is a distraction (enterprise-fork header-incompat). DROPPED crash-hunt; back to semantic probing on
  canonical llvm-project-dup.

## 2026-06-24 — _Nonnull pointer compared to nullptr (dead branch) — SOUND
- Probe: `_Safe f(int *_Borrow _Nonnull p){ if(p==nullptr){ return *p; } return *p; }`, -nullability-check=all.
  /tmp/claude-998/nn.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 clean. The always-false comparison is allowed; the dead-branch `*p` is safe (p IS _Nonnull).
  No soundness issue (the dead branch's deref can't fire with p null). At most a missed `-Wtautological-pointer-
  compare`-style warning (LOW, not filable). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — ensure_init satisfied only inside a maybe-zero-trip loop — SOUND
- Probe: `_Safe f(n, out [ensure_init]){ for(i=0;i<n;i++){ *out=i; } }`. /tmp/claude-998/eiloop.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: '*out' may not be initialized on all paths at return in __attribute__((ensure_init))
  function`. The loop CFG merge keeps *out MaybeInit (the n==0 zero-trip path skips the body) → contract's
  all-paths requirement fires. No FN (caller can't be handed an uninit *out). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.
  Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — ensure_init contract delegation (pass out-param to another ensure_init fn) — SOUND
- Probe: `outer(out [ensure_init])` calls `inner(out)` where inner has ensure_init → rc=0 CLEAN (delegation
  credits *out). Control: `outer2` calls `noop(out)` (noop NO ensure_init) → REJECTED `'*out' not initialized
  at return`. /tmp/claude-998/eideleg.cbs, einodeleg.cbs.
- RESULT: delegation correctly DISCRIMINATES — credits *out only when the callee's matching param carries the
  ensure_init contract (terminator handler delegation branch). No FP (valid delegation accepted), no FN
  (non-contract callee correctly leaves *out uninit). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — D2: documented owned->raw via borrow `(T*)&_Mut *p` (manual §3.1 rule 8) — SOUND (accepted)
- Manual rule 8: to convert _Owned->raw WITHOUT transferring ownership, use `(T*)&_Mut *p` in the non-safe
  zone (prose-only, no fenced example). Probe: `void f(int *_Owned p){ int *raw=(int*)&_Mut *p; *raw=7;
  safe_free((void*_Owned)p); }`. /tmp/claude-998/o2raw.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 CLEAN — the documented escape is accepted as specified (p retains ownership, raw alias formed
  via the borrow). D2 must-accept CONFORMANT. (Contrast F114: borrow-OF-function rule 16 is rejected; this
  owned->raw-via-borrow rule 8 works.) OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. No new D2 finding here.

## 2026-06-24 — IsSafeConstantValueConversion boundary check (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:170-244) — SOUND
- Probe: _Safe constants `signed char =127/=128`, `unsigned char =255/=256/=-1`. /tmp/claude-998/cst.cbs.
- RESULT: 127→i8 and 255→u8 ACCEPTED (fit); 128→i8, 256→u8 (over-range), -1→u8 (neg→unsigned) all REJECTED
  `implicit conversion ... forbidden in the safe zone`. The width checks (`>` for positive→signed sign-bit,
  `>=` for unsigned, neg→unsigned always false) are off-by-one-correct at the exact type boundaries. OUTCOME:
  SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no over-range-constant FN. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — IsSafeBuiltinTypeConversion int->float matrix entry (implicit forbidden?) — SOUND
- Matrix EnableToConvert[12][10]=Y (Long->Float) is precision-lossy; question: allowed IMPLICITLY (FN)?
- Probe: `_Safe f(long l){ float fl = l; }`. /tmp/claude-998/l2f.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `implicit conversion from 'long' to 'float' is forbidden in the safe zone; use explicit
  cast`. The matrix Y is consulted for EXPLICIT casts only; the caller (IsSafeConversion) independently
  forbids implicit int->float regardless of the matrix → manual "implicit int->float forbidden" enforced.
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no implicit-lossy-conversion FN. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — unsigned->signed conversion: widening vs same-width sign-flip — SOUND (F71 guard active)
- uint->long (32->64 widening): ACCEPTED rc=0 (value-preserving on LP64, correct). ushort->long: accepted.
  uint->int + ulong->long (SAME-width unsigned->signed sign-flips): BOTH REJECTED `implicit conversion ...
  forbidden in the safe zone`. /tmp/claude-998/u2l.cbs, u2i.cbs.
- The static matrix [10][4]=N (UInt->Long) is OVERRIDDEN by a value-preserving-widening acceptance path
  (impl checks actual widths, more nuanced than the table); the :148 guard rejects same-width unsigned->signed
  sign-flips (F71's pattern). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — widenings accepted, sign-flips rejected, no FN
  (sign-flip accepted) nor inconsistent-FP (uint->long correctly accepted). Confirms F71 guard working.

## 2026-06-24 — for-CONDITION owned-returning call leak `for(; mk(); )` — FOLDED-F22
- Probe: `for (; mk(i); i++) {}` where mk returns `int *_Owned _Nullable`, used as loop condition (pointer->bool),
  discarded each iter. /tmp/claude-998/forcond.cbs.
- RESULT: compile rc=0 (FN) + valgrind "definitely lost: 8 bytes in 2 blocks, 2 allocs/0 frees" = per-iteration
  runtime leak. FOLD-F22 (for-INCREMENT owned-temp leak): the for-HEADER expression positions (cond + inc) both
  discard their owned temp without a leak check — same root/fix surface as F22 (extend the temp-leak check to
  for-header exprs). Same broad discarded-owned-temp family as F22/F111(comma)/F14(wrappers). No separate filing.

- Probe: `typedef int *_Owned (*mk_t)(void); mk_t fp=mk; int *_Owned p=fp();` (p unfreed). /tmp/claude-998/fpown.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: memory leak of value: \`p\``. The indirect (fnptr) call's owned return is tracked

## 2026-06-24 — owned ARG move via function-pointer (indirect) call — SOUND
- Probe: `consume_t fp=consume; fp(p); consume(p);` (p moved via fnptr call, then reused). /tmp/claude-998/fparg.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: use of moved value: \`p\`` at the second consume. The indirect call moves the owned

## 2026-06-24 — _Owned _Nullable null-checked conditional consume — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Owned _Nullable p = mk(c); if(p!=nullptr){ consume(p); }`. /tmp/claude-998/onull.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 CLEAN. On the NULL branch p is null + not consumed → NO leak FP (analyzer recognizes null-owned
  needs no free); non-null branch consume tracked; no double-free. The null-narrowing + ownership compose
  correctly here. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. (Relates to F108 null-owned-copy false-leak + F43 null-init
  field cast — those are specific narrower triggers; this straightforward null-check-then-consume flow is sound.)
  Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — mutate owner directly while mutably borrowed — SOUND
- Probe: `int x=1; int *_Borrow b=&_Mut x; x=2; int y=*b;` (direct owner-write while b live). /tmp/claude-998/mutown.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: cannot assign to \`x\` because it is borrowed` + note `\`x\` is borrowed here`.
  Exclusive mutable access enforced — a direct write to the borrowed owner while the &_Mut loan is live
  conflicts (NLL: b used after via `*b`). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no mutate-while-borrowed FN
  (the owner-write direction; F11/F21 cover the through-borrow-after-free direction). Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — F115 blast-radius clarification: nested CALL moving owned arg — SOUND (assignment-specific)
- Probe: `int consume_ret(int *_Owned p){ safe_free(...); return 0; } g(consume_ret(p));` — nested call moves
  owned p inside g(). /tmp/claude-998/nestcall.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 CLEAN — NO "use of moved" FP. Nested CALLS are IMMUNE to F115's double-visit; only nested
  ASSIGNMENTS (`consume(b=mk())`) trip it (the non-monotonic "assign-to-owned" check on the re-visited LHS).
  Confirms F115's scope is correctly ASSIGNMENT-specific, NOT a broader all-sub-expression bug. OUTCOME:
  SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. (The CFG double-emits a nested side-effecting ASSIGNMENT as element+child; a nested
  call's arg-move is processed once.)

## 2026-06-24 — &_Mut *p reborrow of nullable p, nullability propagation — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Borrow _Nullable q = &_Mut *p;` (p nullable, result to nullable q) → rc=0 ACCEPTED; runtime
  `f(nullptr)` → survives, valgrind ERROR SUMMARY 0. /tmp/claude-998/derefnull.cbs, derefnull_rt.cbs.
- RESULT: SOUND — `&_Mut *p` is a REBORROW (`&*p` ≡ p, NO load/deref at runtime), so it preserves p's
  nullability (result nullable, correctly assignable to nullable q; the earlier nonnull-q case was rejected by
  correct nullability propagation, NOT a missed deref check). No actual deref → no null-deref UB → no FN. The
  hypothesized "deref of nullable p slipped" is moot: there is no deref. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — NullCheckInfo operator&= && merge order symmetry — SOUND
- Probe: `if(p!=nullptr && q!=nullptr){*p;*q;}` (order A) and `if(q!=nullptr && p!=nullptr){*p;*q;}` (order B,
  swapped) → BOTH rc=0 CLEAN. /tmp/claude-998/andsym.cbs.
- RESULT: the && merge (semanticUnion of null+present checked-expr sets) is ORDER-SYMMETRIC — both operands
  narrowed regardless of order. No asymmetric-meet bug (distinct from F26's mergeDPVD CFG-block meet). The
  ConstTrue/ConstFalse short-circuit handling (:237-256) is symmetric (either-branch checked at top).
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. Candidate #1 → PROBED-SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — NullCheckInfo operator|= || intersection then-branch — SOUND
- Probe: `if(p!=nullptr || q!=nullptr){ *p; }` → *p FLAGGED `nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced` (p not
  narrowed; {p}∩{q}=∅). Control `if(p!=nullptr || p!=nullptr){ *p; }` → CLEAN (p narrowed; {p}∩{p}={p}).
  /tmp/claude-998/orint.cbs, orint2.cbs.
- RESULT: the || intersection merge is semantically correct — only an expr checked in BOTH branches survives
  to narrow the then-branch. No FN (p-OR-q does not narrow p). Together with the &&-union order-symmetry probe,
  the NullCheckInfo &&/|| merge is fully verified SOUND. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — isMoveSemanticTypeImpl array-field recursion gap — SHAPE-REJECTED (unreachable)
- Real code asymmetry: isMoveSemanticTypeImpl (TypeBSC.cpp:381) recurses only `isa<RecordType>` fields, NOT
  array fields; sibling isTrivialDataTypeImpl (:401) DOES recurse arrays. Hypothesis: `struct Outer{ struct
  Inner arr[2]; }` (Inner has _Owned field) mis-classified non-move → leak FN.
- Probe: the type is REJECTED at declaration — `error: type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned'(even
  indirectly), 'struct Inner' contains '_Owned' type`. /tmp/claude-998/movetype.cbs. The "even indirectly"
  type-level guard forbids ANY array whose element type transitively contains _Owned → no array-of-owned-
  containing-type can exist → isMoveSemanticTypeImpl never needs to recurse such an array → the recursion gap
  is LATENT-UNREACHABLE. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED — asymmetry harmless, no leak FN reachable. Candidate #1 →
  SHAPE-REJECTED. (Same pattern as union-borrow OOB: forbidden construct makes the analyzer gap unreachable.)

## 2026-06-24 — __move_to_raw ownership-transfer move tracking — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Owned p=mk(); int *raw=__move_to_raw(p); consume(p);` (use p after transfer). /tmp/claude-998/mtr.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `error: use of moved value: \`p\``. __move_to_raw marks the source moved-from → use-after-
  transfer flagged, no double-free. The documented owned->raw ownership-transfer escape is move-tracked.
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. (raw ptr itself untracked by design — caller's responsibility post-transfer.)

## 2026-06-24 — forget() ownership handling (deliberate-leak escape) — SOUND
- Probe: `forget(p); consume(p);` → use after forget FLAGGED `use of moved value: p`. Control: `forget(p);`
  alone → CLEAN (no leak flag). /tmp/claude-998/forget.cbs, forget2.cbs.
- RESULT: `_Safe void forget<T>(T t)` consumes its arg BY VALUE (move) and drops it (deliberate leak); the
  ownership analyzer tracks the move (use-after flagged) and the by-value-consume suppresses the leak
  diagnostic. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. (NOTE: forget is a GENERIC — borderline OOS — but the aspect
  tested is owned MOVE-tracking through a by-value consume, which is the in-scope ownership analyzer, not the
  generic monomorphization machinery.)

## 2026-06-24 — __take_from_raw ownership establishment — SOUND
- Probe: `int *raw=malloc(...); int *_Owned p=__take_from_raw(raw);` (p unfreed) → FLAGGED `memory leak of
  value: p`. /tmp/claude-998/tfr.cbs.
- RESULT: __take_from_raw establishes ownership on the result (p must be freed; leak tracked). Both raw-transfer
  directions SOUND: __move_to_raw marks source moved (use-after flagged), __take_from_raw establishes ownership
  (leak flagged). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — hetero-redecl PARAM nullability mismatch (safe _Nullable / unsafe _Nonnull) null-deref — FOLDED-F103
- areFunctionTypesCompatibleForHeterogeneousRedecl (TypeBSC.cpp:256-307) STRIPS nullability (:262-263
  stripOuterNullability) and never re-checks it → a _Safe(_Nullable param)/_Unsafe(_Nonnull param) redecl pair
  is accepted (only -Wnullability warning). Probe: _Safe caller `f(nullptr)` via the _Nullable decl reaches the
  _Unsafe `_Nonnull`-assuming def `*p` → runtime SIGSEGV (valgrind Invalid read of size 4, ERROR SUMMARY 1).
  /tmp/claude-998/hetnull_rt.cbs. Validated it's the hetero-redecl (homogeneous _Safe deref `*p` is itself
  forbidden in the safe zone; control (2) confirms the _Safe caller legitimately passes null per the _Nullable decl).
- DEDUP: this is the PARAM-direction variant of **F103** (the RETURN-direction: safe _Nonnull return vs unsafe
  _Nullable, same null-deref). SAME root (hetero-redecl nullability strip + no-recheck), SAME fix surface.
  F103 is marked **VALID — DO NOT FILE (user handling the heterogeneous-redecl area directly, w/ F104/F105)**.
  → FOLDED-F103, NO new filing. (Dedup prevented a wrongful re-file. Distinct from F29 fnptr-assign variance,
  F56/F53/F57 redecl-gate, F104 ensure_init, F105 owned-add — but F103 already covers the nullability leg.)

## 2026-06-24 — ensure_init_if_ret(N) non-zero CondValue edge-matching — SOUND
- Probe: eiir(2) callee inits *out iff returns 2; caller `if(r==2) return x;` CLEAN, caller_bad `if(r!=2)
  return x;` → FLAGGED `use of uninitialized value: x`. /tmp/claude-998/eiir2.cbs.
- RESULT: the PendingCondInit SwitchInt edge-matching credits *out on the return==N edge for non-zero N (=2),
  NOT hardcoded to 0; the r!=2 edge correctly leaves x uninit. Extends the eiir(0) branch-polarity probe to
  non-zero CondValue. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — do-while owned move+reown back-edge — SOUND
- Probe: `do { consume(p); p=mk(); } while(c);` then `consume(p)`. /tmp/claude-998/dowhile.cbs.
- RESULT: compile rc=0 CLEAN; runtime 2 allocs/2 frees, valgrind ERROR SUMMARY 0. The do-while back-edge
  tracks the per-iteration move (consume) + re-own (p=mk) soundly — no leak, no double-free, no spurious
  "assign to owned"/"use of moved". Completes the loop-form owned sweep (for/while/do-while all sound; the
  ownership fixpoint propagates owned/moved across all back-edges). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — owned pointer comparison `p == q` move-tracking — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Owned p,q; int eq=(p==q); consume(p); consume(q);`. /tmp/claude-998/owncmp.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 CLEAN. Comparing owned pointers reads them (op=GetAddr for comparison ops, VisitBinaryOperator
  :2210 — no consume), so both p and q remain Owned and are correctly required to be freed (both consumes
  accepted, no "use of moved" FP, no leak FN). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — sizeof(owned p) unevaluated context — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Owned p=mk(); unsigned long sz=sizeof(p); consume(p);`. /tmp/claude-998/szof.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 CLEAN. sizeof is unevaluated (VisitUnaryExprOrTypeTraitExpr) — p not moved/used; remains Owned

## 2026-06-24 — allocate-in-while-condition idiom (F115 blast-radius bound) — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Owned _Nullable p; while((p=mk(i))!=nullptr){ consume(p); i++; }`. /tmp/claude-998/whilecond.cbs.
- RESULT: rc=0 CLEAN — the owned assignment in the while-CONDITION does NOT trigger F115's double-visit
  "assign to owned"/leak FP. Different CFG structure than F115's call-arg case (`consume(b=mk())`): the
  condition assignment isn't double-emitted as a sub-expression child, so it's processed once. CONFIRMS F115's
  scope is narrow (call-arg + chained-assign positions); the widely-used allocate-in-while-condition idiom is
  SOUND. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND. (Further bounds F115 blast radius alongside the nested-call immunity.)

## 2026-06-24 — reborrow chain loan propagation `a=&_Mut x; b=a; c=b` — SOUND
- Probe: reborrow chain a→b→c (all borrow x), then `x=5` while c live (`*c=9`). /tmp/claude-998/reborrow.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `cannot assign to \`x\` because it is borrowed` + note `\`x\` is borrowed here` (the
  original &_Mut x). The loan on x propagates through the reborrow chain → mutate-while-borrowed caught.
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND — no reborrow-chain loan-loss FN.

## 2026-06-24 — shared &_Const borrow coexistence + write conflict — SOUND
- Probe: two `const int *_Borrow a=&_Const x; b=&_Const x; *a+*b;` → CLEAN (shared readers coexist). Write
  variant `&_Const x; x=5; *a;` → REJECTED `cannot assign to \`x\` because it is borrowed`. /tmp/claude-998/shared.cbs, sharedw.cbs.
- RESULT: shared-borrow semantics correct — multiple immutable borrows allowed (no reader-reader conflict);
  a write to the borrowed referent conflicts with any live borrow (shared or mut). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-24 — array element borrow granularity `&_Mut arr[0]` then write arr[1] — SHAPE-REJECTED (intended §3.7.3)
- Probe: `int arr[3]; int *_Borrow b=&_Mut arr[0]; arr[1]=5; *b;` → REJECTED `cannot assign to \`arr[]\`
  because it is borrowed` + note `\`arr[]\` is borrowed here`. /tmp/claude-998/arrelem.cbs.
- RESULT: the `arr[]` whole-array notation confirms WHOLE-VARIABLE liveness — borrowing one element locks the
  WHOLE array, so writing a different element is rejected. This is the documented array all-or-nothing /
  whole-variable-liveness limitation (§3.7.3, same intended conservatism behind F112's retraction). By-design
  FP, NOT a bug — element-level sub-place liveness is not modeled. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED (intended limitation).

## 2026-06-24 — struct-field nullability narrowing via borrow-ptr-to-borrow-struct — SHAPE-REJECTED
- Probe shape `struct S{int *_Borrow _Nullable p;}; _Safe f(struct S *_Borrow s){ if(s->p) *s->p; }` REJECTED:
  `type of 'struct S' cannot be qualified by '_Borrow'(even indirectly), contains '_Borrow' type`. The
  "even indirectly" type guard forbids a _Borrow pointer to a struct containing a _Borrow field. (3rd probe
  blocked by this guard: owned-in-array, borrow-in-union, borrow-ptr-to-borrow-struct — strong protection that
  renders several analyzer gaps unreachable.) Field-narrowing soundness is already covered (F84 = the
  reassign-invalidation gap, uses a valid shape). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED.

- Probe: `typedef int *_Owned OwnedT; OwnedT arr[2];` → REJECTED `type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned',
  'OwnedT' (aka 'int *_Owned') contains '_Owned' type`. /tmp/claude-998/tdarr.cbs.

  The array-of-owned construct remains unformable → the isMoveSemanticTypeImpl array-recursion gap stays
  LATENT-UNREACHABLE even via typedef. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED — the "even indirectly" type guard is robust
  (canonical-type-based, handles typedef sugar). Confirms the type system's first-line protection is complete here.

## 2026-06-24 — &_Mut of const local (mutable borrow of immutable) — SOUND

  Control: `s.a=1; return s.b;` (b unwritten) → FLAGGED `use of uninitialized value: s.b`. /tmp/claude-998/fullinit.cbs, partinit.cbs.

  clean), partial leaves unwritten field uninit (flagged). Complement of the nested-partial probe (which

## 2026-06-25 — conditional owned init `c ? mk(1) : mk(2)` runtime — SOUND
- Probe: `int *_Owned p = c ? mk(1) : mk(2); consume(p);`. /tmp/claude-998/condown.cbs.
- RESULT: compile clean; valgrind 1 alloc/1 free, ERROR SUMMARY 0. Only the SELECTED arm allocates (dead arm
  not evaluated, C ternary semantics); p owns the result and is freed exactly once. No double-eval/leak/double-free.
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-25 — flag-correlated conditional free (for+break+freed-flag) — SHAPE-REJECTED (intended limitation)
- Probe: `int *_Owned p=mk(); int freed=0; for(...){ if(cond(i)){ consume(p); freed=1; break; } } if(!freed){
  consume(p); }`. /tmp/claude-998/flagfree.cbs.
- RESULT: REJECTED — `use of moved value: p` at the if(!freed) consume (break-path p moved) + `memory leak of p`
  (merge). The code is VALID (p consumed exactly once per runtime path via the freed flag), but the ownership
  DATAFLOW merges p to MaybeMoved at the post-loop join and cannot correlate the runtime `freed` flag with p's
  state (would need path-sensitivity/value-tracking). By-design conservative FP — same class as F112 (whole-var
  liveness) + array all-or-nothing §3.7.3. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED (intended limitation, not filable). Workaround:
  restructure so each path's consume is statically evident (no flag correlation).

## 2026-06-25 — init-analysis early-return path `if(c) return 0; x=5; return x;` — SOUND
- Probe: /tmp/claude-998/earlyret.cbs → rc=0 CLEAN. The init dataflow correctly tracks that `return x` is only
  reachable from the x=5 (c-false) path; the c-true early return doesn't make x MaybeInit at the use (the use
  isn't reachable from the uninit path). CFG-precise, no naive-merge FP. OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-25 — backward-goto loop with owned — SHAPE-REJECTED (backward goto forbidden)
- Probe: `loop: consume(p); p=mk(); if(c) goto loop;` → REJECTED `invalid code structure, jumping back to
  previous labels is not allowed`. /tmp/claude-998/gotoloop.cbs.
- RESULT: BSC forbids BACKWARD goto entirely (only forward goto allowed). So no irreducible CFGs can be formed
  via goto → the ownership/init/borrow dataflow fixpoints only ever process REDUCIBLE CFGs (from structured
  for/while/do-while loops, all verified sound) → irreducible-CFG analyzer bugs are eliminated by construction.
  OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED (structural restriction). (F35 = forward goto-skip, the allowed direction.) Another
  strong structural guard alongside the "even indirectly" type guard.

- RESULT: compile clean; runtime 1 alloc/1 free, ERROR SUMMARY 0. The conditional return transfers ownership
  of the selected arm to the caller (no leak in callee, no double-free). OUTCOME: SHAPE-REJECTED / SOUND.

## 2026-06-25 — generic nullability mangle COLLISION (function + struct path) — CONFIRMED-NEW (distinct from G14/G12)
- Hypothesis (lead): MangleBSC getBSCTypeName mangles via the PRINTED type name; two distinct QualTypes that
  PRINT IDENTICALLY collide. Nullability (`_Nonnull`/`_Nullable`) is AttributedType SUGAR, not a Qualifiers
  bit — and `adjustForRewritingBSC()` (PrettyPrinter.h:102) sets `PrintCanonicalTypes=true`, so
  `splitAccordingToPolicy` (TypePrinter.cpp:268-269) canonicalizes the arg type BEFORE printing → the
  AttributedType nullability layer is stripped → `printAttributedBefore` (:1791-1828, the ONLY place
  nullability is emitted) is never reached → mangled name omits nullability.
- PROBE (function path): `T pass<T>(T x){return x;}` + `pass<int *>()`, `pass<int *_Nonnull>()`,
  `pass<int *_Nullable>()`. `-rewrite-bsc`: ALL THREE collapse to ONE symbol `pass_int_P` (single body
  `static int *pass_int_P(int *x){return x;}`, 3 call sites route to it). NORMAL codegen confirms:
  `nm` shows exactly ONE `pass_int_P` symbol for the 3 specializations. /tmp/ftest9.cbs /tmp/ftest16.cbs.
- PROBE (struct path): `struct Box<T>{T v;}` + `Box<int *>`, `Box<int *_Nonnull>`, `Box<int *_Nullable>`.
  `-rewrite-bsc`: all three collapse to ONE struct `Box_int_P { int *v; }` — variables a/b/c share the type.
  NO redefinition error (silent merge), unlike G14 which errors. /tmp/ftest13.cbs.
- ASYMMETRY (proves generic-mangle-specific, not a general rewriter drop): NON-generic `pass_nn(int *_Nonnull)`
  rewrites PRESERVING `_Nonnull` (`static int *pass_nn(int *_Nonnull x)`). Only the GENERIC monomorphization
  mangle path (MangleBSCContext::getBSCTypeName → QT.print under PrintCanonicalTypes) drops it. /tmp/ftest14.cbs.
- ALSO: `_Owned`/`_Borrow`/`_ArrayElem` SURVIVE this canonicalization (they're real Qualifiers bits, kept by
  getCanonicalType) → `pass<int *_Borrow>` mangles to `pass_int_P_Borrow` (distinct). So the collision is
  NULLABILITY-SPECIFIC, not "all sugar drops here". Confirms distinct mechanism from G14 (G14 = qual-flag gap
  drops _Owned/_Borrow on struct path; here = canonicalization drops nullability on BOTH paths).
- DISTINCT from G14: G14's root is `MangleWithSafeQualifier=false` on the struct record-name path →
  `AppendTypeQualList` drops _Owned/_Borrow; G14's fix (set flag true) does NOT fix this — nullability is
  AttributedType, never emitted by AppendTypeQualList regardless of flag. Different file/line
  (TypePrinter.cpp:268-269 splitAccordingToPolicy vs :1524/:192-211), different qualifier, different symptom
  (G14 = DUPLICATE-def redefinition ERROR; this = silent single-def MERGE). DISTINCT from G12 (Sema-time
  substitution strip → call-site nullability-check FN; this = mangle/codegen-time symbol collision).
- SEVERITY MEDIUM (fails closed): merged body is type-correct for pointer-identity bodies (no crash, no wrong
  runtime value); the semantic loss is that `_Nonnull` vs `_Nullable` contracts are ERASED from the rewritten-C
  / codegen symbol+type space (a `Box<int *_Nullable>` and `Box<int *_Nonnull>` become the same C type). No
  memory-unsafe runtime reached. Parallel to G14 (also MEDIUM, also fails closed).
- OUTCOME: CONFIRMED-NEW defect, not filed (report-only per task). Repro candidate: repro/G17_generic_nullability_mangle_collision.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — CONFIRMED-G17 (FILED): owned-in-union type guard bypassed via generic monomorphization
- `union U<T>{T a; long b;}; union U<int *_Owned> u;` ACCEPTED (rc=0) while direct `union{int *_Owned a;}` is
  rejected (`type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Owned'`). The owned/borrow union-field guard is NOT
  re-checked at SemaTemplateInstantiateDeclBSC monomorphization — UNLIKE the array guard (`Arr<int *_Owned,3>`
  correctly rejected `type of 'int *_Owned[3]' cannot be qualified by '_Owned'`). NOT soundness (analysis tracks
  it: clobber→leak, double-free→uninit-cast, read-unwritten→uninit all caught). MEDIUM conformance/guard-
  completeness asymmetry. Dedup clean (all prior union+owned probes = direct SHAPE-REJECTED; generic untried).
  Found via the GLM-generics push (single-agent parallel). FILED 2026-06-25 on umbrella IJP3AP. Repro G17.
  NOTE: scope gate (post_issue.sh) blocks generics by default; filed via consolidate (G-series path) per the
  user's explicit "push on generics" directive + G01-G16 precedent.

## 2026-06-25 — G17 borrow analog `union U<int *_Borrow>` — FOLDED-G17
- `union U<T>{T a;long b;}; union U<int *_Borrow> u; u.a=&_Mut x;` → ACCEPTED (rc=0); direct `union{int *_Borrow a;}`
  → REJECTED `type of union field cannot be qualified by '_Borrow'`. Same root as G17 (union-field owned/borrow
  guard not re-checked at monomorph) → FOLD-G17 (the _Borrow leg; one fix covers both). G17 blast radius = both
  _Owned and _Borrow union fields via generic.

## 2026-06-25 — borrow-func guard re-check at monomorph (G17 scope-bounding) — ENFORCED (not a bypass)
- `T make<T>(int x); make<int *_Borrow>(5)` → REJECTED `no matching function for call to 'make'` (the
  monomorphized `int *_Borrow make(int)` signature is invalid per err_typecheck_borrow_func → instantiation
  fails). Direct `int *_Borrow make(int)` → `no _Borrow qualified type found in the function parameters`.
- RESULT: the borrow-func guard IS effectively enforced at monomorph (instantiation fails). Combined with the
  array guard (also re-checked), this SHARPENS G17: the monomorph guard re-check is mostly COMPLETE; the
  UNION-FIELD owned/borrow guard is the SPECIFIC, ISOLATED gap (G17), not a systemic monomorph problem.

## 2026-06-25 — F107 over-promotion × owned-in-generic-union (G17 severity-confirm) — SOUND (no soundness hole)
- Probe: `union U<int *_Owned> u; u.b=5; safe_free((void*_Owned)u.a);` (free u.a never written) → REJECTED
  `invalid cast to \`void *_Owned\` of uninit value: \`u.a\``. The F107 union over-promotion does NOT fire for
  the generic owned-union (u.a correctly tracked uninit despite u.b written). /tmp/claude-998/uovp.cbs.
- RESULT: owned-in-generic-union (G17's construct) is FULLY tracked by the ownership/init analysis — leak-on-
  clobber, double-free, uninit-read, AND uninit-free (F107 over-promotion) ALL caught. No soundness hole
  reachable via the G17 guard bypass → CONFIRMS G17 severity = MEDIUM (conformance/guard-completeness only,
  NOT a soundness FN). The analysis robustly compensates for a construct the type guard otherwise forbids.

- GENERICS PUSH SUMMARY (single-agent, GLM down): G17 FILED (union-field guard bypass via generic, MEDIUM, no

  [down] or symbol-level inspection).

## 2026-06-25 — CONFIRMED-G18 (FILED): const-generic INT64_MIN negation-overflow malformed mangle
- getBSCArgName (MangleBSC.cpp:158-172) negative-integral mangle `"n"+to_string(-getExtValue())`; `-getExtValue()`
  for INT64_MIN is signed-overflow UB → "n-9223372036854775808" (embedded '-'). Codegen symbol `g_n-9223...808`
  (as/ld tolerate); `-rewrite-bsc` emits it as a C identifier → invalid C (gcc "expected ';' before '-'").
  int-case fine (g_n2147483648, no overflow). DISTINCT from G14 (qualifier-drop collision) — integral negation
  overflow, same file diff branch. NEW (prior probes: small values + INT_MIN-array-size only). MEDIUM (G14-class:
  invalid -rewrite-bsc output, fails closed). Found via GLM-generics push (GLM down; pursued by source-read +
  nm/-rewrite-bsc). FILED 2026-06-25 IJP3AP. Repro G18.

## 2026-06-25 — unsigned const-generic > INT64_MAX malformed mangle — FOLDED-G18 (blast radius broadened)
- `unsigned long g<unsigned long N>(); g<18446744073709551615UL>()` (uint64-max) → symbol `g_-1` (malformed,
  embedded '-'). The POSITIVE branch `to_string(getExtValue())`: getExtValue() reinterprets uint64-max as int64
  -1, isNegative()=false (unsigned) → to_string(-1)="-1" → "g_-1". /tmp/claude-998/cgu.cbs.
- FOLD-G18 (same getExtValue() root + same fix = APSInt magnitude not int64). BROADENS G18 scope: not just the
  single INT64_MIN edge (negative branch) — the ENTIRE upper-half of the uint64 range (any unsigned const-generic
  value > INT64_MAX) produces a malformed mangle via the positive branch. Each maps to a unique negative int64
  (bijective bit-reinterpret) so no collision, but all malformed (embedded '-') → invalid -rewrite-bsc C.
  G18's fix (APSInt unsigned magnitude) covers both branches. One fix, broad blast radius.

  `g<int *P>` REJECTED "type-id cannot have a name"; nullptr REJECTED ("no matching function" for int param);
  enum constant g<A> → Integral (g_1); _Bool g<1>/g<0> → Integral (g_1/g_0, distinct). So only Type + Integral
  args exist, both handled. /tmp/claude-998/cgptr.cbs, cgenum.cbs, cgbool.cbs, cgnull.cbs.
- GENERICS MANGLER NOW FULLY MAPPED: getBSCTypeName (G14 owned/borrow qualifier-drop collision; char-sub sound) +
  getBSCArgName (G18 integral negation/getExtValue overflow at INT64_MIN + unsigned>INT64_MAX; small values sound;
  non-integral unreachable). G14 + G18 are the only mangler defects. Generics-mangler surface exhausted.

## 2026-06-25 — static-local _Owned guard bypassed via generic (+ silent leak) — FOLDED-F82 (LOW, not filed)
- `void f<T>(void){ static T x; ... }; f<int *_Owned>()` → ACCEPTED (rc=0) while DIRECT `static int *_Owned x`
  is REJECTED ("type of static local variable cannot be qualified by '_Owned'" = the F95 fix). So F95's static-
  local-owned guard is NOT re-checked at monomorph (same meta-pattern as G17's union-field guard).
- Runtime: unmarked `f<int *_Owned>()` assigning a static owned across 2 calls → valgrind "4 bytes definitely
  lost" (clobbered alloc1; the analysis can't see cross-call static state — which is WHY the guard exists).
- WHY FOLD-F82 / LOW (not a soundness hole): dedup → F82 already documents the static-local owned/borrow gate
  escape (isFileContext-only gate), concluded LOW because only UNSAFE context accepts it. Confirmed the _Safe
  backstop STILL HOLDS for the generic route: `_Safe f<T>(){ static T x = nullptr; }` → REJECTED "nonnull pointer
  cannot be assigned by nullable pointer" (int *_Owned is nonnull; nullptr-init rejected; malloc-init non-constant
  → also rejected) — a static-owned can NEVER be validly constructed in _Safe. The leak is unsafe-only =
  programmer's responsibility. New MECHANISM (generic bypasses F95 at monomorph) but same F82 LOW conclusion.
  Distinct from G17 (union case: constructible+tracked in checked code, novel conformance → filed; static case:
  unsafe-only, already-documented F82 → not filed). NOT filed. /tmp/claude-998/cgstat_*.cbs, cgsafe_*.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — conditional move via ternary `consume(c?o1:o2)` — SOUND (conservative)
- After `consume(c ? o1 : o2)`, both `consume(o1)` and `consume(o2)` → "use of moved value" (each). The analysis
  conservatively marks BOTH ternary operands moved (can't know the branch) → no use-after-move FN. The "can't use
  the not-moved one" is correct conservative behavior (matches Rust conditional-move), not a soundness bug.
  Ownership move-tracking through ternary SOUND. /tmp/claude-998/cmov2.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — D1 oracle: ensure_init_if_ret PLACEMENT semantic-checks are warnings-not-errors — LOW, NOT FILED
- spec_verifier D1 on BiShengCLanguageUserManual.md surfaced manual "语义检查" rules implemented as warnings:
  - rule1 (only function params): global `__attribute__((ensure_init_if_ret(1))) int g_a;` and local
    `int *_Borrow __attribute__((ensure_init_if_ret(0))) m;` → warning "attribute only applies to parameters
    [-Wignored-attributes]", rc=0. Manual: error 不能修饰全局/局部变量.
  - rule2 (pointer/borrow type): `int __attribute__((ensure_init_if_ret(0))) x` param → warning "only applies to
    pointer parameters [-Wignored-attributes]", rc=0. Manual: error 第二个形参不是指针/借用.
- DEDUP: new (F73/F100 are ensure_init contract VARIANCE/laundering, not PLACEMENT). 
- WHY LOW / NOT FILED (validate-the-invariant, G16 lesson): warn-and-IGNORE a misplaced attribute is a DEFENSIBLE
  standard clang model — the user IS diagnosed (warning), the attribute is harmlessly ignored (the contract is
  meaningless on a global/local/non-pointer, so no reliance is possible), and NO soundness hole exists (the fn
  type carries no contract → the variance checks prevent any silent caller reliance). Manual "error" is merely
  stricter than a reasonable impl; docs-vs-impl strictness gap, not a clear compiler bug. Distinct from G17
  (G17 SILENTLY accepts w/ no diagnostic; here a warning fires). Recorded for future D1 sweeps; do not re-probe.
  /tmp/claude-998/eig.cbs, eil.cbs, eip.cbs.
