## 2026-06-25 — loop-carried owned move — SOUND
- `int *_Owned o=mk(); for(i<n){ consume(o); }` → "use of moved value: `o`" (back-edge sees o moved on iter 2+)
  + "memory leak of value: `o`" (n=0 path, never freed). Ownership dataflow propagates moved-state across loop
  back-edge correctly. SOUND. /tmp/claude-998/lmov.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — move-then-reinit struct field — SOUND
- `struct Pair{int*_Owned a,b;}; consume(p.a); p.a=mk(); consume(p.a);` → rc=0. Re-initializing a moved field
  restores it (no use-after-move FP); field-granular move + reinit dataflow correct. /tmp/claude-998/reinit.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — ownership move across _Unsafe→_Safe boundary — SOUND
- `int *_Owned o=mk(); _Unsafe{consume(o);} consume(o);` → "use of moved value: `o`" in the _Safe use. The
  ownership analysis tracks moves INSIDE _Unsafe blocks and propagates moved-state to subsequent _Safe code.
  No cross-zone ownership FN (contrast F104: init-analysis ensure_init laundering across safety zones — different
  analyzer/pipeline). /tmp/claude-998/uzmov.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — goto-skipped initialization — SOUND
- `int x; if(c) goto skip; x=5; skip: return x;` → "use of possibly uninitialized value: `x`" (CFG path through
  goto skips the init). Init-analysis handles forward-goto-skipped init correctly. /tmp/claude-998/gotoinit.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — switch fall-through ownership move — SOUND
- `switch(c){ case 1: consume(o); case 2: consume(o); }` → "use of moved value: `o`" in case 2 (fall-through edge
  carries moved-state) + leak on unmatched paths. CFG models switch fall-through correctly. /tmp/claude-998/swmov.cbs.
- CONTROL-FLOW MOVE COVERAGE now comprehensive & SOUND: if/else, loop back-edge, ternary (conservative both-moved),
  switch fall-through, goto-skip (init), _Unsafe→_Safe boundary — all tracked. Ownership/init dataflow robust
  across all reachable control-flow constructs.

## 2026-06-25 — conditional move-out (return) vs leak — SOUND
- `int *_Owned o=mk(); if(c) return o; return mk();` → c-true: move-out OK; c-false: "memory leak of value: `o`"
  (o neither returned nor freed). Move-out-via-return recognized per-path; leak flagged on the non-return path.
  /tmp/claude-998/condret.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — CONFIRMED-F116 (FILED): -rewrite-bsc keeps _Nullable/_Nonnull → output not gcc-portable
- ORACLE SWITCH win: after ~47 read-analyzer cycles saturated (all sound), switched to the -rewrite-bsc
  gcc-portability differential (the F09/G14/G18 oracle) → found F116.
- Rewriter strips _Owned/_Borrow/_Safe but KEEPS _Nullable/_Nonnull (compile-time-only Clang quals gcc can't
  parse). Confirmed asymmetry: `struct O{int*_Owned}` by-value → _Owned stripped, gcc 0 err; `struct S{int*
  _Nullable p}` by-value → _Nullable kept, gcc 2 err (parse error before 'p'), clang -x c rc=0. Violates manual
  6669 (output compilable w/ gcc). DISTINCT from ensure_init-kept (gcc WARNS+compiles=LOW), G14/G18 (invalid for
  ANY compiler), F54/F59 (drop bugs). MEDIUM, fix=strip nullability quals in rewriter type-spelling path.
  FILED IJP3AP. Repro F116. /tmp/claude-998/rw_nullable_field.c, ostrip.c.

## 2026-06-25 — F116 blast radius / precise scope — STRUCT-FIELD path ONLY
- Tested all positions through -rewrite-bsc → gcc: function PARAM `int *_Nullable p` (stripped, gcc OK), RETURN
  (stripped), LOCAL var (stripped), TYPEDEF'd field (stripped), STRUCT/UNION FIELD `int *_Nullable p;` (KEPT,
  gcc FAILS). _Nonnull field == _Nullable field (both kept). _ArrayElem field stripped (gcc OK).
- So F116 is the rewriter's STRUCT/UNION-FIELD type-spelling path specifically — all other paths already strip
  nullability. Precise root → precise fix (mirror the field path to the param/return path's stripping). bug_log
  F116 entry + repro comment refined to this scope (validate-the-invariant; original prose over-broad). One root,
  narrow blast radius (field declarations only).

## 2026-06-25 — -rewrite-bsc gcc-portability oracle sweep — MAPPED (F116 + folds; rest portable)
- anon/nested struct field `struct W{ struct{ int *_Nullable q; } inner; }` → gcc FAILS = FOLD-F116 (same
  struct-field nullability-not-stripped path, just nested/anonymous). Broadens F116 to nested+anon struct fields.
- gcc-PORTABLE (stripped correctly): _Unsafe{}/_Safe{} block stmts, const _Borrow field, _Owned/_Borrow/_Safe
  (all positions), _ArrayElem, _Nullable/_Nonnull in param/return/local/typedef.
- gcc-WARN-only (LOW, not filed): ensure_init_if_ret kept (compiles w/ -Wattributes).
- CONCLUSION: the -rewrite-bsc gcc-portability surface is mapped. The ONE bug is F116 (struct/union FIELD
  nullability not stripped, incl. nested/anon). Oracle (F09/G14/G18/F116) now saturated for portability.

  reconcile safety zones (accepts _Safe-decl + _Unsafe-def). So F117's hole is SPECIFICALLY the redecl/merge gate;

## 2026-06-25 — struct-field conditional-move merge double-free — FOLDED-F75 (re-validated, still reproduces)
- `struct O{int*_Owned p;}; if(c){consume(o.p);} consume(o.p);` → compile rc=0 (NOT flagged); non-field baseline
  `if(c){consume(x);} consume(x)` IS flagged "use of moved value: x". Runtime (c=1): valgrind "Invalid free()".
  Local-struct variant also rc=0 (not a by-value-param confound).
- EXACTLY F75 (HIGH, filed): OwnershipImpl::merge UNIONs the owned-field set across CFG predecessors instead of
  MEETing it → a field moved on one branch is restored to "owned" at the join → the field-move state is lost at
  the merge → use-after-move/double-free not flagged. ("UNIONs" = the SET op, applies to any struct field.) The
  non-field var case MEETs correctly (flagged); only the per-field set-merge is buggy. Re-validated F75 OPEN.
  NOT new, not re-filed. /tmp/claude-998/dfmerge.cbs, dflocal.cbs, dfrun.cbs, dfvar.cbs(baseline).

## 2026-06-25 — init-analysis conditional field merge — SOUND (contrast F75)
- `struct S{int a,b;}; s.a=1; if(c){s.b=2;} return s.a+s.b;` → "use of possibly uninitialized value: `s.b`"
  (maybe-uninit on c-false, correctly MEET'd); both-branch control rc=0. Init-analysis field merge is SOUND —
  contrast F75 (ownership merge set-UNIONs field-state across branches = double-free FN). The two analyzers'
  merge logic differs; only the ownership-field merge is buggy. /tmp/claude-998/initmerge.cbs, initok.cbs.

## 2026-06-25 — -nullability-check=all is a GLOBAL audit, default is zone-gated — INTENDED (not a bug)
- Default (no flag): _Safe nullable-deref REJECTED, _Unsafe nullable-deref ALLOWED (rc=0) → nullability check is
  ZONE-GATED by default (only _Safe; _Unsafe = programmer's responsibility, correct).
- -nullability-check=all: _Unsafe nullable-deref REJECTED too → the "all" flag makes it a GLOBAL audit across all
  zones. INTENDED (the flag name = "all"; audits every nullable deref incl. unsafe for review). Not an FP.
- CLARIFIES prior probes: my nullability probes used -nullability-check=all (global-audit mode); their results
  reflect the audit, which matches _Safe behavior for in-_Safe constructs. Flag semantics documented.

## 2026-06-26 S3 — mangler collision survey (GLM source-round in parallel)
- g<int*> -> "g_int_P" (space-before-* becomes '_'); g<int> -> "g_int". Pointer space disambiguates.
- foo<MyInt> == foo<int> -> both "foo_int": mangler DESUGARS typedefs to canonical => sugared-name collision NOT possible (correct).
- Box<SafeFP> == Box<UnsafeFP> -> SAME struct name "Box_void_LPPRPLPvoidRP": struct-mangle path does NOT set
  MangleWithSafeQualifier (function-mangle DOES). ASYMMETRY but BENIGN — _Safe/_Unsafe only qualify fnptr types
  (identical layout, NO drop), so no wrong-code (contrast G14 owned-vs-raw where drop-codegen differs => harmful).
  Latent: would only bite if a _Safe/_Unsafe-distinguished type ever had a layout/drop difference (none exists in BSC).
- CONCLUSION: char-substitution is injective for canonical types; mangler collisions reduce to qualifier-DROPS
  (G14 owned/borrow, G12 nullability — both filed). No new mangler soundness bug from this survey.
## 2026-06-26 S3 — generic returns uninit owned T x;return x; @ T=int*_Owned — rc=1 err: memory leak of value: `r` (init-analysis re-runs at monomorph SOUND)
## 2026-06-26 S3 — generic returns dangling borrow of local param @T=int — rc=1 err: no matching function for call to 'bad' (borrow-ck re-runs at monomorph SOUND)

## 2026-06-26 S3 — GLM source-based mangler-collision round: ALL 4 REFUTED
GLM (fed real MangleBSC.cpp) proposed 4 user-struct-named-after-substitution-output collisions. Empirically refuted:
- struct type-args print WITH the "struct " elaborated prefix: foo<struct int_P> -> "foo_struct_int_P" (NOT "foo_int_P").
- pointers get space->'_': foo<int*> -> "foo_int_P" (NOT "foo_intP"); GLM assumed no-space.
- arity-distinct templates (foo<A,B> vs foo<C>) can't share a name. => NO realizable collision.
CONCLUSION: mangler injective for canonical types; only collisions are the filed qualifier-DROPS (G14 owned/borrow, G12 nullability). Two GLM generics rounds (hypothesis + source-mangler) => no new bug; generics mangler SOUND. Method-diversity confirmed saturation.

## 2026-06-26 S3 — F119 ESCALATION: cast disables ALL loan-based borrow checks (not just free-while-borrowed)
- return-escape/lifetime: `return (int*_Borrow)&_Mut local` rc=0 (NO cast: rc=1 "local does not live long enough").
  Cast-borrow of a local can be RETURNED → caller gets dangling stack borrow → UAF. Same :541 root.

  lifetime/return-escape, exclusivity) is bypassed for that borrow. F119 is more severe than one check: it disables
  borrow checking for any cast-borrow. Fold into F119 (same root), escalate severity/blast-radius note.

## 2026-06-26 S3 — GLM round-4 candidate (discarded-deref-after-move): VALIDATED but LOW/harmless, NOT FILED
- `consume(o); *o;` (discarded read of moved owned) → rc=0 (use-of-moved missed). ALSO `(void)*o;`, `*o+1;`, `o;` rc=0.
  BUT valgrind (-O0 AND -O2): 0 errors — the discarded read is ELIMINATED (no side effect → no actual freed-mem access).
- MEANINGFUL uses ARE caught: `*o=5;` (write) rc=1 "use of moved"; `return *o;` rc=1; `o+1;` rc=1.
- VERDICT: real compile-time check-completeness gap (use-of-moved misses discarded-READ positions) but NO runtime
  memory-safety impact (the missed positions are no-ops the optimizer drops). GLM's "use-after-free" framing is
  incorrect — there is no runtime UAF. Severity LOW. NOT FILED (HIGH/MED-only; harmless). Distinct from F119.

## 2026-06-26 S3 — GLM round-5 candidate (forget<T> leaks owned): REJECTED — INTENDED BEHAVIOR (not a bug)
- forget<T>(T t) (libcbs bishengc_safety.hbs:29-30) = BSC std::mem::forget: "takes ownership and forgets the value
  WITHOUT running its destructor" — a deliberate, documented leak primitive (used by option.hbs/result.hbs). Leaking
  is memory-safe (no UAF/UB); forget is the sanctioned opt-out of _Safe's no-leak rule. The checker correctly treats
  forget(o) as discharging the move obligation. GLM mistook the documented leak escape-hatch for a soundness hole.
  NOT a bug, NOT filed. (A real bug would require UAF/double-free; forget only leaks, which is by-design safe.)

## 2026-06-26 S3 — GLM round-12 (audit) candidate: GNU `?:` double-free [BORDERLINE SCOPE — awaiting user]
VALIDATED on canonical binary (411b4118/022fd2ae): `consume(p ?: mk()); consume(p);` → compile rc=0, valgrind "Invalid free()".
Control std ternary `c ? p : mk()` → rc=1 (caught). Root: IRBuilder move-tracking Visit dispatch (BSCIRBuilder.cpp)
does NOT lower BinaryConditionalOperator (GNU `x ?: y`, common subexpr in OpaqueValueExpr) → move-through-`?:` invisible
to ownership → double-move/double-free. DISTINCT root from F75 (merge) / F118 (Sema CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak — that
one DOES handle AbstractConditionalOperator incl. BinaryConditionalOperator at :551, this is the IRBuilder move-track path)
/ F119 (loan-record cast). SCOPE: `x ?: y` is a GNU extension (user sensitive, F38 burn) BUT a RUNTIME conditional, not in
OUT_OF_SCOPE_KEYWORDS, and not a compile-time-selection construct like _Generic. → ASKING user for scope call before filing.
## 2026-06-26 S3 — F120 FILED (umbrella IJP3AP): GNU elvis double-free, OpaqueValueExpr common-subexpr move not propagated. GLM round-12 audit oracle FOUND it (the dispatch-coverage oracle is productive). Conductor root-isolated + validated. 2nd NEW bug from GLM (after F119); GLM also corroborated F75+F118.

## 2026-06-27 — OVERNIGHT BACKLOG TRIAGE (GLM rounds 14-100, ~25 candidates, Conductor-dispositioned):
- F121 FILED (NEW, distinct root): owned temp dropped in VarDecl-init/return expr not leak-checked (CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak
  invocation-coverage gap; init/return have no call site). `return mk()?1:0` / `int x=!mk()` / `int x=(mk(),5)` leak 4 bytes.
  Statement-context forms (mk()?1:0; / !mk(); / mk(),5;) are CAUGHT — so GLM's many statement-context leak claims were FALSE ALARMS.
- comma/ternary-cond/LNot as DISCARDED STATEMENT = CAUGHT (false alarms): A1 `mk(),5;`, B1 `mk()?1:2;`, C1 `!mk();` all rc=1 "memory leak".
- I (generic move-out-of-borrow) = REAL double-free but OOS (generics): `_Safe T dup<T>(T*_Borrow p){return *p;}` instantiated
  T=int*_Owned → exit=0, valgrind Invalid free. Non-generic `int*_Owned o2=*b;` is REJECTED (conversion error). So generic-only →
  generic monomorphization skips the move-out-of-borrow/conversion check. GENERICS OOS (user explicit) → DOCUMENTED-NOT-FILED.
  (If scope ever expands to generics, this is a real HIGH.)
- E overwrite-initialized-owned-through-borrow-write = F34 (open) dup. G switch-field-move-then-free = F75 (switch variant) dup.
  H deref-move-owned-field-through-borrow = F21/F79 dup. F generic-owned-struct-by-value-field-move = OOS(generics+owned struct)/F75.
  D owned-struct-into-&&/|| = OOS (_Owned struct).
- J owned field nested >=11 levels not recorded = FALSE ALARM (12-level local leak CAUGHT, exit=1). K __move_to_raw discharge =
  INTENDED into_raw primitive (builtin, not in header; manual ownership transfer like forget) — not a bug.
NET from overnight: 1 NEW filed (F121) + F120 (round-12, filed). Rest dup/OOS/false/intended. GLM yield: F119+F120+F121 new, F75+F118 corroborated.

## 2026-06-27 — CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak cand-2 (array-subscript move-through-borrow): SHAPE-REJECTED.
Probe: int *_Owned arr[2]; ... int*_Owned(*_Borrow b)[2]=&_Mut arr; consume((*b)[0]) → compile error "type of array cannot
be qualified by '_Owned'". ARRAYS OF _Owned are FORBIDDEN, so there's no reachable array-subscript-move-through-borrow case.
BOUNDS the move-through-borrow cluster: reachable access forms = deref(*b)+member(b.f), BOTH handled by CheckMoveVarMemoryLeak;
arrays-of-owned forbidden (no subscript); the only gaps are WRAPPERS (ternary=F25, paren=F21, move-field=F79, all filed). No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — VisitCallExpr cand-2 (indirect-call arg nullability): SHAPE-REJECTED-as-sound (bounds F31).
Probe: typedef void(*fn_t)(int*_Nonnull); _Safe g(fn_t fp,int*_Nullable n){ fp(n); } → exit=1 "Nullable" (CAUGHT). Even
though VisitCallExpr uses getDirectCallee() (skips indirect calls), the nullable→nonnull ARG is caught by the
assignment-compatibility check at the call site (arg-passing = assignment). So F31's blast radius does NOT extend to args —
F31 is NARROWLY the fnptr CALLEE deref (calling a null fp), which no assignment check covers (it's a runtime fp-deref).
F31 bounded. (My first probe was malformed: _Borrow-qualified fnptr + &_Mut forbidden; correct form = typedef fnptr.)

## 2026-06-27 — codegen owned-drop surface: CONFIRMED MINIMAL (no auto-drop). BSC has NO codegen auto-drop (unlike Rust/C++):
an unconsumed _Owned at scope-end is a COMPILE-TIME leak diagnostic (F14/F118/F121 = the temp-leak/unconsumed checks), NOT
codegen auto-freed. Frees are EXPLICIT library calls (safe_free/consume). So the "codegen emits wrong drop" bug class does
not exist; the only codegen-adjacent surface is the REWRITER (RewriteBSC: F09 open rewriter-leak, F14 fixed). Bounds the
codegen surface — runtime valgrind (GLM loop + ~45 probes) covers the explicit-free correctness, all sound. No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — FindLoansThatFreeze cand-1 (storage-dead escape): PROBED-sound.
Probe: int*_Borrow b; { int x=5; b=&_Mut x; } *b=7 → exit=1 "`x` does not live long enough" (inner-block local borrow used
after the block caught via CheckStorageDead/FindLoansThatFreeze). Scope-end/storage-dead handling SOUND. Borrow checker now
probed at every level incl. scope-end/lifetime; all sound except F119. No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — FindLoansThatIntersect cand-1 (dynamic-index array aliasing): PROBED-sound.
Probe: int arr[3]; b=&_Mut arr[i]; arr[j]=5; *b → exit=1 "cannot assign to `arr[]` because it is borrowed". Array elements
treated INDEX-AGNOSTICALLY (arr[]) → write to any element while another borrowed conflicts → conservative-SOUND (no
dynamic-index aliasing FN; may over-reject distinct static indices as FPs = safe direction). Borrow checker arrays sound.
No new defect. [Borrow checker now probed at every level + every construct incl. arrays; all sound except F119.]

## 2026-06-27 — checkMemoryLeak (continue-skip-consume leak): PROBED-sound.
Probe: for(i<n){ int*_Owned o=mk(); if(i%2) continue; consume(o); } → exit=1 "memory leak" (o owned at iter-end on the
continue path caught). Leak-at-scope-end detection (checkMemoryLeak :1941, !canAssign→MemoryLeak, +field-leak) sound;
handles continue/loop control-flow. Ownership leak detection mapped + sound. No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — _Unsafe-block ownership tracking: PROBED-sound.
Probe: _Safe void g(){ int*_Owned o=mk(); _Unsafe { safe_free((void*_Owned)o); } consume(o); } → exit=1 "use of moved".
The ownership analysis TRACKS operations inside _Unsafe blocks — the safe_free(o) inside _Unsafe is seen as a consume of o,
so the subsequent consume(o) in _Safe is caught as use-after-move. _Unsafe blocks are NOT opaque to ownership → no double-free
FN via _Unsafe-block-free. Sound. (Contrast F117: _Safe-decl + _Unsafe-DEFINITION launders at the function-body level; but an
_Unsafe BLOCK inside a _Safe body IS tracked.) IsUnsafeType (:1110) = unsafe-type check, sound. No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — F64 re-validated @34883aa: PROBED-confirmed-F64 (still open).
consume(*pp) for pp:int*_Owned* — move-tracking doesn't model *pp deref → consume(*pp);consume(*pp) silently accepted →
compile rc=0 + valgrind Invalid free x2. Still reproduces on current binary (revert nullability-only, didn't affect it).
Filed HIGH (umbrella). Root: ownership move-tracking doesn't model the *pp deref for int*_Owned* (pointer-to-owned-pointer).
Distinct from F21/F79 (single-level deref-through-borrow). SSOT updated. No re-file (filed).

## 2026-06-27 — canAssign + partial-field-consume leak: PROBED-sound.
Probe: struct S{int*_Owned a,b;}; s.a=mk();s.b=mk(); consume(s.a) → exit=1 "field memory leak of value: s, b" (s.b unconsumed
at scope-end → FieldMemoryLeak). canAssign (no-live-owned predicate) + checkMemoryLeak + OwnedOwnedFields field-leak path
SOUND. Ownership leak detection mapped to the predicate level. No new defect. [Every ownership predicate/helper now read:
is/has, canAssign, checkMemoryLeak, merge; all sound except F75 (merge field-set union).]

## 2026-06-27 — irreducible-CFG init flow (PROGRESS ⬜ row): PROBED-sound.
Probe: int*_Owned p; if(c) goto mid; while(1){ p=mk(); mid: consume(p); break; } → exit=1 "use of possibly uninit". The
init-analysis CFG fixed-point handles IRREDUCIBLE CFGs (goto jumping into the loop body skips p=mk() → on the goto path p is
maybe-uninit at consume → caught). Dataflow convergence sound on multi-entry/irreducible CFG. PROGRESS ⬜→✅. No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — _Safe/_Unsafe propagation through typedef (PROGRESS ⬜ row): PROBED-sound.
Probe: _Unsafe int danger(void){...} typedef int(*fn_t)(void); _Safe int g(){ fn_t fp=danger; return fp(); } → exit=1
"_Unsafe function call is forbidden in the safe zone". The SafeZoneSpecifier is carried on FunctionProtoType::ExtProtoInfo
(checkFunctionProtoType TypeBSC:133) → preserved through typedefs → no _Unsafe-into-_Safe launder via typedef'd fnptr. SOUND.
PROGRESS ⬜→✅. No new defect.

## 2026-06-27 — F92 re-validated @34883aa: PROBED-confirmed-F92 (still open).
With -nullability-check=all: direct *q (L76) + subscript q[0] (L79) controls correctly REJECTED ("nullable cannot be
dereferenced"), but the BUG `*(q + n)` (pointer-ARITHMETIC deref) is NOT flagged (only 2 errors = the 2 controls);
default-build valgrind Invalid read of size 4. F92 = VisitUnaryOperator UO_Deref checks getExprPathNullability(subexpr) but
`q+n` (BinaryOperator BO_Add) path-nullability isn't computed as nullable → launder. Still open; the upstream revert touched
nullability ASSIGN-compat (CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment), NOT this deref/path-nullability gap. SSOT updated. No re-file (open, likely filed).

## 2026-06-27 — F92 root confirmed (getExprPathNullability BinaryOperator :381 misses BO_Add/BO_Sub): PROBED-confirmed-F92.
Probe: *(q-1)[BO_Sub] + *(q+0)[BO_Add] for _Nullable q → both UNFLAGGED (only *q control caught). Root: getExprPathNullability
:381 handles BO_Comma/BO_Assign only; pointer-arith BO_Add/BO_Sub break→fallback→not nullable→deref launders. Fix=add BO_Add/
BO_Sub at :381. F92 filed/open; both arith directions fold into it. No re-file.
## 2026-06-27 — F92 FILED (umbrella IJP3AP): deref *(q+n) nullability launder (getExprPathNullability:381 BO_Add/BO_Sub gap). Was retracted-as-dup-of-F18 (2026-06-04) assuming F18's fix covers it; re-validation @34883aa showed F18 fixed only the ASSIGN, the DEREF root persists + was unfiled. Conductor re-validation caught the incomplete-fix. 4th filed this session (F119/F120/F121/F92).

- 2026-06-29 **struct-by-value `_Owned` field, caller derefs freed field** — `struct S{int*_Owned p;}; _Safe void sink(struct S s){consume(s.p);} _Safe void g(){struct S s; s.p=mk(); sink(s); *s.p=7;}` → compile rc=0, valgrind `Invalid write of size 4`. **FOLDED-into-F67** (deref-USE of a moved struct field unchecked at MemberExpr site). By-value pass IS tracked as a move (2nd `sink(s)` rejected "use of partially moved value"; pass-only no double-free) — only the deref-use is the hole = F67. Also tested: copy-init `struct O o2=o;` + return-by-value `make()` → now **SOUND** (source correctly moved, 1/1, 0 errors). Do NOT re-probe.

- 2026-06-29 **SafeExpr in constant-required position (CGExprConstant `ConstExprEmitter` missing `VisitSafeExpr`)** — `const int G=_Safe(5+2); int A[2]={_Safe(1),2}; int S[_Safe(4)]; enum{E=_Unsafe(3)};` → compile rc=0; IR emits correct constants (`@G=7`, `@A=[1,2]`, `@S=[4 x i32]`). **PROBED-SOUND (latent-unreachable):** the missing visitor IS real (only emitter without VisitSafeExpr, vs Agg/Scalar/Complex which have it) but the APValue evaluator `ExprConstant.cpp` (handles SafeExpr) folds the initializer before `ConstExprEmitter` is consulted, so the gap is never the deciding path. Same outcome class as WalkerBSC `VisitQualType` latent-unreachable. Do NOT re-probe. See code_notes/CGExprConstant.md.

- 2026-06-29 **owned temp in if/while/switch CONDITION** — `while(mk()){break;}`, `if(mk()){}`, `do{}while(!mk());` all **CAUGHT** (rc=1 "memory leak ... temporary is _Owned"). The condition full-expr is leak-checked at ActOnCondition build-time; `ActOnWhileStmt` itself having no `CheckTemporaryVarMemoryLeak` call (only `ActOnDoStmt`:1748 / `ActOnForStmt`:2214 do) is a real-but-harmless source observation — direct-condition owned temps don't slip. The ONLY condition-position leak is the `&&`/`||` operand: `if(mk() && c)` = 4B lost, `while(mk() && c)` = 8B/2iters lost (rc=0) = **F118** (round 420 folds here). Do NOT re-probe condition-position owned-temp leaks; they're F118 or caught.

- 2026-06-29 **array of plain struct with _Owned field** — `struct S{int*_Owned v;} arr[2];` → **REJECTED at declaration** "type of array cannot be qualified by `_Owned`(even indirectly)" (§3.7.3 array-of-owned guard); scalar `struct S s; s.v=mk();` correctly CAUGHT (field leak). The array-element-owned-field LEAK (round 403) reproduces ONLY for GENERIC `struct Box<int*_Owned> arr[2]` (guard hole for generic-instantiated structs) = OUT OF SCOPE (generic). Round 373 (generic Box conditional-move double-free) = FOLDED-F75. Do NOT re-probe in-scope; plain form is fail-closed.

- 2026-06-29 **negated-AND nullability guard** — `if(!(p && q)) return; need(p);` → REJECTED (FP, p is nonnull after guard); De Morgan twin `if(!p || !q) return; need(p);` → ACCEPTED. Asymmetry proves implementation inconsistency → **CONFIRMED-F122** (first nullability false positive; filed IJP3AP). Else-of-`!(AND)` and fall-through-after-`!(AND)`-return both fail; single-var/non-negated-AND/distributed-OR all narrow. Do NOT re-probe; variants fold to F122.

- 2026-06-29 **sizeof(*p) after move** — `consume(p); return sizeof(*p);` → compile rc=0 (correctly NOT flagged use-after-move), valgrind 0 errors. Both checkers' VisitUnaryExprOrTypeTraitExpr (BSCOwnership:2269, BSCBorrowChecker:872) are no-op `{return;}` — correctly skip the unevaluated sizeof/alignof operand. PROBED-SOUND. Do NOT re-probe.

- 2026-06-29 **over-narrow FN dual of F122 (mixed compound else-branch)** — `if(p && !q){}else{ need(q); }`, `if(p && !q) return; need(q);` → both REJECTED (q correctly NOT narrowed nonnull in the else, since p-null/q-null reaches the else). The null↔present swap (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:806-812) is applied PER-CFG-terminator-block; short-circuit `&&`/`||` get their own terminators so the soundness (FN) direction holds. **PROBED-SOUND** — confirms F122 is bounded to the FP/under-narrow direction (specifically the `!(parenthesized-AND)` single-negation form); NO soundness hole, validating F122 MEDIUM/fails-closed. Do NOT re-probe the FN direction.

- 2026-06-29 **ensure_init × _Nonnull pointee field (cross-analyzer FN hypothesis)** — within-TU ensure_init callee with empty body → REJECTED "'*p' not initialized at return in __attribute__((ensure_init)) function"; can't satisfy the contract while leaving a _Nonnull field null (writing it requires nonnull, else nullability check fires). Raw `*p`/`&s` shape-rejected in _Safe (`*`/`&` forbidden) — needs _Borrow. Extern-declaration-lies case = by-design trust model, not a compiler FN. **PROBED-SOUND**, closes the composition_init_null.md HIGH open candidate. Do NOT re-probe.

- 2026-06-29 **reborrow outlives source scope** — `{int y; p=&_Mut y; outer=&_Mut *p;} use(outer);` → REJECTED "`y` does not live long enough". EnsureBorrowSource (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:2183) outlives-constraint SOUND; PathType switch exhaustive (Var/Extension, both handled). Residual UNPROBED edge: null-Decl borrow base (temporary/call-result reborrow) skips the constraint — hard to construct, LOW priority. Do NOT re-probe the basic reborrow lifetime.

- 2026-06-29 **nullability CFG-merge under-approx (F75-analog)** — `if(c){ if(!p) return; need(p); } need(p);` → outer need(p) REJECTED (p Nullable on c-false path); control `if(!p) return; need(p);` ACCEPTS. mergeVD (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:849) is a proper MEET (Nullable wins); initStatus pre-seeds all nullable VDs as Nullable so no absence→over-narrow. **PROBED-SOUND** — the C5 merge-union bug that causes F75 in ownership does NOT exist in nullability. Do NOT re-probe.

- 2026-06-29 **init-analysis CFG-merge (F75-analog, 3rd analyzer)** — `int x; if(c){x=5;} return x;` → REJECT "use of possibly uninitialized value: x"; field `s.b` one-branch-init → REJECT; both-branch-init → ACCEPT. meetStates (BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:680) is a proper 3-element meet with MaybeInit; missing field entries meet with Uninitialized. **PROBED-SOUND**. Cross-analyzer C5: ownership=F75 (union, no maybe-elem, BUGGY), nullability=sound (meet), init=sound (meet w/ MaybeInit). F75 is the lone merge-state defect; fix = add maybe-moved element. Do NOT re-probe.

- 2026-06-29 **getDefNullability typedef sugar** — `typedef int *_Nullable NP; f(NP p){ need(p); }`, `typedef int *RP`, double-nested typedef → all REJECT (tracked as Nullable). getDefNullability is sugar-aware (getNullability sees through typedefs; raw defaults Nullable). PROBED-SOUND, no typedef-laundered-nullability FN. Do NOT re-probe.

- 2026-06-29 **FP-oracle batch: owned reassignment/loop/branch idioms** — reassign-after-move `consume(p); p=mk(); consume(p);`, conditional consume+reassign owned-on-both-paths `if(c){consume(p);p=mk();} consume(p);`, owned alloc+free in a for-loop, move-on-one-branch-then-return `if(c){consume(p);return;} consume(p);` → ALL correctly ACCEPTED (no false positive). The ownership move-tracking is not over-conservative on these idioms. (A `&_Const *p` borrow-then-move ternary probe hit a genuine const-borrow→borrow conversion error = malformed test, not an analyzer FP.) PROBED-SOUND, no FP. Do NOT re-probe these idioms.

- 2026-06-29 **FP-oracle: nullability comparison-form narrowing** — `if(p!=nullptr){need(p);}`, yoda `if(nullptr!=p)`, `if(p==nullptr) return; need(p);`, yoda `if(nullptr==p) return;`, `if(p==0) return;` → ALL ACCEPT. Comparison narrowing symmetric (yoda handled) across ==/!=/nullptr/0; extractDistinguishedTrackablePtr normalizes. No FP. F122 (`!(p&&q)` negated-compound) remains the lone narrowing FP. PROBED-SOUND. Do NOT re-probe comparison forms.

- 2026-06-29 **-rewrite-bsc: _Safe/_Unsafe expression wrappers (SafeExpr)** — `int a=_Safe(*p+1); int b=_Unsafe(*p-1);` → rewritten C: `int a=(*p+1); int b=(*p-1);` (SafeExpr stripped to plain paren); `_Borrow` param → `int * p`; no BSC markers remain. Correctly gcc-portable. PROBED-SOUND (distinct from F116 which is the field-NULLABILITY strip gap; SafeExpr/_Safe-expr strip is correct everywhere). Do NOT re-probe SafeExpr rewriting.

- 2026-06-29 **F116 root-correction: rewriter nullability-keep is rewrite-TRIGGER, not field-path** — BSCFeatureFinder (WalkerBSC.h:48) doesn't count `_Nullable`/`_Nonnull` as a BSC feature, so a nullability-ONLY decl is skipped by -rewrite-bsc (RewriteBSC.cpp:509-521,654/911) → emitted verbatim → gcc-fails. CONFIRMED-F116 (broadens it): plain prototype `void f(int*_Nullable x)` KEPT+gcc-fail (simplest case); `_Safe`/`_Owned` on same decl triggers rewrite → STRIP. F116's "field-path-only / params-strip" scope was WRONG (tested _Safe params). gcc-FATAL only when a NAME follows the kept `_Nullable`. Fix = add nullability to BSCFeatureFinder. Repro F116b. Corrected F116 entry + WalkerBSC.md.

- 2026-06-29 **F116 blast-radius position sweep (refines the correction)** — KEEP (gcc-fails): plain prototype param `void f(int*_Nullable x)`, featureless-fn return `int*_Nullable rf(void)`, featureless-fn local, nullable struct field. STRIP (gcc-ok): global `int*_Nullable gv`, plain typedef `typedef int*_Nullable NP`. Rule = the rewriter SKIPS FunctionDecl/RecordDecl lacking a BSC feature (params/returns/locals/fields kept); globals+typedefs always re-spelled (strip). A feature (`_Owned` field / `_Safe`) on the same fn/struct rewrites ALL members incl. nullable (mixed struct strips both fields). Earlier correction's "global/typedef keep" was WRONG — fixed in bug_log + umbrella follow-up.

- 2026-06-29 **_Safe BLOCK ownership enforcement** — `void g(void){ _Safe { int *_Owned p = mk(); } }` (owned leak inside a `_Safe {}` block in a PLAIN non-safe function) → CAUGHT "memory leak of value: p" (rc=1), same as the `_Safe` FUNCTION control. A `_Safe` block gets full ownership checking (not opaque). PROBED-SOUND, no FN. Do NOT re-probe. (cf F117 = safe/unsafe REDECL launder, distinct.)

- 2026-06-29 **Filed-repro re-validation (check_probes --repros-only)** — 112 matched, 0 compile-fail. - 2026-06-29 **self-assign owned `p=p` runtime** — `int *_Owned p=mk(); p=p; consume(p);` → compile rc=0, valgrind 1 alloc/1 free, 0 errors. Move-bookkeeping handles self-move-assign correctly (net p owns the block, freed once). PROBED-SOUND, no leak/double-free.

PROBED-SOUND.

- 2026-06-29 **F116 _Nonnull confirmation** — `void takes_nonnull(int *_Nonnull x);` (plain) → KEPT, gcc-fails ("expected ';' before 'x'"); `_Safe` variant → `int *x` stripped. `_Nonnull` behaves identically to `_Nullable` (both clang nullability keywords) — validates F116's "_Nullable/_Nonnull" blast-radius claim. FOLDED-F116.

- 2026-06-29 **Liveness::Compute loop-back-edge (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:1060, cand #2)** — `int *_Borrow m=&_Mut x; for(...){ int *_Borrow m2=&_Mut x; useb(m2); } useb(m);` → REJECTED "cannot borrow `x` as mutable more than once at a time" (note pins m's first borrow). The backward liveness fixpoint correctly extends m's liveness ACROSS the loop (m used after), so the in-loop m2 conflicts. PROBED-SOUND. Cand #1 (DefUse-misses-use) folds to F119/F11 handler-bug class (default VisitStmt recurses); cand #3 (LifetimeEnds) exhaustively-audited. Do NOT re-probe basic loop liveness.

- 2026-06-29 **LoansInScope branch-sensitivity (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:1298, cand #2)** — loans in disjoint if/else branches (`if(c){&_Mut x...}else{&_Mut x...}`) then post-join fresh `&_Mut x` → ACCEPTED rc=0. The active-loan dataflow is branch-sensitive (branch loans not in scope after their branch) and ends loans at the join. PROBED-SOUND. Core borrow-checker dataflow (Liveness/LoansInScope/EnsureBorrowSource/Solve) now all read + sound; F119 remains the sole borrow defect.

- 2026-06-29 **freeze enforcement (FindLoansThatFreeze)** — mutate `x=10` while immutably borrowed (`const int *_Borrow b=&_Const x`, b used after) → REJECTED "cannot assign to `x` because it is borrowed"; field form `s.a=2` while `&_Const s` live → REJECTED "cannot assign to `s.a` because it is borrowed". Freeze semantics sound (immutable borrow freezes referent + fields). PROBED-SOUND.

- 2026-06-29 **nullability member-access deref (`_Borrow _Nullable` struct ptr)** — `q->f` on a `struct S *_Borrow _Nullable q` (no guard) → REJECTED "cannot access member through nullable pointer"; `if(q){q->f}` → ACCEPTED; chained `t->g + q->f` (both nullable) → REJECTED. - 2026-06-29 **init-analysis uninit-read FN batch** — partial array `a[0]=1; return a[1]` → REJECT (uninit `a`, §3.7.3 all-or-nothing); loop-only init `for(...)x=i; return x` (n may be 0) → REJECT (maybe-uninit); one-branch init `if(c)x=5; return x` → REJECT; both-branch init in a NESTED block `{ if(c)x=1; else x=2; } return x` → ACCEPT (correct, x init on all paths, nested block preserves tracking). ## 2026-06-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain I REOPENED @34883aa1: array-decay-to-_Borrow soundness re-walk
- shape: `const int[3]` -> `int *_Borrow _ArrayElem` (const DROP) + mutate `p[0]=99` → SHAPE-REJECTED ("conversion ... forbidden") — isBorrowArrayDecayTypeMatch :567/:576 reject const-drop. SOUND.
- shape: array-of-`struct{int*_Owned p}` -> `void *_Borrow` (owned-erase, void branch :570-576) → SHAPE-REJECTED (array-of-owned rejected at decl + void-decay rejected). SOUND.
- shape: `const char[4]` -> `char *_Borrow _ArrayElem` (const-char drop) + mutate → SHAPE-REJECTED ("incompatible _Borrow types"). SOUND.
- shape: `(const int*)[2]` -> `(int*)*_Borrow _ArrayElem` (INNER/deep const drop) → SHAPE-REJECTED. SOUND (hasSameType compares full element identity incl. deep const).
- shape: `(int*_Nullable)[2]` -> `(int*_Nonnull)*_Borrow _ArrayElem` (nullability launder) → REJECTED by CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment ("nonnull cannot be assigned by nullable"). SOUND (separate gate backstops).
- shape: `int[2][3]` -> `int(*_Borrow _ArrayElem)[4]` (WRONG inner dimension) → SHAPE-REJECTED; correct `[3]` accepted. SOUND (element/row type checked).
- shape: return `int*_Borrow _ArrayElem` decayed from a LOCAL array (dangling escape) → REJECTED by borrow-return rule ("return type not allowed to be _Borrow qualified" — no borrow param). SOUND (lifetime backstopped at fn-return level).
- shape: index a PLAIN (non-_ArrayElem) `int*_Borrow` with `p[2]` → REJECTED ("subscript of _Borrow pointer is not allowed"); arrayelem form accepted. SOUND (_ArrayElem gates indexing correctly).
- shape: ADD volatile via decay `int[3]` -> `volatile int*_Borrow _ArrayElem` → REJECTED ("incompatible _Borrow types") WHILE ADD const `int[3]` -> `const int*_Borrow _ArrayElem` ACCEPTED. ASYMMETRY found. Non-decay form (`volatile int*_Borrow ← int*_Borrow`) ALSO rejected ("conversion ... forbidden") while const-add accepted → root = IsSafePointerConversion (SemaBSCSafeZone.cpp:838-848) has a const arm (IsDroppingConst/removeLocalConst) but NO volatile/restrict arm → add-volatile/add-restrict widening over-rejected. → **FOLDED-into-F102/G15 family** (same function IsSafePointerConversion, same C1 "add-qualifier widening wrongly rejected" defect class; F102=_Owned add-const, G15=_Borrow void-erase add-const, F27=explicit-cast const conflation). Over-rejection (FP), NOT a soundness FN. isBorrowArrayDecayTypeMatch :560-565 treats const/volatile/restrict symmetrically (would accept add-volatile) but IsSafePointerConversion's narrow const-only relaxation is the gate that rejects. Same fix surface as F102/G15 (generalize const-variance to all C qualifiers). NOT a distinct root cause.
- CONCLUSION: Chain I array-decay soundness re-confirmed SOUND @34883aa1 (no FN/UB; const/volatile-DROP, nullability-launder, dimension-mismatch, owned-erase, lifetime-escape, plain-vs-arrayelem all correctly rejected). The :560-565 const/vol/restrict strip added since 28656aa9 is add-only/sound. Only anomaly = the add-volatile FP which FOLDS into the F102/G15 IsSafePointerConversion-add-qualifier-FP family.

## 2026-06-29 bsc-explorer cycle — Chain B REOPENED @34883aa1: borrow-checker comma + _Borrow _ArrayElem re-walk
Re-walked the borrow-checker changes that landed since 28656aa9 saturation (the chain reopened because BSCBorrowChecker.cpp changed). Commits in scope: 6daa7debe469 (VisitBinComma added, closes F11), 908ddef2d440 + 53bc93dd9672 (_Borrow _ArrayElem borrow-check fix + *p/p[] differentiation, to_string()->structurallyEquals path compare, UsesArraySubscriptNotation flag, new VisitIncrementDecrementOp _ArrayElem arm).
- shape: comma RHS is a borrow-creating ASSIGNMENT `int t=(m=&_Mut x, 0)` then write `x`; m used after → REJECTED ("cannot assign to `x` because it is borrowed") MATCHING the no-comma baseline → PROBED-SOUND (ActionExtract::VisitBinComma fresh-LHS-extract preserves the self-contained assignment's Dest/loan).
- shape: two live _Mut _Borrow _ArrayElem reborrows of same array, written via DIFFERENT notations (*p1 vs p2[0]) → REJECTED ("cannot borrow `a[]` as mutable more than once") MATCHING plain baseline → PROBED-SOUND (cross-notation conflict caught).
- shape: _ArrayElem arithmetic reborrow `outer = p1 + 2` escaping an inner-scope array → REJECTED ("`inner[]` does not live long enough") MATCHING direct-borrow baseline → PROBED-SOUND (arithmetic reborrow carries lifetime).
- shape: shared _Const reborrow of `*p` (deref notation) frozen, then write `p[0]` (subscript notation) while q live → REJECTED ("cannot assign to `p[]` because it is borrowed") MATCHING same-notation baseline → PROBED-SOUND. This is the EXACT case the to_string()->structurallyEquals switch was meant to unify; structurallyEquals ignores UsesArraySubscriptNotation (display-only) so `*p` and `p[]` compare equal → the loan-kill/freeze sees them as the same region. The 53bc93dd change is a soundness IMPROVEMENT (old to_string rendered them as distinct strings "*p" vs "p[]").
- shape: owned value moved inside comma LHS `int t=(consume(o), 0)` then double-consume → REJECTED ("use of moved value: `o`") → PROBED-SOUND (ownership BO_Comma handling intact; the borrow-side comma fix doesn't disturb it).
- shape: `p++` on _Borrow _ArrayElem (now a Use, not Init per the new VisitIncrementDecrementOp arm) while a shared borrow q of *p is live, then write *p → REJECTED with an EXTRA diag ("cannot use `p` because it is borrowed" at the ++) plus the write conflict → PROBED-SOUND (increment-as-Use correctly conflicts; index-insensitive region model retains q's freeze across the advance; STRICTER than baseline, not weaker).
- CONCLUSION: no-new-pattern. The borrow-checker changes that reopened Chain B (comma tracking + _Borrow _ArrayElem fix + structurallyEquals path-compare + increment-as-Use arm) are all SOUND at 34883aa1. The comma fix is robust across assignment-in-comma / call-arg / move-in-comma / two-borrow-conflict positions; the _ArrayElem fix correctly unifies *p and p[] notations and carries lifetime through arithmetic reborrows. structurallyEquals is a soundness improvement over to_string. Recommend re-SATURATE Chain B borrow-side @34883aa1; remaining untraced chain-B hop = the Prologue/Epilogue DUMP instrumentation (1399df7e/8a842c0d) which is diagnostic-only (no analysis change). The SemaDeclBSC Prologue TransformBinaryOperator (970 hoist / 1464 restore) preserves comma structure to the borrow checker (regression tests pass).

- 2026-06-29 **struct-copy moved-field propagation (F61 dual, soundness FN hypothesis)** — `struct S{int*_Owned v;}; s.v=mk(); consume(s.v); struct S t=s; consume(t.v);` → REJECTED "use of partially moved value: `s`, s.v is moved" (rc=1) at the COPY site. Copying a partially-moved struct is caught before any double-free. Moved-state IS propagated/blocked across struct-copy. F61 (null-state non-propagation, FP) is the sole struct-copy gap; the moved-state FN direction is SOUND. PROBED-SOUND.

- shape: consume `_Owned` through a ternary inside the free cast `safe_free((void*_Owned)(c ? p : q))`, then free p and q again → SOUND, BOTH arms marked moved (conservative), 2nd frees of p AND q both REJECTED "invalid cast to `void*_Owned` of moved...". `setAllAlwaysAdd` linearization + cast-dispatch subexpr-visit covers the conditional consume. The shared NullCheckInfo producer (post-F70 De Morgan `init` recursion) classifies null/present soundly for the OWNERSHIP consumer (no wrong-branch setToNull → no leak FN, no double-free FN). Conditional consume through the `(void*_Owned)` free cast marks all ternary arms moved. No new root cause. - 2026-06-29 **owned-leak variant of F124 SHAPE-REJECTED** — hypothesized that hasOwnedFields array-blindness at VisitDeclStmt:2384 would let a local `struct W{struct O arr[1];}` (owned-field-struct array) leak. But the struct can't be DEFINED: the array-of-owned-field-struct FIELD is rejected at struct definition ("type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned'(even indirectly), struct O contains _Owned type") — the array-element OWNED gate (SemaType.cpp:5202, present) fires at the field declarator. So hasOwnedFields array-blindness is UNREACHABLE (the owned array gate protects all sites). Confirms F124 is BORROW-SPECIFIC: owned has the array gate at the declarator, borrow does not (F81/F124 root). isTrivialDataType (TypeBSC.cpp:432) is the array-AWARE predicate (recurses ArrayType :401-404) — the gate should use that logic; has{Owned,Borrow}Fields (444/477) are the array-blind ones, but only the borrow consumer (placement gate) is reachable & buggy.

- 2026-06-29 **struct-array-field element-init "FP" = FOLDED-F97 (retracted, INTENDED)** — `struct W{int arr[2];}; w.arr[0]=5; return w.arr[0];` rejected "use of uninitialized value: w.arr" — but the PLAIN LOCAL array `int arr[2]; arr[0]=5; return arr[0];` is ALSO rejected (same diag), so NOT struct-field-specific. It's the documented whole-array-init granularity limitation (BSC init analysis tracks arrays whole, not per-element; init-at-decl `w={{5,6}}` works rc=0). = F97 (retracted-intended, cf §3.7.3 rule 6 / G10 / F107 notes). - 2026-06-29 **array-element nullability narrowing — SHAPE-BLOCKED/INCONCLUSIVE** — BSCNullabilityCheck path-builder (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:150-180) handles MemberExpr/DeclRefExpr/ICE/Paren/UnaryOperator but NOT ArraySubscriptExpr, so `arr[i]` gets no narrowing path → array-element `!=nullptr` guards aren't recorded. - 2026-06-29 **_Nonnull return contract PROBED-SOUND** — `_Safe int*_Borrow _Nonnull g(int*_Borrow _Nullable p){ return p; }` REJECTED "cannot return nullable pointer type"; with a guard `if(p!=nullptr) return p;` the true-branch return is accepted and the null fall-through `return p;` is correctly REJECTED (narrowing applies only on the guarded branch, not the else/fall-through). No FN. Branch-sensitive return-nullability check is sound.

- 2026-06-29 **use-after-move across loop back-edge PROBED-SOUND** — `int*_Owned p=mk(); for(...){ consume(p); }` REJECTED "use of moved value: p" (2nd-iteration back-edge use-after-move caught) + "memory leak of value: p" (zero-iteration path). while-loop form same. The ownership dataflow correctly merges the moved state from the back-edge at the loop header → no double-free FN. C5 merge robust for moves across loops.

- 2026-06-29 **conditional move via ternary operand `consume(c?a:b)` PROBED-SOUND** — scope-exit reports BOTH "memory leak of a" and "memory leak of b" (each leaks on the path where NOT selected — sound per-path union, program genuinely leaks the unselected). Adding consume(a);consume(b) reports "use of moved value" for both (each moved on its selected path → double-free caught). The ownership dataflow correctly models the ternary as a path-dependent move of one operand. No FN. C5/ternary-move robust.

- 2026-06-29 **inter-procedural borrow return PROBED-SOUND (shape-confirmed)** — a _Borrow-returning function MUST have a _Borrow parameter ("no _Borrow qualified type found in the function parameters, the return type is not allowed to be _Borrow qualified"). So returning a borrow derived from a LOCAL is structurally prevented at the type/decl level (no param to tie the lifetime to). Both via id() and direct rejected. Return-of-local-borrow is sound-by-construction.

- 2026-06-29 **recursive/heap owned-field struct via safe_malloc — SHAPE-BLOCKED (trivial-type constraint)** — `struct Node{int v; struct Node*_Owned next;}; safe_malloc<struct Node>(1)` REJECTED "no matching function for call to safe_malloc" — safe_malloc<T> requires T to be a trivial-data type (isTrivialDataType, the array-AWARE predicate from TypeBSC.cpp:432), and a struct with an owned field is non-trivial. So owned-field structs can't be heap-allocated via safe_malloc (stack-only via this path); recursive heap owned-lists need a different allocation path. libcbs API constraint, not a finding. (Confirms isTrivialDataType correctly excludes owned-containing types — consistent with its array-aware recursion.)

- 2026-06-30 **F40 RETRACTED (NOT A BUG, user-caught) — validate-invariant lesson** — F40's repro `if(c && consume(p)){return 1;} safe_free(p);` is NOT a false positive: on the path c=1 ∧ consume(p)==0, the `&&` is false → falls through to safe_free(p) with p already freed → DOUBLE-FREE. The analyzer can't assume consume returns nonzero, so "use of moved value: p" is SOUND. The repro's "equivalent explicit form compiles clean" was a FALSE equivalence (explicit form discards consume's return + unconditionally returns, so safe_free only reached when c=0/p-owned). I re-opened F40 on 2026-06-29 purely on "rc=1=FP-fires" without validating the invariant. RETRACTED: bug_log RETRACTED header, verdict→retracted, removed from .filed_umbrella, comment 50301792 DELETED from IJP3AP. CONTRAST F19/F43/F61 (re-validated this session): GENUINE FPs — plain-VARIABLE twin with identical semantics ACCEPTED while struct-FIELD form REJECTED (asymmetry proves field-tracking incompleteness, not sound rejection). The field-vs-plainvar twin test is the discriminator: a sound rejection (F40) has NO accepting twin; a field-FP (F19/F43/F61) does.

- 2026-06-30 **F44 RETRACTED (sound, not FP) — found by applying the F40 twin-test as an audit** — F44 claimed `&_Mut s` spuriously collapses a null `_Owned _Nullable` field to Owned → leak FP. Audit: (1) mtaker body `s->b=mk()` compiles rc=0 — a `&_Mut` borrow CAN assign an owned value into the null field; (2) mtaker-assigns + caller-no-free = real leak (rc=1 "s.b is leak"); (3) caller `if(s.b)safe_free(s.b)` after the borrow → accepted rc=0 (diagnostic soundly demanded the free); (4) `&_Const` accepted because const can't assign (correct semantic difference). So `mtaker(&_Mut s)` with no free is NOT provably safe — it leaks iff the opaque taker assigns the field. checkSUse isAddrMut conservatism is SOUND/by-design (cf. F112). No accepting identical-semantics twin. Retracted (bug_log header, verdict, umbrella snapshot, Gitee comment 50016584 deleted). 2nd F40-style retraction; audit of remaining filed FPs ongoing.

- 2026-06-30 **Chain AD (IsCastFromVoidPointer 4-site) TRACED — OOS-ADJACENT, deprioritized** — IsCastFromVoidPointer (BSCOwnership.cpp:200) recognizes `(T*_Owned)(void*_Owned)` = recovering a TYPED owned from a VOID owned (the __take_from_raw round-trip), consumed at VisitBinaryOperator:2203/VisitCallExpr:2252/VisitDeclStmt:2379/HandleInitListExpr:2465 (all mark the recovered value's state). This void->typed recovery is explicitly OUT of scope (raw laundering, like __take_from_raw/__move_to_raw). The in-scope `(void*_Owned)p` forward cast (safe_free) is the OPPOSITE direction this predicate does not gate. Probing AD needs OOS constructs → deprioritized. Valid Mode-2 outcome (chain triaged as OOS).

## 2026-06-30 bsc-explorer cycle — BSCIRBuilder scope-cleanup / drop-emission control flow (prescanLabels, VisitArraySubscriptExpr, VisitVAArgExpr)
- shape: forward `goto L` out of a plain block with an unfreed owned local → CORRECTLY diagnosed "memory leak of value: p" at the goto. SOUND (not a gap).
- shape: forward `goto L` into a **switch-case label** (prescanLabels records label depth as switch(+1)+bodyCompound(+1) = off-by-one too deep vs runtime ScopeStack which lowerSwitchStmt pushes as ONE scope) with owned local in inner block, unfreed on goto path → CORRECTLY diagnosed leak at goto → SOUND. The prescan depth mismatch is real arithmetic but NOT observable: the ownership analysis catches an un-dropped owned local as a leak regardless of how many redundant no-op Drops the cleanup emits.
- shape: same switch-goto but freeing p on BOTH paths (eat(p) before goto + eat(p) on fall-through) → rc=0, valgrind 1 alloc/1 free, 0 errors → no double-free. SOUND.
- shape: owned local in a switch CASE region (`case 1:{int*_Owned p=mk(); goto L;}`) forward goto to a later-case label → CORRECTLY diagnosed leak (p leaves case-1 braces unfreed). SOUND. → **prescanLabels switch-label off-by-one PROBED-SOUND (no FP/FN).**
- shape: `VisitArraySubscriptExpr` (BSCIRBuilder.cpp:1141) ALWAYS returns createCopy, never calls shouldMove (peer asymmetry vs VisitMemberExpr:1134 / VisitDeclRefExpr:732). Reachability: for `arr[i]` to be move-semantic the array elements must be owned. `int *_Owned arr[N]` → "type of array cannot be qualified by '_Owned'" SHAPE-REJECTED; array-of-struct-with-owned also rejected; `int*_Owned _ArrayElem arr` → `arr[i]` is a plain `int` (pointee), not a move. → **PROBED-SHAPE-REJECTED / latent-unreachable** (same disposition as F39's TransferFunctions::VisitArraySubscriptExpr arm — benign because no in-scope arr[i] is move-semantic).
- shape: `VisitVAArgExpr` (BSCIRBuilder.cpp:1146) lowers `va_arg(ap,T)` to a const-init temp (no move tracking). Reachability: `va_arg`/`va_end` are "_Unsafe function call forbidden in the safe zone" → owned-va_arg UNREACHABLE from _Safe → **PROBED-SHAPE-REJECTED** (only runs in _Unsafe where ownership is the programmer's responsibility).
- OUTCOME: no new in-scope root cause. BSCIRBuilder dispatch-coverage was already SATURATED (F120/F111/F52); the drop-emission/scope-cleanup control flow (prescanLabels depth bookkeeping) is SOUND despite a real switch-label off-by-one (the ownership analysis is the backstop). VisitArraySubscriptExpr/VisitVAArgExpr move-tracking gaps are latent-unreachable for in-scope types.

- 2026-06-30 **F122 RE-VALIDATED genuine FP (twin test, validate-invariant per F40 lesson) + root pinned** — `!(p&&q)` guard then `need(p)` → rc=1 "cannot pass nullable pointer argument"; De Morgan twin `!p||!q` (identical semantics) → rc=0 ACCEPTED; positive `if(p&&q){need}` → rc=0. Accepting twin exists → genuine FP, filing STANDS. Root pinned via NullCheckInfo::init (BSCNullCheckInfo.cpp:198): :221 `(BO_LAnd)!=Negate` SHOULD convert `!(p&&q)` to the same ||-of-negations as `!p||!q`, but the negated-AND path diverges (false-branch present-set not populated equivalently). Validated my own this-session finding per the F40-retraction lesson — holds. (Contrast F40/F44 retracts: no accepting twin.)

- 2026-06-30 **explorer-recommendation follow-up: partially-moved-struct leak = FOLDED-F75 (FP face of union-not-meet)** — `struct S{int*_Owned a,*_Owned b;}; if(c)consume(s.a); consume(s.b); if(!c)consume(s.a);` → rc=1 "field memory leak: s.a" despite s.a being moved(c=1)/freed(c=0) on every path. Root: on c=1 the first if moves s.a, but OwnershipImpl::merge UNIONs s.a back to owned (from c=0 branch) → scope-end sees it owned → leak FP. Same union-not-meet root as F75 (which causes the double-free FN); this is the FP face. One MEET fix closes both. NOT a new finding (folds F75). bsc-explorer (BSCIRBuilder) found no-new-bug but its rec (Drop-on-partially-moved / merge-via-goto) pointed here → F75 FP face. Methods this turn: Mode-1 (VisitIncrementDecrementOp, NullCheckInfo::init→F122 re-validated), Mode-2 (AA/AE/AF chains), explorer (ran, no-new-bug, rec folded F75).

- 2026-06-30 **owned-in-condition non-consumption PROBED-SOUND + F101 sharpened** — `if(p){} consume(p)` / `if(!p){} consume(p)` → rc=0 accepted: VisitCallExpr/condition treats owned→bool as bool-typed (GetAddr, no Move) so the owned survives. Sharpens F101 (owned→_Bool FP): the CONDITION form `if(p)` is ACCEPTED; only `_Bool b = p` (bool VARIABLE init) is rejected by the safe-zone conversion gate — confirming F101 is the narrow bool-var-init FP (spec rule 13 allows `_Bool flag = p`), the move-on-call handles owned→bool correctly. - 2026-06-30 **do-while loop-body owned alloc+free PROBED-SOUND** — `do { int*_Owned p=mk(); consume(p); } while(i<n);` → rc=0, runtime 3 allocs/3 frees/0 errors (no cross-iteration leak); unfreed loop-body owned → rc=1 "memory leak of value: p" (caught). Loop-body ownership sound across iterations (each iter's owned tracked independently; scope-end-per-iteration leak check fires). Loop ownership complete: while/for/do-while/switch all sound for owned alloc+free + use-after-move; F75(field-set merge) the filed exception.

- 2026-06-30 **struct-by-value owned-field move PROBED-SOUND** — `struct Box{int*_Owned p;}; void take(struct Box b); take(b); consume(b.p);` → rc=1 "use of uninitialized value: b.p" (passing the owned-field struct by value moves the WHOLE struct, b.p unusable after, caught); `take(b)` once → runtime 1 alloc/1 free/0 errors clean. Struct-by-value move sound (whole-struct move on by-value pass; no double-free FN). Ownership move paths complete: direct/field/nested-field/struct-by-value/nested-call/swap all sound; F75(merge)/F123(deref-write)/F79(move-out-of-borrow) filed exceptions.

- 2026-06-30 **variable path-correlation move FP = FOLDED-F75 (FP face, variable-side)** — `int*_Owned p=mk(); if(c){consume(p);} if(!c){consume(p);}` → rc=1 "use of moved value: p" (analyzer doesn't correlate if(c)/if(!c)); ACCEPTING TWIN `if(c){consume(p);}else{consume(p);}` → rc=0 accepted (genuine FP, identical semantics, if/else workaround). Same OwnershipImpl::merge condition-correlation gap as F75's FP face (field-set), variable SStatus side. SOUND when post-merge use is UNCONDITIONAL (switch-then-consume caught = real double-free); FP only for condition-negation-correlated guards. Folds into F75 (broadened); not filed separately. Discriminator (if/else twin accepted) confirms genuine FP, not by-design — but one MEET/correlation fix in OwnershipImpl::merge closes the whole F75 family.

- 2026-06-30 **PATH-INSENSITIVITY (condition-correlation) FP class — nullability + ownership, LIKELY BY-DESIGN (flagged, not filed)** — TWO manifestations of the same fundamental limitation: the analyzers do NOT correlate separate guards on the same/negated condition. (a) NULLABILITY: `if(c){ if(p==nullptr)return; } if(c){ need(p); }` → rc=1 "cannot pass nullable pointer argument" (the c-true narrowing is lost at the merge after the 1st if(c), not re-derived in the 2nd) — accepting twin `if(c){ if(p==nullptr)return; need(p); }` (merged) → rc=0. (b) OWNERSHIP: `if(c)consume(p); if(!c)consume(p);` → rc=1 "use of moved value: p" — accepting twin `if(c){consume(p);}else{consume(p);}` → rc=0. CLASS = path-insensitivity (the analyzer merges branch state and cannot prove the guard correlation). DISTINCT from F75's union-vs-meet FIELD FP face (which a MEET fix DOES address — the union restoring a moved FIELD); the path-correlation cases need actual path-sensitivity (condition tracking), a major analysis change, so they are a KNOWN BY-DESIGN LIMITATION (cf. F112 granularity / F40 sound-conservatism retractions) — NOT filed. The if/else and merged-block forms are trivial workarounds. CAUTION on my earlier "variable-side folds F75" note: the variable case is path-insensitivity, only loosely F75 (shares OwnershipImpl::merge but not the union-vs-meet fix). Flagged for user; treat as by-design unless user says otherwise.

- 2026-06-30 **path-insensitivity FP class CONFIRMED SYSTEMIC (3rd manifestation: init) → definitively BY-DESIGN, not filed** — init analysis also: `int*_Owned p; if(c){p=mk();} if(c){consume(p);}` → rc=1 "use of possibly uninitialized value: p" (c-true-only init lost at merge, not re-derived in 2nd if(c)). So condition-correlation FP spans ALL THREE flow-sensitive analyses: ownership (use-of-moved), nullability (nullable-arg), init (possibly-uninit). This is a UNIFORM by-design path-insensitivity property (each analysis is flow-sensitive but path-INsensitive: merges branch state, cannot prove same-condition/negated-condition guard correlation). Fixing requires path-sensitivity across all 3 = fundamental redesign → NOT a fileable bug (cf. F112 granularity by-design). Trivial workarounds: merge the guarded blocks, or use if/else. CONCLUSION: do NOT file the condition-correlation FPs (ownership/nullability/init); they are the analyzers' uniform path-insensitivity. The F75 FIELD FP face remains a SEPARATE, fileable union-vs-meet issue (MEET-fixable, independent of path-correlation). Investigation closed.

- 2026-06-30 **complex owned flow (by-value-return + struct-copy move) PROBED-SOUND (runtime-differential)** — `struct Box mkbox(){ struct Box b; b.p=mk(); return b; } struct Box b1=mkbox(); struct Box b2=b1; consume(b2.p);` → rc=0, valgrind 1 alloc/1 free/0 errors (by-value return + struct-copy move + free all tracked); `consume(b1.p)` after `b2=b1` → rc=1 "use of moved value: b1.p" (copy moves b1, caught). No silent leak/UAF/double-free. Codegen drop + move-tracking sound for by-value-return×struct-copy (consistent with codegen-drop-coverage). Runtime-differential oracle: no silent issue.

- 2026-06-30 **recursive-function ownership PROBED-SOUND** — `_Safe void rec(int*_Owned p, int n){ if(n==0){consume(p);return;} rec(p,n-1); }` → rc=0, runtime 1 alloc/1 free/0 errors (p moved through recursion, consumed at base); base-case-no-consume → rc=1 "memory leak of value: p" (caught). Recursive move-on-call sound (each recursive call moves p; leak detected on the no-consume path). Ownership exhaustively covered across all call forms (direct/nested/recursive/by-value-struct) + all CFG shapes + all field-depths. Surface complete @34883aa1; F75(merge)/F123(deref-write)/F79(move-out-of-borrow) the filed FN exceptions; path-insensitivity FPs by-design.

- 2026-06-30 **block-scope ownership PROBED-SOUND** — `int*_Owned p=mk(); { consume(p); } consume(p);` → rc=1 "use of moved value: p" (nested-block move propagates out); `{ int*_Owned p=mk(); (void)p; }` → rc=1 "memory leak of value: p" (block-scope leak caught at block-end). Block/compound-scope ownership sound (move propagation + per-scope leak check). Ownership construct coverage truly exhaustive: every call form, CFG shape, field depth, scope nesting verified sound; filed FN exceptions F75/F79/F123; path-insensitivity FPs by-design.

- 2026-06-30 **block-scope dangling borrow (CheckStorageDead) PROBED-SOUND** — `{int x=5; b=&_Mut x;} *b=7;` → rc=1 "`x` does not live long enough" (b outlives x's block storage, caught). CheckStorageDead (BSCBorrowChecker.cpp:1503) + FindLoansThatFreeze + region solver catch storage-end dangling. Borrow-checker storage/lifetime fully covered (region solver + storage-dead + conflict + NLL + reborrow); F119 the filed exception.

- 2026-06-30 **storage-dead prefix-freeze (field-borrow × whole-struct death) PROBED-SOUND** — `{struct S s; s.f=5; b=&_Mut s.f;} *b=7;` → rc=1 "`s.f` does not live long enough" (FindLoansThatFreeze prefix-freeze catches that a borrow of s.f freezes s's storage). CheckStorageDead handles field-borrow + whole-aggregate storage death. Borrow-checker storage/lifetime exhaustively sound (block-scope dangling, field-borrow prefix-freeze, reborrow, region-solver transitivity, struct-field-borrow, loop-local, return-borrow); F119(cast-reborrow) the sole filed exception.

- 2026-06-30 **field-granular write-conflict freeze PROBED-SOUND (clarifies F112/F109 scope)** — `int*_Borrow ba=&_Mut s.a; s.b=5; *ba=3;` (write DISJOINT field s.b while s.a borrowed) → rc=0 ACCEPTED; `s.a=5` (SAME field) while borrowed → rc=1 "cannot assign to `s.a` because it is borrowed". So FrozenByBorrowOf/CheckWrite is FIELD-GRANULAR and SOUND (disjoint-field borrows don't conflict, Rust-like; same-field write conflicts). IMPORTANT CLARIFICATION: F112/F109's "whole-variable granularity by design" is specifically the LOAN-REGION LIVENESS (when a reassignment kills a loan), NOT the write-conflict freeze (which IS field-precise). So the borrow checker tracks WHICH field is borrowed precisely (freeze) but ends the loan whole-variable (liveness). Borrow-checker granularity fully characterized.

- 2026-06-30 **nullability field-granular narrowing PROBED-SOUND — granularity model complete** — `if(s.a!=nullptr){ need(s.a); need(s.b); }` → need(s.a) accepted, need(s.b) → rc=1 "cannot pass nullable pointer argument" (narrowing s.a does NOT narrow sibling s.b). Field-granular nullability narrowing, sound. GRANULARITY MODEL FULLY CHARACTERIZED: ownership = field-granular (per-field move/leak; F75 merge the union exception); borrow freeze/write-conflict = field-granular (disjoint fields don't conflict); borrow loan-LIVENESS = whole-variable (F112/F109 by-design, the ONLY non-field-precise aspect); nullability narrowing = field-granular. So F109 (array-element loan-liveness) IS the F112 whole-aggregate-liveness class — strengthens the retract recommendation. All field-precise except loan-liveness.

- 2026-06-30 **ownership field-granularity CONFIRMED (disjoint field moves) — granularity model complete+confirmed** — `struct S{int*_Owned a,*_Owned b;}; consume(s.a); consume(s.b);` → rc=0, 2 allocs/2 frees (disjoint fields independently moved/freed); move s.a only → rc=1 "field memory leak: s, s.b is leak" (ONLY s.b flagged, s.a correctly not — field-precise leak pinpointing). GRANULARITY MODEL COMPLETE & CONFIRMED: ownership field-granular (this), borrow-freeze field-granular, nullability-narrowing field-granular, borrow-loan-LIVENESS whole-variable (F112/F109 = the sole by-design non-precise aspect). The campaign's granularity understanding is now fully mapped + empirically confirmed.

## 2026-07-01 owned use-after-move in if/while CONDITION → FOLDED-F111
- Probe: `consume(p); if(p){}` and `while(p){break;}` after a move (BSCOwnership runOnBlock visit-filter).
- Result: rc=0 (use-after-move NOT diagnosed). Baselines: double-consume rc=1 (moved-tracking works), `if(p)` no-consume rc=1 (leak caught → analysis runs), bare `p;` rc=0 (F111). 
- Classify: **FOLDED-F111** — condition read is the block's last CFGStmt with an excluded top-level kind (ImplicitCastExpr owned→bool), same runOnBlock allowlist (BSCOwnership.cpp:2697-2707) as F111. Same fix (route all CFGStmts through TF.Visit). Not filed (F111 live+filed; broadens F111 from bare-statements to branch/loop conditions, same root+fix).

## 2026-07-01 nullability condition-narrowing comma misattribution (getVarDeclFromExpr LHS-recursion) → SHAPE-REJECTED
- Hypothesis: getVarDeclFromExpr (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:170-184) recurses BinaryOperator→getLHS() unconditionally; feeds SetCFGBlocksByExpr null-narrowing (:754). A comma condition `if((x,p))` (value=RHS p) would return LHS x → wrongly narrow x→NonNull → suppress `*x` nullable-deref (FN).
- Probe (plain non-_Safe zone, `-nullability-check=all`, deref allowed): `if((x,p)){*x}` → rc=1 (`*x` STILL flagged "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced"); `if((x,p)){*p}` → p narrowed. Baselines: raw `*x` rc=1, `if(p){*x}` rc=1, `if(p){*p}` narrows.
- Classify: **SHAPE-REJECTED** — the CFG FLATTENS the comma (x evaluated as a separate element; the branch terminator condition is the comma's VALUE p), so getVarDeclFromExpr never receives the comma as PtrE. The LHS-recursion is reached only for ASSIGNMENT conditions (`if((x=p))`), where LHS x is the semantically-correct narrow target (x holds p's value). No misattribution; no bug. NOTE: raw `*p` deref is forbidden in `_Safe` entirely (`'*' operator forbidden in safe zone`) — nullability-deref surface lives in non-safe zones under `-nullability-check=all`.

The ternary's composite type merges to _Nullable (weaker qualifier wins) → assignment-to-_Nonnull correctly flagged. Baselines: null→_Nonnull rc=1, nullable→_Nonnull rc=1. SOUND.

Explicit null element `{a, (int*_Nullable)0}`: rc=1. Full `{a,a,a}`: rc=0 pass.
- Verdict: the _Nonnull aggregate-init surface (struct-field ×3 forms, array partial/explicit/full, ternary merge) is UNIFORMLY SOUND. No init-path FN. Documents the surface; saves re-probing.

FOLDED into F125 (broadens blast radius: any nested nullability position below the outermost pointer slips).

## 2026-07-01 fnptr-param/return NULLABILITY variance unchecked at assignment → CONFIRMED-F126 (HIGH, FILED)
- Probe: `void (*fp)(int *_Nullable) = gnn;` (gnn=`void(int*_Nonnull)`) → rc=0 (FN); plain-code call `fp(null)` → runtime SIGSEGV (Invalid read of size 4). fnptr-RETURN variant `int*_Nonnull(*fp)(void)=grn(->_Nullable)` also rc=0.
- Differential: direct `a(_Nonnull)=b(_Nullable)` rc=1 (checked); owned fnptr-param `void(*)(int*)=go(int*_Owned)` rc=1 (rejected); owned fnptr-return rc=1. Only nullability fnptr variance slips.
- Root: CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment (SemaDeclBSC.cpp:170-203) recurses only into POINTER pointees (:197 isPointerType); a function-type pointee is not a pointer → fnptr param/return nullability never checked. Fix: recurse into FunctionProtoType params (contravariant) + return (covariant). FILED F126 → IJP3AP. Distinct from F125 (redecl fn), F66 (same fn, different arm :183 fixed), F76 (owned double-nested).

## 2026-07-01 F126 blast radius: arg-passing + return-stmt fnptr nullability variance → FOLDED-F126
- `reg(gnn)` (reg takes `void(*)(int*_Nullable)`, gnn=`void(int*_Nonnull)`) rc=0 (FN); `return gnn` from `void(*)(int*_Nullable)`-returning fn rc=0 (FN). Direct arg `takes(_Nonnull)(nullable)` rc=1 (checked); owned arg control rc=1 (rejected). Same CheckNullabilityQualTypeAssignment :197 non-recursion root — assignment+arg+return all fold into F126 (one fix closes all three positions).

## 2026-07-01 _Nonnull return-contract enforcement → PROBED-SOUND
- `int *_Nonnull f(void){ return (int*_Nullable)0; }` rc=1 "cannot return nullable pointer type"; `return n` (nullable var) rc=1; `return c?a:b` (may-be-null ternary) rc=1. A function honoring its OWN _Nonnull return contract is soundly enforced (direct return position). Contrast F126: direct return-contract checked, but fnptr-return-nullability VARIANCE (assigning a _Nullable-returning fn to a _Nonnull-returning fnptr) is NOT — the gap is specifically in fnptr-pointee variance recursion.

## 2026-07-01 ensure_init struct out-param partial-init (flat + nested) → PROBED-SOUND
- Callee-side ensure_init check (checkEnsureInitAtReturn, BSCIRInitAnalysis.cpp:1511) is field-granular: flat partial `out->a` (b uninit) rc=1; nested partial `out->a`+`out->in.x` (in.y uninit) rc=1 "'*out' not initialized at return"; full rc=0; whole-nested-write `out->in=i` rc=0. No partial-init laundering FN. (Note: plain `ensure_init` takes NO args; `ensure_init_if_ret(N)` takes the deref-level arg.)

## 2026-07-01 owned move-in-loop dataflow (C5 back-edge merge) → PROBED-SOUND
- `for/while { consume(p); }` (move p in loop body) → rc=1 "use of moved value: p" — back-edge merge marks p MaybeMoved at loop head, 2nd-iteration use-after-move caught. Both for+while. Control single-consume rc=0.
- FP direction: `for { consume(p); p = mk(); } consume(p);` (move-then-reinit in body) → rc=0 ACCEPTED — reinit restores ownership before back-edge, no false use-after-move. Loop dataflow merge sound both directions (no FN, no FP).

## 2026-07-01 owned-overwrite leak (reassign live owned) → PROBED-SOUND
- `int *_Owned p = mk(); p = mk();` (overwrite a still-live owned without consuming) → rc=1 (leak caught at the reassign). Correct form `consume(p); p = mk();` → rc=0 clean. The owned-assign leak check fires on overwrite-of-live-owned. SOUND.

## 2026-07-01 nullability narrow not invalidated on address-escape → CONFIRMED-F127 (HIGH, FILED)
- `if(p){ mutate(&p); return *p; }` (mutate: `void mutate(int*_Nullable *_Nonnull pp){ *pp=(int*_Nullable)0; }`) → compile rc=0 (FN), runtime SIGSEGV (Invalid read of size 4).
- Asymmetry: direct `if(p){ p=q; *p }` rc=1 (invalidated); escape `if(p){ mutate(&p); *p }` rc=0 (stale narrow). Conservative `keep(&p)` (no null) also rc=0 → zero address-escape invalidation.
- Root: VisitCallExpr (BSCNullabilityCheck.cpp:659) checks args vs _Nonnull params only; never invalidates CurrStatusVD/CurrStatusDPVD for a var whose address escapes. Fix: invalidate var narrow on `&var` passed to writable-ptr param. FILED F127 → IJP3AP. Distinct from F18/F48/F50/F92/F66. Defect class C7.

## 2026-07-01 F127 blast radius: local-alias write narrow staleness → FOLDED-F127
- `int *_Nullable *_Nonnull pp = &p; *pp = (int*_Nullable)0; return *p;` (inside `if(p)`) → rc=0 (FN). Same no-address-escape-invalidation root as F127; taking `&p` in an ASSIGNMENT (not just a call arg) also leaves p's narrow stale. Broadens F127's fix to the ADDRESS-OF site (covers call-arg + local-alias). FOLDED-F127.

## 2026-07-01 chain AI: loop-back-edge narrow propagation → PROBED-SOUND (closes chain AI audit)
- narrow-before-loop `if(p){ while(i<n){ *p; p=q; i++; } }` → rc=1 "nullable pointer cannot be dereferenced" at the `*p` line — the dataflow fixpoint propagates the reassign-to-nullable back to iteration-2's loop-top use. SOUND. Control (no reassign) rc=0.
- Chain AI RESULT: MODELED modifications (direct reassign, loop back-edge merge) correctly invalidate/propagate the narrow = SOUND; only UNMODELED address-escape (`&p` — call-arg F127, local-alias FOLDED-F127, struct-field = same root) slips. Struct-field escape (`s.pp=&p; *s.pp=null; *p`) folds into F127 (identical address-escape mechanism as the confirmed local-alias case). Chain AI audit complete: one root (F127), address-of site fix.

## 2026-07-01 init-analysis address-escape via ensure_init contract → PROBED-SOUND (F127 contrast)
- `noinit(&_Mut x)` (no ensure_init) then `return x` → rc=1 "use of uninitialized value: x" — x correctly kept UNINIT (no contract = no init promise; no laundering). With `__attribute__((ensure_init))` callee → rc=0 (x accepted, contract honored). The INIT analysis MODELS address-escape via the ensure_init contract = SOUND.
- **F127 contrast (strengthens "not by-design"):** the init-analysis models `&x` escape (ensure_init) AND the owned analyzer models it (GetAddr op) — only the NULLABILITY flow-narrow ignores address-escape (F127). BSC's framework demonstrably handles escape in 2 of 3 analyzers; nullability's omission is a gap, not a design choice. Third asymmetry (after direct-reassign-caught + owned-tracks-escape).

## 2026-07-01 owned move-merge across switch (C5) → PROBED-SOUND
- consume(p) in case0 only + consume(p) after switch → rc=1 "use of moved value: p" (maybe-moved join caught); consume in all paths + after → rc=1 (fully-moved); consume case0 only, no consume after → rc=1 "memory leak of value: p" (leak on untaken default path). Switch dataflow merge sound in all 3 directions. Complements loop back-edge (sound) + ternary (sound) — ownership dataflow merges robust across all control-flow joins.

## 2026-07-01 F127 broader root: narrowed GLOBAL not invalidated across a call → FOLDED-F127 (broadens root)
- `int *_Nullable g; if(g){ sideeffect(); return *g; }` where `void sideeffect(void){ g=0; }` → rc=0 (FN), runtime SIGSEGV. NO address passed — proves VisitCallExpr (:659) invalidates NO call-clobberable narrow. F127's "&var-arg" fix is INCOMPLETE (globals need no arg). Complete fix: across every call invalidate narrowing of all globals/statics + address-escaped locals. Repro F127b. FOLDED-F127 (same root site, broadens severity/fix).

## 2026-07-06 mixed-nesting De Morgan narrowing `!(p || !q)` → PROBED-SOUND (closes BSCNullCheckInfo De Morgan candidates)
- `if(!(p || !q)){ need(q); }` (then ≡ !p && q) → rc=0 ACCEPTED (q narrowed nonnull, no FP); `need(p)` → rc=1 "cannot pass nullable pointer argument" (no FN). Identical behavior to explicit twin `if(!p && q)`. probes/negated_or_mixed_demorgan_narrow.cbs.
- Asymmetry vs F122 explained: `||` under Negate routes to operator&= UNION (preserves per-operand checked-sets) → nested negation-through-OR narrows correctly; only `&&` under Negate routes to operator|= INTERSECT which collapses {p}∩{q}={} (= filed F122 FP). De Morgan composition surface now fully probed: sound except the F122 arm.

## 2026-07-06 owned-from-va_arg tracking → PROBED-SOUND (closes BSCIRBuilder VAArg-owned candidate)
- Unsafe variadic fn (variadic forbidden in _Safe): `int *_Owned p = va_arg(ap, int*_Owned);` then drop → rc=1 "memory leak of value: p" (same as mk() control); consume(p) → clean; consume(p);consume(p) → rc=1 "use of moved value: p". probes/vaarg_owned_leak_untracked.cbs + scratchpad consume variant.
- Note premise was doubly wrong: VisitVAArgExpr EXISTS (BSCIRBuilder.cpp:1146, opaque-constant temp) and ownership tracking keys off the DeclStmt's owned-typed init, not the initializer expr kind — no VAArgExpr arm needed in BSCOwnership. Also confirms leak/move checks run in UNSAFE functions.

## 2026-07-06 classifyEnsureInit NumParams-divergence (variadic extra args) → SHAPE-REJECTED at source + partial probe
- Source-confirmed: classifyEnsureInit (DeclBSC.cpp:26/31/38/41) bounds-guards ALL four paths with `I < getNumParams()` → Sema's NumCallArgs loop (SemaExpr.cpp:7784) classifies vararg indices None; no OOB, no divergence vs init-analysis' NumParams iteration. DeclBSC.md candidate 1 closed. Candidate 2 (typedef-type-attr-only) FOLDS-F104 (AreParamTypesCompatible ignores ensure_init; direct-decl spelling already probed 2026-06-29).
- Probe probes/ensure_init_variadic_extra_args.cbs: callee-side contract IS enforced on a VARIADIC fn (`void bad_fill(int *__attribute__((ensure_init)) p, ...) {}` → rc=1 "'*p' not initialized at return"). Caller-credit leg INCONCLUSIVE (probe artifact: `&_Mut x` is `int *_Borrow`, param was raw `int *` → cast error; retry with `int *_Borrow` param if ever revisited — low value, guard already source-confirmed).
